# Thread: From Fruit Bat to Great White
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-fruit-bat-to-great-white.8091/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdaptogenicBeing — 2013-08-29T16:49:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-fruit-bat-to-great-white.8091/post-94163>

Greetings everyone. I wanted to drop in and talk about some of the success Ive had since finding dr K and this site.

A bit about me. Ive been dealing with an inflammatory issue every fall/winter for the past 11 yrs. My fingers and toes would swell to the point of looking like sausages. Ive always been a "biohacker" of sorts and after conventional medicine had very little to offer in my case the hacking came more from necessity than fun. I had been told it was chilblanes and that I didn't show signs of Reynaud's. Regardless it was very much affecting my ability to work and just have a half way enjoyable life.

This led me into the path of raw veganism. I had read much talk in the natural hygiene movement that condemned meat fish and other foods as being at the root of the decline of the human condition(much of this based on some wonky science and nothing more). I was sure at that time I had found my magic bullet and for 3.5 years I followed a raw vegan diet mostly centered around fruit and veggies. I wont lie it did make me feel awesome for a period of 8 months. This was followed by a very slow decline. No matter how much I ate I always had a sense of depravation and more importantly the inflammation never went away and at some points I can say it got worse.

Towards the end of last summer I began to show signs of adrenal fatigue and leaky gut. I was unsure as to what it was at the time. In one last attempt to make myself better through raw I did a juice feast spanning 3 months mostly based on fruit juice(please hold back your laughs). Not only was it fruit based but it was during the winter as well. This only made things WAY worse. I decide to give up veganism all together after hearing Dave Asprey on a podcast. I listened to that podcast 3 times and finally became convinced doing what I was doing wasn't working. Failed N=1. Get over it and try something new. During the time of playing with Bulletproof I was sticking to the cyclical keto diet and what do ya know? When I was on no carb days my swelling disappeared(this was late Feb) The swelling in my extremities would perk back up on my reefed days. Sometime later I read the blog post Why We May Need Carbs for Performance and that was my eureka moment! Keto was probably my answer.

My adrenal/stomach issues continued to get worse however. At first I thought that it was the consumption of too much red meat so I cut back on that and upped my leafy green consumption(at the time I had just begun to read Jacks blogs. I was not yet on the seafood wagon) I noticed that as I ate more greens my brain fog got worse ie I would forget where I was for a few seconds at a clip, racing heart. All of these insane issues and keep in mind Im 31.

To make a short story long: I went to doctors. One of the offices is one of the top in the boston area. I was told to go on stat drugs and was handed a card for a psychiatrist. This was all in my head of course. I asked for an ASI or some sort of hormone panel to be done. I was shot down. I then sought out a functional med doc. He gave me the ASI I wanted as well as a slew of other tests. My cortisol ended up being low. He said that every thing else in the ASI was fine. Since listening to Dr K's webinars I now question this as my doc didn't look at the ratios. He also made the statement "progesterone means almost nothing in men". So ill need to dig that info up and review.

Anyway I am following a combo of both leptin reset and EPCOT. I here recently introduced Resveratrol and D-ribose into the mix. My brain fog has vanished. I no longer wake @ 2am with night sweats. And as for doing CT: Ive been a fan of Wim Hof for years and have been fascinated by the Tibetan tummos since I was in highschool. So ya I do it. I love IT! I am very much looking forward to following seasonal eating as laid out by Dr K/nature this coming fall and winter. I may have finally come across the info that will put the pain in my feet and hands to rest.

Be Well
Chad

# Thread: Chocolate
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.8094/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lsaremi — 2013-08-29T17:47:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.8094/post-94183>

I have been making chocolate out of coconut oil coconut flakes, Stevia, and cacao powder.... Is this ok to have?

Sent from my iPhone using Tapatalk - [now Free](http://tapatalk.com/m/)

### Danco3636 — 2013-08-29T18:55:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.8094/post-94204>

I make a delicious creamy Chocolate treat.
I use as a base organic coconut butter. 1/2 a jar.
Mix in some extra MCT oil and or coconut oil to make it a bit more creamier.
I then add in a teaspoon or two of organic cocoa - I use the Bulletproof organic 100% cocoa.
I then add in a 1/2 teaspoon of Bulletproof fresh ground vanilla.
Mix it up real good with a spoon, chill and then let warm back up to room temperature.
Turns out delicious.... A few table spoons or so as a great chocolate treat.
I don't add a sweetener as I don't need it but I can see how a dash of Stivia would bring out a sweet taste to it as well.

\- Cheers!

### Danco3636 — 2013-08-29T18:56:07+0300

# Thread: Relationship between prolactin and GH.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relationship-between-prolactin-and-gh.8096/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mally — 2013-08-29T18:38:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relationship-between-prolactin-and-gh.8096/post-94201>

Can you all help me understand the relationship between prolactin and growth hormone?

From what I've read on the forum, Kruse seems to be saying that a messed up circadian rhythm cycle causes a lack of prolactin surge which causes a lack of GH secretion (and possibly cortisol secretion later on in the early AM).

But I've also read that the most common picture is high prolactin, high IGF1 indicating high GH), low AM cortisol and high fasting blood sugar.

And that makes sense because bromocriptine/Cycloset would reduce the high prolactin and thus reduce the high GH which would lower the fasting blood sugar and raise the AM cortisol.

But I can't make a lack of prolactin surge work with lowering high prolactin levels from the second scenario.

Does Cycloset lower growth hormone or increase it? What is the relationship?

What have I missed?

Mally

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-29T18:55:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relationship-between-prolactin-and-gh.8096/post-94205>

CT 7 gives a great explaination of the circadian hormone cascade... [http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/](http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/)

Cycloset - lowers prolactin, increase am cortisol, and am dopamine

### Mally — 2013-08-29T19:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relationship-between-prolactin-and-gh.8096/post-94209>

Yes, CT 7 is exactly what got me totally confused.

It is saying that the prolactin surge is what is responsible for the GH surge.

But Cycloset lowers prolactin, it doesn't increase it.

So how does that work? It seems like Cycloset should lower GH secretion (because it is too high already in those with high prolactin) yet CT 7 is saying the opposite, I think.

I've missed something, just not sure what!

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-29T19:17:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relationship-between-prolactin-and-gh.8096/post-94210>

> [Mally said:](/goto/post?id=94209)
>
> Yes, CT 7 is exactly what got me totally confused.
>
> It is saying that the prolactin surge is what is responsible for the GH surge.
>
> But Cycloset lowers prolactin, it doesn't increase it.
>
> So how does that work? It seems like Cycloset should lower GH secretion (because it is too high already in those with high prolactin) yet CT 7 is saying the opposite, I think.
>
> I've missed something, just not sure what!

IIRC GH has two peaks, one at night and one during the day during its particular hormone circadian cycle. but I'll have to go back and look.

I'll have to go to my personal wiki and see if I can find the info I've put together on cycloset - but it in increase both dopamine and cortisol.

### Mally — 2013-08-29T19:51:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relationship-between-prolactin-and-gh.8096/post-94214>

Thank you Shijin13!

Also, supplements like mucuna are supposed to lower prolactin supposedly by increasing dopamine. This is similar to Cycloset as a dopamine agonist.

But these supplements also promote themselves as potent growth hormone releasers.

I'd just really like to know how both of those things (lower prolactin and higher GH) could be possible.

# Thread: The emf michael jackson link i spoke about????
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-emf-michael-jackson-link-i-spoke-about.8098/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-29T20:47:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-emf-michael-jackson-link-i-spoke-about.8098/post-94227>

Posted today on Aug 29th, 2013. Read it.
[http://www.cnn.com/2013/08/29/showbiz/michael-jackson-death-trial/index.html?hpt=hp\_t2](http://www.cnn.com/2013/08/29/showbiz/michael-jackson-death-trial/index.html?hpt=hp_t2)
Los Angeles (CNN) -- A nurse collapsed on the witness stand after describing her unsuccessful attempt to convince Michael Jackson not to use propofol to treat his insomnia.
"I can't do this anymore! I can't do this anymore," Cherilyn Lee cried at the end of Wednesday's testimony in trial to decide if concert promoter AEG Live is liable in Jackson's death. As she broke down, she was assisted by others in the courtroom including one of the lawyers.
Lee broke into tears after telling jurors that she believed people, including Jackson and her own mother, have died because they listened to bad advice from doctors who over-prescribe drugs.
The certified nurse practitioner -- who specializes in holistic health care -- continues her testimony Thursday about trying to help Jackson in his desperate search for sleep in the last months of his life.
MIchael Jackson's ex-wife cries on stand Paris Jackson's deposition Compare Michael Jackson in 2001 to 2009
Who's who in Jackson trial
Jackson would have turned 55 on Thursday had he not died from an overdose of the surgical anesthetic propofol on June 25, 2009.
AEG expert: Jackson was a drug addict
The singer's mother and children are suing AEG Live, claiming the company that was promoting and producing his comeback tour negligently hired, retained or supervised Dr. Conrad Murray.
The company's lawyers argue Jackson chose and controlled Murray and that its executives had no way of knowing about the treatments he was giving that ultimately killed the pop icon.
Murray used propofol infusions to put Jackson to sleep each night for two months, beginning soon after Lee stopped treating the singer's insomnia with IV drips of a vitamin cocktail.
Lee began treating Jackson in his Los Angeles home on February 1, 2009, days after he signed a three-year contract with AEG Live for a world tour, which would start with 50 shows in London to debut in July.
"My concern was that he was drinking Red Bulls," she said. He drank several cans of the energy drink during their first meeting. "I was thinking his tiredness and fatigue was related to that."
Jackson "started to feel really great" and "looked healthier" after a month of her IV treatments of Vitamin C and other nutrients, she testified.
But he still couldn't sleep more than five hours a night and with rehearsals for his "This Is It" tour cranking up in April "he needed something a little more," she testified.
Jackson rejected her recommendation that he have a sleep specialist visit his home to study his insomnia or that he cut down the lights and music in his bedroom, she said.
Earlier testimony suggested that Jackson had already given up on Lee's methods and decided that propofol, which German doctors had used to treat his insomnia during a 1997 tour, could be his answer for rest.
Jackson and Murray tried to recruit a Las Vegas anesthesiologist to join them on the tour in late March 2009, according to Dr. David Adams' video testimony shown to jurors last week.
"I just need you to help me get my rest," Adams said Jackson told him. "They were pretty vague, but on hindsight I know what they were talking about."
Murray, not Adams, eventually took the job.
Jackson was apparently still looking for a doctor to give him the propofol when Lee visited his home the morning of April 19, 2009.
Anesthesiologist: Jackson recruited me to help with insomnia
"He wasn't quite himself," she testified. "He just seemed really stressed or something. He said at certain points he was under a lot of pressure to finish rehearsals and he said 'I've got to get my sleep so I can do this.'"
Jackson told Lee he wanted her help in getting propofol infusions instead of the vitamin cocktail IVs, she said. Lee didn't know about the drug, so she looked it up in her Physician's Desk Reference manual.
"I remember telling him that it wasn't something he wanted to use at home," she said. "It wasn't a safe medication. It was definitely not a medication for insomnia."
Lee's handwritten notes from that day described their conversation: "I went as far as to say I understand you want a good night sleep -- want to be 'knocked out' -- but what if you don't wake up," she wrote. "He said 'I'll be ok. I only need someone to monitor me with equipment while I sleep.'"
Jackson "kept telling me 'You don't understand, doctors are telling me it's safe just as long as I am being monitored,'" Lee testified.
It was then that Lee collapsed on the witness stand. "It's so unfair," she cried. "I am so sick."
As Los Angeles County Superior Court Judge Yvette Palazuelos recessed court for the day and sent jurors out, AEG Live lawyer Sabrina Strong, who was sitting in the rear of the courtroom, ran to the witness stand to console the witness.
"That's not appropriate," Jackson lead lawyer Brian Panish protested. "Lawyers don't do that. It's not appropriate for lawyers to come out of the audience in front of the jury."
"Appropriate or not, it happened," Palazuelos said.
Panish argued that Strong was trying to "curry favor" with the jury by appearing compassionate. He demanded that the judge admonish her in front of the jury. The judge suggested he put his request in writing for her to consider.
Jackson's belief that propofol could help him sleep dated back to the late 1990s, according to another witness who testified Wednesday.
Dr. Catherine Quinn, a dentist who specializes in giving anesthesia during dental procedures, said Jackson asked her to infuse him with propofol in 1998.
"He told me that he has trouble sleeping," Quinn testified.
"I said that's inappropriate use of anesthesia," Quinn said. "He needs to speak with his physician about sleep aids. I told him that the sleep that you get with anesthesia is not real sleep, it's not restful sleep. He told me that it's the best sleep he ever had."
A drug addiction expert who testified Tuesday that Michael Jackson suffered a "quite extensive" drug addiction acknowledged Wednesday there was no evidence the singer used more painkillers than medically necessary.
The conclusion by Dr. Petros Levounis that Jackson was dependent on painkillers was not a revelation, considering Jackson himself announced it when he cut his "Dangerous" tour short to enter a rehab program in 1993.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-29T20:49:02+0300

# Thread: Seasonal Cold Thermogenesis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-cold-thermogenesis.8100/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BradTheBreather — 2013-08-30T01:44:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-cold-thermogenesis.8100/post-94268>

I live in a place with pretty warm summers and while I do love my ice baths, I can't help but feel that I'm fighting nature. If one of the main tenants of epi paleo is to eat seasonally, is it fair to extend that seasonal attitude to cold thermogenesis? By that I mean in the winter you do ice baths and maximum cold exposure, but during the summer you actually do the opposite with heat by spending time in saunas, not using A/C, using carbs as a fuel source, etc.

I understand that this is not a good idea if I want to live as long as possible, but might the going in and out of cold adaption have an effect on your biology in some way? For instance, a low carbohydrate diet is one way to manage bacterial and fungal overgrowth, but might there be some forms of beneficial bacteria and fungi that also thrive on a higher (still fat dominated) carb diet? I'm wondering if anyone has tried this

### caroline — 2013-08-30T02:47:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-cold-thermogenesis.8100/post-94283>

Great questions ......I hope someone has some imput.....

### yewwei.tan — 2013-08-30T04:27:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-cold-thermogenesis.8100/post-94294>

I do remember Dr Kruse mentioning that he originally thought that the adaptations to CT were likely seasonal-only, but that further empirical self experimentation on himself and others found that cold adaptation was kept right through the summer months. ie: You can reap the benefits of CT regardless of the season in which it is performed.

So it comes down to the question of: Do you badly need the benefits that CT can provide?

Got a major leaky gut? Then maybe aggressive CT through summer can be beneficial. Or maybe you're already pretty healthy in general, and then you can probably cut both the duration and frequency of CT, but still keep it in there regularly to maintain your brown fat stores for when winter rolls around again.

\---

Regarding carb intake. it should generally be the case where low carb diets serve to starve out any "bad" bacteria that is present. Re-population of "good" bacteria can generally be done via ingestion of pro-biotic foods, and your body's natural ability to restore gut flora (eg: from the appendix).

Paul Jaminet has speculated before that because some fungi can use ketones as fuel, that ketogenic diets may worsen certain fungal infections -- [http://perfecthealthdiet.com/2011/10/jimmy-moore’s-seminar-on-“safe-starches”-my-reply/](http://perfecthealthdiet.com/2011/10/jimmy-moore%E2%80%99s-seminar-on-%E2%80%9Csafe-starches%E2%80%9D-my-reply/)

Anyway, my personal take is that you should do your own n=1 experiments. Health of gut flora is generally pretty easy to track, by assessing stool quality (bristol score) and frequency. So long as those are in check, and doing consistently well (4 on the Bristol stool chart), you're doing good.

One should do whatever it takes to get to that baseline of good gut health, which again depends on your n=1 (eg: if you have H-pylori, you probably should be on a low carb diet regardless of season, with lots of oregano oil and anti-bacterials)

Once at that baseline, then why not just do some self-experimentation? Add some carbs (say 150-200g a day during the summer months), do that for a week, and then see how your gut health assessment is going along. If bad, revert back to what you know is safe, or maybe push up the carbs only slightly.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-30T05:16:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-cold-thermogenesis.8100/post-94302>

I haven't been following Dr. Kruse as long as most people on these boards, but I have been digging through all his blogs in chronological order, checking cites, asking questions, etc. for the past month or so - so take this with a grain of salt.

This is my current understanding based off of observations, especially surrounding Dr. Kruse comments. Read the CT-series and look through the comments, I have them compiled in Evernote, but there are too many to look through at this moment. Essentially, this is what I \*think\*:

(1) If you are diseased, and I'm speaking about most if not all diseases, go keto+CT full time to reverse disease. I think the key here is nutritonal ketosis and not ketones from post exercise ketosis, MCTs, ketone esters, etc. If your disease falls under diabesity, then you can use coconut oil/mct oil to get your engine going.
(2) If you are a performance oriented individual, the PPP is critical and CT should be used often if not full time - CT during training, during long events, for recovery, etc.
(3) Longevity: this is the grey area for me - would it be better to cycle your diet and 'biological state' (i.e. cold vs. warm adapted) with the seasons, OR, would you want to do ketosis+CT year round, and maybe even throw in some other things if you're looking for maximal life extension such as IF or CRON?
(4) Performance/PPP caveat: it seems as though with certain sports, you may want to really hammer down epi-paleo and CT and ketosis; for instance, long distance endurance events create massive ROS at cytochrome 1, and carbs do as well, you don't want to mix these. Now, let's say you are a MMA fighter, can you take 24-36mo's to fat and cold adapt, enter the PPP, and then use glucose AND ketones/fatty acids to fuel performance? Dave Asprey has alluded to athletes in the future using both fuels simultaneously, and Dr. Attia uses SuperStarch which refills glycogen with minimal/no insulin spike, thus maintaining ketosis... he has also said he can alter carb timing to maintain ketone production even when eating massive amounts of protein and carbs (and, keep in mind, he does not eat epi-paleo nor does he do CT). Dave has also said the can get back into ketosis within 24hrs after binging on carbs when eating out or something, and I've seen people on these boards say that they can get back into ketosis much easier now after a 'cheat' than they could've in the beginning.

So, there is a large grey area for me when it comes to longevity and/or specific performance. My idea right now, for myself, is: I live in Illinois, USA, and will for the next 10-24 months. I have what I believe to be HPPD, as well as some muscular imbalances and a disc bulge in my upper cervical area. My plan? Slowly cold adapt while eating a cyclical ketogenic clean epi-paleo/bulletproof diet, and as winter comes on drastically ramp up CT and maintain strict ketosis during the winter solstice for about 3-4 weeks. Use this in addition to transcranial red light photobiomodulation, and after I'm 'fixed', get back into martial arts, then, maintain cold adaptation throughout the year BUT eat with the seasons. That may create a mismatch, but, I will know based off of how I look, feel, and perform, as well as monitoring labs when the funds are available. I'm from northeastern/eastern European ancestry anyhow, so I'm sure cold adaptation would suite me well. I think the only caveat would be when I leave the state or country with my fiance, but, if I'm engaging in Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu competitions, or MMA or something, I'll maintain CT but eat with the seasons. Wim Hoff is cold adapted yet eats a diet seemingly opposite to someone who would live in a polar region, and he has some amazing abilities. I think cold alone would offer a huge performance advantage, then eat with the seasons for longevity. So, if I lived in Latin America where it was warm, but I was doing MMA/BJJ, do CT for recovery and performance enhancement, but eat a diet that was completely local, so probably a bit higher in carbs.

Another question that come to mind, is, what happens if you farm or start growing your own food. If I was doing CT and competing in martial arts, lived in Illinois soon, but grew my own Avocados, wouldn't that create a mismatch, especially when winter truly comes? I guess only self experimentation/quantification would tell.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-30T05:18:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-cold-thermogenesis.8100/post-94304>

# Thread: Labs/Tests to ask for tomorrow for my Hallucinogen Persisting Perceptual Disorder
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-tests-to-ask-for-tomorrow-for-my-hallucinogen-persisting-perceptual-disorder.8102/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-30T08:04:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-tests-to-ask-for-tomorrow-for-my-hallucinogen-persisting-perceptual-disorder.8102/post-94315>

Hey all, I have a really quick set of questions to ask. Awhile back I posted on the thread I created, "tDCS and My Existentialist State of Mind," about a lot of the symptoms I've been having. Everyone was very supportive and Dr. Kruse dropped some knowledge in there that I really appreciate. What I'm certain I have is **HPPD**, or, **Hallucinogen Persisting Perceptual Disorder** ([http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hallucinogen\_persisting\_perception\_disorder](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hallucinogen_persisting_perception_disorder)) caused from use of an anticholinergic deliriant drug that _may have caused_ acute anticholinergic syndrome - again, that's what I believe brought on HPPD which also causes me to have anxiety and bouts of depersonalization and maybe depression in addition to all the **trippy things I perceive** in my visual field. I'm an optimist/realist and I have also been researching a lot for the past 18 months, and I know that there are things such as neurogenesis, synaptogenesis, myelinogenesis, mitochondrial biogenesis, and so on, which all occur in our amazingly complex thermoplastic brains. Long story short, my family and I have very little money as I've posted before. It's hard enough for me to afford food, let alone getting extensive labs. Luckily, my mom (or maybe grandmother) called and set up an appointment with my grandmother's doctor who I have seen before for these problems, although I said they were occipital migraines rather than HPPD because I didn't want to come clean about my substance use at that point. He's pretty intelligent - in all honesty I think I probably know more bio-hacking information than he's heard of, and I know that's conceited coming from an 18 year old without a fully developed brain who has know medical training.... but he's a typical mainstream MD. He does believe in some alternative things, though, such as doing yoga and meditation as well as getting acupuncture. I believe if I get labs or ask for referrals to neurofeedback practitioners, and receive them through him, it is covered under my mothers insurance. **So, what should I ask for?
**
I know the VAP test can be used as a proxy for many other things, but my symptoms are all neurological/psychological/arising from the brain. Are there any tests that may be covered by insurance or discounted that I should get to monitor my brain? **Any tests that can serve as a proxy?** Maybe hormones associated with my circadian cycle/signalling? **Neurotransmitters**, since I've used substances like MDMA, MDA, and anticholinergics (DXM, diphenhydramine, and what I was laced with the day I suffered greatly - embalming fluid or PCP)?

**Any referrals I should ask for** besides some testing? I was thinking of asking for a referral to someone for a Functional Neurologic Examination, maybe a neurofeedback practitioner, maybe an EDMR based psychologist, but I don't know what else to ask for. My appointment is tomorrow at 2pm (Illinois time), so hopefully someone can give me some recommendations by tomorrow evening so I can ask. I plan on going to the library first and printing Dr. Kruse CT-6 post as well as some of my forum posts, in addition to some of Dave Asprey's stuff so he doesn't just look at me and think I'm crazier than I may be ;-)

Well, I understand if this is overwhelming and not worth answering, but I hope someone gives some input. Thanks in advance for ANY help I receive!

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-30T13:40:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-tests-to-ask-for-tomorrow-for-my-hallucinogen-persisting-perceptual-disorder.8102/post-94331>

Big Papa

I'm not entirely sure what labs might be of help to you today...

# Thread: BODY IN CRISIS MODE - help please?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-in-crisis-mode-help-please.8105/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### yorkiegirl — 2013-08-30T13:52:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-in-crisis-mode-help-please.8105/post-94334>

Hi, I am new to this forum, and in need of some serious help and advice on what to do/where to start?

I have been suffering with chronic chronic insomnia for 9years now, started when I got Crohn's IBD - all the stress and inflammation started it. Overactive nervous system.

I can't fall asleep or stay asleep (without taking a lot of prescription meds getting by on 3hrs a night only anyway), last Xmas they stopped working, I have been going 4/5 nights a week awake, it is SO severe and extreme, when I do get off to sleep I get 1 1/2hrs to 3 hrs if lucky. I have no sleep wake cycle or circadian rhythm. Tired but wired (now taking Phos serine helping a bit). I am also hypothyroid and think I have thyroid resistance, and have for a long time. Diagnosed 12 years ago with TSH of 43 (Yes, suicidal by this time).
Take T3 and Erfa (NDT). UK endo's don't believe in RT3!. He will not even consider the pills do not work. The sleep deprivation is and has caused a cascade of events.

I am in remission with Crohn's, 3 years now (a lot of operations), my weight dropped to 42kg, have now put on 57kg. But I have no skeletal muscle or muscle mass whatsoever.(lack of GH? - can't do IF or HITT..yet?) ageing fast.The muscles in my face are 'pulling down with such force', have to have icepack on face everyday to try stop the pain. Just pure body fat - very upsetting. I have been exercising 4/5 times a week to try and help, but all to no avail. Swimming mostly, I realise this is probably wrong as well to do, but wanted to try and tire myself out!. How can I get a circadian rhythm.Sleep Cycle again? I am extremely tired but never ever ever SLEEPY. (used to be able to sleep 14+plus with hypo symptoms).

I have lost all my breast tissue(no prolactin?).Severe hair loss (bone dry), mood and anxiety horrendously bad. Hypoglycaemia.In Osteopena section too.Insulin resistance, eat too many carbs and addicted. Increased appetite all round (probably down to sleep deprivation).All my hormones are out of whack.My body is in a catabolic state,, and just don't know what to do. Low Blood Pressure 71/31 pulse 48 (am I alive?), can't do Adrenal stress test because take so many pills, but now realise that high cortisol nighttime,(why I have only just found out that now..) low in day (can't function, no motivation/interest -most I can do is shopping list), ferritin 30 (trying to get up).

From what I have gathered I shouldn't go too low in carbs because of T3? So is Leptin rx, Keto or just Paelo better? What is if suffer low blood sugar? Can hypo people use all that fat?

Saw private Endo, who says I have hypoadrena and need Hydrocortisone, but due to bone loss don't really want to do this. Just so pleased came across this site, and needs to help myself.

Also my glands (left side of neck) hurt badly (have done for17 years), can feel them throbbing especially when body struggling to function. Have been eating lots of protein as suggested for breakfast - and now fat -no problem huge appetite.

My body is shutting down- it's just out of control -?

Sorry so long, but any help/advice appreciated.

Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-30T15:35:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-in-crisis-mode-help-please.8105/post-94338>

### caroline — 2013-08-31T03:37:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-in-crisis-mode-help-please.8105/post-94423>

Welcome to the forum ..... I am not going to call you by your handle because I think it sucks!! I wish you would pick something much more positive to call yourself - please ...

Please start a journal \[in the journal section\] right away so we can help you on this great journey to optimal. It will keep you committed and motivated - we all need to help each other. You never know when someone else's story has a huge impact on you and you can help motivate others as well.

If you can manage it - become a silver or gold member - altho you definitely don't have to ....the webinars and Q&A's are an amazing resource that help tremendously. Please listen to Dr. K.'s podcasts and get his book so you get a quick overview of all of this .....THE EPI-PALEO RX ...on amazon

The reason I suggest this stuff is because it has helped me tremendously .... it ups the commitment level. You really need to go all in - 100% and you will get changes you never thought possible.....There are plenty of really great success stories here.

read Mr. Pinkies journal the last couple of days....just astonishing what she and her family have achieved in the last year.

There is a ton of reading here and you have to keep re reading the blogs and all the comments ....and, please, keep asking questions.

You need to take charge of your own life and health and that is what Dr. Kruse is teaching us all to do.

### caroline — 2013-09-01T02:24:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-in-crisis-mode-help-please.8105/post-94539>

You need to listen to Jack's latest Blogs ASAP .... they are a great way to take in a lot of information quickly \[did I say that already?\]
You need to look at the EMF exposure in your life - the most important part of all this. Yes - food is important \[epi-paleo\] but it is only 10% of the equation. We are all living in EMF soup and it is severely altering our field of existence.

You need to be observing light cycles ....... so no lights on after the sunset. Basically when the sun sets ...you have to too!

Limit TV and computer time. Take everything out of your bedroom - like clock radio and tv and cut the power to your bedroom at night if you can. I have a separate switch for my bedroom. Do you have a smart meter on your house?

Sorry - this is a lot to throw at you I know - please ask questions.....

### caroline — 2013-09-01T02:31:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-in-crisis-mode-help-please.8105/post-94541>

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-water-everywhere-but-not-a-drop-to-drink.8106/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2013-08-30T15:54:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-water-everywhere-but-not-a-drop-to-drink.8106/post-94342>

great article on where there's water, and where there isn't water...

[http://www.resilientcommunities.com/the-best-locations-for-plentiful-water/](http://www.resilientcommunities.com/the-best-locations-for-plentiful-water/)

the interactive map is awesome too [http://aqueduct.wri.org/atlas](http://aqueduct.wri.org/atlas)

looking at the map, you can tell why Water was one of Jack's biggest drivers...for his location, along with sun

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-08-30T17:48:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-water-everywhere-but-not-a-drop-to-drink.8106/post-94351>

That interactive map site is major win... thanks so much for posting this!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-30T19:38:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-water-everywhere-but-not-a-drop-to-drink.8106/post-94361>

Our problem begins with water. The Earth is covered in it, but most of it is sea water we and plants can not use. Life needs fresh water. 71% of the Earth is covered with water. 96.5% of it is in the ocean, 1.7 % glaciers and polar regions, 1.7 % in ground water and lakes and rivers, and 0.001% is in air as water vapor of the clouds. Out of all that, only a total of 2.5 % of the water on our planet is fresh water in ice and groundwater. Less than 0.3% is in lakes rivers and our atmosphere.

This is a real issue for modern life, because modern depletion rates are astounding. If you read the aqua-diversity literature as I do, you would see in 2010 a major meta-analysis showed that the combined effects of pollution, dam-building, and agriculture run off , conversion of wetlands, and the introduction of exotic species to new habitats is destroying the fresh water cycle we depend upon. We are not going to run out of sunlight, but we are already running dry on water and no one seems to know it. I’d suggest you Google Viktor Schauberger name as a water scientist and see what he found about this phenomena. You might also like to read about Alick Bartholomew’s story of water to get “totally informed” on this issue.

When water falls, oxygen levels also fall for life in kind. They are inextricably linked, evolutionarily speaking. In fact, after the Cambrian explosion life did well for about 250 million years and then the atmospheric oxygen levels suddenly dropped from 30% to 15% in a short time. This extinction event was called the Great Dying Event. During the Permian-Triassic transition oxygen fell abruptly and caused Earth’s largest extinction. It killed 57% of all phylogenetic families, 83% of all genera and 90% to 96% of all species. 53% of all marine families were wiped off the planet, 84% of marine genera, about 96% of all marine species and an estimated 70% of land species, including all insects.

This shows you just how important water and oxygen are to life. Without the ability to generate energy from aerobic metabolism life had to get a do over, from an environmental trigger.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-30T19:38:40+0300

### AnnieW — 2013-08-30T20:26:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-water-everywhere-but-not-a-drop-to-drink.8106/post-94367>

Mountain building events or bolide impact>smoke and ash causing sun blackout>climate change and ocean pH and salinity change>death/extinctions. That's basically the way I learned it. I had a professor in college who didn't necessarily poo-poo global warming, but explained it as us coming out of an ice age, so according to him, the warming we experience today is part Earth's natural cycle. He essentially said people are blowing global warming out of proportion. Now I'm not so sure about that given what I am learning about EMF.

One class which was really interesting was hydrology. We all had to do presentations on various issues relating to water. I chose to do mine on PCBs and used the Hudson River as my study area. That was probably the most shocking research I ever did. Another guy did his presentation on trying to maintain the health of the Chesapeake Bay and prevent algal blooms, especially since the bay is surrounded with farms and thus fertilizers. Water is so important. It kills me when I hear about people flushing their medications and God knows what other chemicals down the toilet. How much of that really gets filtered out?

I cannot stand tap water. It smells so bad and I can always tell when it's been treated. I'm glad I've been drinking spring water since I was in college.

For you guys who are teachers, this is a great website. This company sells interactive tabletop models you can buy which deal with water, pollution, waste, and recycling:

[http://www.enviroscapes.com/](http://www.enviroscapes.com/)

Where I work, we have the watershed pollution model. We bring it into the public schools and demonstrate to different age groups. Kids like seeing how it all works. We use dry Kool-Aid for the fertilizers, cocoa powder for the construction dirt, and chocolate sprinkles for the dog poop. Oh, and Hershey's chocolate syrup for the industrial waste. Then we take a spray bottle and spray it all over the model and the kids watch all of this stuff run into the pond in the community. It's a great visual example of how what we do ends up in our environment. Then we talk to them about what they should do to take care of their community and what to do if they see someone dumping anything down the storm drain. It's a really fun and educational exercise.

Then in my mind I think...don't drink the tap water kids, because the city is polluting it with flouride. LOL

### nonchalant — 2013-08-30T20:48:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-water-everywhere-but-not-a-drop-to-drink.8106/post-94369>

You could use Mountain Dew for the fluoride...

Oh, that's right, fluoride is BeneficialForTeeth and NOT a pollutant. I forgot.

# Thread: How long to notice effects of Armour

### fireforlunch — 2013-08-30T17:45:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-to-notice-effects-of-armour.8107/post-94350>

Found a wonderful doc who takes a more holistic approach and is willing to treat my hypothyroid with Armour.

She was willing to go off the numerous amount of labs that I've had in the past 3 months and then will do her own labs in a month.

I've been on it since Tuesday - 60mg once per day.

I was wondering how long does it take to feel the positive effects?

My recent thyroid labs are as follows:

TSH - 4.69
Free T3 - 1.9
Reverse T3 - 14.5
Free T4 - .8

thanks for your input

### cantweight — 2013-08-30T17:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-to-notice-effects-of-armour.8107/post-94352>

I felt effects from Armour very quickly....within 2-3 days. My hair showed changes in texture and color...it darkened. I felt a slight elevation in general....maybe mood and energy....just a slight overall better feeling. And the tightness in my throat went away.

If you are feeling nothing maybe your dose is not high enough. If I remember correctly....it was back in 2006....my holistic doc started me on a dose and we kept raising it until there was some improvement in symptoms, we also tested every 6 weeks. At one point we raised it and I had a rapid heart beat...we backed it down immediately and I have been at that dose ever since. 150mg daily.

### nicld — 2013-08-30T18:27:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-to-notice-effects-of-armour.8107/post-94358>

For me, 90 mg of Armour did not help even though I have been on it for 18 months. Just saw a new doc and took one look at my labs and saw that my RT3 was way to high and the Free T3 way to low meaning that Armour was just not getting converted properly. Just stated taking Cytomel 2X a day to help the T3 get converted along with iodine and selenium. If you are not feeling better ask about adding that too.

Good luck

### MeghanK — 2013-08-31T03:29:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/himalayan-salt-lamps-shield-emfs.8109/post-94420>

Which brand is yours?

### MeghanK — 2013-08-31T03:33:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/himalayan-salt-lamps-shield-emfs.8109/post-94421>

There is absolutely no salt mine to be found anywhere in this Himalayan region, in fact the closest salt mines are in Pakistan.
**
Pink "Himalayan" salt contains fluoride and heavy metals.** IS THIS TRUE????

Heating salt crystals with the minimal amount of heat coming out of a lightbulb is going to release MINIMMAL negative ions if any.

An approaching or passing thunderstorm is going to be producing significantly more negative ions than a block of salt being heated by a lightbulb, in fact just outdoors generally should have more.

There is a pretty good chance that the heating has nothing to do with releasing ions and that you may in fact only generate negative ions (minimal) in the first few minutes of plugging it in/turning it on based on the photoelectric effect.

If heating salt in fact released negative ions, anytime you add salt to a boiling pot of water, cooked food containg ANY salt, microwaved food containg ANY salt, ate salt... you'd be releasing 'tons of negative ions!!!'. However, none of these situations produce appreciable (if any) amounts of negative ions.

Your shower (moving water) is likely to produce far more negative ions than a block of salt with a lamp in the middle. **THOUGHTS???**

### MeghanK — 2013-08-31T03:34:35+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/himalayan-salt-lamps-shield-emfs.8109/post-149918>

I think it has a piezoelectric effect in that it absorbs radiation... like quartz except better - my dentist has one next to each machine she uses... the idea being that the rock will absorb the radiation instead of your cellular water... shungite and quartz do the same thing - as always, do your own research...

[https://books.google.com/books?id=P...zoelectric effect himalayan salt lamp&f=false](https://books.google.com/books?id=Pzun0xgvVuMC&pg=PA1744&lpg=PA1744&dq=piezoelectric+effect+himalayan+salt+lamp&source=bl&ots=42zAn7su3i&sig=S8R9pxfuxBZCC5dGc3mvuKoMAuQ&hl=en&sa=X&ei=wRuLVLf9Goi4oQSjooDICg&ved=0CEQQ6AEwBg#v=onepage&q=piezoelectric%20effect%20himalayan%20salt%20lamp&f=false)

If you want negative ions, Dr. K recommends this ion generator/light in his sleep and cirdadean biology store:
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0...iveASIN=B000W8Y7FY&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000W8Y7FY/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B000W8Y7FY&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20)

which is here:
[http://jackkruse.com/catalog/sleep-and-circadian-biology-store/](http://jackkruse.com/catalog/sleep-and-circadian-biology-store/)

or you can listen to this guy: Alan Reed - he knows more about EMF and rocks than most IMHO... this interview is only good for the rest of today - and it is awesome
[http://emfsummit.com/alan-reed/](http://emfsummit.com/alan-reed/)

### nicld — 2014-12-12T21:00:27+0200

I have this and really like it. I use it while I am getting ready in the morning but might have to look into using the ion part in the evening. The DD even came one morning and sat on the couch right next to the lamp. Have to try and remember to use it more on her too.

I also have 2 salt lamps, one at work and one at home. When I started using the one at work I got all sorts of weird feelings and could only have it on for a limited time. Now it is better and I have it on all day. Also have the one at home on during the evening and it does put out such a nice soft light.

# Thread: My n=1: Stoppingantioxidantsinhopesofrestoringinsulinsensi tivityandstoppingsleepapnea
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-stoppingantioxidantsinhopesofrestoringinsulinsensi-tivityandstoppingsleepapnea.8110/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### avjgirsijdhtjhs — 2013-08-30T19:50:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-stoppingantioxidantsinhopesofrestoringinsulinsensi-tivityandstoppingsleepapnea.8110/post-94363>

**Don't know whether here, or "Meet and Greet", or "The Epi-Paleo Diet" is the best forum to put this thread in.**

Starting my n=1: Stopping antioxidant supplementation in hopes of restoring insulin sensitivity and getting rid of my sleep apnea.

My background info:

1 - I'm a 5 foot 10 inch tall guy that was born in 1983.

2 - I found out about the health effects of omega-3s in 2010 and started taking them in the form of fish and fish oil.

3 - In order to counter the ease of oxidation of omega-3s (or at least their ease of oxidation outside of the body) I also started taking 400 IU Vitamin E (as DL-Alpha Tocopherol Acetate per day in 2010.

4 - Sometime in 2010, my sleep got EXTREMELY unrefreshing. I'd wake up exhausted and would remain that way all day (this's how it's been literally every single day (zero exceptions) since then). This is even after getting 7 to 9 hours of sleep each night for at various times a few weeks straight at a time (though for probably at least two thirds of this time, I'd wake up after 5-6.5 hours of sleep, get up and pee (no lights turned on, and no sunshine coming through the windows, since it's still dark out), and then either get back in bed and try to fall back asleep, where I usually had like a one in three chance of falling back asleep, or turn on the lights and be "up" for the day).

5 - Last year at the end of November, and having found out that I could see if I had sleep apnea (though I've weighed in the mid and even low 160's (pounds) clothed in shorts, t-shirt, shoes, pockets stuffed, etc for much of the last three years), and having found out that there're plenty of people that aren't fat at all that have obstructive sleep apnea, I found out that you could wear a recording pulse oximeter at night to check for O2 drops, so I bought one. On the 2 or 3 nights that I wore it at night, I had lots of back-to-back (like as in ~1 minute apart) O2 drops into the usually low 90's to mid 80's. I also had a sleep study in early January of this year, but did not fall asleep. I also have not had anybody obseve me sleeping in these last three years. I also had my adenoids removed as a 3rd grader (so 8 to 9 years old), because of some sleep breathing abnormalities that my mom had observed me doing (and at the time and ever since then until November of last year, I had never even known or realized that I almost certainly had obstructive sleep apnea as a kid - at least not until November of last year, when I had my "AHA!" moment and realized I had almost certainly had it as a kid, and then bought an oximeter).

6 - Okay, so 6 days ago I saw this: [Sleep apnea is a manifestation of the metabolic syndrome](http://www.neurosono.com.br/arquivos/1075346903.pdf). I started taking Chromium that day, in hopes of lowering suspected insulin resistance (my fasting blood sugar (and having taken no other blood sugar raising substances like caffeine or nicotine, etc (had only taken a stool softener and magnesium citrate and water that morning)) at 1-something pm on the afternoon in October of last year was 102 mg\\dl, and after that, I decided to cut out the potatoes and breakfast cereal (that were the two highest glycemic things i commonly ate), started drinking apple cider vinegar and psyllium fiber with meals that were even moderately glycemic (both help lower the glycemic index of the meal), and then in the spring of this year took it even further, and started eating the vast majority of my carbs as vegetables that were no higher glycemic index than legumes (which I ate a lot of)).

7 - Two days ago, I found out that antioxidants can cause insulin resistance, so I stopped taking the Vitamin E that day (the only other antioxidant supplement i take is some vitamin C 2-ish times a week to increase iron absorption (don't take a multivitamin either)).
**
So here are some (hopefully) juicy and interest piquing (for you) quotes followed by the article or study they're taken from (also, the reason that some of the quotes are a little fragmented is because when I see some text on a webpage that I want to remember, I highlight\\copy the text, and then save the page with the highlighted text as the title of the saved webpage, and because you can only have so many characters in the file's name, I often have to cut off part of what I wanted to highlight in order to get the saved page's filename short enough - that, and I don't feel like hunting through the articles\\studies to find the full quote). And also note that all of my further comments in this post will be in red:**

First, it has beenreported that in humans and animals, insulin-
dependent diabetes mellitus can lead to an overall
depression in ventilatory control mechanisms,
91
whereas both leptin deficiency and leptin resist-
ance may lead to respiratory depression in obesity,
especially during sleep.
92
Second, the reported
inflammation of upper airway tissues may be
related primarily to obesity, a condition of systemic
inflammation. Third, obesity/insulin resistance, by
releasing growth factors, may lead to soft tissue
edema in the neck. Finally, male type of obesity
(central) may impact more severely on upper
airway function than female type of obesity
(peripheral).
93
Thus, the observed hypercytokine-
mia, hyperleptinemia, and hyperinsulinemia/visc-
eral adiposity, through central and peripheral
effects, may lead to a collapse of the upper airway
during sleep. <------- What I am hoping is that stopping the vitamin E and possibly including some sort of exercise will be all that is needed to decrease suspected insulin resistance that is causing highly suspected sleep apnea. If not that, then I figure I should try the Leptin Reset in addition to stopping the vitamin E.

[Sleep apnea is a manifestation of the metabolic syndrome](http://www.neurosono.com.br/arquivos/1075346903.pdf)

high doses of antioxidants may interfere with cellular processes in a way that increases insulin resistance

He said oxidative stress may be working not to damage the body but to inhibit enzymes that hurt the body's ability to use insulin early on in the development of diabetes, and that antioxidants remove this protective mechanism.
[
Antioxidant Vitamins Raise Insulin Resistance and Render Exercise Useless](http://diabetesupdate.blogspot.com/2009/10/antioxidant-vitamins-raise-insulin.html)

Exercise increased parameters of insulin sensitivity (GIR and plasma adiponectin) only in the absence of antioxidants in both

the increase in insulin sensitivity following physical exercise are almost completely abrogated by daily ingestion of the commonly used antioxidants vitamin C and vitamin E

mitohormesis, exercise-induced oxidative stress ameliorates insulin resistance and causes an adaptive response promoting endogenous antioxidant defense capacity

mediators of endogenous ROS defense (superoxide dismutases 1 and 2; glutathione peroxidase) were also induced by exercise, and this effect too was blocked by antioxidant supplementation

in addition to the increase in insulin sensitivity following exercise-induced ROS formation, we also observe an induction of all relevant ROS defense enzyme expression levels, namely

were obtained in models of continuous exposure to increased levels of ROS (24, 25), whereas our current findings and those of other studies (23, 26) may reflect transient increases in ROS

strongly suggest that oxidative stress can be instrumental in preventing type 2-diabetes

[Antioxidants prevent health-promoting effects of physical exercise in humans](http://www.pnas.org/content/106/21/8665.full)

[Antioxidants may raise diabetes risk: study](http://www.reuters.com/article/2009/10/06/us-diabetes-antioxidants-idUSTRE5955Z620091006)

[How increased oxidative stress promotes longevity and metabolic health: The concept of mitochondrial hormesis (mitohormesis).](http://www.jenage.de/assets/pdfs/library/ristow_zarse_EXPERIMENTAL_GERONTOLOGY_2010.pdf)

Right now, I'm reading this stuff on vitamin E half-life, since I've been taking 400 IU per day of Vitamin E as DL-Alpha Tocopherol Acetate for about three years now ------> [Quantitation of \[5-14CH3\]-(2R, 4â€™R,
8â€™R)-a-Tocopherol in Humans1â€“3](http://jn.nutrition.org/content/early/2011/06/29/jn.111.138925.full.pdf)

3 - In order to counter the ease of oxidation of omega-3s (or at least their ease of oxidation outside of the body) I also started taking 400 IU Vitamin E (as DL-Alpha Tocopherol Acetate per day in 2010.

4 - Sometime in 2010, my sleep got EXTREMELY unrefreshing. I'd wake up exhausted and would remain that way all day (this's how it's been literally every single day (zero exceptions) since then). This is even after getting 7 to 9 hours of sleep each night for at various times a few weeks straight at a time (though for probably at least two thirds of this time, I'd wake up after 5-6.5 hours of sleep, get up and pee (no lights turned on, and no sunshine coming through the windows, since it's still dark out), and then either get back in bed and try to fall back asleep, where I usually had like a one in three chance of falling back asleep, or turn on the lights and be "up" for the day).

5 - Last year at the end of November, and having found out that I could see if I had sleep apnea (though I've weighed in the mid and even low 160's (pounds) clothed in shorts, t-shirt, shoes, pockets stuffed, etc for much of the last three years), and having found out that there're plenty of people that aren't fat at all that have obstructive sleep apnea, I found out that you could wear a recording pulse oximeter at night to check for O2 drops, so I bought one. On the 2 or 3 nights that I wore it at night, I had lots of back-to-back (like as in ~1 minute apart) O2 drops into the usually low 90's to mid 80's. I also had a sleep study in early January of this year, but did not fall asleep. I also have not had anybody obseve me sleeping in these last three years. I also had my adenoids removed as a 3rd grader (so 8 to 9 years old), because of some sleep breathing abnormalities that my mom had observed me doing (and at the time and ever since then until November of last year, I had never even known or realized that I almost certainly had obstructive sleep apnea as a kid - at least not until November of last year, when I had my "AHA!" moment and realized I had almost certainly had it as a kid, and then bought an oximeter).

6 - Okay, so 6 days ago I saw this: **[Sleep apnea is a manifestation of the metabolic syndrome](http://www.neurosono.com.br/arquivos/1075346903.pdf)**. I started taking Chromium that day, in hopes of lowering suspected insulin resistance (my fasting blood sugar (and having taken no other blood sugar raising substances like caffeine or nicotine, etc (had only taken a stool softener and magnesium citrate and water that morning)) at 1-something pm on the afternoon in October of last year was 102 mg\\dl, and after that, I decided to cut out the potatoes and breakfast cereal (that were the two highest glycemic things i commonly ate), started drinking apple cider vinegar and psyllium fiber with meals that were even moderately glycemic (both help lower the glycemic index of the meal), and then in the spring of this year took it even further, and started eating the vast majority of my carbs as vegetables that were no higher glycemic index than legumes (which I ate a lot of)).

7 - Two days ago, I found out that antioxidants can cause insulin resistance, so I stopped taking the Vitamin E that day (the only other antioxidant supplement i take is some vitamin C 2-ish times a week to increase iron absorption (don't take a multivitamin either)).

**So here are some (hopefully) juicy and interest piquing (for you) quotes followed by the article or study they're taken from (also, the reason that some of the quotes are a little fragmented is because when I see some text on a webpage that I want to remember, I highlight\\copy the text, and then save the page with the highlighted text as the title of the saved webpage, and because you can only have so many characters in the file's name, I often have to cut off part of what I wanted to highlight in order to get the saved page's filename short enough - that, and I don't feel like hunting through the articles\\studies to find the full quote). And also note that all of my further comments in this post will be in red:**

First, it has beenreported that in humans and animals, insulin-
dependent diabetes mellitus can lead to an overall
depression in ventilatory control mechanisms,
91
whereas both leptin deficiency and leptin resist-
ance may lead to respiratory depression in obesity,
especially during sleep.
92
Second, the reported
inflammation of upper airway tissues may be
related primarily to obesity, a condition of systemic
inflammation. Third, obesity/insulin resistance, by
releasing growth factors, may lead to soft tissue
edema in the neck. Finally, male type of obesity
(central) may impact more severely on upper
airway function than female type of obesity
(peripheral).
93
Thus, the observed hypercytokine-
mia, hyperleptinemia, and hyperinsulinemia/visc-
eral adiposity, through central and peripheral
effects, may lead to a collapse of the upper airway
during sleep. <------- What I am hoping is that stopping the vitamin E and possibly including some sort of exercise will be all that is needed to decrease suspected insulin resistance that is causing highly suspected sleep apnea. If not that, then I figure I should try the Leptin Reset in addition to stopping the vitamin E.

**[Sleep apnea is a manifestation of the metabolic syndrome](http://www.neurosono.com.br/arquivos/1075346903.pdf)**

high doses of antioxidants may interfere with cellular processes in a way that increases insulin resistance

He said oxidative stress may be working not to damage the body but to inhibit enzymes that hurt the body's ability to use insulin early on in the development of diabetes, and that antioxidants remove this protective mechanism.

**[Antioxidant Vitamins Raise Insulin Resistance and Render Exercise Useless](http://diabetesupdate.blogspot.com/2009/10/antioxidant-vitamins-raise-insulin.html)**

Exercise increased parameters of insulin sensitivity (GIR and plasma adiponectin) only in the absence of antioxidants in both

the increase in insulin sensitivity following physical exercise are almost completely abrogated by daily ingestion of the commonly used antioxidants vitamin C and vitamin E

mitohormesis, exercise-induced oxidative stress ameliorates insulin resistance and causes an adaptive response promoting endogenous antioxidant defense capacity

mediators of endogenous ROS defense (superoxide dismutases 1 and 2; glutathione peroxidase) were also induced by exercise, and this effect too was blocked by antioxidant supplementation

in addition to the increase in insulin sensitivity following exercise-induced ROS formation, we also observe an induction of all relevant ROS defense enzyme expression levels, namely

were obtained in models of continuous exposure to increased levels of ROS (24, 25), whereas our current findings and those of other studies (23, 26) may reflect transient increases in ROS

strongly suggest that oxidative stress can be instrumental in preventing type 2-diabetes

**[Antioxidants prevent health-promoting effects of physical exercise in humans](http://www.pnas.org/content/106/21/8665.full)**
**[
Antioxidants may raise diabetes risk: study](http://www.reuters.com/article/2009/10/06/us-diabetes-antioxidants-idUSTRE5955Z620091006)**

**[How increased oxidative stress promotes longevity and metabolic health: The concept of mitochondrial hormesis (mitohormesis).](http://www.jenage.de/assets/pdfs/library/ristow_zarse_EXPERIMENTAL_GERONTOLOGY_2010.pdf)**

Right now, I'm reading this stuff on vitamin E half-life, since I've been taking 400 IU per day of Vitamin E as DL-Alpha Tocopherol Acetate for about three years now ------> **[Quantitation of \[5-14CH3\]-(2R, 4â€™R,
8â€™R)-a-Tocopherol in Humans1â€“3](http://jn.nutrition.org/content/early/2011/06/29/jn.111.138925.full.pdf)**

Starting my n=1: Stopping antioxidant supplementation in hopes of restoring insulin sensitivity and getting rid of my sleep apnea.

My background info:

1 - I'm a 5 foot 10 inch tall guy that was born in 1983.

2 - I found out about the health effects of omega-3s in 2010 and started taking them in the form of fish and fish oil.

# Thread: How to make coconut oil palatable?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AnnieW — 2013-08-30T23:03:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94394>

I love cooking with CO and the energy it gives me. It's not the taste...I love the taste. I think it's a textural thing, and psychological because it's solid fat and for some reason it grosses me out. Is there anything I can do to make it more tasty? I've tried melting it and taking it that way, which isn't horrible but it's not great either. I've tried eating it solid out of the jar and it is almost gag inducing for me. I love the taste, it's just the texture. Any suggestions?

### nicld — 2013-08-30T23:32:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94397>

Uhmmm mix it with unsweetened flaked coconut, stevia and cocoa powder. It is AWESOME. I call it my coconut crack.

I too have tried to eat it solid and it is a little off putting. Kinda just let it sit in the mouth and melt.

### caroline — 2013-08-31T03:44:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94426>

MCT oil stays liquid ...altho it doesn't have any taste.......I love coconut!

### endless — 2013-08-31T05:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94440>

Blend it into coffee or tea...

### PrimalPam — 2013-08-31T18:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94496>

Since coconut oil liquefies at room temperature, try letting it get a little warmer and just put a spoonful in your mouth.

I put it in my coffee every day and don't even notice it.

How about just putting a couple of tbsp.'s on a salad?

### Grantmeliberty — 2013-09-02T08:39:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94664>

Melting point for coconut oil is 78 degrees F. Depends upon how warm your house is. I have been using coconut oil for about 8 years. I love it.

### nicld — 2013-09-02T17:33:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94684>

I put the solid stuff in my morning coffee too and now that it is cooler here it did go back to solid.

# Thread: Ready to exit this hormonal storm
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ready-to-exit-this-hormonal-storm.8113/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-08-30T23:29:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ready-to-exit-this-hormonal-storm.8113/post-94396>

Today I got my BHRT progesterone cream, the final piece of the balancing therapy.

I applied my first dose right away. I am just a day late but the doc said to start as soon as I get it and keep going to day 28 even though cycles have been 25 day.

I feel like I have been in the middle of a horrible storm and I WANT OUT. Sleep has been getting crappy again and energy is still low. I am hoping with all of the supplements and new meds that the seas will part.

Finally, I found a doctor that listens and says that I can get better, hallelujah!!!

He said that for some reason my body is NOT using the T4 to convert the T3 that I am getting from the 90 mg in the Armour that I am taking. He reduced the Armour and added cytomel 2X a day. He also let me get some BHRT cream for the low progesterone and bad Pg/E2 ratio.

So, now I have a plan to get balanced and here's what I am doing.

Armour - 60 mg in the morning
cytomel - 12.5 mg 2X a day
Progesterone 100 mg cream - 1 gram 2x a day
Selenium - 200 mcg a day
Iodine - Iodoral 12.5 mg 1x a day
Betaine HCL- 1200 mcg 2-3X a day
Fish oil -
Vitamin D - 8000 iu's a day
B12 - 3000ug with folic acid in it 1X a day
B complex
Iron - 65 mg 1X a day
Biotin
Probiotic
Digestive Enzyme
Bitter Melon - 1X before CT

Leptin Reset with my BAB everyday and that usually means some bacon.
CT almost everyday with long cold ones on the weekends.
Try to reduce the EMF's.
Blue Blocker glasses at night.
Rewarming and grounding in the sun.
Try to NOT bundle up so much and embrace the cold.
Eat seasonally which means eating a ton from my garden.
Besides BAB, eat when hungry and stop when full.
Eat more seafood and grass fed beef and pork. Hopefully I can get my hands on some more venison too.

Have to admit that I am kinda of scared that this will not work but I am trying to have faith that it will. I am sure that there may be some more bumps in the road and more tweeking. I have also learned that I am in charge of my health.

So hopefully I am finally on the road to optimal. Hang on, this might get bumby.

Nicole

### JanSz — 2013-08-31T01:07:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ready-to-exit-this-hormonal-storm.8113/post-94401>

/////////

Would I then apply 2 grams then and what is the benefit of applying it all at once?

Where would the best place to apply it and I did read to change spots?

### caroline — 2013-08-31T02:49:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ready-to-exit-this-hormonal-storm.8113/post-94409>

Sounds like you have a great plan Nicole .... just keep at it and tweaking and movement isn't always forward. There maybe some twists and turns in the road...

You are a strong woman - and you are working all the protocols ....just trust in all of this and yourself ....you are now in charge!!!

# Thread: A new biomarker for aging emerges
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-biomarker-for-aging-emerges.8114/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JanSz — 2013-08-30T23:53:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-biomarker-for-aging-emerges.8114/post-94398>

in e-mail from Spectracell.

[http://www.spectracell.com/media/up...act2013intjepidmetabolic-markers-of-aging.pdf](http://www.spectracell.com/media/uploaded/2/0e2369161_1376149572_2067abstract2013intjepidmetabolic-markers-of-aging.pdf)

A new biomarker for aging emerges

In this large study, blood samples from over 6000 people were collected and analyzed for 280 different metabolic markers. When the researchers correlated biomarkers to clinical aging, they found that one biomarker - **glycosylated tryphtophan (C-glyTrp)** - emerged that was particularly interesting since it had not been previously identified as a marker of aging, yet it correlated well with both chronological age and age-related traits such as lung function and bone density. The authors stated that C-glyTrp "may point to novel molecular pathways involved in aging," especially since tryptophan metabolism has previously been linked to aging in animal studies.
(International Journal of Epidemiology, July 2013)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Aussies and the donut hole!

### caroline — 2013-08-31T02:30:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aussies-and-the-donut-hole.8115/post-94407>

So ..... in the Q&A the other day - Dr. K. talked about the ozone layer and the donut hole over Australia.

I am wondering how we should be dealing with this .... extra precautions? How should us Aussies be dealing with this?

I feel great, don't crave carbs \[eat very few\] don't burn at all - even when out in the hot sun for hours and hours. I got extraordinarily brown ...that has never happened before - I would always burn.

My vitamin D is high - at least it was - I will check again.

What have we missed?

Jude - what is your experience?

Nick - lucky guy with a magnetico .....please tell us how that is going...

Freesia and Corriander ...what is your take on this?

Any other Aussies?

Another thing that I don't understand - Dr. K. sits in the sun and turns himself into a lobster......

### freesia — 2013-08-31T07:11:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aussies-and-the-donut-hole.8115/post-94443>

Hey Caroline,

My plan is to CT right on through this coming Summer like I did the last one. There might be a week in January when I'm camping away from home and don't have access to ice, otherwise I'll try to CT every couple of days.

### Nick — 2013-09-01T05:07:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aussies-and-the-donut-hole.8115/post-94566>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=94407)
>
> Nick - lucky guy with a magnetico .....please tell us how that is going...

Hi Caroline

I need to catch up on the Q&A on this one, sounds like there have been some bombshells going off. Low Carb, Epi-Paleo and CT all year doesn't sound like too much of head bender for us in Australia. Thanks Jack, for throwing a bit more love to us Aussies!

To answer your question Caroline, The Magnetico is awesome! We have two now, one 10G for my wife and I, and 1 5G for our 3yo. he has been the main driver for the magnetico as Jack gave us some pretty focussed advice a several months ago about how to tackle his massive list of allergies and eczema. Turns out he was sleeping next to all of our wireless devices on the other side of the wall and a big constant 12mgauss constant field from a fridge. We have removed as much emf as possible, esp at night, all wifi is gone, all phones turned off, no electrical devices turned on in any bed room, fridge has been moved and magneticos purchased. We also turn off lots of lights after sun down, eat early and feed the kids lots of fatty meats wherever possible. I would like to say we do this perfectly but our experience with a 3yo falls short from time to time, bananas in winter ahhhhhh. At least he eats heaps of good quality meat.

In three months we have seen his skin clear significantly, we are using around 1/4 of the steroid creams and in his last batch of testing we won back several food groups and other allergens. We still have a way to go with the nuts and eggs but we are feeling hopeful. Our n=1 experience with the Magnetico and a 3yo is that it takes time to adjust. My wife slept with hime for about 3 months as he was agitated during the night and was wriggling in his sleep a lot. She made sure he didn't start scratching and open up his skin. We persisted because even though the sleeps were tough, he showed positive signs quickly, for instance when his skin became infected he didn't need antibiotics, for the first time he was able to fight off the infection himself, his skin started clearing and he generally looked much more calm during the day. We gave the Magnetico a break for a about three weeks where his sleep is now much better than it has ever been and we are about to start up again. After talking to the guys at magnetico we will put it under his bed which will drop it to 2G and look to bring it back up to 5G as soon as we can. Hopefully this brings us the best of both worlds.

My wife and I love the 10G one we have on our bed, it is like the 'sleep machine', not much else to report as we are both pretty healthy and don't have complicated health issues that we are battling (at leasts that we know about, we will start testing ourselves soon). We just now need to get another 5G one for our 6yo. Expensive, but as they don't wear out or break down they are a crazy great value investment over a lifetime considering the benefits they bring.

Hope this experience helps anyone dealing with eczema and allergies, it is a terrible thing to deal with as a family, physically and emotionally. Everyone has opinions and we tend to be frowned upon for not doing enough by people on the outside looking in, even though we have been at our wits ends. The specialists here in Brisbane just offer advice on creams and wrapping which changes in every consult. This has been extremely frustrating. Dr Kruses explanations however have been like a life raft thrown our way with concrete actions we can do, even though I don't understand a lot of the detail yet. The results are starting to come through and if this trend continues then we will have achieved a remarkable outcome.
