# Thread: Really sad.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-sad.8173/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-09-07T23:28:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-sad.8173/post-95162>

for one, I had a nice post all written up and bam, hit the spell check and it disappeared and was not in the auto save

I have been feeling off since about 2006. Now that I know way more about hormones then I did back then, I wanted to see what my labs looked like back then. I got the reports yesterday and it made me really sad and kinda pissed!!!!

Back then the doc I was seeing only did the TSH and that was 1.25 and was apparently was "in spec". She never did any other thyroid testing at this time. This was just before I was diagnosed with Celiac.

Then in July of 2007 she ran the Celiac tests and it was confirmed that I had Celiac. She redid the TSH and this time is was 1.51 but again, that was the only one.

Still was not feeling well and had to switch doctors and so had more testing done in Nov 2007. This time the TSH was 1.72 plus I finally got some more thyroid testing done. My free T4 was .9 and my free T3 1.8 and marked as low. Seriously, I was out and nothing was done about it. I was feeling like crap and she did not know enough to help me, just sad. My vitamin D was also low and had cortisol testing done and at this time it was pretty good, but not for long.

I still was not feeling well so I insisted on seeing an enochronologist, I thought this was going to be it. He would find something and I can start feeling better. it was also about this time that I had been burning the candle at both end and was getting burned, it was a rough time in my life. So in January of 2008 I saw the endo doc.

This time my FT3 was 2.7 pg/ml and the FT4 was 1.1 ng/dL. The big this was that my AM cortisol was .7 and the range was 4-24 ug/dL. Really, that was not a clue that something was off. He did all of my female hormones too but I do not know what time of the cycle that these were done in but I know he never said that I needed to have them at a certain time, nor does it say on the labs. Again, disappointing. And then there was the Vitamin D, which again came in low and was marked at low on the report. He never mentioned anything about this. All he said is that all the labs were within "range" so there was nothing that he could do except write me a script for a weight loss drug since that was my main complaint. I walked out of the office WITHOUT a script. I did NOT have an eating problem, I had a hormone problem.

Like I said, reading these lab reports, just makes me sad. Sad in that how many other people are told that every thing is "in spec" but they still feel like crap and no one can help them. Still a little peeved that I have been off for over 7 years and never got the help.

But now, I am way different. I am in control of my health and educated enough and strong enough to tell a doc what I think, why and what treatment I feel would work best for me.

So, now I am spreading the word about hormones and if one does not feel well, make sure that they educate them selves and to tell a doc what test they want done and to never stop.

I am done being sick and tired and am in control of my health thanks to all that I have learned from this sight. Dr. Kruse provided my with the information to save myself and to that I owe a big thanks.

### caroline — 2013-09-08T02:43:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-sad.8173/post-95173>

What a great post! Dr. K. has always said " when you get sick and tired of being sick and tired you" will start to make progress. He also always says ....never settle......and that is precisely what you are doing! well done you

### caroline — 2013-09-08T02:49:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-sad.8173/post-95174>

I just put in another support ticket ..... when we are trying to limit our computer time - this is making it very difficult...

### nicld — 2013-09-09T00:39:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-sad.8173/post-95276>

Yep, I got tired of being sick and tired and trying so hard to not gain weight.

# Thread: Finding a home and Cell towers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdaptogenicBeing — 2013-09-08T00:53:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95166>

Howdy! Please forgive me if this was talked about in the EMF boot camp. I didn't become active on this site until a short while ago. My question is this: For those of you who have made moves to areas with lower EMF how did you approach the locations of cell towers. My thinking is that I should move to a small town that has decent cell service to help limit the possibility that one could end up on a mountain top up the street from me. I look back at homes up the mountains around me that I used to think were very cool. Now many of them sadly enough have towers just up above them.

### fitness@home — 2013-09-08T04:03:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95189>

I have an app on my iPad that finds all towers in the area. Was amazed at how close some were to the building I work in.

We went out today just driving around in the country areas of Magnolia, Stage Coach, Conroe, Montgomery, Cut n Shoot, New Waverly, Splendora, Huntsville, etc... Way out on some of these remote roads and there would be a tower sitting in the middle of the field. Another thing I noticed is there are usually two towers relatively close to each other.

It would be a bummer to buy some property and then have a neighbor lease land to a cell company for a tower .

### caroline — 2013-09-08T04:56:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95195>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=95189)
>
> I have an app on my iPad that finds all towers in the area. Was amazed at how close some were to the building I work in.
>
> We went out today just driving around in the country areas of Magnolia, Stage Coach, Conroe, Montgomery, Cut n Shoot, New Waverly, Splendora, Huntsville, etc... Way out on some of these remote roads and there would be a tower sitting in the middle of the field. Another thing I noticed is there are usually two towers relatively close to each other.
>
> It would be a bummer to buy some property and then have a neighbor lease land to a cell company for a tower .

I wondered about that too. In one of the webinars or Q&As \[maybe the last one\] Jack talked about there being 2500 acres beside him with nothing on it ..... but how could you be sure that it will stay like that?

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-08T17:15:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95235>

Here's what I've been using to look for optimal places to live... [http://www.antennasearch.com/](http://www.antennasearch.com/)

what's interesting there are places we've considered as possibilities that have been scratched off the list due to the region having more antennas than where we currently live... and we've got a freaking satellite dish yard/communications hub less than 2 miles from our house....

### AdaptogenicBeing — 2013-09-08T17:36:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95238>

Wow!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

I checked my old address............................120 towers within 4 mile radius!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
Everything from cell tower to the air force base!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

My new address only has 6

### fitness@home — 2013-09-08T17:52:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95240>

> [AdaptogenicBeing said:](/goto/post?id=95238)
>
> Wow!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
>
> I checked my old address............................120 towers within 4 mile radius!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
> Everything from cell tower to the air force base!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
>
> My new address only has 6

Hey, at least you've been moving in the right direction

### cantweight — 2013-09-08T17:55:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95242>

That is wild! We went from 50 big towers to 1 big and 9 unregistered. The closest one over a mile away. From 177 antennae to 20. Also over a mile away. Crazy.

We did exactly what you said AB...looked for an area with shoddy cell service and little internet. We didn't want to be disconnected...not an option with a husband in IT....but I didn't want to pull up a dozen other peoples wireless networks in my bedroom like our old house.

Noticeably better, but whether or not it is enough....I guess time will tell....

# Thread: Determined to find a way
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 119
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Determined — 2013-09-08T01:54:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-95167>

Ok, so I'm being brave & starting a journal on the forum cos u all look like a great bunch of ppl & I haven't seen any nasty stuff - looks like a v safe place for newbies like me to share our journey!
To try and summarize - I'm 52 & have tried oh so many ways to feel 'right' over these yrs. It all sort of started abt 14 yrs ago w fatigue. I tht I was gng thru menopause but found out I had Hashi's. & high homocysteine, low B12 & iron, so the Dr wanted to start me on thyroxine but having always been into ' natural' & a vegetarian for the previous 5 yrs, I wanted to avoid that. So I googled and looked and found all the good looking raw vegans exposing the wonders of raw veganism & so i was off (again, experimented w this in my 20,s).

I def felt & looked better for awhile.& also took a b12 & iron supp for awhile. And there were no antibodies by abt 6 mths later, so Thinking i was on a winner continued for another 18 mths until I felt like I was running on 3 cylinders once more, despite eating the best fuel I could put into the gas tank ( organic ,raw no wheat, green smoothies, cacao, all that stuff to try & improve the brain fog & energy slumps) & the thyroid Abs came back. And high BLOOD glucose levels w it- yikes - too many bananas? Felt thirsty all the time - no matter how much I drank - it all makes sense now Dr K! And those 3 cylinders sometimes lowed to 2.

Something was wrong so more research & reading abt raw paleo (Aajonus - but I can't eat raw meat - yet - sorry!) so have been pretty much cooked paleo now for the last 4 yrs & I knew it might take a while to restore the good fats in my depleted body - boy, did I wolf down the meat when I finally decided to let go of raw vegan! I remember my mums lamb roasts smelt oh so good & always loved my tiucker! In the early days It took a while to get over my conditioning & be convinced that high good fat wasn't going to make me enormous! That's taken a while too.

When I wasn't getting the results that others on Paleo said they were - wt loss / energy/ bounding out of bed in the morning - I internalized that & tht it was just me - ie ate too much of this or that, ate a gluten free cake for morning tea that week & so on. Went to a BHRT Dr, got on some missing hormones ( low Pg, low DHEA,LOW Vit D) / vitamins (progesterone 10%, DHEA cream, Vit D 10,000 q 2 days, iodine spray daily) (she also prescribed Estrogen cream but as I'd NEVER used OCP I flt like a pumped up balloon on this so stopped it- water retention/ tension) But even w these, which i tweaked in various ways (where/ when & how much) I still never felt right.

She then tried me on HGH but I also felt very wrong on this too ( & worried abt the mercury preservative in it!!) & whilst others reported wt loss, energy etc i felt like crap - like a wrung out dish rag - worse on it but tried it for a while 'might come good' so that was a few 800 buck vials down the drain!

Finding Dr Kruse's site feels like I have found the missing piece of my health puzzle! In ONE week I feel a lot better already - calmer, balanced, more centered, in bed by 8:30 , up at 6 , almost jumping out of bed, got 5 out of 6 cylinders gng some days which is amazing to me, who wld have tht that something as simple as a circadian rhythm could be so profound. Well i do now after reading Jacks blogs. SO interesting & though my brain hurts I absolutely love it!

I only started last Sunday (2/9/13) -bought the book on Kindle on Friday & went Epi Paleo on Saturday - turned out all the lights on Sunday & started trying to reduce EMF exposure ( this is going to be harder - live in an apartment block in Cns - pick up 5 Wifi's arnd me, plus my work entails catching a Dash 8 at 10,000 feet to get to/ from work twice a week (got the barefeet on the metal Dr - wld a Colantotte magnet help???) plus that dang donut hole over us & abt 1000 more bucks to get the Magnetico to Oz - more to that which I won't go on abt here).

Even a simple version of CT has been astounding for me. Been taking a cold shower to start- dont have a compression shirt or skin thermometer yet ( any recommendations for where to buy fellow Aus Krusers?) turning off the hot completely - the aversion to that was pretty strong to start off & I think cold Cairns tap water- whilst it takes your breath away is not the temp it shld be ( planning to head up to volcanic Lake Barrine one weekend - that will be FREEZING right now as its still chilly on the Tablelands).

But got to visit my DH who lives 1500 feet above sea level in Qld this weekend - we do long distance due to work -yes MORE air travel - not good! & def notice difference in my sleep quality after that but he does have a VERY COLD SHOWER water & red skin - takes your breath away!

Just a bit of resistance Day 1 w the lights out & no TV, but reading him bits n pieces to try & win him round. whilst a healthy dude who meditates twice a day - his stress levels always better than mine because of this - he is still leptin resistant evidenced by a big gut & a love of scones w jam & cream & cake & potatoes & porridge - reckons its his Scottish ancestry. He can see a difference in me tho so tryin to b supportive & hoping my effects will continue & convert him too.

Anyway that's my story so far, got a long way to go ( realizing my cortisol levels are upside down - still a bit buzzy at night & sleepy in AM, but SO much better already) and hope to see more results soon. Have never ever done this on net b4 so I must b v inspired! Thanks Dr Kruse for all ur amazing thoughts & look fwd to learning MORE & healing this body of mine (& hopefully helping others too - ESP the pregnant women I work w) xxxx

### nonchalant — 2013-09-08T02:13:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-95168>

Hi Determined, love your screen name! Welcome! Sounds like you are doing well already.

### caroline — 2013-09-08T02:24:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-95169>

What a great start to a journal Determined .... and I love your handle!! There is so much great stuff here - so keep reading and asking questions! Please listen to Jack's Podcasts - they give you much needed information quickly.

If you can swing it ...become a silver or gold member. The webinars and Q&A's are fantastic.....but, of course, you don't have to. Dr. K has a very direct way of speaking and you can ask questions. There was a special - I don't know if it is still available tho.

Keeping a journal really ups our commitment level and we learn so much from each other ...so glad you are here

Have you read the easy start guides and all the stuff on the home page? Cold showers are tough - it is much easier to get into a tub with ice!! You will have to trust us on that one!

It is very hot where you are isn't it? I have wanted to go there but haven't made it yet.

once again - welcome to this great journey of a lifetime - we won't be getting a do over ...this is it....

There may be others here getting a magnetico too - I would guess that shipping a bunch together would be cheaper. Make sure you read all the stuff here first tho ... a couple have made unintentional mistakes with regards to size etc.

### Determined — 2013-09-11T10:50:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-95581>

Not a great day - feeling a bit whacked right now! Had to travel Brisbane to Cape York - abt 4 hrs all up flying & no access to my normal paleo food etc here. I normally bring a pile of good stuff w me but couldn't because of the connecting flights. So whilst I grounded the whole way I'm still stuffed but prob not as bad as usual!
I did manage to eat my BAB & haven't been hungry all day just drinking ice water.

But boy I am looking fwd to my pub meal tnt - a 'man size' steak is in order.

I'm still mentally digesting Jacks blog as its pretty devastating for us Aussies to hear that & I don't feel I have much power to do more than I can at present! Heck I can't even access RO water & def not desalinated RO.

then I had a taxi driver telling me the 1 in 50 yr disaster is due and that it will be a cyclone bigger than Yasi ( terrifyingly giant) will prob hit Cairns this yr. OMG! My DH were talking abt buying property on the Sunshine Coast eventually - maybe sooner the better.

I haven't done CT for 2 days ( my cold shower version) but will tnt & looking fwd to getting home to my own bed on Friday so I can get into it well & proper w the ice.

Good stuff to report- I haven't had any indigestion for a week ( was thinking I had H Pylori) & I'm feeling much more aware & mood elevated w more energy for others - which hasn't been the case for some time - other than my job which I love.

This stuff really makes you re-think your priorities - I'd been studying (another) Masters ( Nurse Pract Women's Health) & working FT -w all this weekly air travel & now I'm wondering how to downsize & simplify life & thinking i may not continue this study.

cos I'm not sure i want to take on the full medical model in my work - Nurses ( including Nurse Practitioners in Aus where they r trying o break into a tight System controlled by AMA & big Pharma will def not be able to practice outside the box like JK is able to - you' d be burned at the stake!!!)

The truth be told I've never had faith in western medicine hence why I studied Acupuncture but working in the system has always given me a good income w some form of 'security' - ie regular money. and the way i work has allowed me to work in a wholistic and supportive way w the women I serve in pregnancy/ post partum - been a Homebirth midwife & remote work in Indigenous communities for 15 yrs. However do I want to continue to work in the western medical model?
Plus the stress of the study is shocking for my cortisol. Is it worth it ? So much to ponder!

I love that you are a homebirth midwife! Wow that is a rewarding career! I was a trained doula before I got very sick and could no longer work. I had wanted to become a midwife, but after coming back from such poor health I have a passion to help others do the same, to become whole again. I am in school to become a nurse practitioner and I understand what you mean about western medicine bullshit. I have a approached school with a mindset that I will regurgitate the crap they want me to and then go practice with a doc that gets it. I have found a few holistic practices and an integrative practice that I would be thrilled to work with. I refuse to dish out more of the shit that hurts people. Big pharma can kiss my ass! lol

You have a special place in my heart as a midwife....such amazing women....

The midwife that delivered all 3 of my children just saw my daughter for her first well woman visit....that was such an odd, yet beautiful feeling. My daughter hopes this woman will still be practicing when she has children of her own....at 13 yrs old that better be a long ass time from now...lol

Keep blogging and growing and healing

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-95865>

EE5 gives you foundational pieces why these things are happening. Electron come from the splitting of water.......but what determines the circuit in which they flow? That is the action of the SQUID. Flow can go anywhere in the CNS and PNS. If your function is not present in an area it is a sign to me either your electron splitting ability is low or your field sucks........ flow is directed toward your neocortex at night and away from it in day time........ and eventually to your bad places when add in enough energy by way of the 3 fundamental laws of nature. ...best way to fix it? Go Epi paleo and carefully read the last two blogs and the many more coming........CT is for all the time.

When we lose energy we emit black box radiation. Large transitions can take place sharply and rapidly in response to minute inputs or losses of energy of a coperative, coherent or metastable environment. When you have a lowered energy state, you are no longer stable.....so any other destabilizing force( think energy loss) has massive effects in many systems.

### Determined — 2013-09-29T00:37:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-96411>

Youd be VERY welcome to do that Caroline - when i find that special place I could supply the land, but how about a TIPI? (assisted a women birth her baby in a tipi once, by candlelight, by a river, very beautiful, i was the only person clothed, prude that i am! She spent her post natal time there too, lotus birth, ie placenta still attached to bub, lucky it was a hot spring & the cord was off in 2 days! a record) He must be 20yrs old now that memorable tipi baby!

It was such a lovely 'space' to be in, I would LOVE a tipi, plus you can stand up in there & have a fire in the middle, how cool is that. The American Indians knew how to do it.

That brings up memories of the first time I went to work in the Australian desert with the Pitjantjatjarra people. As part of our cultural orientation we medical mob got to camp in the desert with a local Aboriginal family.

stayed in a 'wiltja' - just a simple roof shelter made of branches. Of course we also had the luxury of a 'swag' ( a mattress with canvas outer) & it was winter & there was ICE on the ground in the morning. I often wondered how the hell Aboriginal people could have lived in the desert in winter (or Summer!), where it is MINUS degrees o/n in winter, replete with ice and no clothing. They didnt use many skins in the desert because of their very nomadic lifestyle required for water/ hunting.

Of course fire, dogs and other human bodies helped, but now i know they were COLD adapted. When you see photos of desert family groups prior to white contact, they all had the most beautiful shiny skin & eyes, slim muscular bodies and a very proud stance- i saw the last remnants of these people when i first went out bush in the early nineties.

None left now & of course metabolic syndrome, T2DM and all the other neolithic diseases run rampantly through all the tribal groups (though there are always rumours of a few ppl who choose to still live traditionally out in the desert...spooky stories of sightings etc). But so Sad to see the destruction in families and lives i seems to be on a larger scale than what we see in the West. Very premature deaths.

Back to the fantasy you mentioned, might be able to supply most of your requirements but not sure about the handsome fish grillin man, you might have to BYO!

I have never heard of a lotus birth .....why is that better? You have had amazing experiences - I hope you are keeping a diary and write a book some day.

### Determined — 2013-09-29T03:25:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-96424>

thx Caroline, never tht abt writing a book, just cos my experience is like a lot of fellow midwives and remote area nurses (whom i know anyway). I do feel really privileged to have spent so much time w Aboriginal ppl and like your lovely experience, many have the most beautiful souls/ hearts that i have been very honoured to experience... I will tell you a story one day of one beautiful elder whom i went to a birth conference with & "spoke of the sacred duty of the midwife", all the audience was in tears! so was i! She told me a story of being sick in the desert prior to white contact, and of walking over 100 miles w her family to get some special herb to heal her. It was an AMAZING story!!!!

Lotus birth - just a snippet of its benefits..leaving the cord uncut & attached to the placenta till it drops off.... not everyone's cup of tea, but it does ensure baby gets ALL of its blood supply - about an extra 60-80mls after the birth (babys TOTAL blood volume is only 80 mls/kg e.g around 240mls all up in a 3kg baby), which is of enormous benefit (& the research is NOW affirming.. (mothers always instinctively knew - we havent cut the cord early at homebirths for as long as I have been around!), it has benefits for bonding ie cant move around much when a placenta is attached (the cord gets very dry, like a twig!) but some ppl believe that because its 'part' of the baby ie all came from the same beginnings (am sure Jack would be able to describe the embryology here!)- an extension of itself, that when the baby is 'ready' to let it go, ie its of no further use (some would argue its of no use once the blood flow has stopped) that its the 'right' time for it to separate, claiming baby is calmer, more secure, more whole as a consequence.

I don't really know about any of the so called 'spiritual' benefits, or of better human beings because of it, but i am supportive of parent's choices and if this is what they want to do, fully support them. What i do know, is that because of the 'appendage' of having a dry cord & placenta attached (specially wrapped/ salted etc), it does SLOW everything down. Ie Ppl cant come in & pass the newborn around, its just not practical, and so baby & mother often stay very cocooned in their own little blissful new world for a few days till the time comes when the cord separates & baby is free of the unwanted appendage! ie none of this going to shopping mall on Day 2 after birth if there is a placenta attached! Breaks my heart to see women up & about racing around straight after birth.

I think the Chinese are very right in nurturing the mothers 'Jing" (essence, vitality) very carefully after birth! Nurture your JING or will definitely come back to bite you (& take heed of Dr Jacks warnings about Circadian rhythms, good water & dietary proteins, & EMF as well - OF COURSE!).

### Determined — 2013-10-01T00:06:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-96542>

A little update as I sit and wait for my plane - stewing in EMF ....hmm
I'm feeling pretty GOOD - it's been 4 weeks on leptin reset and this is what I'm noticing...

Following circadian rhythms I'm usually asleep by 9pm - up naturally around 5:30am w the sun

Tolerating the BAB much more now - not 'gagging' on the protein - have upped the fat in morning though - raw cream in tea ( occasionally a coffee) eating mainly 2 meals a day w some Brazil's for my selenium around 2 pm & not a bit hungry

Doing CT twice a week - going up to 3x this week. Can bear it for around 35 mins - then I start getting shivery - but can keep going up to 45 - my max at present - I think my belly fat is shrinking but not sure - subtle - and no weight loss at all but my energy is fantastic so I'm going on that! Think the wt can wait - I'm about 10kgs more than I want to be.

Yesterday at work I got SO MUCH done - my mind clear & strong - I also have SO much more energy for others. I realise I used to clos myself off to all except the women I was seeing for care. Just didn't have any vitality to spare on anyone else - my goodness how I used to DRAG myself thru a day! Iam just absolutely AMAZED at how good I am feeling on more days than off now.

Seem to be sweating more ? Buts the 'build up' to the wet season is beginning here in the tropics - just the very start of it w increased humidity

Loving being more aware of nature - getting in the sun- touching lying rolling around on Mother Earth

Reading heaps more - fiction & non fiction - less on the computer and iPad ( wish I could read more of Jacks stuff though!!!) will have to kill trees and print the blogs out me thinks?- another book perhaps Dr K?

Don't watch any TV anymore so prob lots less stress that way too

Am convincing my DH who has seen a remarkable improvement in me - from daily adrenal fatigue to much more upbeat happy and energized person - he's even waiting for me to order my magnetico ( tax man be kind!)

All in all I ain't going back - only forward w this - most incredible thing ever.

Questions on my mind - when do u stop eating as per leptin reset? I'm not sure I'm sensitive & guess I'm NOT cos no wt loss tho other signs coming.

### Determined — 2013-10-21T01:41:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-98447>

Thanx everyone! It is too bad their minds are so full of fear. How's this my father had a stroke about a yr ago - on his scan they found he'd prob had one prior also ( prob when he had his hip replacement) - luckily both fairly mild ( so far) - so his Dr told him that although his Cholesterol was 'normal' he had better take a statin drug 'in case' - I DID tell him to stop funding Big Pharma & abt some of the research. But hes still loyal to his Dr! This man lives on cream buns, chocolate & Coke (must have good genes or something!!)

In terms of wt loss -My diet is perfect Epi Paleo - no fruit - high fat - moderate protein ( xcpt for the BAB)

Example day
BAB: 2 lamb chops broiled in butter
3 eggs
Some organic broccoli & a little leek
Chai tea w coconut manna
Dandelion coffee w coconut manna

Lunch: usually nothing but occ a salad w prawns at the paleo cafe (fantastic to have this in Cairns)
Or a tin of Alaskan red salmon w Lemon juice & lettuce

Dinner : some protein ( ie a piece of snapper or a piece of GF beef) & steamed veges ( low carb but sometimes a bit of sweet potato - eg cauliflower, broccoli, zucchini) w raw butter of course!
2 pieces of dark cacao homemade ( YUMMY) chocolate

That's it !!!! Truly I shld be skinny!!! Nondairy no grains low low carb
But I hear what ur saying Hemming - I am feeling 100000000 x better than I did 3 mths ago - I prob need to let go of the weight issue once and for all ( that's pretty big for me in the family I grew up in where wt was constantly on the agenda and one sister who succumbed to anorexia because of it - and now literally lives on weight watchers meals ( shld I say plastic)

Love my Circadian rhythms & doing CT around 3x per week - even LIKING it!

Lava I love u blab it all out!! People sometimes exhaust me and I don't feel 'smart' enough w all the theory ( yet) I just want to be a shining silent example! My folks did say ' you look sort of very healthy' !!! That must count!

Thx again everyone for ur comments xx

**Subforum:** Beginners Area

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hansel89 — 2013-09-08T13:34:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transition-to-epi-paleo-early-wakeup-bab.8176/post-95219>

Dear Dr Kruse & Other Members,

I am male, 24, and a recent university graduate.
I train 6 out 7 days doing rowing, weightlifting and yoga.
I've just recently transitioned from raw vegan to epi paleo and i am currently overweight weighing around 98 kg.
Since you advise working out in sunlight i intend to switch around my training hours and work out at 1 pm instead of at 4 am upon wake up.
I have a few questions i would be delighted to have your opinion on.

\* As i wake up everyday at 4 am it is still dark outside. Shall i wait do some light activities like meditation and have my breakfast as soon as the sun rises perhaps a couple of hours after?
\* For breakfast what is your opinion on having a drink consisting of ghee, mct oil, coffee, collagen protein, pure liquid vanilla, liquid stevia? I ask this since i prefer avoiding having to digest a more solid meal as well as time, energy efficiency and preferring a liquid meal in the mornings.
\* How about raw milk as snack during the day and for pre bed having either raw honey/ collagen protein with mct oil and krill oil ?

Thank you for reading and looking forward to hearing your suggestions.

# Thread: How to make an ULF antenna from a tree.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-an-ulf-antenna-from-a-tree.8177/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### CindyB — 2013-09-08T21:38:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-an-ulf-antenna-from-a-tree.8177/post-95260>

Just came across this very interesting copy of an article from Nature magazine (1978):

www-ee.stanford.edu/~acfs/ULF%20Tree%20Potentials.pdf‎

The graphic showing the time series of measurements taken from the tree simultaneous with traditional "20,000 turn steel-cored solenoids" ULF antennas at Stanford University is a very convincing piece of evidence for using a tree antenna. The paper includes instructions on making the antenna.

Watch out "Tree of Heaven" in my yard!

Other interesting snippets from the above paper:

"Finally, although it is not clear at present what effect induced ULF electric fields may have on the growth and other vital processes in a tree, the link between these ULF electric fields and geomagnetic field fluctuations suggests that some environment-related changes in trees could also be influenced by changes in geomagnetic activity. These changes may have a natural origin (for example, the changes that occur during a solar cycle) or they may be caused by a variety of human activities (by modern d.c.-powered mass transit systems, which can produce large amplitude ULF electromagnetic fields)."

"Presman (12) noted that electromagnetic fields usually have an adverse effect on living processes."

Where (12) is: Presman, A. S. Electromagnetic Fields and Life (transl. Sinclair, F. L., ed. Brown, F. A. Fr) 155 (Plenum, New York, 1970)

# Thread: If the 1859 EMF event happens today the costs would be north of 2 trillion dollars
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-the-1859-emf-event-happens-today-the-costs-would-be-north-of-2-trillion-dollars.8179/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-09T05:06:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-the-1859-emf-event-happens-today-the-costs-would-be-north-of-2-trillion-dollars.8179/post-95310>

In 1859 known as the Carrington event was the most intense CME ejection in our modern history, creating auroras farther south than the Tropic of Cancer. Telegraph services experienced serious disruptions; random unreadable messages were coming through their instruments, and some operators even reported experiencing electrical fireworks sparking from their equipment.

Today NASA subcontractors estimate the risk of a similar CME is at 12% in the next ten yrs. If this were to happen today with the modern infrastructure all tied to the electric and semiconductor grids the damage to the world economy would be north of 2 trillion dollars, according to Homa Karimabadi.

Luckily, though, the Earth has a natural defense against the constant attack from the sun's CME by our magnetosphere. Earth's rapidly spinning metal core generates a magnetic field similar to that of an ordinary bar magnet. This magnetic field extends far into space at least 37,000 miles (roughly 60,000 kilometers). Most charged particles cannot easily cross magnetic field lines. Instead, something called Lorentz forces, created by the earth's magnetic field, deflect the particles' paths. The magnetosphere therefore acts as an invisible shield around our planet, barring the highly charged particles from the sun from plummeting through our atmosphere. There are holes in this shield with the largest one in the Southern hemisphere.

Despite its protective qualities, however, the magnetosphere also has vulnerabilities. Particles can still enter our magnetosphere by a process known as magnetic reconnection. Solar wind carries with it its own magnetic field, and when this magnetic field points in the opposite direction as Earth's magnetic field, the field lines connect in an explosive process that catapults charged particles into the magnetosphere.

Karimabadi and Loring worked with John Dorrelli, an expert on magnetospheric topological analysis of NASA's Goddard Space Center. Their work helps us find the location of the magnetic field lines from different sources, explains Karimabadi. Using NICS' supercomputing resources, they have become the first to create such a map to develop global kinetic simulations of the magnetosphere and space-weather effects. The team used Nautilus and Kraken to create their visualizations and simulations. A better understanding of how space weather affects our magnetosphere allows scientists to more accurately predict the impact of solar activity on our planet. With improved predictive capabilities, we can more effectively prepare for space-weather events.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-09T05:13:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-the-1859-emf-event-happens-today-the-costs-would-be-north-of-2-trillion-dollars.8179/post-95311>

The Earth’s magnetic field provides a protective cocoon, but it breaks during strong solar storms. These storms, also known as geomagnetic storms, can cause massive power outages, disrupt communications and navigation systems, damage Earth’s satellites and induce corrosive electrical currents through gas and oil pipelines that cause them to age faster and fail quicker. During periods of intense geomagnetic storms, flights must be rerouted around Earth’s magnetic poles to avoid the dangers of exposing staff and passengers to high levels of radiation. Astronauts especially are at risk of exposure to unhealthy or even lethal amounts of radiation, even when they are inside their spacecraft.

Because the sun can eject plasma in any random direction, the odds of a serious CME being aimed directly toward Earth are slim. However, the risks posed by dangerous space weather are not to be taken lightly . In 1989, a series of CMEs and solar flares caused a severe geomagnetic storm that left millions of people in Quebec without power for more than nine hours. Blackouts spread south across America’s northeastern coast. This geomagnetic storm was so intense that the northern lights, which are normally only visible in parts of Canada, could be seen as far south as Florida. The storm in 1859 was even more intense, creating auroras farther south than the Tropic of Cancer. Telegraph services experienced serious disruptions; random unreadable messages were coming through their instruments, and some operators even reported experiencing electrical fireworks sparking from their equipment.

CMEs pose a significantly greater risk to Earth than other potentially dangerous astronomical events. The likelihood of the sun emitting a CME depends on an 11-year solar cycle. We are currently in solar maximum 24 right now. During the peak of the solar cycle, the sun can emit several of them every day. At the minimum of the solar cycle, the sun may radiate only a couple per week. In the last 200 years, our planet has already experienced two geomagnetic storms intense enough to cost us billions, even trillions of dollars. There is an estimated 12-percent chance in the next 10 years of a solar storm of the magnitude of the 1859 Solar Superstorm hitting Earth. That would cause over $2 trillion in damage.” This amount is equivalent to a whopping 30 times the amount of damage caused by Hurricane Sandy in 2012, which cost the United States roughly $65 billion.

As our world becomes increasingly dependent on electricity, we also become more and more vulnerable to the consequences of severe geomagnetic storms. A storm that merely disrupted telegraph services 150 years ago would do a whole lot more damage today. “Imagine having no power and communication for over three to six months and over large regions of the United States. We are simply not prepared to deal with such a calamity,” says Karimabadi.

A geometric storm on the magnitude of the 1859 superstorm narrowly missed our planet in July of 2012. Some of you may remember that aurora were seen close to the equator last summer and many people in NASA did not believe it until picture came in to prove it. If this particular CME had occurred a few days sooner, our planet would have suffered devastating consequences similar to what happened 67 million years ago.

# Thread: Dehydration leads to Magnesium depletion by these drugs:

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-09T18:16:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration-leads-to-magnesium-depletion-by-these-drugs.8180/post-95340>

If you are losing water to your environment for any reason at all you will also lose magnesium......so what drugs can also exacerbate a field effect?

**What Drugs Deplete Your Body of Magnesium?**
Acid Blockers
Cimetidine (Tagamet)
Esomeprazole (Nexium)
Famotidine (Pepcid and Pepcid Complete)
Nizatidine (Axid)
Omeprazole (Prilosec OTC)
Pantoprazole (Protonix)
Ranitidine (Zantac)
Rabeprazole (Aciphex)

Antacids
Aluminum and magnesium hydroxide (Maalox, Mylanta)
Aluminum carbonate gel (Basaljel)
Aluminum hydroxide (Amphojel, AlternaGEL)
Calcium carbonate (Tums, Titralac, Rolaids)
Magnesium hydroxide (Phillipsâ€™ Milk of Magnesia)
Sodium bicarbonate (Alka-Seltzer, baking soda)
Antibiotics (a few examples)
Amoxicillin (Amoxil)
Azithromycin (Z-Pak)
Cefaclor (Ceclor)
Cefdinir (Omnicef)
Cephalexin (Keflex)
Ciprofloxacin (Cipro)
Clarithromycin (Biaxin)
Doxycycline (Doryx)
Erythromycin (E.E.S.)
Levofloxacin (Levaquin)
Minocycline (Minocin)
Sulfamethoxazole and trimethoprim (Bactrim, Septra)
Tetracycline (Sumycin)
Antiviral Agents
Delavirdine (Rescriptor)
Foscarnet (Foscavir)
Lamivudine (Epivir)
Nevirapine (Viramune)
Zidovudine, AZT (Retrovir)
Zidovudine and Lamivudine (Combivir)
Blood Pressure Drugs
Hydralazine (Apresoline)
ACE inhibitors:

Enalapril and HCTZ (Vaseretic)
Angiotensin II receptor blockers:

Valsartan and HCTZ (Diovan HCT)
Diuretics, loop:

Bumetanide (Bumex)
Ethacrynic acid (Edecrin)
Furosemide (Lasix)
Torsemide (Demadex)
Diuretics, thiazide (and any combination drug that contains HCTZ or hydrochlorothiazideâ€”dozens of drugs contain this)

Candesartan and HCTZ (Atacand HCT)
Chlorothiazide (Diuril)
Chlorthalidone (Hygroton)
Hydrochlorothiazide or HCTZ (Hydrodiuril)
Methyclothiazide (Enduron)
Metolazone (Zaroxolyn)
Diuretics, potassium-sparing:

Possibly the potassium-sparing diuretics, however this is not conclusive
Diuretics, sulfonamide:

Indapamide (Lozol)
Cardiac Glycoside
Digoxin (Digitek, Lanoxicaps, Lanoxin)
Central Nervous System (CNS) Stimulants
Methylphenidate (Metadate, Ritalin)
Cholesterol Agents
Cholestyramine (Questran)
Colestipol (Colestid)
Corticosteroids
Betamethasone (Diprolene, Luxiq)
Dexamethasone (Decadron)
Hydrocortisone (Cortef)
Methylprednisolone (Medrol)
Mometasone (Elocon)
Prednisolone (Pediapred Liquid)
Prednisone (Deltasone, Liquid Pred, Sterapred)
Triamcinolone (Aristocort cream)
Inhaled corticosteroids:

Flunisolide (Nasarel, Nasalide)
Futicasone (Flonase)
Triamcinolone (Azmacort inhaler)
Hormone Replacement Therapy / Oral Contraceptives
Diethylstilbestrol (DES)
Estradiol (Activella, Climara, Combipatch, Estrace, Estraderm, Estring, EstroGel, Femring, Menostar, and many others)
Estrogen-containing drugs (hormone replacement therapy and birth control)
Estrogens, conjugated (Premphase, Prempro)
Estrogens, esterified (Estratab)
Estropipate (Ogen)
Ethinyl estradiol (found in many birth control pills)
Levonorgstrel (found in many birth control pills)
Immunosuppressants
Cyclosporine (Sandimmune, Neoral)
Tacrolimus (Prograf)
Nonsteroidal Aromatase Inhibitors for breast cancer
Anastrozole (Arimidex)
Osteoporosis
Raloxifene (Evista)
SERMs (Selective Estrogen Receptor Modulatorsâ€”used for breast cancer)
Raloxifene (Evista)
Tamoxifen (Nolvadex)
Toremifene (Fareston)
Sulfonamides
Sulfa antibiotics, some diabetic medications
MISC:
Alcohol
Calcium supplementation (prolonged or in excess)
Coffee
Estrogen dominance
High cortisol levels
High-sugar diet (glucose, fructose, sucrose)
Malabsorption of any sort (celiac or Crohnâ€™s disease, pancreatitis, crash dieting, anorexia)
Mineral oil

Check your BUN/creat ratio if you are on any of these drugs......you will likely find that your level is greater than 7:1 and you will know you got a big water issue and a smaller magnesium one.

# Thread: Issues with double-posting in forums and blog comments
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/issues-with-double-posting-in-forums-and-blog-comments.8184/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tellmisty — 2013-09-10T01:56:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/issues-with-double-posting-in-forums-and-blog-comments.8184/post-95371>

Hi all,

Several of you have reported problems with the site and forum recently. We are working with our new web host technician to resolve these issues as quickly as possible. Feel free to discuss the issues you're having in this thread, but please also submit a support ticket with a detailed account of the problem you're having so we can provide the web host with as much information as possible. It will help us greatly if you provide your IP address, OS, and browser. You can do this by visiting [www.ipcow.com](http://www.ipcow.com), clicking "More Info", and copy-pasting all of the information that site provides into the support ticket. That way our web host can search the error log for your IP address so we can try to isolate the issues.

Thanks for your continued patience. There may be downtime later this evening (after 12am CST, 9/10) for some scheduled site updates, but we don't anticipate the site being offline for longer than a couple hours at most.

I find the format of the blog posts seems to change sometimes. It will look like it is formatted maybe for mobile devices or something (side columns disappear, and font and style are different) and then will seem to randomly switch back to the original style? And the number of comments will change - noticed this on the last blog especially, where in that "mobile format" it usually had just one comment.

When posting in the forum, sometimes "go advanced" button led to a page where the post preview was there, but the edit box was empty and there was no active "Submit Reply" button.

"Post Quick Reply" button seemed to work, but sometimes there would be a message saying not allowed to post again in less than 30 seconds and to try again. I did NOT "try again" and my posts were posted (always just once).

All of the above difficulties were new in the last couple of weeks and I thought maybe had to do with changing providers so figured someone was working on it. I will try to log these events if they continue to happen - with screenshots if possible.

Oh, one older problem that still doesn't look like it's sorted is the avatars one. Only a few members have avatars in the forum. I have one set up in my profile, but it does not show. This is nowhere near as annoying as not being able to access comments on the blog posts or the double posting problems, but thought I would mention it is still an issue.

# Thread: Alternating currents/electromagnetic effects on human biology
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alternating-currents-electromagnetic-effects-on-human-biology.8185/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### digital — 2013-09-10T02:49:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alternating-currents-electromagnetic-effects-on-human-biology.8185/post-95380>

[http://cancerres.aacrjournals.org/content/64/9/3288.full.pdf](http://cancerres.aacrjournals.org/content/64/9/3288.full.pdf)

"low-frequency pulsed electric fields have been claimed to stimulate bone growth and accelerate fracture healing (3). However, as the frequency of the electric field
increases above 1 kHz, the stimulatory effect diminishes. Under these conditions, although a greater fraction of the fields penetrates the cells, due to the parallel resistor-capacitor nature of all biological membranes, the stimulatory power greatly diminishes as the alternating cell membrane hyper-depolarization cycles are integrated such
that the net effect is nulled. At very high frequencies (i.e., above many MHz), although the integration becomes even more effective, a completely different biological effect is observed. At these frequencies tissue heating becomes dominant due to dielectric losses. This effect becomes more intense as frequency, field intensity, or tissue dissipation factor increases"


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Ovarian Cancer & Epi-Paleo
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### digital — 2013-09-10T02:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95381>

Dear Jack Kruse-

I want to thank you so much for the consultant we did with you back earlier this year.

My mom in law was diagnosed with stage 4 ovarian cancer, spreaded from the ovaries up to the lungs and was given 2 months with surgery impossible (massive surgery that would spin 12+ hours requiring two teams, each with 4 surgeons, and a high probably of other risks associated).

After her diagnosis in Jan 2013, we did the consultant with Dr. Kruse. She converting to epi-paleo diet, walked barefooted, took advantage of the photoelectric effect and took a few of the recommend supplements. Her Aug CT scan showed a major reduction of cancer to less than 20% of what it was. She just completed her 5-hr surgery moments ago and is in recovery now.

Thank you so much for your help and service!

\-digital

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-10T03:05:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95383>

What an amazing success. thanks for sharing digital

### nonchalant — 2013-09-10T03:35:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95387>

### digital — 2013-09-10T03:47:03+0300

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=95387)

> Just 8 months, Digital? Sounds wonderful! Hope she recovers well from the surgery.

Truly amazing. We actually started seeing the effects 3 months after implementing Dr. Kruse's protocols... the CT showed 30% reduction then (~ Apr). By July/Aug CT's the masses (cancer/tumors) stabilized (to 20% of original) and hence proceeded to surgery.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-10T03:52:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95390>

This is my job......When a human is down I must extend my hand.

How many conquests mankind has had in exploring outer space and yet we fail to explore their own inner space. In my view, it is better to look ahead and explore what we do not know, than look behind and regret things we should have done. Stop wishing things were easier, and begin to wish you were better! Keep your edge by continuing to work and improve the skills and knowledge that are useful to you by exploring new horizons instead of repeating yesterdays fallacies. Exploring your inner masterpiece bring you wisdom because you begin to realize when you do not know the right decision to take in life, you begin to imagine what would be the worse decision I could make and you seek to avoid it. There is a magic in this action of personal exploration. I am very glad your mother chose to begin! No disease is impossible to beat with quantum biology.......the problem is no one believes it.................YET.

That will change too.

### Gracelind — 2013-09-11T06:47:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95549>

Hi digital

Your MIL's experience is very moving and inspiring for me as we lost our mother to OVCA in 2007.

Has she had any chemo? Do you know her CA-125 results?

Please do update on her condition. I got to know quite a few OVCA patients in a forum when my mother was ill. It's the kind of forum where, when members leave, it's usually because they have died. I can't tell you how much women with OVCA need hope and inspiration ... and a way of eating and living that improves quality of life.

So thank you for posting her experience reducing tumor size. I'm so happy for her and all of you who love her.

### Gracelind — 2013-09-11T07:04:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95555>

Hi digital

I hit "send" too soon!

I also want to send my best wishes for your MIL as she recovers from her surgery. It's invasive in the extreme and requires a long recovery. With the Kruse Protocol, though, I hope it will be faster and easier than otherwise.

Remember to take care of yourselves, and you, take care of your wife. This must be very hard on her. We learned very quickly that when one member gets cancer, the whole family "gets cancer" in different ways--but it affects everyone's lives physically and emotionally. We often felt that our lives had been put in a blender and swirled around till they were almost unrecognizable. If you all are eating Epi-paleo, barefooting, etc, you'll all be healthier, too.

When a doctor would have difficulty in diagnosing a patient problem, she would be brought in as a specialist in what ever ailment was thought to be the patients problem. She would go through the motions of an exam, but she was really psychically looking for the real problem with the patient. She had an 86% hit record. She would also assist in operating rooms during operations. Located in Los Angeles. Don't remember her name. I always thought I would like to do that.

I sit in the outer room of my my tent now with my feet in the wet grass oh man it feels good ) but radio on all day long, the kids are listening to dvd's and stuff... inside almost all day... so noisy, fighting and screaming. I do not get how people want to live like this. Today morning I took the 3 yo guy with me in the forest, and we hugged trees.. found some berries, and it was so nice, so silent and beautiful, a sacred feeling. But inside the house, I hate to be there. The feeling is so dead. The childrens mom (my sis.) are not a happy person, very unsatisfied, fights with her DH, talks bad to her children... not able to find solutions for issues.. and so on. But they are all so posessed with their electronics. they just are unable to see the connection with their issues.

I miss my home in Finand. I might go back in a month or so. I might find another solution, I actually already know what to do. But I am thankful for this experience too. I needed it. There is alwasy so much to learn, sometimes it is painful.

@ Audrey... I am willing to join you for sure.. lol I need to go hardcore now. I am starting to feel the circumstances around me are draining my energy. I need to take better care of me. # Thread: Big fat BAB

**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx

### cantweight — 2013-09-10T03:32:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-fat-bab.8189/post-95386>

Audrey, I think you were paying as much attention as I was when we commented on Jedeye's post....it was an ask jack....oops.

Anyway, this big fat tweak I made has been amazing. I am seeing results from it like crazy. Today was 132 gr fat breakfast.....cant imagine the looks i will get from people when they ask me what I am doing.

I have had twinges of hunger mid day....my breakfast is at 6am since I am getting the kiddies out to school again these days....so by 2 or 3 I feel peckish. A glass of raw milk has been doing the trick, sometimes a piece or 2 of bacon if there is any leftover in the fridge.

Then dinner is just fish and veggie or meat and veggie.

I feel great...I am able to handle a much more intense CT with that much fat...my belly got so red this morning that my stretch marks stayed purple for hours....it was weird.

My hair and nails are unreal...having a bout of acne....whenever I lose fat I know my body is detoxing some nasty stuff so just doing my best to help it along.

Just printed the monster blog....going to read now

Sorry again Jedeye!!

### Hemming — 2013-09-10T09:17:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-fat-bab.8189/post-95420>

I've noticed the same about acne - it got a few pimples in the beginning but now my skin is better than ever and I have a long history of acne. When I cut out grains a year ago it helped massively but I think the last step was taken on the epi-paleo diet. On top of that my skin is softer than ever and I don't burn in the sun.

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-10T15:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-fat-bab.8189/post-95437>

ladies - Fat and I have this amazing love affair - I can no longer eat massive quantities of protein at breakfast. but fat. oh HELL YEAH.. There are points where the only thing that I can stomach is BPC and scoops of coconut oil. What's crazy is there are days that that's all I eat until 2pm or so. HUNGER doesn't exist any more and the thought a a large breakfast totally repulses me.

follow your instincts on this one ladies...

### AnnieW — 2013-09-10T15:47:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-fat-bab.8189/post-95440>

So what are ya'll eating to get that much fat in the AM? I could overdose on bacon every morning but it gives me indigestion.

### cantweight — 2013-09-10T16:06:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-fat-bab.8189/post-95443>

> [AnnieW said:](/goto/post?id=95440)
>
> So what are ya'll eating to get that much fat in the AM? I could overdose on bacon every morning but it gives me indigestion.

Annie, my fat is coming from unsalted grass fed butter, heavy cream, and coconut oil. I mix it in my coffee. Jack doesnt recommend coffee for those in an altered field, with high BUN/creatinine ratio as it is deyhdrating....have to do your own N=1 there. (see recent ask jack about bullet proof coffee)

Perhaps a decaf chai tea or some other tea would be good as well. I have 3 tablespoons of each split into 3 drinks. I dont drink drip coffee so it is just 1 espresso shot and one hot water shot (americano) per cup. Not 3 cups of drip coffee....I would explode from caffeine!

I also eat a pastured egg or 2, or some sort of meat....bacon, leftovers....whatever is there.

This mornings BAB was 126 gr fat and 6 gr protein.

I had a similar experience to Gretchen. I forced high protein BABs for a while but was developing a severe aversion to them. Got to the point I was gagging and dry heaving trying to get down massive quantities of protein. Took me months to get to where i am now....but i really think I found my sweet spot. Hunger is mild to none, like gretchen I do the AM fat load and Im set till 2- or 3 o'clock. I have a glass of raw milk then and dinner around 6 of meat or fish and vegetable.

Feeling great!

### caroline — 2013-09-13T05:06:21+0300

Claudia, I used to fall apart at season changes too. Dont wait until fall to stop fruit...I think that may have been one of my downfalls. If you need to lose weight you really do have to follow the leptin reset. All of us that are switching to high fat already did the reset with the mega protein BAB....I hated it, hard a hard time getting it down, but forced it and had some profound shifts in my body. Textbook to what the doc said will happen. If i have more than 25 carbs a day I crave sugar. It is also important for me to make sure my carbs come from veggie or dairy. If I feel desperate I eat a square or 2 of dark chocolate almonds..so it mostly nut, also Audrey got me hooked on ricotta cheese with a little vanilla stevia.

I have a ton of weight to lose and am working my ass off to find what works for me....I lost 4 lbs last week and am seeing a shift in my waistline (I guess it is still there somewhere..lol)

Have you added in CT? Game changer

# Thread: New from Texas needs help~
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LynnF — 2013-09-10T17:19:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95448>

Hi guys, glad I found this place. Believe me when I say my search has taken me all over the Internet and to more than one doctor. But I'm probably in great company there. You all know how many blogs and sites there are, and how much conflicting information, too.

I've had digestion and weight issues for my entire life. I've tried everything under the sun. Now I am getting new food sensitivities added all the time, extremes from constipation to diarrhea, pain in my right side that sent me to the emergency room (for the first time in almost thirty years), and after seven hours and two shots of morphine, sent home with a "We can't find anything wrong, follow up with your doctor." I did, he sent me to a Gastro who handed me some MiraLax and said my problem is all about constipation. Well ok, then.

I'm here because I guess I'm on my own. I'm on Medicare so I think a more natural practitioner is out of my pocketbook and out of the question.

I'll find my way around here, any suggestions are welcome.  Hello, everybody!!

### nonchalant — 2013-09-10T17:44:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95450>

Hi LynnF, welcome!
Do you mean the hospital let you keep your gall bladder?

### LynnF — 2013-09-10T18:35:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95453>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=95450)
>
> Hi LynnF, welcome!
> Do you mean the hospital let you keep your gall bladder?

Yes. They did! I thought it must be that, but they said no.

### Huck — 2013-09-10T18:59:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95454>

Starting out, make sure you follow the prescription for what to eat and when to eat it, minimize the use of artificial light, spend time outside in the yard barefoot, get plenty of sun, and do cold baths. Minimize RF, but you will need to do some reading to more fully understand what that means.

And don't get discouraged when you are not fixed in 2 or 3 months. Many of us have seen lots of small changes that have resulted in huge improvements over a year or 2. Don't worry if you don't understand everything you read here. I don't and I have been at it for almost 2 years, but I do understand the big picture. For example, you need to eat a big protein breakfast 1st thing in the morning. The most important thing is that you do it. It is nice if you also understand why, but it works whether you understand the why or not.

### fitness@home — 2013-09-10T20:21:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95457>

Hi LynnF, welcome aboard! We do have some members in Dallas. Maybe they will chime in with some doctor recommendations. Huck is right, it does take time for some to find the right path and feel better. We are all so unique. This site and the blogs are a great source of information.Good luck with your search!

### LynnF — 2013-09-10T20:32:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95460>

Thanks guys. I appreciate all the input. I'm trying to read Getting Started.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-10T21:13:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95473>

Read the getting started...........then Read the Leptin Rx and CT protocol..............then pay heed to the fake light............when the sun sets it must set in your house. Look to the glasses and the folks here will help you in understanding that. Brain Gut 11 and 9 do those details..............then the biggie............EMF mitigation. Best advice.........if you want to go it alone...........Read EMF 1 onward to the current blog post...........after you get the basics above sorted in your head..............My adivice.........start a journal and begin to ask the starfish here questions..............many people will lend you a hand. I watch the advice closely so if I think it is not so good I will causually mention that too............the key is to begin in earnest and do not look back. Check you beliefs about diet and biology at the door...........they are useless here and we are all about using nature's laws of the universe to guide what is best for us.............

### caroline — 2013-09-11T00:46:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95495>

Welcome LynnF......keep reading and asking questions and start a journal as Dr. K. suggested...

# Thread: Blood Pressure Back Up
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-back-up.8193/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-09-10T20:59:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-back-up.8193/post-95464>

This makes 2 years in a row I noticed the same pattern with my blood pressure.

Blood pressure is high and requires medication in winter. In late Spring, it starts coming down and stays at or near normal over the Summer. Starting in September, I see a significant jump in blood pressure of about 20 points (systolic). Getting lots of sun brings it down some. I'm expecting by October, I will be back on a low dose of medication with increasing dosage over the winter.

### nonchalant — 2013-09-11T17:35:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-at-the-picture-everyone-is-outside-and-thin-why.8197/post-95626>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=95577)
>
> Today we have so many clues to make our own life working in a world we see as hostile.

"A world we see as hostile" -- that's it in a nutshell. We work all our lives to make money to cushion ourselves from hostile nature. We insulate ourselves from it at all times, at all costs. When camping in the wild, we pack lots of equipment/food to keep safe. When going on an outing with young kids, we need a large vehicle to carry all the supplies. We worry about eventual retirement and not having enough buffer. We fight against nature our entire lives.

### fitness@home — 2013-09-11T18:28:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-at-the-picture-everyone-is-outside-and-thin-why.8197/post-95630>

Thinking about my childhood (my first decade was in the 60s). Life certainly has changed.

We played OUTSIDE spring, summer, winter, and fall. Built "forts" in the fields and woods, rode bikes constantly when weather permitted, had to be called inside for dinner and bedtime, loved winter outside - as long as my snowmobile suit and boot liners weren't soaking wet, and ate fruit off the trees in summer (mmmmm mulberries!) & tomatoes and cukes right off the vine.

No air-conditioning...just a big ceiling fan in the hallway the pulled the cooler air in through the house at night (but it was very noisy!). In the evenings we spent time outside playing (where it was cooler) and on the bench swing under the black walnut tree.

My Mom would hang clothes on the line outside to dry for six months out of the year (saved $ on electric bill too). Dad was an avid hunter and fisherman, spending weekends outside if he wasn't working OT. My brother trapped muskrats all winter, walking a good many miles each day.

I feel old...

At least I didn't throw in the "I used to walk five miles in the snow to get to school each day" . That was one of my great-grandmother's stories. She used to carry a baked potato in her mittens to keep hands warm and then eat it for lunch!

# Thread: If you want to know why Omega 3 PUFA are the one real anti-inflammatory O3 read this
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-want-to-know-why-omega-3-pufa-are-the-one-real-anti-inflammatory-o3-read-this.8198/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-11T01:48:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-want-to-know-why-omega-3-pufa-are-the-one-real-anti-inflammatory-o3-read-this.8198/post-95508>

PUFA omega 3's are difficult to understand from PUFA 6 chemistry because how they work is based upon quantum principles in the CNS using their pi electron clouds with iodine and the water in CSF. In the human neuro-immune system omega 3 PUFA's are converted by macrophage's to an intermediate compound called 13S and 14 S epoxy-maresin. Maresins are produced by macrophages from DHA and they exert potent pro-resolvin and tissue homeostatic actions on the MHC1 proteins. These alter their action. Resolvins are compounds that are made by the human body from the omega-3 fatty acids eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) and docosahexaenoic acid (DHA). They are produced by the COX-2 pathway especially in the presence of aspirin. This happens to be why aspirin is helpful in heart disease. No other reason. The aspirin effect is greater in men than women because men absorb more EMF than women to do electrify there DNA and protein water micelles. Experimental evidence indicates that resolvins reduce cellular inflammation by inhibiting the production and transportation of inflammatory cells and chemicals to the sites of inflammation. Maresin's promote the conversion of macrophages from M1 phenotype to M2 phenotype that specifically does not stimulate inflammation in the human body.

1\. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21601924](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21601924)

2\. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23504711](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23504711)

# Thread: I am New and need some guidance on how to start taking the supplements?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-new-and-need-some-guidance-on-how-to-start-taking-the-supplements.8204/post-95634>

Hello Everyone!

My name is Suzette and I am new here today . I have purchased all the Jack Kruse top 10 supplements and I would like to know if there is a protocol for how to take them (which is first and what times of the days to take them?). So far, I was following Dave Asprey s Bulletproof diet and lost 70lbs but I am still severely obese, diabetic T2 and with a host of other illnesses. In this case, I wanted to start over again and try to do the Leptin Rx reset and the CT, so I will need all the optimal supplements I can get . It is my understanding that all fat soluble vitanmins should be taken together. Here is what I put together:

At Breakfast:
1\. DHEA
2\. D3/K2
3\. Omega 3 (Fermented Cod Liver Oil/Krill Oil)
4\. MSM
5\. Selenium
6\. Lugold's 5% solution
7\. CoQ10

At Lunch:
8\. One-per-day Multi-Vitanmin tablet
9\. BioPQQ

At Dinner:
10\. Vitanmin C 2000mcg absorbic Acid in my Ice Tea

At Bedtime:
11: Magnesium 800mg
12\. Melatonin 30mg

Any advice or suggestion would be appreciated.

### Optimalbound — 2013-09-11T21:58:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-new-and-need-some-guidance-on-how-to-start-taking-the-supplements.8204/post-95641>

Welcome, Suzette! Congratulations on losing the 70 lbs already. Well done!

If I understand Dr. Kruse correctly, exogenous melatonin is not recommended (except possibly when doing long flights across several time zones), and that you should work on fixing your circadian rhythm and get off the melatonin supplementation.

Good luck! I think you've come to the right place.

### caroline — 2013-09-12T00:46:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-new-and-need-some-guidance-on-how-to-start-taking-the-supplements.8204/post-95649>

Welcome Suzette - I love your name! It seems you have done very well so far - great news.

You really need to read the easy start guides very closely. Start the leptin reset \[RX\] with epi-paleo.

read the easy start guide for C/T

follow circadian light cycles

Most importantly - check your EMF exposure at home and at work ....extremely important

Drink spring water only

Do you have Dr. Kruse's book? THE EPI-PALEO RX \[from Amazon\].....it will give you a great overview for all of this in simpler terms.

Please listen to all of his podcasts - they are a simple and quick way to get this information too.

And .....just keep reading all the blogs - especially the ones that pertain to you - to start with. Read and reread and ask lots of questions.

Please start a journal - It shows commitment and involvement and we can get to know you and help...

This is an amazing journey of a lifetime .....

I forgot to say .... it is really important to get testing done ...There is a lot of great detail about what testing you need in his book.

About all the stuff you bought .....In my opinion, you put the cart before the horse. It is more helpful to test first and read and then see what you might need..... someone else will have some ideas for you - I am sure

### suzgaray — 2013-09-12T04:43:18+0300

Hello Caroline,

I am very well versed in the Paleo/Primal diet AND have read MOST new updated information that is out there whether it be Jack Kruse, Dave Asprey, Chris Kresser, Mark Apple, Dr. Bernstein, etc... you name it I have read it-grin. I DO have all my labs done and completed to best of my abilities in terms of cost worth. I am Diabetic so I get labs every three months and check for Vit D every 6 months and a few other critical tests to determine my optimal health. Taking all these supplements listed above are very common for me for the past 2 years. I JUST wanted to know when it is best to take them so if you know that's great if not I will hope someone else does. I am DEAF and no podcasts are accessible to us so I can only rely on reading materials.

Thanks!

Suzette

### suzgaray — 2013-09-24T03:21:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-new-and-need-some-guidance-on-how-to-start-taking-the-supplements.8204/post-95881>

Hi Caroline,

I agree with you I am just so OVERWHELMED with too much information and being DEAF the only information I can get my hands on is what I can READ. I am at standstill point and feel paralyzed on not able to move forward trying too many different things out desperation to get off complete insulin for T2D, but since I am poor (Medicare) there is limit ability to get all these test done except HbA1c every 3 months and once a year VitD3 test that my PCP will allow :-(. I did do the WellnessFX test but it was just basic. So how do I start all over again?? I have read Dr. K's epi-paleo book many times, I fully understand what T2D have to do (but he strongly recommends a full battery of test BEFORE you start his regime, which I cannot afford so I go with my hunch and what I already know) and I want to start CT with dunking your face first, the slowing moving into deeper levels of CT. I have stopped the IF and started eating BAB in the mornings with my BPC and continue to eat LC (more like 80/20 effective) I just refuse to go back on 12 different meds like before (last 15 or so years!). I only willing to take fasting insulin when my BG is greater 200mg which happens about 20% of the time but 80% of the time it is between 120-160 mg and just keep focusing on getting the numbers down through diet and exercise, I DO take my nighttime insulin Levimer 30 mg faithfully. Since I stopped most meds and have lost 70lbs on Paleo/Primal diet I feel so much alive and better energy, BUT now after 18 months something has to change or I am done :-(.

Just venting my frustration :-( sorry. Many of us Deafies are the last group of people to get proper help because of language barriers, but I AM an avid reader and I try my best to do things on my own by learning through trail and error so far its been good journey, but I want to move out of this rut to lose the last remaining 100lbs and be off the last diabetic meds before I am 50 (48 years old now) that is my ultimate goal :-(.

### suzgaray — 2013-09-24T03:23:19+0300

### JanSz — 2013-09-24T18:04:34+0300

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=95676)
>
> If you're well versed you'd know my favorite vitamins are called a steak............

> [suzgaray said:](/goto/post?id=95882)
>
> Yes and I have it 3X a week . Does not do enough for me though :-(

I would do Fatty Acids Analysis and figure out my DHA/EPA/AA status.

Then decide between steak (O6) and oysters (O3) as general category.

Without the benefit of analysis, with history of SAD or Paleo diet my guess is that 6 months of mostly shelfish and fish diet is in order to bring up likely lagging DHA.

How to procced further without guidance from Fatty Acid analysis, I am not sure.
Could use suggestions.

One, CT intolerance supposedly indicate O6 excess.

Supplementation with fish or krill oil is out in my book, because of overwhelming amounts of EPA in them.

\-----------
diabetic T2 is indication that body expects long winter to come now

in winter there is cold, carbohydrates are not available,

increase fat consumption from summer 60% to winter 70%
\-------------
Good vitamins are usually worthless.
They contain minimal amounts of items that you may actually need and you do not need the other items.

Spectracell micronutrient analysis is an excellent way to figure out your status.

Spectracell Comprehensive Nutritional Panel
[http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular\_vitamin\_analysis\_spectracell.html](http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular_vitamin_analysis_spectracell.html)
Price: $350.00
(Or less than $100 with doctors orders + insurance)
\-----------------------------
Magnesium is big item.

Magnesium threonate ------gets to brain & spinal cord
Magnesium Malate------gets to skeletal muscles and cardiac muscles
Magnesium orotate (big one) key for ATP synthesis,

...

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)
