# Thread: How do you get enough fat with seafood?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-09-11T23:36:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/post-95644>

After reading the other thread about some of the ladies filling up on fat in the AM and craving it now, I realized that is so me right now. All I want is fat and TONS of it. I'm not sure which fat to use with seafood though. Bacon and I don't seem to get along too well  At least not in large quantities. I have been using coconut oil with my seafood off and on, but I can actually see a difference when I do this. My skin and hair both lose the softness and moisture they gained when I adopted epi-paleo.

I do eat a ton of fat for my BAB, but lunch and dinner are always seafood for me. I add an avocado for some fat, but I'm not sure what else to do. Ideas?

I'm honestly not feeling seafood these days at all unless it's wild salmon sashimi, which I feel like I could live on! I had some a few weeks ago on a trip to WA and came back and drove over an hour to a great fish market in Philly to stock up. I bought 20 lbs and it's almost gone now! It's an expensive habit I need to break . . . not to mention I'm not too sure about the radiation from Fukushima and the problems that's causing with Pacific seafood.

### BJK77 — 2013-09-12T14:22:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/post-95705>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=95671)
>
> Lots of avacado stuffed with seafood

I eat tons of avocado. That and sea veggies are my only carbs over winter, but even the avocado just doesn't seem filling enough these days. All I want is FAT and lots of it!!

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=95659)
>
> BJ, when you eat CO your skin and hair suffer?

Not from the CO, but I think because it's blocking the all the good stuff from the seafood (DHA and EPA). I used to use coconut oil all the time before epi-paleo and when I first started using CO I had improvements in my hair and skin, but when I went epi-paleo the improvements were even greater. Now if I cut back on seafood or eat it with a saturated fat I notice I lose a lot of the benefits I've seen from seafood. Honestly, my hair is just amazing these days. I hate to say that and sound braggy, but seafood makes my hair unbelievably shiny, smooth and soft. It feels like silk. My hair stylist can't get over it.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=95669)
>
> I love salmon sashimi so much too - I really could live on it. It is a pretty expensive habit but such a great treat!

It is expensive Caroline! I crave it constantly though! I'm gonna have to get another job just to afford my addiction

> [Sammy said:](/goto/post?id=95682)
>
> Is coconut oil and seafood a bad combination??

Saturated fats block the absorption of DHA and EPA from seafood.

### caroline — 2013-10-11T03:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/post-97485>

BJ ....I don't worry so much about the fat with seafood - maybe I should. I just eat a buffet of stuff at every meal. I will have a little of this and a little of that. I eat alot of pate \[everyday\] and BPC - so that ups the fat quotient and I usually have a little raw meat with a lot of fat - and salmon. I usually eat bits and pieces over an hour while doing other things.......but raw definitely feels so much better... I am not saying this is the right way to do it - but it sure works for me...

### Gagnrad — 2014-02-14T09:21:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/post-110127>

[http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-4-what-was-homos-solution/#IDComment454695395](http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-4-what-was-homos-solution/#IDComment454695395)

So I suppose _tend_ to use the monounsaturates with the seafood and the dripping and lard with the beef and pork, but if you've got some special French recipe that uses butter and cream with mussels or something you don't have to give up making it.

### Gracelind — 2014-02-14T09:45:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/post-110131>

Hi BJK77.

You didn't specify your issue with bacon. I've always loved it and didn't notice any problem till a couple of years ago. It now makes me cough and choke. I thought it was the dry crunchy texture, but, no. Turns out that "naturally cured" bacon is made with lots of celery powder or salt. It adds as much or more nitrate to the bacon as the "non-natural" sodium nitrate used in standard curing but being a veggie can be called "natural." Same thing for many processed meats such as sausage, ham, etc.

Turns out I'm extremely sensitive to celerty and celery salt! Who knew?

You may well have a different issue--just thought I'd mention one possibility.

Lover of salmon sashimi here, too!

### Ken23 — 2014-06-05T02:38:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/post-129698>

Is pecan or some other type of raw nut butter safe to use when having seafood?

### yewwei.tan — 2014-06-05T02:54:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/post-129700>

> [Ken23 said:](/goto/post?id=129698)
>
> Is pecan or some other type of raw nut butter safe to use when having seafood?

Depends on your inflammation status. In general though:

1.  it's easy to get too much Omega-6 fats from nut butters because they are too easy to eat
2.  nut butters have higher probability of going rancid (bad O6 fat and possibly bad protein due to heat treatment)
3.  nut butters are inferior to actual nuts from the perspective of cultivating good gut flora (whole nuts contains the skin and other compounds that don't get digested until they hit the large intestine)

Personally, I don't use nuts with seafood, and if I did, I'd just roughly chop the nuts and not blend it into a butter; the last time I did some chopped hazelnuts lightly toasted in butter and poured over raw salmon was divine

### Ken23 — 2014-06-05T19:26:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/post-129821>

I figured eating the whole nut was best. Just seeing if changing it up a bit was all that bad.

That does sound delicious

# Thread: Garcinia Cambogia supplement for health and weight loss
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garcinia-cambogia-supplement-for-health-and-weight-loss.8209/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Claudia — 2013-09-12T20:48:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garcinia-cambogia-supplement-for-health-and-weight-loss.8209/post-95714>

Has anyone heard of this supplement and do you think it has any place in an Epi-Paleo diet as an added supplement?

It seems to help the liver process food better, helps with neurotransmitters (improving mood), curbs appetite, and is said to be good for leptin resistance.

I am skeptical but there are excellent weight loss claims as well.

### Claudia — 2013-09-12T21:09:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garcinia-cambogia-supplement-for-health-and-weight-loss.8209/post-95717>

I just noticed the Garcinia Cambogia is in Dr. Mercola's weight loss supplement as well. It's one of several other ingredients.

### Albert83BCN — 2013-09-12T23:57:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garcinia-cambogia-supplement-for-health-and-weight-loss.8209/post-95731>

It's in a lot of fat-burning formulas mainly targeted to bodybuilders, I'm pretty sure I must have used it sometime long ago in one of that products, since I only have found one fat burner that I can really tell that taking it makes a difference and does not have Garcinia in it, I'd say that GC its not a big deal, at least for weight loss.

In fact, If you look at [http://examine.com/supplements/Garcinia+Cambogia/](http://examine.com/supplements/Garcinia+Cambogia/) a site that is pretty reliable and unbiased, there seems to be little evidence as well, who knows if it still might have some benefits that are worth it, Examine.com did not report not much more in the other areas as well.

### Claudia — 2013-09-13T06:20:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garcinia-cambogia-supplement-for-health-and-weight-loss.8209/post-95763>

Thanks for the response. I will check that out. Dr. Oz seems to be pushing it. Not that I follow him, lol.

# Thread: How was your DC and RC today?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 130
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### prAna303 — 2013-09-12T21:06:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-95716>

I think it is time to change gears now...

Not that i think enough is said about foods but we must get a more complete vision on how we can change ourself and others. Focus not only on the sides that are sick or ill but the ways how we do other things.

DC, how was your DisConnection today?

RC, how was your ReConnection today?

In this weird hyperspeed present we need to share how we do live in every aspect, so many clues we can share. We need to take back nature as a guide, find the masters and listen to that inner voice that we got from our parents and their parents.

So please join this trip and share what you think is important to become a difference in modern world today.

### prAna303 — 2013-09-12T21:24:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-95718>

My RC today...

Went to the sea once again, it is getting colder every day. The air was about 12C and water 9C and sun shining from a clear blue sky. Did some freediving for a couple of hours mixed with some climbing. Not a single soul in my sight, a single sailboat but nothing more. I can really sense that i kind of live in a bubble, everyone is at work and i play... The most important thing is that during all this i can think for free, no distractions... This is pure luxury today.

Biked home, went to local coffeeshop to discuss art and taste with a close friend. Everyone is surfing their iPhones / iPads today, no one is returning your gaze, if they happends to see it they look down. So weird that everything that is human is erased... Read some books and did som painting, the connection between hand and brain is so important... I love the touch of my pen against paper, the smell of ink.

Went to the woods for some running, same thing here... The iPhone is king, nobody leaves it behind. Guess why training today is deadly, your gadgets kills everything. The sensation of wind against bare skin, the sun kissing your back, the wet grass against your feet, all this is packed into an app that beeps and send your data to your Facebook profile... Sometimes i would like to slap them in their face to try to make them understand but hello, can they change? Perhaps when their life is crashing down...

All of this sums up to a perfect day, so many hours of simple nature makes this boy happy.

### BJK77 — 2013-09-12T21:57:07+0300

I too am envious of your life prAna303. I dream about having that kind of freedom and time to spend in nature and with loved ones, but for now it's still just a dream. One day . . .

My RC today was simple, but wonderful nonetheless. I ate both breakfast and lunch outside under a huge oak tree and also did some yoga and meditation on the cool, wet grass.

Unfortunately I also had to spend several hours at work in an office environment and tonight I must attend a school event for my daughter. I am hoping to have time to get outside and do a little walking through my backyard woods after that though. That seems to help my sleep.

### prAna303 — 2013-09-12T22:22:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-95723>

> I have to say that I'm somewhat envious of the freedom and opportunities you have - something we should all strive for. My DC today only goes to hugging a tree in a natural reserve

The biggest thing for me is when i found my meaning, my purpose everything synced up to the age of 15. This was the last year i was really true to myself and others and eight years ago i went back. I think we all can do the small steps and end up in big change, i did the other way. Changed everything from one day to another because i could not be true to myself otherwise.

Trees are so important to us, not just hugging but how the act, live and age. They are like living memories of the past. We have a saying amongst the old swedish lapps that when you really want to communicate with someone in distance, connect with a tree and you should feel how the person you "talks" to feels...

### fitness@home — 2013-09-12T22:45:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-95726>

My RC today was pitifully small compared to yours.

Spent an hour at the park this morning before work with the sleeping ducks. The stars were out when I got there and so beautiful. When I left, the sun was not quite yet over the horizon. There was enough light to reflect on the lake and watch the fish jumping.

The high point was watching an eagle fly toward its nest at the far end of the lake. Still amazes me that I can hear the traffic on the I-45 corridor and at the same time see a bald eagle flying.

Not even close to your league, prAna303, but here goes:

RC yesterday: Walked barefoot in the rain to an underground well to collect a few gallons of drinking water. Swam in the cold ocean with Inger.

DC today: After doing TriMeter measurements, I disconnected my clock radio, microwave oven and UPS at home. Disconnected the lift control box under my desk at work (made the TriMeter reading drop from 2.7 milligauss at edge of desk (100+ right next to the keyboard!) to <0.3.

Still have lots of work to do, but doing what I can with what I have, right now.

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-24T18:22:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-95955>

How am I RC today. I'm printing out a document I need to read for my new job/client and I'm going to go outside the house and sit on the grass in my front yard and "Work"

my DC will be working on line from home via a hard wired computer - no wifi.

Interesting thing happened yesterday - my first day back on the job out side the pentagon. I got a migraine and it took a conversation on Dan's Circadian biohacker's page to come to the following realization:

_"So I had what I think was a revelation last night around 3am. my migraine was linked to two things - forgetting to take my afternoon dose of thyroid meds and my first day not being under florescent lights. took my thyroid meds, had some sweet potato w/my ceviche along with CoQ10, mag and lots of water. So Jack changing the type of light you expose your self to during the day - directly impacts the photoperoid (not sure if I'm spelling that right) b/t it directly impacts the Vitamin A production. and conversion of T3 into your hormones. So when you alter Vitamin A production in some people as a result in changing or decreasing light - a migraine is a possible symptom of dropping Vitamin A levels? I'm not sure if this is even close to right on this but it makes sense. Obviously this isn't the whole picture... but I'm beginning to make more connections?"_

I was so used to artificial florescent lights (Even with the blueblockers all day) that not being under them yesterday - when my body expected it that I ended up w/a migraine. How crazy is that.

So my biggest disconnect is actually functioning in natural light! Which in itself is a reconnection...

### prAna303 — 2013-09-28T09:53:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-96361>

Change...

DC: Something has to go and this time it will be computers. Been off the internet for some days now and thru this winter this will be the norm. Had some meetings with people who needed my help with their kids and this is a gamechanger for me. When i meet up with humans that can not walk or move in a natural way my head and heart hurts, this is a problem with many today and it is getting bigger day by day. The painful thing is that i see whatÂ´s wrong but not many will do as i say... For me this means isolation for a while to crack that nut, the way to get inside peoples head and make a change. To think i need the mountains and for most of my days i will be there, checking some stuff on my computer now and then.

RC: Analog, slowly over the past eight years i knew that this is what i long for... To be standing on the other side of the gap of insane technology usage. If one should make a stand you must make that gap bigger. Been on stage, shooting for a film for some times now and that resonates with me, the analog way of life with real humans of flesh and blood.

As said in Kodaks InCamera, about the usage of film for movies instead of digital cameras:

Cinelicious “likes digital” but “loves ﬁlm.” Why is ﬁlm so important to you?

I have great childhood memories involving Super 8 cameras, but mainly I just like looking at it better. I believe that humans respond to celluloid when they see it in motion. It resonates with them emotionally. We say, ‘respect the past and embrace the future.’ It’s important to know where we come from, so we don’t get lost on the path to where we are going. We strive to stay rooted in how digital decisions derive from a photochemical process. No one has ever asked us to make film look more like digital. As a creative community, it’s important to have a choice of tools.

That kind of sums it up for me...

### caroline — 2013-09-28T11:47:54+0300

### caroline — 2013-10-04T10:12:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-96856>

My RC for today.........I was getting into the rock pool at the ocean and a young girl said to me "it is cold - but once you are in it will be fine" I told her how cold was really good and she said "I love being cold"

We talked for about 30 minutes. She is this amazing, lovely 10 year old. I asked about school - if she is a good student......she said "yes I try really hard"
I asked what amazing things was she going to do when she got older......she thought for a moment and then told me she loves horses and has her own ...and she has been riding since she was little. She shows her horse and jumps and she said she is really good at it.

I said one day I will see you in the olympics .....she became quite shy and said "I don't think I would be good enough"

I told her that a thought can change her DNA ...nothing is impossible.....She really thought about that ....and then said ..."yes - I can do that"

At that point her Nan called her to get out of the water. Later she came to say goodbye and that she had enjoyed talking to me. What an extra ordinary young girl.... I was so lucky to spend time talking with her.....

RC/DC: We have live entertainment at lunch time in the summer - outside in a park - in front of our performing arts centre.

I was so overcome by emotion and was choking back tears ..... and I don't know why. It could be a connection to my Dad - but nothing comes to mind. ### caroline — 2013-10-11T10:43:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97516>

Wow .... How powerful. ....you enrich my life Peter every time you share something with us. .... I value you so much....thanks.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-11T11:32:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97518>

I feel that i must share another side of what we call life today, a world that do not depend on the latest version of iPhone, updates on Facebook or endless dramas of modern life. I want to prove that even some of the simplest actions in life can be exactly what energizes us, the small nuances that we miss with all external noice.

Since i do something that many consider weird, strange and not productive i face resistance everywhere i turn BUT that also gets me going. If you have been hearing for years that you must do this and do that to do what i do, then you grow thick skin. Just fighting against the crowd that you must wear shoes, eat carbs, wear warm clothes and NOT be in the ocean by yourself... The big security world that is around us, but wait, who is not ill today? They say i am extreme because of what i do BUT i am not the one who are having problems with sleep, getting a flu three times a year, crashing down like a 747. I often just smile and say that it is ok for them to think so, i mean, i would have died about seven times with their way of thinking about this way of living life.

It is sad but it is up to everyone to get a life and live, i do not move a single inch on this... Even if it cost me alot of possibilities of luxury, the big win for me is that i live, and live to tell a story, live to act in nature upon itÂ´s laws.

For many of us this is going to be a solo journey, but that is also part of life... Fix the unit called human and then you will not be alone anymore, when you are working more like a complete version of yourself you simply GET company, even if you will not want it.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-12T08:54:46+0300

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=97567)
>
> Peter ... I hope you are keeping journals? I have probably asked you that before.........

Not a regluar diary...

I spend many hours writing down things but not in some sort of organized way, if someone should read they would not understand.

Why i do this is to deal with life as it is passing by, i can solve my own problems in an instant if i write it down, it leaves my mind instead of getting stuck in thoughts... I gave up journals when i decided to stop using my computer in a negative way, today it is only for research, yes i call this forum research also.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-12T11:44:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97603>

RC: Seeing some people with posture problems...
DC: Since i do the above in a normal environment i will have to DC afterwards. Going to the sea, some statics and then my own "weirdo" thing... I often go lie under a tree, with my head connected (touching) the stem. I always choose trees that looks strong and beautiful... I prefer firs, they are strong and silent, not noicy like birch. Told you i was weird.

### SeaHorse — 2013-10-12T14:20:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97606>

Peter you said "For many of us this is going to be a solo journey, but that is also part of life... Fix the unit called human and then you will not be alone anymore, when you are working more like a complete version of yourself you simply GET company, even if you will not want it."

So true....from some perspectives I have become a loner or anti-social, but I have held the line against the kind of socializing that deadens me...Being with this more "complete verson" of myself I always feel connected to the natural world and I choose to be with people that I can feel connected to. The Non-violent Communication author Marshall Rosenburg calls this listening for what is alive in others and speaking from what is alive in yourself.....that's the only kind of talking I'm interested in these days.... My life is enriched by the people and animals that I'm drawn to now...which is why I feel attached to all of you guys....this searching is an enlivening state and all our discoveries and wonderment come through on this thread so clearly...xo to you all

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-12T16:08:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97614>

As long as you ask yourself harder questions then the answers you currently have, you are breaking through; but the moment you begin to accept things as they are, you are psychologically dead. Do not pursue what should be, but understand what is.

Beautifully said... you speak my mind.

It was a beautiful 12 degree C and no wind at all. I tanked sun power for hours in the yard in bikini, laying on the grass on a wool plaid with my bare feet touching the grass. I worked on a project, and watched the 2 sheep eating grass in the yard. Perfect work.  A little hard though.. Emme was way too interested to chew on my paper and pen, kissing me, and she wanted to drink my tea too. I had a nice rooibush vanilla tea... in the end I gave up.. and let her drink it. But man was it beautiful... those moments are the most beautiful to me. I feel so totally connected with everything around me it is pure magic

> you simply GET company, even if you will not want it."

Yes... I have experienced this
I always crave the loneliness.. IDK why. I am so happy then. I like to have the animals around.. and children for sure. And just a very few special people to me. But that is about it. I feel weird. But I cannot bear to have people too close in my life me that I do not feel drawn to. It is different at work, there I socialize with all kind of people and I love that! But I do not let them into my sacred place and home. I am getting so sensitive to energies.. peoples energies. I am a bit overwhelmed

Peter I too use internet only for reserach. Yes... this forum is educating, and very much so

### caroline — 2013-10-12T23:47:48+0300

How beautiful Seahorse, I always love your introspections and how you express yourself......and Peter, of course - as far as I can see - you knock it out of the park.

Inger - that said it all for me - being around people that I am not drawn to ....It is almost impossible. Our life needs to have power and momentum and wonderment and joy in the simple things and connection to nature .........

I can only spend time with people that are truly alive ..... that narrows the field somewhat.....

### prAna303 — 2013-10-13T10:17:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97692>

DC: Took a morning ride, biking... three hours of wonderful sunrise. Biking is nice for your mind but not your body but i know how i reconnect. It is +1C this morning and it is smelling of snow now, winter coming. They promise snow on friday so i really hope they are right.

RC: Going out into the wild tonight, the sky will be clear i hope, stars everywhere. This is the time when i start to move outside again, more time in the cold and windy mountains. In the summer i do not feel this force pulling me out to sleep in the wild, to warm i guess. Going to search for a small cave i think will be in a spot that i climb, it is a small one and if i find it i will make it mine, it is a powerful place.

Think i posted this video before but it wakes the feeling of being active AND alive in the cold of winter...

\[video=youtube;audGDTvwYyU\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=audGDTvwYyU\[/video\]

### caroline — 2013-10-13T10:43:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97699>

This film made me realise for the first time in 7 years that I miss the winter and the snow of Canada. The wonderful clean, crisp, fresh air. The wonderment of the first snowfall. A very alive feeling that I had forgotten all about. I was so thrilled for all the beautiful sunshine and warm breezes and much less clothes when we moved to Australia...but winter here is a non event I guess ...and oddly - I am always cold here in winter....

### prAna303 — 2013-10-13T11:17:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97706>

... If you look around today, everyone is saying that cold should be avoided at all times. Today they learn parents in Sweden that babys should always wear hats up until they are six years old, in other words 24/7/365.

We send away sick people to warm countries in the winters, everyone is going on trips around new year here, it is to cold and they need the warm weather, they say.

From day one we learn that cold is deadly, is it strange that many must let this change grow slowly? It is a big change for most of us.

### SeaHorse — 2013-10-13T15:05:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97710>

I also left Canada after thirty years.... if I knew then what I know now I could have lived better in Newfoundland....the lack of sun is a challenge that can only be met by getting super cold, grounded, hydrated and unplugged....which I didn't do. I lost too much energy and disrupted too many rhythms to recover without the sun, but I'm happy to be in a four season world in Vermont (Newfoundland had two seasons: cold rain/drizzle/fog and warm rain/drizzle/fog).....you are always welcome Audrey to have a "summer break" here and play in the snow.

The movie trailer almost gave me a case of virtual CT! Beautiful images.

### caroline — 2013-10-14T00:03:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97743>

The little boy in the video ....that is how I imagine Peter in my mind's eye ...always curious, always questioning and super cute
Thanks Seahorse .......a trip to Vermont some day would be awesome!

When I was in Kyrgyzstan awhile ago ....my very favorite day was when we were in the mountains after they had had their first big snowfall ...we were all like children - making snow angels, building a snowman, throwing snowballs .....we all has so much fun. There was so much spontaneity and laughing and just sheer joy.It was quite thrilling.....and at the end of a 30 day trip ..that one day was everyone's favorite ...far and away, and we had seen and done some amazing things. We, also weren't dressed for that day - only sneakers and lite jackets - not gloves or scarves or anything.

Our gorgeous young guide stripped down to his shorts and was paddling around in a stream - totally instinctive ....C/T....

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-14T01:07:47+0300

### Lahelada — 2013-10-14T04:08:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97756>

I took an unexpectedly long walk today. The sun was out,the city was quiet as this is a long holiday weekend .The paraiso trees are in full flower and they line almost every street in Buenos Aires. Their perfume permeates the city at this time of year and I felt so uplifted by the heady scent.The park was busy with people lounging on the grass , playing with their dogs or kids or both,old ladies were dancing outside the community centre in a spontaneously arranged dance. Kids were fascinated with the fire juggling clowns. No this was not an organised event, just Buenos Aires entertaining Buenos Aires. Right now there is a tango trio practising and the wind carries the music to our open window.
One of the reasons why I like living here , people with all their faults are still real. During my walk I spoke to three complete strangers.We don't know each other but we have community.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-17T16:19:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-98144>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=98119)

> Just one word: S N O W

JEALOUS!

today RC was awesome so far. I walked Chaos to school barefoot, as usual. On the way back to the house to begin work- I was totally focused on the moment of what I was doing. Walking, and observing nature. as I turned the corner - a squirrel started running straight for me. I continued to walk towards him and he continued on until we met. i stopped, he stopped then he came up parallel with me looked up at me as I looked down, and he sniffed my ankle - I could feel his wiskers - and off he went about his business. I continued walking, turned to look back and he had stopped and just sat there looking at me... he had no fear of me. he wasn't worried about my actions at all, it was as if he sensed I was nature

### prAna303 — 2013-10-17T22:51:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-98175>

Amazing weather today... Got up at 04:15 this morning and snow was falling... Up in the mountain and doing my first run in falling snow. About 0C so the snow chilled me quite nice. Later on some nice biking in snow blended rain and this sums it up to a wonderful day... Like i said before, i LOVE winters in Sweden.

### Inger — 2013-10-18T10:29:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-98251>

I love the winters here too Peter. So crispy and fresh air.. and the shock I get every time I dive/dip in the river, I am addicted to it huh! I never get that in the summer, water is way too warm. Now it "climbs" closer to 0 degree C every day....

I so much more like to dip shortly many times / day instead of a long dip, because the nicest moment are the first ones  and I do not need to shiver after..lol

You got up so early? Wow! I sleep until 7 AM every day now, that is when it starts to get a tiny bit lighter outside.... but maybe the snow does it. Snow makes everything so bright  It might snow here today, no snow so far! Just a little rain mixed with some snowflakes.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-18T13:19:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-98255>

> You got up so early?

Mostly because i had to check if the snow had started to fall and bingo, just a couple of centimeters...

RC: Early trip again, ended up sleeping by a tree on the frosty and snowy ground... The funny thing is that i scared the shit out of some people, they thought i was dead, lying under a tree in shorts only at -3C. Perhaps i will have that spot by my self from now on. Did some salesjob yesterday, selling crosscountry skiing equipment, crazy amount of customers, about 1400 in six hours. Biked home afterwards in -3C and snowblended rain, good reset after being inside with "normal" people with iPhones and loads of fake light.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-24T19:06:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-98883>

RC: Been doing some testing on myself this week... Since i always felt like the natural way is the right way for me i have been eating outside every day and doing it sitting on the ground with my bare feet. It just felt like the way to go when i updated my own OS this week, my friends bought a new iPhone AND iPad Air. To early to tell if something is different but it feels better, in my mind any way. Been searching for a cave nearby but no good result yet, hope to find it before the real winter starts, planning to sleep many good nights there this winter.

DC: Bought a new fountain pen, just to minimize computer time.

Every one kept asking Chaos if she wanted a jacket and she kept saying no - but after the wind picked up she asked for it.. so I gave it to her... she always kept moving... people would come up and put her hoodie up and she'd flip it back off... her response - can't slow down electron flow!!!

### caroline — 2013-10-25T01:04:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-98932>

my R/C
I was in my apple store yesterday because my laptop is sick. We got talking about the new ipad soon to be released here and I, of course, mentions the high EMF output from them. I asked the young man where he keeps his iphone and of course you know what he said! I told him about the latest studies and Charlie Teo's concerns for many years now. He actually was listening very intently and said he would stop putting it there or next to his head. He said he spends a couple of hours a nite talking to his girlfriend and then puts his phone under his pillow. He was pretty worried. I told him to put it on airplane mode and fling it across the room and use speaker.

I asked him if he wanted to have kids .......he really was getting it! He said to me ....so basically I am frying my balls???
the answer is Yep!
So then I had the same discussion with the other young man - he, at least, doesn't keep his iphone in his pants pockets.
they really do get it on an intuitive level when you ask them if they want to have kids.......

### prAna303 — 2013-12-07T10:29:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-102694>

At last the real winter is here...

Had some of the best starters for years now, been away from the digital world a lot during past month.

Slowly turning my world more analog by restoring and repairing my old turntable. It is alive now and kicking and music is art again. This is something that really gets me going now, listening to vinyl because many do not really know how good it sounds and feels. Had some friends over and we did some testing, cd vs vinyl vs digital (computer) and everyone was shocked how analog can build a room, a presence, like a timetravel.

Another funny thing is that i also restored my first real computer, a commodore 64 with an old tv as a monitor. And yes, my own gut feeling was right. When i use a modern computer with tft monitor i feel drained. Not at all the same feeling with this old thing. So many small details that makes difference, no mouse or trackpad, not as much artificial lights from that old tv and also the feeling of a real tool. New computers are filled with fun and joy and that oldie must be programmed and used as a machine, it does not junk code inside...

As i said, snow is here. Only 30cm but everything is white... My mind and senses are cleaned in just a week. Spent my thursday on a mountain top, looking at the view for about 4 hours, complete white out. Nothing else than a horizon with snow covered mountains. Just updated my sleeping skin, a completely new reindeer skin, redy for winter sleep in the woods.

Just a picture from last year since i do not use my camera anymore, it is exactly the same feeling now.

### Inger — 2013-12-07T11:02:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-102696>

Wow.. how beautiful! We have just a mm snow here.. that is raining/melting away today. Hope for snow like you have too! So cool with the analog way and the vinyl plates. I want such a thing too now. I seldom listen to music anymore because it feels just so different than live. I found a old CD that had some very good songs so I listened to them a few times with ear phones dancing... super dance music, it was so fun... but later I measured the EMF coming out from the ear phones and it was.... crazy!!! I did not imagine it could be so bad... but my head does felt a little weird after listening with them.

I am way too little outside right now. But when I am, I always am amazed what a difference to being inside. It is crystal clear to me by now what a difference it have to make in our health... especially the colder months with that crazy fresh and crispy air and special feeling... very peaceful like everything is hibernating

### prAna303 — 2013-12-07T11:09:08+0200

Sound is waves and we feed on them...

### SeaHorse — 2013-12-07T14:46:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-102711>

Beautiful picture Peter...we're having a stop and start beginning to winter, it was so cold last week and then got up to rain/drizzle and fog the past few days. It's colder again this morning so the ice on the pond is flat and smooth....I'm hoping we can actually go skating...but now I'm thinking I just want to slide around in slippers....forget the metal blades on the skates...we'll see where that experiment leads me...

Last week we saw a live concert of the Irish traditional. group Lunasa....they have the most wonderful acoustic sound particularly because they have a string bass in the mix...flute, irish pipes, guitar, fiddle and bass....it's the beautiful balance of sounds and we were in the first few rows so I was soaking up the bass and soaring with the flute...fantastic!

I still have all my old records, but no player at the moment...good idea for a Christmas present for me from my kids....they will get magneticos from me and I'll ask for a stereo from them.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-07T18:59:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-102732>

It was 84 degrees at 230 PM yesterday here. In 1.5 hours arctic front came in and it was 33 degrees at 530. I went in the pool last night at 55 degrees. This AM the pool is 47. It is 36 degrees now and raining cold with 22 mph winds. CT baby.

### prAna303 — 2013-12-10T18:15:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-103068>

Winter, time where things happen.

As if i where thrown out of a plane, freefall thru sky, magic days are here.

On sunday, got the chance to see a dance performance once again. When i resonate like this i fall in love, this time also in one of the dancers... Okey, it is easy to look at someone and feel that they look good but sometimes you get something more, a level added. She was performing at a level beyond everyone else, fearless, weightless, silent and with powers of few. When i see something like this i get a rush that will last for months, for sure.

On monday, minus 16C, whiteout and many hours in the mountains... Mostly in shorts only, feeling more alive than ever.

Today, found a bordeaux wine that taste like heaven... Now of to the cafÃ©, moleskine and fountain pen, good old analog way.

Ciao

### prAna303 — 2014-01-03T17:34:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-104661>

> How do you survive brutal cold? If you gain the ability to generate an DC electrical field that matches the weather’s electric field, this electric field acts to repel the cold. CT magic.

Read this on the blog and must say it works... Been doing most of my running in the winter in shorts / thin shoes for years now, many cross country skiers think i am crazy because they have a ton of clothes on. Slowly i have pushed myself with comfort to -10C without any problems, it feels simply wonderful.

When i do sleep on the gound in the winters i feel like there is a sphere around me that makes me warm, i can lie almost naked on the ground under a tree for hours, down to -8C. In a strange way i feel more balanced around -5C and colder that around 0C, something is happening in the minus world, BIG time.

Cite: Nonchalant

> Holy mackeral! Do any of us do that, or would that be a more advanced trait of cold adaption?

I think most can do it right now, most of our fears about cold is teached for many generations now. I do it many days and nights and always feel good but then i have always loved cold and never thought of it as BAD.

### prAna303 — 2014-02-01T11:36:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-107700>

DC: Off computer for some time now... This thing is quite weird i feel, the more others use their iGadgets i turn the other way. Only checking this place nowdays...

RC: Oh, so many things. Sleeping outside is great again, it is cold at last and i feel great. We had an epic storm before christmas and now the whole world around me is changed, the complete nature is changed, i got my forest back. Where i used to meet others, nobody dares to go, i feel like i won a million bucks. Gone another level with my music, started to do it myself, piano and guitar. Since i do not take classes i just do as i feel is right. Been asked to join a group of people to just sit and discuss the world, like the old school thinkers. Now many turn my way and ask tons of things, almost everyone here is sick or ill now, is it the suns activity that destroys them? I get asked what i do because the gap is widening... Funny, things that they thought was insane starts to attract them now.

I have a clear horizon and intend to keep it that way. Not many things can get into my life now, they have to match every connection i have with nature otherwise they go down the bin. My taste have changed to much the last four years, i only need and want the analog version, the fine details. It cost a lot, not just in money but also in how i realte to others, it is a price i am willing to take.

Up until recently ... I feel like I don't relate to my old friends - nothing has changed there .... but holey cow - lately I seem to attract people where ever I go and every single time they want to know stuff. I am wondering what I am giving off? Obviously energy/electrons ...but why is it so much more noticeable?

There is so many people on a daily basis - I decided to stay in yesterday and recharge my battery! I was so content just staying home and sitting on my balcony and watching the ocean and the trees and birds etc...

huge changes are happening.... I have a front row seat to .... me

### prAna303 — 2014-02-05T21:47:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-108530>

When bad is good.

Got a chance to put all this at test. Was on one of my daily trips in the mountains, took a fall, hit a tree after a couple of meters and beat up my whole right side quite bad. Nothing broken, a little blood and bruises, but not really able to move in a good manor.

Took a while to collect my thoughts and make a plan, the basics for making the memory in my body work. So i took it to what others around me thinks is extreme, used nature in a manic way, almost like a addict and it worked...

\- Loads of natural water from a little river nearby
\- Loads of fat and raw salmon
\- Grounding at minus 5 C for a couple of hours a day
\- Slept outside (biggest effect in this one)
\- No digital, no technology just analog vinyls
\- Consumed music with my mouth wide open, vibrations
\- Played piano

After three days i got a intanse pain in my whole body, think like when you started to get your first teeth. I felt, this is good, i am growing again and after a day i woke up... No pain, my head was clear and almost feeling high, my bruises age gone and all that is left is the cuts.

With this i feel like new, so a really bad thing can maybe be needed to start up the currents in our semiconductors. With that overload it felt like restoring a battery, needed to clear up some sleeping components.

With all of these signs, my sleeping spot had to change, from woods to the beach i felt. So, last night, instead of my pine i went to the beach to find all ice is gone, stars lights up my night and boom... Sleeping like a baby and waking up to the first sunshine in well over two months. So even if it is one months early, for me and nature, it is spring.

### prAna303 — 2014-04-14T23:47:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-121502>

Is this why?

When doing my freediving i get a feeling of deep pleasure, not just a goofball happines but something else.

If i simply let the element take me in, let me be part of it i adapt. I have no training of regular diving because i felt that it would stop me from acting in a proper manor. When i put myself in a position where i can not use my lungs for breathing, do i let the surface of my skin breath? If i let that element get to me and not use a wetsuit like many others do, can i get a balanced way to breath under water? Air is important to life for sure but when i am down there is it a thermodynamic problem i try to solve. When one part is missing and together with the cold of water, the air in water can i stay there longer than my friends? Yes i think but also the cold makes me work.

Just some late night rants.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-15T02:03:23+0300

### prAna303 — 2014-04-16T14:14:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-121814>

Had a date with a friend last day... She is like a perfect storm these days, slowly going into a eating disorder, trouble with her husband and slowly breaking down.

Thought a lot on the mountains today and perhaps she could solve the issues like this...

No doubt she is loosing energy... And with the error in eating, she is going down fast... But what to do if the can not stand to eat?

When we are in a state of that kind of panic, our systems has to apply and try to keep the unit alive. This means, sublime things goes first, like senses. She said she thought food smells like hell, to top notes is gone for her. We try to only get thru the days with our biggest limbs not the finest things that need tons of energy to work. This must be a problem to solve.

If we loose the ability to control food intake, the unit would adapt in the wild to use other sources of energy. And yes, she used to be a surfer so i told her she is missing that huge source of life, today she only surfs the iPhone. It is all about how we can use our whole unit to sense the elements. When i told her that this is perhaps everything i do just to survive today she kind of clicked.

She is on the road to hell but if you pay attention to small doors, you can escape, and she has. I got an e-mail that she is leaving her man and taking up her surfing again, she sat the whole night by the sea and like she said, it all became very clear. If she can not eat the energy, she must be in it for every second possible.

Is this natures way to solve that complex issue of eating? When times get rough we have a memory of that and starts a mode that eats the elements instead of man made foods, yes i do think that.

### prAna303 — 2014-04-24T00:36:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-123241>

Did some new stuff today... A discussion between unknown humans with only one connection, in a soft place with wine.

Not a single subject that was based on rules but the focus became my point of view... In the end, every single thing can be talked about with quantum physics in mind and as a guide. We talked life, love, work, illness and other stuff. I was the odd one once again, not having a regular work, regular family, regular way of thinking but it was nice...

The shift is going on and i feel hope for the future, even if i feel that i must live most of my time off-grid there is an attraction to life and other people... Now i understand that i survive because of these things and makes me see the world in a much more clear vision...

So, try it for yourself, gather up in groups of five to seven, share a bottle of wine and talk... Just like we used to before internet and likes on cyberspace.

And in memory of someone i lost but maybe will find again...

### prAna303 — 2014-05-05T15:20:52+0300

### cantweight — 2013-09-12T23:37:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-the-mood-to-hack-tissue-mineral-analysis.8214/post-95730>

Thanks for taking the time to look at this....lots of lows, no highs, very little toxic metal which is awesome because that was not always the case for me

Really curious about the significant mineral ratios....dont quite understand them and my calcium to potassium and calcium to magnesium are way off.

\*When I preview this post all the info runs together and i cant get it spaced. My number is first and the ideal number is second. In the ratios the third number is % of ideal\*

MINE IDEAL

Calcium 22 40 Below range

Magnesium 3 6 Below range

Sodium 1 25 Way below

Potassium 1 10 Below range

Iron .6 3.5 Below range

Copper 2.7 2.5 In range

Manganese .008 .04 Below range

Zinc 17 20 In range

Chromium .051 .12 Below range

Selenium .045 .18 Below range

Phosphorus 13 16 In range

Zinc, copper, and Phosphorus look great. Sodium is crazy low. I have craved salt since my 20'sâ€¦cant get enough. Fainted in public once and was taken to hospital, only thing they found was low sodium.

Toxic metalsâ€¦barely registered on chart. Woo hoo!

Lead .017
Mercury .038
Cadmium .001
Arsenic .013
Aluminum .30

Nickel, Cobalt, Molybdenum, Lithium, Boronâ€¦.all below range

Significant Mineral Ratios

MINE IDEAL % of IDEAL
CA/MG 7.33 6.67 110

CA/K 22 4 550

NA/MG .33 4.17 8

NA/K 1 2.5 40

ZN/CU 6.3 8 79

CA/P 1.69 2.5 68

Thanks for any input

T3 uptake 32 (24-39%)

TSH .329 (.450-4.5 uIU.ml)

Tested positive for single mutation C282Y hemochromatosis. No surprise. My dad has hemochromatosis and my daughter tested positive as carrier, my sons negative.

BUN/Creatinine ratio went from 11 in spring to 15 in fall!!! ugh!!!!

# Thread: Low sodium
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-sodium.8217/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-09-13T03:54:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-sodium.8217/post-95751>

I have had issues with sodium levels since my early 20's chronically low to the point of passing out from it.

I crave salt and feel like I ingest quite a bit, sea, Himalayan pink, etc. I cook with it, add it to my food, eat plenty of things meat and vegetable that come from the sea.

Where does the salt go? Why can't I seem to use it? Drs never seemed to care about it more than to mention it was low when they go over labs. Perhaps it is why I am so tired? How does one raise salt levels other....just eat more? Seems its not being used properly.

This one confuses me.

### nonchalant — 2013-09-13T04:40:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-sodium.8217/post-95754>

Dr. Kruse mentioned Cerebral Salt Wasting Syndrome, could that apply?

### caroline — 2013-09-13T05:08:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-sodium.8217/post-95757>

It must be connected to something else I would think....We still aren't smart enough are we....

### cantweight — 2013-09-13T14:25:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-sodium.8217/post-95765>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=95754)
>
> Dr. Kruse mentioned Cerebral Salt Wasting Syndrome, could that apply?

I checked it out non, but I dont have the symptoms. No polyuria, or polydipsia...my BUN/creatinine is 11 so not terribly dehydrated as would be expected.

I think I am going to hit up a doc that takes insurance and have her run labs...see if i can figure this out. I dont even know what I am looking for to be honest.

Low: sodium, mag, potassium, and calcium. Great copper, zinc, and phosphorus. Generally feel good, active mind, happy, centered, addressing field through grounding most days for at least and hour, moved to good area, limit exposure, magnetico is on its way right now( for my son, but I will lay there and do my school work all day!). I just have zero energy with a mind that wants to plan and do a million things. This salt thing has been going on for 20 years. From reading I gather there may be a connection between the lows and a hypersexual state I often experience...libido overdrive. Dr Kruse touched on where that can be a negative.

Yeah Audrey....this is totally over my head. Not as simple as just add salt it seems....wish this all came to me as easily as it seems to for others here...need an optimal brain!

### yewwei.tan — 2013-09-25T14:04:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forums-all-back-up-and-running-yeah.8222/post-96059>

Bleh, someone screwed up the url encoding code. Some special characters are not being escaped properly, and that includes parens "(" and ")".

So for example, Hemming's log has the phrase, which when linked to from the forum, leads to the URL: [http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/show...ight-(from-the-other-end-of-the-scale)/page19](http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7553-Getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-\(from-the-other-end-of-the-scale\)/page19)

The correct URL is: [http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/show...weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale/page19](http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7553-Getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale/page19)

Note the absence of the "(" before "from" and the ")" after "scale".

Only something the programmers behind the site can fix unfortunately.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forums-all-back-up-and-running-yeah.8222/post-96254>

Hey Guys,

We should not have lost any forum posts. If you see anything screwy, send an email to support and we'll look into it! [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted]).

I'm about to post a notice about passwords ... we don't know how deep the hackers went in to the database which holds all your forum passwords. We don't think they were necessarily interested in any non-admin passwords but ... it made me nervous and I actually loaded 1Password onto my devices and changes my passwords all across the net as a precaution. I had been using pretty much the same few passwords everywhere (as I know a lot of you do, too), and I didn't like the idea of hackers having my passwords and a linked email address that could fall into the wrong hands.

By your own admission - you have been a science geek since you were a little kid....how many people get accepted into a neurosurgical residency? I suspect that you have been vacuuming up every bit of information in your path your whole life and I also think it is somewhat because of your life circumstances and a Mother who had an amazing intellect and probably not in a traditional manor ....from what little you have said. I think that she must have poured everything into you that she could not access for herself - for what ever reason.

So - you had an amazing reservoir of disparate information ....and then, one day, the penny dropped.....and here we are.

I can see with my two daughters - The oldest is very street smart ...but she always had difficulty learning because of dyslexia which no one ever picked up on \[me included\] until she was about 17 ....so she learned how to cope in a very real way. My youngest daughter is very book smart - but when it comes to life - I sometimes think she got left at the station.

So - we all have our own unique abilities.........I think I have gone off the rails here....

> When mom’s make this observation of steam coming off a child’s head they carry the cultural belief that wearing a hat is beneficial. This is why many mom’s tell their children to wear their hat in cold weather. When i saw this i laughed out loud... When i was a kid i did not use a hat even if it was -15C and when i started school the teachers always tried to force a hat on me... Even if i was crazy it was for a good thing.

Jack, you are definitely curious. And your curiosity has turned my curiosity about all of this into an obsession. I turned 60 this year, feel great, and am trying to find a way to make this more than an obsession. If I can swing it, I would love to go "back to school" and study quantum biology in great depth. Very fascinating. The article you cited in a link on DHA stopped me cold \[Crawford, et al\]--made me think that evolutionary trends may or may not be "positive", like large land animals losing brain capability because of a lack of marine food--yet, that is the evolutionary trend. The whole description of electron resonances, brain vortices, properties of water, pi-electron cloud densities, fractal patterning,...the list goes on--is just stunning in its breadth of science. And yet, ultimately, the integration of these various parts leads to better understanding of how we all work. Knowing that can only lead to a realization that living congruent to the laws of physics is the only real path to health. I feel like we are all like the frog in a slowly heated pot. Ultimately, we will boil to death without taking any action. That is a fractal patterning of CT! ) Think about it. We get hotter (keep warm) because it makes us feel good, yet we should be getting colder so that we can live better.

Q'Rad Randy

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-24T21:33:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ee-5.8226/post-95965>

So, Q'Rad Randy

how are you applying some of what you've learned to your current situation given your local???? Inquiring minds want to know. I know you've been CTing for a while but interested in what other things you've pursued.

### QuantumRadical — 2013-09-24T23:57:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ee-5.8226/post-95984>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=95965)
>
> So, Q'Rad Randy
>
> how are you applying some of what you've learned to your current situation given your local???? Inquiring minds want to know. I know you've been CTing for a while but interested in what other things you've pursued.

Shijin13. Good questions. To tell you the truth, I have been doing some experimentation. I skipped CTing for about 6 weeks to see if the benefits were long lasting. Well, no they are not. I went right back into it, and after just one session, many of the benefits returned. Interesting. I felt I had a lot more energy, less aches and pains, and overall better mood. I also eat a lot of seafood--raw oysters, sashimi, salmon, etc. I also sleep on a ChiliTechnology cooling pad. I set it to the lowest setting, 46 degrees. It really helps to get to a deep sleep. I just purchased the ChiliTechnology cooling mattress and hope DW will use it to sleep better. She doesn't sleep well at all and refuses to even read The Book that I got here, Jack's Book. The mattress should arrive today via UPS!! Jack's last couple of blogs also put me over the edge and convinced me to purchase the Magnetico pads to go under the cooling mattress. I hope that is a winning combination. I am really excited about the magnetico pad. It is so congruent with the quantum biology that Jack is describing in his blogs. I hope the effects are more than subtle.

To celebrate the beginning of Fall, I have also started doing two ice baths a day. My ice baths (my CT sessions) usually are around 50-55 degrees and last about 30 minutes. Every once in a while I get the water down into the high 30's--brutal but effective (I can only last about 15 minutes, but I am starting to tolerate the colder water better). I hope to do two 1 hour CT sessions by New Year's. I am also going total ketosis with a heavy emphasis on the right fats. Three and a half years ago I weighed exactly 200lbs. This morning I was 179lbs and have been at this weight for about a year now without even trying. I am testing the winter theory of weight loss and think my ideal, ketosis, CT'd, epic-paleo weight should be around 165, maybe 160. We shall see. I am very fair-skinned and avoided the sun like the plague, but over the last couple of years, I get out in the sun as much as possible. I can now sunbathe for about 45 minutes without burning (that's 45 minutes each side ). This has certainly helped my Vit D levels not to mention my energy level.

I also supplement Vit D, Vit K2, iodine, magnesium, etc., etc. My cold baths are also enriched with epsom salt, magnesium chloride flakes, hydrogen peroxide, and baking soda.

I frequently wear orange-tinted glasses at night to mitigate the blue light issues. It definitely helps me to sleep better.

I also stay pretty active, and lift heavy things frequently.

One of the interesting things I have noticed is that I wake up in the morning without an alarm clock almost to the minute each morning. It is like that rush of cortisol is on a timer. I am asleep, then within seconds I am wide awake from the cortisol rush. It is like a switch, not gradual at all. No wakeful grogginess anymore. I wonder if this is what optimal feels like or is it a cortisol issue? My bet is that it is a measure of optimal. I do sleep a lot better than I used to.

The science of all of this is absolutely fascinating. But the recipe for health is pretty simple--get cold, eat a lot of seafood, do things to mitigate the emf problem, get some sun, sleep well, and don't sweat the small stuff. And get as much oxytocin as possible!

Let me put it bluntly. When I follow Jack's protocols, I feel better. When I drop off the wagon for even a couple of weeks, I can feel the difference in every dimension. I just wish more of my family would follow my lead, but you just can't make the horses drink. Jack's stuff works!!

Q'Rad Randy

### cantweight — 2013-09-25T17:26:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ee-5.8226/post-96079>

I feel like a huge weight has been lifted....this blog was the one that finally slapped me upside the head and shook me saying wake up dumb ass and pay attention this is your answer.

How much time I have wasted worrying about food and supplements, damning grains etc. Never has this been an issue of being fat, of battling various illnesses, its simply been about what I have denied my brain for years and the impact that has had on the body as a whole. From 2006 through 2008 I ran 104-105 degree fevers almost everyday between the hours of 4 and 6 PM with a corresponding cortisol spike. That is some crazy shit for a brain to endure...I am grateful to have recovered from that at all!

I have regained the ability to speak, write, read...etc. So i know it is healing. I am so excited to apply what i have learned from this blog. So excited!!!

Going to continue with lots of fat, some protein, tons of well water and add my new protocol of jumping on the trampoline in the cold temps with the sun shining on as much skin as I can expose without startling the neighboring cows, horses, and goats

I will continue to ground at least and hour a day and lay on my sons magnetico while I do my school work.

Man I have such a sense of peace from this...I see my whole family becoming oxygenated, hydrated, and energized with much more simplicity than i had been able to see before.

Thanks Jack!

Audrey I am honestly blown away by what is happening with my little guy. In spite of all i have learned I was still skeptical of what the magnetico would do. I expected to see nothing and hope something on some level was happening. It has not even been 2 weeks and he is doing really well. Exploded in a strange rash after night 1...rash is worse in morning after being on pad, his attitude and behavior freaked night 2/3. Since then he has been calmer than ever, speech is excellent and I saw zero involuntary movement today or OCD crap. Zero. He has a weird orange scaly crap on his scalp mostly on the parietal lobe....not a typical dandruff or cradle cap....from what I read it may be from chelating. Going to call Dr Bonlie tomorrow and talk about DMSA.

I laid on it today and had the strangest experience. I shivered hard like I was CTing and had to get up twice in the hour I was laying on it to pee urgently. That was something I always experienced in the cold tubs that I havent had for some time now. It was very odd.

OMG I am not naked on the trampoline! lol! Definitely making sure hubby gets plenty of oxy....makes it harder for him to say no to more magneticos

I went whole hog and got their mattress ([http://www.chilitechnology.com](http://www.chilitechnology.com)), California King, plus the Magnetico's Super Sleep System (Cal King). So I have a 14 inch mattress on top of 2 layers of magnets that weigh 360 lbs. Great workout carrying all of this in boxes up a flight of stairs. Tonight is the first night I will be sleeping on the combination. I had a Chilitechnology cooling pad before and loved it but DW sleeps poorly so upgraded the whole bed. Kind of an experiment in Quantum Sleeping!! The cooling pad can be set as low as 46 degrees which results in about a 55 degree bed conducive to very deep sleep.

Yes, I have been pretty healthy all of my life, but there is a reason why I follow Dr. Jack's protocols. I was just not feeling right about 3 years ago and modern medicine's only recommendation was to take Statins because my cholesterol was a little high. I had also put on a few pounds and couldn't lose the weight even with going very deep Paleo. I wanted more discussion about all of this with my doctor and instead just got a prescription. So I fired the Doctor on the spot in front of his staff and have never looked back. My current Doctor is the same one that Gretchen visits and she is great. I discovered Jack's blogs in August of 2011 just a couple of months after he started posting and now read just about everything religiously, plus I try to read his cites so I can evaluate the actual scientific research. Nothing like an N=1 to validate what you read. But I started embracing the cold and eating a lot more seafood, and feel lightyears better. Still think I can go deeper into this and I suspect that I will see even greater improvements--thus the magnetico and the chilitechnology mattress. I tell people that I feel better now at 60 than I did at 35. I have every intention and desire to be randy when I am 110 years old.

Yes, I listened to Jack's sex webinar--twice. I think he was right on in every respect. It is too bad that our schools don't teach sex education like that. They should.

One can't have too many friends!! )

Our ability to adapt and change will set us apart from the majority of people who are unwilling to change or slow to change. Some times the only difference between winning and losing is one inch of an attitude. Your attitude will determine your altitude in life. You are absolutely unique. Completely designed to stand out from the crowds, the norm and even the self doubt. When you carry around self doubt it is akin to riding your bike with brake engaged. Be exactly who you are. Be peculiar. Be authentic. Be special. Be unique.

Very interesting idea ..... but as I question everything, I have several concerns.

a) Seems like the electrical component of the bed and/or pad would be a significant source of EMF. Dr. Kruse has previously stated his dislike for water beds. Does Chilitechnology have some of the same risks of a heated water bed?

b) I have personally discovered the benefits of sleeping w/ my bedroom circuit breaker switched off. Since this device is an electrical device, it would require the bedroom circuit breaker turned on ....... or a really long extension cord to another room.

c) Have you tested your bed with a body voltage (BV) meter? Would be very interesting to see if the Chilitechnology adds to the BV reading. In my case, I found the BV reading on my bed dropped by 80% w/ the breaker off.

# Thread: What may electrify DNA/RNA?  A clue to the future of the blog.......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-may-electrify-dna-rna-a-clue-to-the-future-of-the-blog.8227/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-23T05:59:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-may-electrify-dna-rna-a-clue-to-the-future-of-the-blog.8227/post-95811>

In a cell or in an organ, under appropriate conditions a phenomenon quite similar to a Bose condensation may occur in substances which possess longitudinal electric modes. If energy, from any source is fed into these electric modes and thence transferred to other degrees of freedom of the substance (semi conductor), then a stationary state will be reached in which the energy content of the electric modes is larger than in the thermal equilibrium (zero entropy system) . This excess energy is found to be channelled into a single modeâ€”exactly as in Bose condensationâ€”provided the energy supply exceeds a critical value (this critical value is C2 in C2M= E for biology). Under these specific circumstances a random supply of energy is thus not completely thermalized, but partly used in maintaining a coherent electric wave in the substance so that it may carry huge amount of information in energy formats.

### QuantumRadical — 2013-09-25T06:03:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-may-electrify-dna-rna-a-clue-to-the-future-of-the-blog.8227/post-96042>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=95811)
>
> In a cell or in an organ, under appropriate conditions a phenomenon quite similar to a Bose condensation may occur in substances which possess longitudinal electric modes. If energy, from any source is fed into these electric modes and thence transferred to other degrees of freedom of the substance (semi conductor), then a stationary state will be reached in which the energy content of the electric modes is larger than in the thermal equilibrium (zero entropy system) . This excess energy is found to be channelled into a single mode—exactly as in Bose condensation—provided the energy supply exceeds a critical value (this critical value is C2 in C2M= E for biology). Under these specific circumstances a random supply of energy is thus not completely thermalized, but partly used in maintaining a coherent electric wave in the substance so that it may carry huge amount of information in energy formats.

Jack, I think I totally understood what you just said.  I think!? The energy doesn't get dissipated as heat but is instead captured in the electromagnetic field which can then be used to pass along information in that particular electric mode. it is all about energy capture and productive use of that energy rather than chaotic heat dissipation.

Q'Rad Randy

The energy doesn't get dissipated as heat but is instead captured in the electromagnetic field which can then be used to pass along information in that particular electric mode. it is all about energy capture and productive use of that energy rather than chaotic heat dissipation.
>
> Q'Rad Randy

Way to go Randy!!

I wonder if this would explain what I suspect is the ability of magnets to help me regulate my temperature, to stay comfortable in the cold as well as the heat. In fact, CT also seems to confer this benefit.

# Thread: Brand New and Loving Life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### veelansu — 2013-09-23T22:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95835>

Hello everyone. 49 y/o female. I am new to the forum. I am going to start a diary like everyone else to track my progress. I have been on the Leptin RX for about 40 days, and OMG what a difference a few days make. I have had the most wonderful success with this way of eating so far that I feel like I finally found out what has been going on in my body since the year 2000. That was the year that I was told I had to get a hysterectomy and rather than do some research, I followed blindly. I learned a very valuable lesson to not trust blindly any more. But that surgery, loss of my ovaries, caused havoc on all my hormones and general health. So many things started going wrong. I went from 140 to 210 lbs in under 6 months. I was told it was not my thyroid since my TSH was within range. I was always cold, my hair was falling out, so many other horrible symptoms. I started furiously searching online for relief. I found it in the form of several yahoo groups that got my thyroid under control with the help of a very understanding doctor. The issue was that I still struggled with my weight. I could not understand why...why...would the weight not fall off. This is years of looking for answers, low carb, low cal, low fat, etc. Nothing worked for long, and I would always plateau around 165. Then would regain always ending up around 180-185. So frustrating. Then this past June, I went to the doctor and my Total Cholesterol was 349. Could not believe it. My grandmother died of a heart attack at the age of 52. She was overweight and died with a smile on her face, dancing at a party. Still, I was not looking forward to following in her footsteps. I searched online again but this time with a different mind set. Rather than look for a diet, I searched for what would cause someone to not be able to lose weight, and I found this great site. After 40 days, my total cholesterol is 197. along with other great lab numbers. My weight, I went from 182 to 167. I am still losing and loving life. I am going to post in the lab area to see if I need to do anything else to optimize my heath. I still have some cortisol and aldosterone issues that I am trying to resolve, but the progress so far has really made me a believer. Thanks Dr. Jack, and all the members who provide such valuable information for us to take control of our own health.

### Inger — 2013-09-23T22:29:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95836>

Welcome to the forum, Veelansu! What great progress you have made, wow! Very kind of you to share it with us.
This is such an exiting, never ending journey

### fitness@home — 2013-09-23T23:36:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95843>

Welcome Veelansu! Can't wait to follow your journal.

### caroline — 2013-09-24T03:16:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95879>

Welcome Vee ...what a wonderful story that will give so many hope. It sounds like you are 100% committed and fully engaged! Please do start a journal - it ups the commitment level and it is fun to look back and see your progress. This journey to your own personal n=1 won't always be linear ....we are all a work in progress ....but this is our journey of a lifetime... we don't get a do over.

Do you have Dr. K's book? he goes into a lot of detail about labs etc. Have you listened to his podcasts? you get lots of great information quickly. The webinars and Q&A's are an amazing resource as well. Think of it as the best investment you can make in your health.
