### veelansu — 2013-09-24T08:45:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95913>

Yes, I bought the book the first day. I downloaded all the podcast but have not listened to them yet. I'm trying to absorb the book and the blog posts. So much great information. I am so committed to this. Especially with the great progress I'm making. I have good will power, so I'm being really strict with the program. The journal will help me also to keep myself straight in those moment when I may stray. Keep me honest so to speak. My daughter is a pastry chef and I have a huge sweet tooth so it's been challenging at times, but I've been strong and kept my distance from the restaurant she works at. She also helps by not bringing me anything even when I ask. . She a good kid.

### caroline — 2013-09-24T09:26:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95917>

Honestly ....it isn't about will power really. Are you doing the BAB? If you are eating enough fat and protein within 30 minutes of rising ...it knocks out all cravings....it absolutely does. I wouldn't thank you for even a bite of cake - wouldn't be able to do it and everyone else will tell you much the same thing.

We had a restaurant for 35 years and I made everything from scratch ....fresh desserts and homemade bread every single day. Now, if I want to treat myself to something it is blue cheese and red wine and seafood ....and I can't wipe the smile off my face

### Determined — 2013-09-24T09:33:10+0300

No fruit or anything for now except a bit of dark raw chocolate and cream in my coffee in the morning. Might have to give that coffee up too though as I have an AI disease ( Hashi's) and coffee is very dehydrating as per Jack.

### veelansu — 2013-09-24T23:30:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95972>

I definitely do the BAB, and I am not hungry most of the day. I start getting hungry about 4 or so. Then I get home from work and eat dinner. The thing is that after dinner, I always get a hankering for something sweet. Always. I don't know if it's just a habit, or a real craving. What I have been doing is making an avocado chocolate pudding. Avocado, coconut milk, coconut oil, organic cocoa powder, stevia and some coconut sap. Process it all together, then eat about 1/4 of the batch after dinner. That helps. I don't do this every night, just when I really can't stop myself. My issue with sweets is not about hunger. It's about seeing something and wanting to shove it in my mouth. I've been better these days on the Rx. I can say no, but I still look at them with longing. .

### caroline — 2013-09-24T23:42:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-95976>

Have you tried just a piece of dark Chocolate? I think we are all the same!

Make sure you aren't having dinner too late....I find any later than five and I am screwed \[literally not firguratively .....and I prefer 4:00 - 4:30

### veelansu — 2013-09-25T02:21:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-and-loving-life.8229/post-96008>

I cannot eat that early. I work until 5:30 so I don't get home till 6. I usually eat between 6:30 and 7 depending on what I am cooking and how long it takes. Dark Chocolate has sugar which is not good for me as my glucose is still kind of on the high side. That's why I made the pudding. No sugar, same great taste, low glycemic.

# Thread: Options for People Who Can't Be in the Sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/options-for-people-who-cant-be-in-the-sun.8230/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### calishaye — 2013-09-23T22:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/options-for-people-who-cant-be-in-the-sun.8230/post-95837>

Hi Everyone,

This is my first time posting. I have been busy reading Dr Kruse's blogs and have really been enjoying every morsel of information. It is apparent that sun exposure is critical for health. I am wondering what some options are for people who cannot be in the sun. My daughter has a mild form of EPP, which does not allow her to be in the sun when the UV rays are 3 or higher. As far as I know, your body cannot make Vit D when the rays are that low. There are some people who can't even stand indoor lighting without painful reactions. At the moment, there really isn't anything conventional doctors are able to do for them, so other than D3 supplementation, are there other options available for these people, and is there a toxic dose of D3 supplementation?

Here is a website for the EPP Foundation if anyone is interested in learning more about it. It's a tough disease because even a little sun exposure can cause extremely painful reactions, and imagine having to go through life without being able to be in the sun.

I'm curious about the cold thermogenesis as well. Is that something that could be used to regenerate a faulty liver perhaps? That is the cause of the EPP, a breakdown in liver function where the porphyria enzyme is not being flushed out of the liver so it builds up. I do not know why the sun exposure is related to the liver, I would love to hear Dr. Kruse's ideas about that since he seems to be a master at the sun and how it affects our bodies.

Any thoughts would be so appreciated.

Thank you.
Jill

[http://www.porphyriafoundation.com/about-porphyria/types-of-porphyria/EPP](http://www.porphyriafoundation.com/about-porphyria/types-of-porphyria/EPP)

### caroline — 2013-09-24T04:12:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/options-for-people-who-cant-be-in-the-sun.8230/post-95890>

Welcome Jill. I have to say I know nothing about your daughter's problem ..that would be so tough.

Have you read the C/T blogs? I would say that is definitely the way to go. C/T lowers inflammation and that is what you want to do. Have you seen the C/T easy start guide? There are a ton of people here having huge success because of C/T.
It is our primordial state of existence.

Have you listened to Dr. K's podcasts? they are an easy way to get a lot of information quickly. Do you have his book? The leptin RX - It gives you a great overview of all of this and of course the webinars and follow up Q&A and you can ask questions in "ask Jack"

Please start a journal in the "journal section" and we can get to know you and help out when we can. You can also help us/ others by telling us about your daughter.

Have a look at some of the other journals - people here are dealing with all kinds of stuff and getting great results.
Please have a look at a journal by "Mr. Pinkies" and "canweight"

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-24T06:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/options-for-people-who-cant-be-in-the-sun.8230/post-95900>

CT and deep ketosis is huge for her..........CT especially......she can use the visible spectrum but she can use red light phototherapy. This is something you will have to look into.......and you need to realize the way to deliver it with someone with EPP is also unusual......thru the nose into the base of the brain into the olfactory bulb.
Protoporphyrin IX is a biochemically widely used carrier molecule for **divalent cations** that are very commonly found in the transition metal portion of the periodic table. This is where their reaction to EMF becomes interesting. People forget visible light is part of the EMF spectrum. I bet after the Sept 2103 webianr you become a lot more interested in what I just said here above. Together with iron (Fe 2+) the body of the heme- group of hemoglobin, myoglobin and many other heme-containing enzymes like cytochrome c in mitochondria and catalase are formed. Complexed with magnesium-ions (Mg 2+) the main part of the Chlorophylls are formed. This chemicals are used to make energy from sunlight. Complexed with zinc-ions (Zn 2+) it forms Zinc protoporphyrin. The key is the hydroxyl free radical............and the loss of polarized macromolecules in cell membranes can not polarize nearby water molecule in the plasma in these kids.....when you add light to the mix you generate a specific type of reaction that drives a massive inflammatory reaction by generating the hydroxyl free radical.....this is where the burning and itching come from......if left long enough T2D, cancer, or liver failure come fast because of the obstruction of the biliary tree.......light sucks energy from your childs plasma (water) .....This disallows coherent oscillations on RBC membranes because of a lack of optimal water chemistry.........One of the keys here is to realize it is not a UBV spectral issue.........where Vitamin D is made. It is the visible spectrum that is the problem. This implies that the frequency of the EMF corresponds to the altered protein........there is a specificity..........Trust me.......I am glad you asked this.......it sets the stage for Sept webinar........The point I am making to you in a circular fashion is the wavelength of the EMF matters to your daughter........alter it and you alter her life. She can make Vitamin D with a Vitamin D light. Most kids with EPP get burned thru glass. UV light usually can not go through glass. Fake light is even worse for your daughter I bet. Fluorescent lighting specifically.......because it s loaded with blue light and is associated with a massive Electromagnetic action........certain light cause protein conformational changes caused by the excitations of the frequency of the EMF.........very few studies have been done on this in EPP because no one realizes that frequency of EMF has a specificity for protein folding and this alters the action of these proteins in water..........

# Thread: The Psychiatric Drug Crisis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-psychiatric-drug-crisis.8231/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-09-23T22:46:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-psychiatric-drug-crisis.8231/post-95839>

Very interesting article ... below are a few selected passages.

It’s been just over twenty-five years since Prozac came to market, and more than twenty per cent of Americans now regularly take mind-altering drugs prescribed by their doctors. Almost as familiar as brands like Zoloft and Lexapro is the worry about what it means that the daily routine in many households, for parents and children alike, includes a dose of medications that are poorly understood and whose long-term effects on the body are unknown. Despite our ambivalence, sales of psychiatric drugs amounted to more than seventy billion dollars in 2010. They have become yet another commodity that consumers have learned to live with or even enjoy, like S.U.V.s or Cheetos.

Yet the psychiatric-drug industry is in trouble. “We are facing a crisis,” the Cornell psychiatrist and New York Times contributor Richard Friedman warned last week. In the past few years, one pharmaceutical giant after another—GlaxoSmithKline, AstraZeneca, Novartis, Pfizer, Merck, Sanofi—has shrunk or shuttered its neuroscience research facilities. Clinical trials have been halted, lines of research abandoned, and the new drug pipeline has been allowed to run dry.

In the nineteen-forties and fifties, schizophrenic patients in some asylums were treated with cold-induced “hibernation”—a state from which they often emerged lucid and calm. In one French hospital, the protocol also called for chlorpromazine, a new drug thought to increase the hibernation effect. One day, some nurses ran out of ice and administered the drug on its own. When it calmed the patients, chlorpromazine, later named Thorazine, was recognized in 1952 as the first drug treatment for schizophrenia—a development that encouraged doctors to believe that they could use drugs to manage patients outside the asylum, and thus shutter their institutions.

[http://www.newyorker.com/online/blo...ac-ssri-mental-health-theory-discredited.html](http://www.newyorker.com/online/blogs/elements/2013/09/psychiatry-prozac-ssri-mental-health-theory-discredited.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-24T00:21:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-psychiatric-drug-crisis.8231/post-95850>

No one has a clue...........but the details of how the cortex works is in EE5. And more is to come...........

I am not an expert but this is what I see. Your cortisol is lowish in the morning and spikes at nighttime - it should go the opposite way. The hs-CRP needs some work. You are dehydrated based on your Bun/c ratio. Your liver is under a stress. Your iron and ferritin is high for a woman, I believe this can indicate inflammation in your body. You are hypothyroid based on your Reverse T3 but you really need more labs - Free T3 and T4 so you can get your ratio with the Reverse T3. Your testosterone is obviously high but I don't know much about hormones. You also need to work on your blood glucose level.

I have a spiking night cortisol as well. There is a PS (phosphatidylserine) supplement that is helpful with this issue. I am also mindful of the lights in the evening - wearing the orange glasses. For the iron, you might need to limit the red meat and eat more fish. I also had thyroid issues and normalized my high reverse T3 with Cytomel. Milk Thistle is very good for your liver. It should definitely help. Drinking more water is another plus.

gall bladder out
size 14 when I got pregnant
Gave birth to a 10+ pound baby girl who after 3 months found out that there was something genetically wrong with her.
Right now she is 15 in chronological years but 1 year developmental.
was morbidly obese (260 pounds)
started going to the gym before classes. I think this is when the hormones all fell apart
working out at the gym at least 1 hour 4-5 times a week plus some on the weekends at home and developed a severe case of sacral ileitis in the hips and bursitis in them as well as the knees, pretty sure I tore something in my shoulder doing a bench press and that’s when the bottom dropped out.
doc finally diagnosed me with celiac and have been wheat free ever since.

March 2010 I finally got my engineering job but it was 45 minutes one way from our house. But I do really like my job and I am good at it.

bit insulin resistant

July 2011 LBM 140, actual weight was 193, height 68 inches.
2011 had a TSH done and it was 2.5
my T3 was 2.5, fairly low. I convinced him to let me start Armour in January 2012 and am up to 90 mg per day.
turned 40
08-13-2013, 03:39 PM----------I am due to start my next cycle maybe on Friday
08-20-2013, 11:58 AM---------follicular time of my cycle
08-31-2013, 01:56 PM--------I am in the second half of the cycle and that seems to be worse for me.
09-04-2013, 09:43 PM-------I am due to start my cycle on the 9th

I left my iodine and other half of cytomel on the kitchen table
09-05-2013, 09:12 PM----------I am on 100 mg/gm Pg 2X a day.
09-08-2013, 06:12 PM----------Tomorrow the next cycle is suppose to start but I am hoping that with being on Pg, it might wait a bit but either way, doc said to take it to day 28 (tomorrow is 25)

It would be a good idea to clarify your insulin status before you start again increasing your DHEA.
You may also want to check for PCOS.
Insulin resistance (specially with PCOS)= high testosterone.
Your TotalTestosterone is high but FreeTestosterone is low.
Your binding proteins are super high.
That (the binding proteins) may change if you get thyroid hormones in order,
but to do that you need to do preparatory work first.
(Other than 100% of dr Kruses recommendations)
Get Spectracell and Fatty Acids tested, as I posted above.

I am not a doctor. I am a man.
I use mostly serum when testing.

The article from lef.org that I posted on my post above, quotes serum values.

See what Johnathan Wright MD says about advantages and disadvantages of various tests,
he also touches on types of hormonal applications affecting test results.

>

I am not a doctor. I am a 73yo man.

There are at least two problems with cortisol.

1, to have it in just right amount on average (over 24 hr period)

2, to have that cortisol in right amounts in the right time (good Circadian Rhythm).

Following dr Kruse's protocols should help in both.

\---------------------------------

How did you come to conclusion that your 7keto-DHEA is low?
I see:
DHEA-Sulfate 180.1 ug/dL
LDL Cholesterol Calc 105 HIGH mg/dL 0-99
Progesterone 3.6 ng/mL
Estradiol 22.0 pg/mL
49 y/o and had a total hysterectomy at the age of 36----------->menopausal
Cortisol (saliva) 6.7 ng/ml 3.7-9.5 (morning)
Cortisol (saliva) 2.7 ng/ml 1.2-3.0 (noon)
Cortisol (saliva) 1.1 ng/ml 0.6-1.9 (evening)
Cortisol (saliva) 1.0 ng/ml 0.4-1.0 (night)
Testosterone, Serum 121 HIGH ng/dL 8-48
Free Testosterone(Direct) 0.3 pg/mL 0.0-2.2
\-----------------------

I am assuming that you would get improvement with better levels of:
DHEAs=(275-400 μg/dL)
progesterone=(15-20)ng/mL
estradiol=(30-40)pg/mL

DHEAs & estradiol via direct supplementation
progesterone (and cortisol) are likely to see improvement if you check and correct your likely low pregnenolone.
Myself, for that purpose, I use
Pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix 150mg tabs from Nutricology (over internet)

With higher DHEAs levels you are likely to see higher Total Testosterone, so you may need to change your testosterone supplementation.

You have very high SHBG (Sex Hormone Binding Globulin) (High TT but low FreeT)
There is some chance at correcting that by working on your thyroid.
You are supplementing with thyroid hormones and getting very high RT3.
High RT3 is a way body dumps T4:

TSH <0.006 LOW uIU/mL 0.450-4.500
Thyroxine (T4) 8.0 ug/dL 4.5-12.0
T3 Uptake 45 HIGH % 24-39
Free Thyroxine Index 3.6 1.2-4.9
Reverse T3, Serum 28.4 HIGH ng/dL 9.2-24.1

You also have a good chance at identifying many of your deficiencies using this two tests:

Spectracell Comprehensive Nutritional Panel
and
FAPCP Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)

////////////////

post your fasting insulin level

# Thread: The 10th.... STATE OF MIND (Part I)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-state-of-mind-part-i.8234/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barry — 2013-09-24T00:23:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-state-of-mind-part-i.8234/post-95851>

This is my attempt at connecting my dots. My dots are the previous 9 posts Iâ€™ve written about the most important things for your health and performance. I cover many of them again along with a few new ones, but the real message is in between the linesâ€¦.

â€œBefore I learned the artâ€¦..â€

Life is art. It consists of various different colours, consists of many different textures and many different forms. Arguably, and most importantly, its beauty and its worth are governed by your own individual perception.

We are all artists and we can choose whatever colours, textures and forms we like. More importantly, we can choose how our piece of art is perceived. However, there is a big problem in modern society and those that are trying to master their art. The problem is that people are not really choosing their own colours, they are not choosing their own textures and they are not choosing their own forms. These choices in todayâ€™s world are, for the majority, being made for you by others. These â€œothersâ€ are born out of research, literature, studies and reviewsâ€¦ interpretations of these, and the beliefs and dogmas that ensue. So with all of this freely available information, advice and recommendations, people have forgotten how to think and decide for themselves. They donâ€™t know how to decide or how to choose, the choices are many, which leads to a paradox of choice.

Furthermore, and this goes a bit deeper; but people seem to have lost the ability of self-perception. Living, health, performance and success are now all things that are dictated to you by society and not necessarily oneself. People are trying to succeed and achieve things that are determined by others and not themselves. It makes completely no sense and is probably one of the major causes of ill-health and unhappiness. Do not feel like you have to do what others or â€œdogmaâ€™sâ€ tell you to do. Do what makes you feel happy regardless. People want you to live the way they live and to stay that way. They donâ€™t like it when you do things differently and it works out. So create your own fishbowl and synthesize your own happiness. Perceive your own happiness, go on feel and do what you believe is right.

**Missing the Point**

With this optimized life that everybody is chasing, the bottom line is that you must get the point. The point is that health/fitness/performance is not a 1-dimensional being. Itâ€™s not just about nutrition, itâ€™s not just about exercise and itâ€™s not just about EMFs. The whole is greater than the sum of its parts. The physiological, physical, biological and even spiritual mechanisms that make us tick depend on an array of variables both controllable and for the large part, uncontrollable. This is all related to the dots and how they are connected. So a study on a food, medicine or type of exercise cannot be applicable to us all. It is simply impossible to accept the outcome of any study on a group of subjects, if the very dots that make them tick are not in place in the first instance.

We now have paleo this and paleo that. Grass-fed pasture raised meats, omega 3 eggs and kale smoothes. We have eccentric loading, HIIT and movnat. We have IF, Ketosis, PPP, Metcons and bulletproof x, y and z. But you know what, and this hurts me to say, but those things really mean jack shit (excuse the pun). Iâ€™ll try and put this into a bit more context. I have been a die hard â€œyou are what you eatâ€ for a long time. I make my living now working in the world of sport and performance and Iâ€™ve also been a competitive athlete and have lived a healthy lifestyle for most of my life. But the more and more I look at the woods, and focus on the big picture, and look beyond conventional wisdom, the more I realise that there are far superior things that govern our health and physical existence. These are the things which I have learnt here through Jacks blogs and others. These are the things which are the very essence of what makes us tick. The simple laws of nature, the process by which our bodies make energy, and the ways in which we process our thoughts. I am now so convinced that our health and fitness is governed by these factors that given the ultimatum of â€œPaleo foods and Runningâ€ or â€œNature and the Outdoorsâ€â€¦ I would choose the latter. Itâ€™s the reason Iâ€™ve stopped answering peoples questions about how many carbs I eat and how many miles I run. Not only are these things completely N=1 based, they are simply the bricks and mortar, not the foundations and framework. #

What Iâ€™m trying to say here is that we have ironically covered ourselves in a cloud of magic bullets which are blinding our view of what really matters. This is why you have hard core low carb zealots that are still overweight. Itâ€™s the reason you still have marathon runners and triathletes that are still not fit. And itâ€™s the reason you still have â€œKrusersâ€ that still canâ€™t sleep, still have auto-immune diseases and still canâ€™t reach ketosis.

We need to take our heads out of the magic bullet clouds. We need to stop counting our carbs and stop timing our work-outs and stop looking at our keto strips. These are the very things that actually have the least influence on your health and fitness and are more likely to be causing you stress. The real bullets, that actually can work, are sitting right under our noses. Jack is explaining these things in detail. Your health and optimized life is governed by how your genes express themselves, and this is controlled by your natural environment and your state of mind.

**It ainâ€™t Rocket Science**

Everything is simple when you know how. The knowing part though is the challenge. Do you need a Masters or PhD to understand Jacks blogs and his concepts? If you want to understand the biochemistry and quantum mechanics and how these are tied together, then you probably do or at least be someone with an extremely high intellect, its not music for the masses. I have a degree and masters in this area, work the field of health and performance and spend a lot of my spare time learning more. However, I have a hard time trying to understand it all and I even read the â€œFor non-Geeksâ€ part of the blogs. However, and this is one of the most important points I want to get across, the application is simple. Cytokines and hydrogen bonding might confuse people but walking on the grass barefoot is simple. Semi-conductors and Schumann resonance are complex topics but reading a book instead of staring at the computer screen is easy. Cytochromes and transcription factors are for geeks but eating real food in season can be done by anyone.

I could go on. The point is that the science of rocket technology is complex but a monkey can press the button. Iâ€™m not quite sure why people just donâ€™t get this. I think itâ€™s a major hindrance and it could be related to the Bruce Lee quote which started this post with â€œOnce I understood the art, a punch was just a punch, a kick just a kickâ€. As intelligent beings, we want to understand things first before we do them. In order to really feel something, to truly live and breathe it, it does help to have a deep understanding of whatever that something is. I might be answering my own question here. It may be the stumbling block for people that is preventing them from doing the simple things (which have complex science behind them). Not having a deep understanding of ketosis, photoelectric currents, inflammation, methlyation and so on might mean that they canâ€™t truly practice or embrace the simplicity of the application.

**Move slowly and you see more**

What ties in with this simplicity is the practice of moving slow both physically and mentally. Nothing happens overnight and there are no golden tickets to health. Yet people appear to expect superhuman transformations after a few ice baths, a few grams of D-Ribose and a few swings of the kettlebell in the fasted state. However, the adaptations necessary to change the pathways and mechanisms that make us tick require a huge amount of molecular and cellular changes. The machinery such as enzymes, receptors, carrier molecules, hormones and signalling molecules take time to build. Once theyâ€™re built, they need time to work and they need to learn how to work efficiently and effectively. Finally, they need infinite time so that they can all work together in harmony.

The essence is time. Take your time marching up that mountain. Iâ€™ve mentioned this before in a previous post; pave your own path to the top. The growth occurs on the side of the mountain, not at the top. So spend your time and effort on the climb. This gives time for your machinery to be built, it allows them time to clock up some mileage and it means that they will have more time to get to know each other. This is all about the dots and how they connect. Itâ€™s as cut and dry as I can put it. So donâ€™t rush about trying to go ketogenic or intermittent fast or do metcons and WODâ€™s. More importantly, donâ€™t even think too quickly about any of this. If you move more slowly up the mountain, you will observe more. The more you observe the more you absorb. And this leads to the irony of the whole story, by moving more slowly and thinking less, you start to understand more.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: The 10th.... STATE OF MIND (Part II)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-state-of-mind-part-ii.8235/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barry — 2013-09-24T00:29:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-state-of-mind-part-ii.8235/post-95852>

**The 10th.... STATE OF MIND ..Part III**

**Learn how to suffer**

So the merits of applying simplicity and taking your foot off the gas hopefully makes sense. However, there is something that materializes out of this process that begins to take you out of your comfort zone. This something is the associated â€œsufferingâ€ which is a vital component of the whole mission.

â€œSuffering and struggling are the essence of a life worth living. If you are not pushing yourself beyond your comfort zone, if youâ€™re not demanding more from yourself, expanding and learning as you go, you are living a numb existence. Youâ€™re denying yourself an extraordinary trip.â€

This suffering forces you to learn and this makes you understand. What this leads to then is a complete adaptation. The adaptation is the gold mine and it is the light at the end of the tunnel. That adaptation means that you get better at whatever it is that has caused you the suffering in the first place. It means your body produces more machinery. It makes the machinery work harder and it makes the machinery work in unison. To put some context around this, it might mean you become better at making ATP, it might mean you become better at dealing with inflammation, it might mean you become better at dealing with negative thoughts and have a stronger state of mind.

The more you suffer, in a mostly controlled fashion but even sometimes uncontrollable, the more the adaptation occurs. I think this suffering can manifest itself if you start to do things simply and slowly in several ways. Iâ€™ll use my own situation as an example. Running for long distances, without food, is very simple but causes suffering. It can the physical stress which causes muscle damage, it can be physiological stress which causes inflammation and it can be psychological stress that causes negative thoughts. However, the more I have overcome these stresses, and the more I have suffered whilst experiencing this, the more I have adapted. My muscles have become more conditioned, my engine has become more efficient and my mind has become stronger. To a lesser extent but in the same vein, I donâ€™t wear compression garments, I donâ€™t use ice baths, I donâ€™t take anti-inflammatories and I donâ€™t take energy drinks or electrolytes. Why? The same reason and I have already written a separate post about this, but if you keep on blocking the acute stress then you inhibit the very adaptation that you want to achieve. Iâ€™d rather let the adaptation occur, but it requires suffering. Although I am only referring to performance here, I think this concept applies even more so in day to day life. There are many other different everyday forms of suffering that people are just not learning about. Not having access to wifi or internet can be a form of suffering. Not being able to drink coffee or to eat bread anymore could be a form of suffering. Changing job or moving house could also be a form of suffering. Youâ€™ll hear people say â€œoh, I canâ€™t live without x, y or zâ€â€¦. but of course they can, if they are prepared to suffer.

So suffering is an integral part of the game. The simpler and slower your life becomes, the more suffering you will and should have to endure. I never said this was going to be easy. If it was easy then everyone would do it. However, if you do the simple things well, in a slow manner, then you should naturally move further away from your comfort zone. The further away you move, the more suffering you will face. The more suffering you face, the closer you will get to becoming optimized.

All this suffering Iâ€™m talking about â€“ physical exertion, performance stressors, less luxuries, financial losses, more pain etc is basically your own orchestra of suffering, and the conductor of this orchestra is , your STATE of MIND.

**Root Cause Analysis**

This wouldnâ€™t be a â€œBarryâ€ post without stirring the pot. After all, the truth usually hurts and my aim is always to tell it how it is whether people like it or not.
Iâ€™m going to strip things down here to identify the root cause. I spent 6 yrs as a process chemist prior to what I do now. I spent a lot of time investigating technical issues and doing root cause analysis. So Iâ€™ll try and do the same thing here.

There are several smoking guns when it comes to ill-health but there is one smoking bazooka. If you want to limit your choices, get the point, embrace the simplicity, move more slowly, learn how to suffer while eliminating two of the biggest enemies in the game â€“ artificial light and EMFs, then you need to get off the very device you are probably using right now to read this. The light and EMF issues do not need to be explained by me, Jack is doing this in-depth. I think though that damage to our well-being goes much deeper and it is all related to the points I have tried to convey so far. Hereâ€™ a practical example to help explain what I mean:

So you wake up and within minutes (or worse, while still in bed) you pick up your smart phone to check for the latest tweets, blogs or facebook posts. You may have left a comment on a forum and you are eager to check the response. First mistake â€“ this instant free access to information immediately increases your choices and bombards your mind. You then have breakfast and read an article that 100g of protein might be too much for ketosis and the omega 3/6 ratio should be 4:1 and not 6:1. 2nd and 3rd mistake: you are not keeping things simple and you are moving too fast. During lunch then or during â€œworkâ€ you surf the internet to read about IF and IGF-1. You find that some guruâ€™s are doing 24 or even 36hr fasts and that Jack is only eating one meal a day during the summer. Mistake 4: you are missing the point, thatâ€™s the path they are on, you need to focus on yours. Later on in the day you listen to a podcast and hear about â€œbiohacksâ€ that make you superhuman and feel â€œawesomeâ€. Arguably the biggest mistake â€“ these are things that essentially inhibit the adaptation, you are going to miss out on a part of the suffering.
Later on, you read about PPP, D-Ribose, emWave2 and Iodine. Mistake 6, youâ€™ve just added salt to the wounds. These are the trees and not the woods. The woods are the laws of nature that are the very foundation of our evolutionary biology. Taking 10g of D-ribose a day, plugging in your apps/aids and counting your daily CHO intake are not the foundations.

Finally, and I want to get to the whole crux of the matter here. Just what have all those â€œmistakesâ€ done to your state of mind ?? Youâ€™ve just been given more choice, more information, more expectations, more short-cuts and more magic bullets. You also have reduced your necessity to think for yourself and you are losing your ability of self-perception. What follows is a change to your thought process. You now think that you have to eat 80g protein instead of 100g, you think that you have to eat 1 meal a day, you think that you have to use apps and aids to sleep better, you think that you have to take D-Ribose to become PPP adapted, you think that you have to fast for 18hrs instead of 16hr to lower IGF-1. But, and a big but, it is likely that what this has done is created the very thing you want to minimise â€“ stressful thoughts ! Even further, to really be controversial, what if all these thoughts are actually detrimental to how your genes express themselves. Is there anyone out there that for example is sleeping worse now than before when they knew little about blue light, CCGâ€™s, leptin and schumann resonance ?? Are you going to bed now in more of a â€œworriedâ€ state because you know that the bit of TV you watched or the food that you eat 3hrs before bed, is â€œpossiblyâ€ going to affect your sleep ? Did you switch off your genes that produce ketones because you stressed over eating 75g of carbs instead of the â€œrecommendedâ€ 50g ? Remember, a thought can change your DNA.

There is a huge paradox here of course before people try to shoot me down. All this freely accessibly information with blogs, forums, articles, journals etc etc is of course very useful and Iâ€™m sure many have benefited from the advice here on Jacks sites and others. Iâ€™m also looking at this from a healthy personâ€™s perspective who wants to stay healthy and improve performance, Iâ€™m not trying to reverse disease. However, the question now to be asked is this, is there a tipping point to the amount and type of online information available to us today ? Do the cons outweigh the pros ? Yet again, context comes into it. Should you live your life through forums and social media ? I think not. Should you believe everything you read and accept all advice ? Again, no, this goes back to listening to yourself and finding your own path. Should you be trawling through every blog and forum, constantly searching for answers and more new theory ?? Iâ€™m conscious about going into too much of a ramble here because thatâ€™s not what Iâ€™m about. Iâ€™ll get back to my own bullet points and make it clear. What Iâ€™m trying to say in plain English is this: todayâ€™s easily accessible info about everything related to health is preventing you (in certain contexts of course) from thinking for yourself, moving slowly, understanding more, applying the simplicity, suffering more and adapting more. At the same time you are being hit by two of the biggest factors that got us all into this mess in the first place, blue light and EMF.

### fitness@home — 2013-09-25T17:18:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-state-of-mind-part-ii.8235/post-96076>

Barry, you make your points very well and give a lot of food for thought. Off to ponder...

### nonchalant — 2013-09-25T17:45:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-state-of-mind-part-ii.8235/post-96082>

Ok, while I'm cleaning my weapon here, let me just say that I love this part:

> All this suffering I’m talking about – physical exertion, performance stressors, less luxuries, financial losses, more pain etc is basically your own orchestra of suffering, and the conductor of this orchestra is , your STATE of MIND.

It is truly amazing what was once sheer torture is now enjoyable. Waking up with the sun, being cold, avoiding bread/fruit/sodas, getting blasted by the hot sunshine. After learning the reasons for this suffering, my attitude has turned around completely. I embrace situations that I used to avoid. And I feel powerful, as I reap the benefits.

I am trying to reverse disease, so I have a different approach. But you are right, blue light and artificial EMF are the root cause. But the Earth's decreasing magnetism doesn't help.

### Inger — 2013-09-25T21:23:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-state-of-mind-part-ii.8235/post-96102>

I liked your post Barry
I totally get what you are trying to say.

(me too had to remove the () from the link to be able to read it... maybe some quantum magic..)

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-25T22:48:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-state-of-mind-part-ii.8235/post-96114>

Barry - I have to say initially your premiss that suffering is necessary to achieve success was off base - but as I continued to read through this post - it reminded me of a peer-to-peer coaching session I did on monday. my peer - wanted a silver bullet to her issues. and she wanted to fix her issues yesterday and that the current course she was on was worse than anything previous. the reality is any change, good or bad, places you in a position that is uncomfortable. perception determines if where you are at in that given moment is suffering or not.

My n=1 all change leads to something better, and there is no suffering, I am constantly moving forward. Are there instances where I'm uncomfortable with my decision, a changing environment, or pursuing a new endeavor - yes. but there is no suffering. Are finances tight -yes, but there is no suffering. With each change, I find new freedom.

What is different between me and the peer I counseled earlier this week. She's suffering, and I'm not. we both still have migraines but I am functional, she's not but wants to be. thoughts change our DNA. I see the world through a different lens than I did 6mths ago or even 2years ago. I have replaced suffering with actionable change. they might actually be the same thing. regardless for me there is no suffering, only forward progress. each day is better than the previous.

Now the real question - what is different btwn me and you and our perception of our current states? I think its our perception of where we currently are at this given point in time, and that's all. I am the same as you and you are the same as me, only we express our selves and our thoughts differently due to the efficiencies or inefficiencies within our own microcosm, yet we both affect each other with our perspectives. so as I open schrodingers's box is the cat suffering or is the cat in a state of bliss? or is the cat changing as a result of both of us looking at it from our respective point of view?

# Thread: The 10th.... STATE OF MIND (Part III)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-state-of-mind-part-iii.8236/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barry — 2013-09-24T00:37:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-state-of-mind-part-iii.8236/post-95853>

If donâ€™t believe me, which is fine, then simply try this out yourself. Just like paleo asks you to try it out for 30 days, why donâ€™t you do the same with internet and forums and social media. Donâ€™t worry, Iâ€™m not trying to shut down Jacks forum. Simply give it a break and come back so everyone can share their experiences. Start with a 24hr fast even ! 10yrs ago, there were no iPhones , iPads, Netbooks, Facebook ,Twitter or even health guru forums, so what makes you think it is impossible to do without them now ? A potential root cause deserves a potential solution. Use what you have already learnt instead of trying to learn more, apply the basics and think for yourself. Read a book instead of surfing the net, speak to your friends and family instead of tweeting them, look in the mirror instead of analysing your labs. When you know better, you do betterâ€¦. Or to put a real twist in it, when you â€œthinkâ€￾ you know better, you do better.

**We donâ€™t know it all**

These are just my thoughts and how I perceive things. Iâ€™m not saying that I am right and I will be the first to admit that I donâ€™t know it all. Furthermore, and this goes for all guruâ€™s out there, but even the scientific truth can still not be taken as gospel. Due to the diversity and variability of the human body and human existence, there cannot be a universal truth. The clichÃ©s are correct, no one dress size fits all and there are different horses for different courses. On top of that, but a lot of what is proclaimed about health and fitness is down to individual interpretation. Youâ€™ve then got commercial basis and confirmation bias. I think there is even a subconscious bias. For example, again using myself, most of you will know about my FST approach. I believe in it and practice it, therefore I know it works and want it to work so subconsciously I am biased towards it.

As science is infinite and evolving, itâ€™s also an interpretation that creates a belief and even a bias. This is why things donâ€™t always add up. This is why there is no one way for us all to eat, train and live. We might have evolutionary biology, quantum mechanics and Einstein laws but there are still things that humans are doing today that cannot be explained. This ties in with this whole concept of epigenetics. We are not the same as we used to be. We can adapt in a lot of ways. Not only that, but I think there are deeper, spiritual even â€œsubâ€￾ subconscious thought processes that can govern our existence. Maybe this comes across as hippy â€œquackâ€￾ talk but sometimes the paranormal can become normal. Or put more simply, isnâ€™t not fair to just accept that what may appear crazy at first can turn out to be true? I have witnessed and observed enough in my field of work to make me question everything we thought we knew was right. We have people that appear to do everything wrong yet succeed and vice versa. We have lifelong smokers that never get cancer and healthy non-smokers that do. We have fat obese people that are happy and thin fit people that are sad. Can you â€œthinkâ€￾ yourself better? Can you â€œthinkâ€￾ your muscle to grow and your fat to burn?? There are people living in cities, which never eat fish, which drink fluoridated water, and do very little exercise yet never suffer from any Neolithic disease. There are paradoxes all over the place when it comes to health and how our bodies work.

What Iâ€™m hinting at here is not that good circadian biology, fluoride free water, low EMF and seafood is bad for you. What Iâ€™m trying to question is whether there is something even more powerful that can determine our well-being even if we are living sub optimally. After all, the people on this forum and general palesosphere are the minority. The majority of healthy people that live long, healthy and disease free are not sitting in ice baths sipping on mackerel head smoothies. When you think this way, you start to question what seems right. Maybe there is something that overrides everything else. Could that something be your State of Mind??

**Wrap up**

I believe that it is the way in which we live and think that determines our health. Itâ€™s not our food, itâ€™s not our exercise, itâ€™s not our supplements and itâ€™s not because we were â€œborn that wayâ€￾ i.e. our genes. Itâ€™s not a case of â€œwe are what we eatâ€￾, itâ€™s more like â€œwe are how we live and thinkâ€￾. How we live and how we think is governed by how we embrace the simplicity and apply the laws of nature. This process is determined by paving your own path in a slow manner which creates a journey whereby you decide on the directions to follow. You then enjoy the journey but you let it go to places you are not familiar with where you experience the suffering but adapt. This is how you get the point and this is how you understand the art. And if you want this journey to flow naturally, then get off the internet the minute you feel youâ€™ve reached the tipping point, which for practically everyone who is reading this, is right now. I believe in practicing what you preach, so thatâ€™s what Iâ€™m going to do. Finally, and most importantly of all, how you perceive everything and ultimately how all the dots connect, is all down to your own State of Mind.

I will finish this post with a bit of irony. The quote Iâ€™ve been using since my first post is from a track by Nas, it goes â€œBeyond the walls of intelligence, life is definedâ€￾. The reason Iâ€™ve used this is because I donâ€™t believe that life can really be explained through our intelligence, I think it goes far deeper, as Iâ€™ve indicated. In addition, I never intended on writing 10 posts, and I never thought I would finish by telling you that the most important thing about your health is your state of mind. But it is. And to double the irony, Iâ€™m doing this on a neurosurgeons website. However, the real irony is that the title of the track where I got the original quote from contains the words that make up the title of this last post; itâ€™s called â€œNew York State of Mindâ€￾.

Seems a little trivial to end my swan song with words from a Rapper, so Iâ€™ll use something with a bit more credit and something which sums up what I have just written about:

â€œWhether you think you can do something or canâ€™t, you are rightâ€￾ Henry Ford

Iâ€™ve said more than enough,

Iâ€™m out.

"My teaching, if that is the word you want to use, has no copyright. You are free to reproduce, distribute, interpret, misinterpret, distort, garble, do what you like, even claim authorship, without my consent or the permission of anybody.â€￾

Or put more simply, isnâ€™t not fair to just accept that what may appear crazy at first can turn out to be true? I have witnessed and observed enough in my field of work to make me question everything we thought we knew was right. We have people that appear to do everything wrong yet succeed and vice versa. We have lifelong smokers that never get cancer and healthy non-smokers that do. We have fat obese people that are happy and thin fit people that are sad. Can you â€œthinkâ€￾ yourself better? Can you â€œthinkâ€￾ your muscle to grow and your fat to burn?? There are people living in cities, which never eat fish, which drink fluoridated water, and do very little exercise yet never suffer from any Neolithic disease. There are paradoxes all over the place when it comes to health and how our bodies work.

What Iâ€™m hinting at here is not that good circadian biology, fluoride free water, low EMF and seafood is bad for you. What Iâ€™m trying to question is whether there is something even more powerful that can determine our well-being even if we are living sub optimally. After all, the people on this forum and general palesosphere are the minority. The majority of healthy people that live long, healthy and disease free are not sitting in ice baths sipping on mackerel head smoothies. When you think this way, you start to question what seems right. Maybe there is something that overrides everything else. Could that something be your State of Mind??

**Wrap up**

I believe that it is the way in which we live and think that determines our health. Itâ€™s not our food, itâ€™s not our exercise, itâ€™s not our supplements and itâ€™s not because we were â€œborn that wayâ€￾ i.e. our genes. Itâ€™s not a case of â€œwe are what we eatâ€￾, itâ€™s more like â€œwe are how we live and thinkâ€￾. How we live and how we think is governed by how we embrace the simplicity and apply the laws of nature. This process is determined by paving your own path in a slow manner which creates a journey whereby you decide on the directions to follow. You then enjoy the journey but you let it go to places you are not familiar with where you experience the suffering but adapt. This is how you get the point and this is how you understand the art. And if you want this journey to flow naturally, then get off the internet the minute you feel youâ€™ve reached the tipping point, which for practically everyone who is reading this, is right now. I believe in practicing what you preach, so thatâ€™s what Iâ€™m going to do. Finally, and most importantly of all, how you perceive everything and ultimately how all the dots connect, is all down to your own State of Mind.

I will finish this post with a bit of irony. The quote Iâ€™ve been using since my first post is from a track by Nas, it goes â€œBeyond the walls of intelligence, life is definedâ€￾. The reason Iâ€™ve used this is because I donâ€™t believe that life can really be explained through our intelligence, I think it goes far deeper, as Iâ€™ve indicated. In addition, I never intended on writing 10 posts, and I never thought I would finish by telling you that the most important thing about your health is your state of mind. But it is. And to double the irony, Iâ€™m doing this on a neurosurgeons website. However, the real irony is that the title of the track where I got the original quote from contains the words that make up the title of this last post; itâ€™s called â€œNew York State of Mindâ€￾.

Seems a little trivial to end my swan song with words from a Rapper, so Iâ€™ll use something with a bit more credit and something which sums up what I have just written about:

â€œWhether you think you can do something or canâ€™t, you are rightâ€￾ Henry Ford

Iâ€™ve said more than enough,

Iâ€™m out.

"My teaching, if that is the word you want to use, has no copyright. You are free to reproduce, distribute, interpret, misinterpret, distort, garble, do what you like, even claim authorship, without my consent or the permission of anybody.â€￾

### suzgaray — 2013-09-24T04:53:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-95892>

Hello My name is Suzette and I am DEAF (proud to be!) and this is my first attempt to write a journal here. Even though I have Ph.D. and the first Hispanic Deaf to get it in USA, written English is NOT my primary first language. I much rather prefer ASL or what we call VLOG (video signing!). However, I decided to embarked on this journey and would like to try doing a written journal here. I guess I should apologize if my English written is not clear or doesn't follow proper grammar. I just want to be able to write my mind freely so if I don't make any sense oh well at least it beats talking to myself .

Where do I begin?? Hmmm, to keep a long story short. I am T2D, and was on 12 different meds for the past 15 years and I felt like I was getting sicker, more tired, and in pain all the time. I finally decided to do something about it because my healthcare was more interested in feeding me PILLS then helping me find a way to better health. I believe this is true for many of us who are Govt./medicare dependents. I think we are the "gennie pigs" for trying new meds and collecting data whether its successful or not they don't care. So with that in mind, I started the Paleo/Primal diet under Mark's Apple, then moved to Tim Ferriss 4 hour body, then moved to Dave's Asprey's Bulletproof diet. BF diet worked the best for me with BPC in the mornings and IF protocol following eating 2x a day be done before 8PM. After 9 months I lost my first 70lbs (I am obese about 150lbs overweight so almost half way there!) I must say that I did not follow my doctor's advise to continue those meds, I threw them all away and went cold turkey had BG so out of whack for awhile (200-300 mg) but after the horrible storm and sickness (the usual WD and symptoms of letting go of gluten, sugar, following low carbs, etc..., about 6 weeks I finally was able to breath and see the light at the end of the tunnel and I keep focus on that. My sugars are better (maybe not to norm, but at least workable for me!) averaging in 120-145 mg range, headaches, body pains, night sweats, fatigue, all were gone. I could now focus more on the diet and the new energy gave me the desire to work out, see it all was worth it. The only meds I DO take is Levirmer at bedtime because I have that critical Dawn phenomena effect where my fasting sugars are still too high in the mornings (140-160 mg) but after mid morning it drops below 120 mg. I believe after I get a better grip on my eating habits and lose more weight it will better. I finally found Dr. Kruse stuff by accident in the forum of Dave's Asprey website. I was curious to know who he was and what his views were. I immediately bought his book EPI-Leptin Rx book and read it several times and decided to give it a try which means eating BAB at least 50g of protein in the morning, continue to follow paleo diet but little or no starches, AND soon will embark on CT protocol in hopes this will be the right direction to move forward. I have been stuck and regained 10 lbs in the past 4 months (not bad considering what others who have similar backgrounds-wink!). Soooo, this is where I begin.

Thanks for "listening" .

### JanSz — 2013-09-26T00:21:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-96125>

> [suzgaray said:](/goto/post?id=96116)
>
> Hi Caroline,
>
> Unfortunately, I do not tolerate meat fats well ( I barf!) so the only fats I seem to do well is MCT oils, avocado, bacon, grassfed beef, goat, elk, lamb, tallow, grassfed lard, etc... Can I continue to use these??

Except for MCT and avocado, all above have preponderance of omega6 no matter how organic or grass fed it is.

Proteins come with fat.
There are also separate fats.

The idea is to divide fat sources into three categories.

Souce of Omega 6
Source of Omega 3
Source of energy

Without benefit of testing, the chances are that you (and most everybody else) have way too much of omega 6.

I suggest that for the next six months you stay away from foods that contain lots of omega 6.
Omega 6 is everywhere, it is very hard to avoid it, you will not be 100% successful, do not worry you will not starve via lack of omega 6.

Additionally, unless found otherwise by testing, do not use fish oils or krill oils. They have unhealthy high levels of EPA.

......................
from salt sea waters

oysters
clams
crabs
octopus
snails
sardines
anchovies
mackerels
herrings
\----
\----
salmon
tuna

...
Eating can food watch for any oils they come with, often it is oil with high omega 6 content, drain it out or get other brand.

...

### caroline — 2013-09-26T08:10:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-96172>

I can see that you are a worry wart! I understand completely that you want to do everything you can to help yourself.

Please check out other journals. Lots of people here are dealing with some very tough issues. Have a look at Cartweight's journal and Mr Pinkies journal. Mr. Pinkies (Kate) has lost way over 100 pounds this past year and is dealing with autism.

The really important points about the leptin reset are....tell me if I am boring you to tears - ok?

Eat a BAB within thirty minutes of rising. We also need to have the oxytocin talk!

Leave at least 4 hours between meals and before bed. Absolutely no snacking! If you are having coffee or tea - just black

Drink spring water - lots

Eat seafood as much as possible and lots of fat...

Get outside and walk in the grass or dirt or ocean. (where are you btw). Lay under a tree

C/T

Avoid fake light ..,.. When the sun sets - so should you

What is your EMF exposure? Are you limiting/avoiding wifi? ...this maybe the most important thing for you.

I am going to go back and re read what Dr. K. Said to you and get back to you....

About C/T.....I put a bag of ice on my tummy when watching tv. I just fill a large Baggie with water and freeze.
You can start by wearing a tight compression shirt and putting the ice on top. The compression shirt helps to collapse the fat cells.
I now just wear a light t- shirt and then after awhile put the ice right on my tummy.

But again - just do what you can for now. I promise that in a few weeks you will have it all down pat. And then it comes down to figuring out what works for you - what you Need to do for your own n=1

Sorry - I was just trying to make it simpler and maybe I have confused you more?

Is your computer wired in? get rid of wifi.....

> About C/T.....I put a bag of ice on my tummy when watching tv. I just fill a large Baggie with water and freeze.
> You can start by wearing a tight compression shirt and putting the ice on top. The compression shirt helps to collapse the fat cells.
> I now just wear a light t- shirt and then after awhile put the ice right on my tummy.
>
> But again - just do what you can for now. I promise that in a few weeks you will have it all down pat. And then it comes down to figuring out what works for you - what you Need to do for your own n=1
>
> Sorry - I was just trying to make it simpler and maybe I have confused you more?

I am going to cut and paste this list for daily reviewing till it becomes automatic . THANKS!

### nonchalant — 2013-09-26T18:30:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-96217>

Suz, would you be able to put a ground rod in the soil near your bedroom? Instead of grounding through your home's electrical system, an improvement would be to ground directly to the soil.

### suzgaray — 2013-09-26T23:42:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-96226>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=96217)

> Suz, would you be able to put a ground rod in the soil near your bedroom? Instead of grounding through your home's electrical system, an improvement would be to ground directly to the soil.

### nonchalant — 2013-09-27T02:14:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-96245>

This is the one I bought. I was surprised at the low cost. It is made of brass, with a copper layer on the outside. But it is 8 feet long, and quite difficult to pound into the ground. You may be able to find a shorter one somewhere. I absolutely love mine, the grounding sheet is so good for my back. The fact that the rod goes so deep means it gets down to the moist soil (an issue here in Texas) for better conduction. You would also have to buy a grounding rod clamp to attach the wire.

I also tried grounding with a short metal tent stake, while on vacation. I wasn't impressed, but it is much easier to just push a short spike into the dirt.

[http://www.homedepot.com/p/ERITECH-1-2-in-x-8-ft-Copper-Ground-Rod-611380UPC/202195737](http://www.homedepot.com/p/ERITECH-1-2-in-x-8-ft-Copper-Ground-Rod-611380UPC/202195737)

### suzgaray — 2013-09-30T04:37:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-96478>

Hi Caroline,

the saying "everything here is about context?" not sure what that means-grin. However, I am doing well this past weekend I have done the following:

1\. Drank 2-3L of Spring water
2\. Added tuna, shrimp, and scallops to my diet (at least each day I ate one of them!).
3\. Walked or hiked 5 plus miles each day
4\. Did my first ever CT in the swimming pool!! Had my partner watch me to make sure I didn't have a heart attack or pass out, but the water temp in the pool today was 60 degrees (ok, I know its not 55 degrees, but still was a challenge!!) I was able to stay in it for 20 minutes and afterwards the following had happened:
a. My skin felt great and soft!!!
b. my BG before was 131 dropped to 118!
c. All of my usual body pain either disappeared or I felt so "light" and calm
d. decreased hunger/cravings was noticeable later in the evening???

5\. I lost 5lbs over the weekend!
6\. Started to wear orange glasses earlier in the evenings when the sun went down instead of 2 hours before bed.

Hmmmm, so far good (though not perfect with sticking to Paleo 100% but not bad either). Tmw, Monday I will start doing CT 3x a week to see what noticeable differences it will make by the end of the week . It will start to get cold at night 50 degrees or so. I will try the evening time to do CT and see if it further aids with sleep?? I still taking 800mg Mag Malate during the day and Mag Threonate & Melatonin 10mg before bed. Wondering if I should switch since I heard that Mag Threonate is for brain functioning and Mag malate might be better choice for nighttime to aid with sleep??

Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated .

### nonchalant — 2013-09-30T04:41:12+0300

### caroline — 2013-09-30T07:10:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-96485>

I think you are doing just great! I am very proud of you. The pool sounds great and you will get used to it as the temp drops.

"everything in context". Is one
of JK's very favorite phrases. It doesn't matter what works for him or me or anyone else...yoy have to find your own n=1. So if you were to ask him "should I take more thyroid meds" how could he possibly answer you unless he was your Doc and could lay eyes on you and know all your labs?

Dr.K always tells us ....we need to have a partner Doc to get to optimal health.

Remember he is just the guy with the flashlight......he can't fix anyone....even his own family. He speaks about his wife on another thread about how he was 5 years into this before she jumped on board!

Please keep asking questions and especially if you don't quite understand what we say.

Congrats on the 5lbs ...and it seems C/T is already working its magic! If you ever feel like giving up always remember that you just have to start all over again.....Not worth it!! Always come here for support if needed.

If you have a choice buy other than tuna....but don't worry about it just yet...once again .you are doing so well - trust what Dr. K is saying ...But continue to question everything including him.

### caroline — 2013-10-02T01:44:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-96661>

Please don't be disheartened .... a lot of people here have made amazing progress - but it can take awhile. Remember - you didn't get broken overnite so give this process time to work ...it isn't a linear journey......

You are already seeing the positive benefits of C/T .......hang in there! It is daunting - but it is doable and we will support you.

Break it down a little....

How hard is a BAB? you get to eat bacon and eggs.... or a roast leg of lamb for breakfast and have a BPC

50 gr. of carbs or less .... just saute some greens or mushrooms in butter or CO

C/T - you said you enjoyed being in the pool ..that is really good for now and, actually it is great because the water temp will drop and you will become accustomed to it. Get your partner to put on some compression gear \[if they need it\] and get in with you.

I don't mean to minimise your feelings in any way - but if you want to get better you need to do better. This has been the case for all of us. We need to have absolute and 100% commitment on this journey .....we don't get a do over.

This doesn't mean that we don't slip up sometimes and lose focus ...of course we do ......but we all want a happy and healthier life and it takes our head in the right space to get there....and we have this amazing guy with his flashlight

You are a very intelligent woman and you deserve so much credit for all that you have achieved ....don't stop now..your future is only as bright as you want to make it. You are the limiting factor....

### caroline — 2013-10-11T05:24:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-97489>

Personally - I would cut way, way back on carbs. If I were to eat one tortilla - I would probably have 12! well - not any more because I wouldn't have even one. That is what is driving your cravings. Don't think of carbs in those terms. Only have some sauteed kale or mushrooms or a little bit of tomato or cucumber. Maybe reread the epi paleo Rx again ...has your partner read it? I know it is hard when there is someone else who isn't eating epi paleo. But - it would be good if she would do it to - she wants to live a long healthy life too - no?

your dinner should just be some seafood/steak and maybe a bit of salad - or even bacon and eggs ...forget the fancy stuff. I do way better if I eat very simply and I love it that way - don't always have to think about food.

I always have some raw meat dry curing in the fridge and bacon and pate that I make and cans of salmon in the pantry.
etc. - and I often throw a leg of lamb in the crockpot .....and don't forget about bone broth....

Then I buy fresh salmon and shrimp etc. and just mix and match.

You could make sushi using cauliflower rice.....did you tell us before that you have lots of paleo cookbooks or am I not remembering that right?

Did you see the recipe for the seafood stew in the recipe section?

Carbs on the average range from 30-50g
Sugars on the average range from 4-8g
Protiens on the average range from 36-49g

Protein-83-123g

### veelansu — 2013-10-20T07:41:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-98395>

Also, there should be no counting calories. Just count carbs and stay low. And maximize proteins and fats in the morning with the BAB. Dinner should be where your carbs are to confuse your brain and recreate the primal pathways. Once you are leptin sensitive, you change to max carbs in the morning. That's what I understood. If I'm wrong in any way, please someone jump in and correct me.

### caroline — 2013-09-27T04:23:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.8241/post-96261>

Coffee update........

I only had a headache briefly in the afternoon - drank some water
I ate more fat in the morning and that solved the problem of the insane hunger
The most important thing is - I am drinking a lot more water .....that is what I was hoping for!
BM's are a lot better.........I was hoping for that too.

But - my sleep was just awful lat nite. I spent way too much time on my computer and didn't wear my blueblockers. I really do know way better than that.

I am going to do better today....

How is everyone else doing.......Randy? Inger? Determined? What benefits are you noticing? if any....

I really do miss the BPC - I guess cause it is easier with my nespresso machine - but I will save a ton of money and spend it on R/O water from Italy instead...win/win

### caroline — 2013-09-27T04:35:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.8241/post-96262>

I forgot to say ....I feel calmer....I think I was getting somewhat hyper - not jittery tho. Now I feel laid back and in the zone.....whatever that means exactly ...

### caroline — 2013-09-27T11:48:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.8241/post-96270>

My Dear Inger ....do what you need to do .....Trust your instincts. I know how hard it is - I contemplate the same thing practically daily. On one hand - you have to set things straight ...but- you don't want to totally break someone's spirit. Your situation is a little different because she is a grown woman with children who will suffer at her hands. But - I don't think it is our place to destroy another person either....no matter how right or wrong it might be.

I talked for hours with a woman I had just met in Sydney last month. She was such a lovely young woman ...kind, articulate, bright and extremely caring. She was just about to be married and her Greek father hadn't spoken to her in over a year because she was marrying a man who isn't Greek ....and he won't attend the wedding or speak to her fiance. It was so heartbreaking that this man would throw this beautiful daughter away and not want to see her again.
She told me, in my case, it would just destroy this person and I can not do that....no matter how much I want to some days. That doesn't totally feel right either .......I definitely think we should not allow another person to abuse us - I think maybe it needs to be done simply and kindly and fast and with love in our heart - not with venom we feel at times - no matter how right we feel we are. I guess it needs to be done for them - but is that our responsibility?
I am just going around in circles here - as I usually do.....

### caroline — 2013-09-27T12:37:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.8241/post-96281>

You know Inger - I often think of a young girl who used to work for us. She was such a good worker and smart and handled people with kindness and a generous spirit. She was very respectful to us and she was such a pleasure to have around. One day I was talking to her parents and telling them what a lovely daughter they had and they must be so proud. Her Dad told me thanks - she is lovely - now ....but she used to be a horror at home. He finally had to sit down with her and tell her it wasn't acceptable to treat her family like she was. Long storey short ...she just really hadn't realised what she was doing and as long as everyone kept taking it - she kept dishing it out. I have never forgotten that conversation ....mostly because this young woman was truly lovely. When she left to go to school - we got the most beautiful letter from her which I still have.

### caroline — 2013-09-27T12:40:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.8241/post-96282>

Inger - what you said is beautiful and exactly right I think....if you cannot do it with love and respect - then you shouldn't do it. I need to remember that...thankyou

### nonchalant — 2013-09-27T16:45:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.8241/post-96289>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=96281)
>
> He finally had to sit down with her and tell her it wasn't acceptable to treat her family like she was.

Wise words, said calmly.
I imagine those words were not heard at first, but after the daughter finished her tirade and went off to think, she could unemotionally remember his statement, and finally realize that they were the truth. I think that often, even though what you say is dismissed by the other party, those words can ring again later in their minds, because the truth is special, and part of our minds pay attention to it always.

### fitness@home — 2013-09-27T20:06:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.8241/post-96297>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=96289)
>
> I think that often, even though what you say is dismissed by the other party, those words can ring again later in their minds, because the truth is special, and part of our minds pay attention to it always.

Another truthism...tested and verified in my own household within the past 24 hours . Both parties have since taken in and processed the other's perspective.

### caroline — 2013-09-27T23:09:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.8241/post-96306>

This is a great conversation for a coffee thread! Dawn and Sally ....you are both wise and caring. It is all about respect for the other person - regardless of their perspective. I need to remember this. We need and want respect for ourself - we have to be able to give it as well.

### Determined — 2013-09-28T01:07:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.8241/post-96323>

was going well w the coffee 'fast', but had to break it this mrning as needed a little 'tlc', seems its all tied to my emotional state! Gee it was nice. So its one thing amongst many!

### caroline — 2013-09-28T01:26:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.8241/post-96329>

Yesterday went really well ... no headache.....more water.....energy level good. I think I slept well.....the one thing I am noticing is I don't seem to remember the nights. I don't think I was up last nite at all - but don't remember - that is so odd.
I must be sleeping very soundly I guess.

I am missing the act of having coffee tho......of all the acts to be missing - that shouldn't be one  Gotta get my priorities straight!

We shouldn't have decaf ....is that right? does anyone remember why?

### caroline — 2013-09-29T07:12:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.8241/post-96432>

Coffee update.......has everyone deserted me?

It is going really well and I seem to be sleeping better. I went to bed really early - 7:30 ... I am such a swinger ....
I was up three times but it was because I was thirsty. I did pee but that wasn't the primary reason I woke I don't think.

I woke this am at around 5:30 ..... That is a crap load of sleep! I am dreaming a lot.

I like how I feel without coffee - I have done this before but wasn't dialed in enough to appreciate the subtle differences....when you know better....everything gets way, way better.....

### Inger — 2013-09-29T21:53:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.8241/post-96449>

### caroline — 2013-10-01T23:56:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.8241/post-96648>

Still off coffee ......I seem to be a party of one?

I don't think this will be forever.....I really love coffee. I will just need to limit myself to one or two in the morning. I love BPC ...and BPT isn't cutting it - for me anyway.

# Thread: Hshi's and high RT3 - best way forward?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hshis-and-high-rt3-best-way-forward.8245/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Flora — 2013-09-24T18:00:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hshis-and-high-rt3-best-way-forward.8245/post-95951>

Have very recently found out I have Hashi's from thyroid lab results:
TSH-2.55
FT4-9.8
FT3-2.52
RT3-240
FT3/RT3 ratio- 10.5
TGAB-20.69
TPO- 17.97

Have also had thyroid ultrasound which didn't find anything unusual - no nodules or enlargement.

I've spent days reading everything I can find on this and StopTTM site to help me work out the best way forward.
My Dr says she doesn't treat unless ABs are above 50 but I have an appt. tomorrow to show her results of scan and T3/RT3 results (which I got myself) and I want to know whether to press for treatment.

My main symptoms are fatigue and lack of stamina plus aches and pains (mostly in legs) and inability to lose weight over the last year.
I have followed Jack for all that time and eaten epi-paleo. I'm now (for last 3 weeks) doing leptin rx and daily cold showers (will soon progress to bathtub) and re-started with blue blockers/ minimal lights after sunset and early bed/early rise, more oxytocin, wet grass grounding first thing in morning plus more when I can. Also have trimeter and have cut out excess EMFs where poss. in the house and also live way out in French countryside so virtually no external EMF pollution. Sleep has improved a bit with this (less waking, easier to go back to sleep, no bathroom breaks) but still never really feel refreshed in morning.

My other labs have all improved since last lot nearly a year ago, but only thyroid test then was for TSH (which has reduced from 3.35). But LDL (176) and Trigs (67) both reduced and HDL (83) up. HSCRP (0.9), DHEA (375), Vit D (61) and Ferritin (227) (all not previously tested) all seem pretty good.

However Pg /E2 ratio only appears to be 50 (if I've done the calc correctly) but I have used trans-dermal progesterone cream for around 10 years now! so would have thought it would be at a good level. Would the trans-dermal still show up correctly in a serum test? Or am I just not absorbing it?
Have also taken Mg malate for months but recently added D-ribose and DHEA (was 50, now 25mg after labs).

What confuses me is that Jack says AI almost always shows up with poor Vit D and trashed DHEA and mine aren't. So would I be right in thinking that I am mostly on the right track and what I've been doing for the last 12 months has actually improved the Hashi's? Has anyone with Hashi's actually managed to reduce ABs this way, with no drugs?

What I'm really wondering is how do I get that RT3 number down? i.e. do I need to press Dr tomorrow for either NDT or T3 only to address it, or do I wait until the next labs (6 months unless I pay for them myself) and see how they are then?
But I still have symptoms and I guess that's what's important - so do I go with meds to improve them or persist with what I'm doing?

I feel I should be able to work this out for myself but can't...so any input / help / discussion very gratefully received!

Many thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-24T18:18:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hshis-and-high-rt3-best-way-forward.8245/post-95954>

When D and DHEA are not trashed it means one of two things.......your taking exogenous supplements or your environment is a really bad field..........
