As to the field I'm in - well I'm literally surrounded by fields and forest with no large towns and no industry anywhere remotely near - no pylons - just a single power line that stops at our house entrance (doesn't even go over the house as we are the end of the line).
Yes we have wifi but my laptop has ethernet cx and we turn wifi off at night. Phone line is corded and I rarely use mobile.
I've wracked my brain to think of other sources (no microwave and have reduced vacuum cleaner use (such a sacrifice!), never use hairdrier now) but nothing else shows up on trimeter.
What could I be missing?
I did spend hours on the laptop with wifi (before I knew about EMFs) last winter reading all your blogs -could I still be dealing with that?

### Flora — 2013-09-26T16:35:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hshis-and-high-rt3-best-way-forward.8245/post-96203>

**Hashi's and high RT3 - best way forward?**

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=95954)
>
> When D and DHEA are not trashed it means one of two things.......your taking exogenous supplements or your environment is a really bad field..........

Many thanks for response Dr K, but am very confused as I thought a really bad field WOULD trash those levels.
I supplemented some D last winter - but not all summer - and only started DHEA recently, so am surprised it would get to optimal level so quickly - but great if that's the case!

My field is definitely not terrible - am in deepest rural France with no neighbours, no near power lines (just our own), no fake light at night and have checked inside EMF levels with trimeter to mitigate as much as poss.

So, if I have reasonable confidence in the above, and working through step by step, I'm thinking that it's likely adrenal fatigue causing the high RT3? (I know I need an ASI but can't find anywhere yet that does that in France)

So first step in following the Adrenal Rx is to restore Pg levels to increase BDNF and improve brain signalling.
My Pg /E2 ratio (I have calculated, so could be wrong) is 50 (E2 - 41pg/ml, Pg - 2,5 ng/ml (n=0.1-0.2 for menopause))- despite having supplemented trans dermal nat progest. cream for about 10 years. Does this mean I am not actually absorbing the cream / not taking enough / should try oral tablets instead? Can I do anything to change this?
I am doing all the other steps in that protocol, so do I just continue and hope that several more weeks will improve things?

Saw my Dr. yesterday and she'd never heard of RT3 and said all she could give me was some synthroid - but didn't want to (nor do I want to!) as she wouldn't normally treat until TSH reaches above 7 and my thyroid is 'burnt out'!!! AAAGH
However when I mentioned NDT (she hadn't heard of that either), she did say she could refer me to a naturopathic doc - so have an appt there in a couple of weeks.

I just don't want to do any further harm and get back to normal - it really scares me when I read all the horrible things that can happen from having Hash's - and although I'm taking responsibility for my own health I don't yet have the confidence that I'm interpreting the blogs correctly and doing it right.

However, I am loving my morning barefoot walks on wet grass in my PJs with the dog, followed by Bab, followed by long cold shower (which I actually look forward to now!). I yearn for the ocean though, (3.5 hour trip  ) Hoping to get there for a week before too long...

Can anyone who' s 'been there and done it' give me any help / encouragement / ideas.

Many thanks

### Grizz — 2013-10-02T01:17:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hshis-and-high-rt3-best-way-forward.8245/post-96657>

Flora,

You have a complicated explanation for a simple iodine deficiency. Iodine & Selenium are essential for the thyroid and it gets VERY sickly without it. Start with selenium to get your Hashi under control, then add the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol:
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)

Dr. Dach Explains

> As you know, the problem with Iodoral (iodine) relates to the patient with Hashimoto's thyroiditis with elevated TPO and/or thyroglobulin antibodies. These patients may go into Hashitoxicosis after supplementing with iodine, exhibiting hyperthyroid symptoms possibly requiring hospitalization for thyroid storm.
>
> We are finding Hashimoto's thyroiditis to be more common than originally thought, almost epidemic, and it seems to be increasing. On a routine basis, initial evaluation includes thyroid antibody levels. In addition, all patients routinely must have a serum selenium level drawn. I have found that in almost all patients with elevated antibodies, selenium supplementation will normalize and drive down antibody levels on serial lab studies.
>
> In order to prevent the thyroid storm and other adverse effects from iodine in Hashimoto's patients, as you know, these patients must be supplemented with selenium first. This is why we first routinely draw a serum selenium, and for those cases below 135 ng/ml, we give 200-400 meg of selenomethionune for three weeks before starting the iodine supplementation at 6.25 mg (half the 12.5 mg tab) every other day.
>
> Starting with a lower dose of iodine avoids the various adverse effects of skin itching and metallic taste and GI symptoms that can be reported at first. These are usually transient, and after a week or so, the dose can be safely increased to a full tablet daily.
>
> Occasionally, we see a typical Hashimoto's patient with an elevated TSH around 5 or 6, and also an upper range free T3 around 350 to 400. These patients respond to selenium supplementation well, and follow-up labs usually show free T3 coming down to the 280-300 level, which in retrospect indicates the patient initially exhibited a slight thyrotoxic effect of the Hashimoto's thyroiditis, which then cooled down after the selenium. At this point it is safe to start the iodoral. If a Hashi's patient with a slightly overfunctioning thyroid and coexisting low selenium level is then started on iodine without first optimizing the selenium level, this will aggravate the thyroiditis, and possibly throw the patient into thyroid storm.
>
> Leaving the area of Hashimoto's and going to the general patient (with normal antibody levels without Hashi's), another big issue with iodine supplementation is that the TSH may go up, which is then interpreted by the family physician as a sign of hypothyroidism. This interpretation is an error, as the elevated TSH is cosmetic and unrelated to underlying thyroid function in these cases.
>
> A good example of this is a 55-year-old woman who came to see me after taking an iodine supplement from a health food store, hoping to shrink her goiter. The high school kid at the health food store gave her the wrong dosage instructions, and she had been taking a huge overdose of iodine for months. She felt fine, and a routine thyroid lab panel at her primary care physician showed a TSH of 65! The doctor was very concerned and told her that she "blew out" her thyroid with the health food store iodine, that she was severely hypothyroid, and gave her a prescription for Synthroid for her "hypothyroid" state.
>
> Here was a lady sitting in front of me who felt fine and had no symptoms of hypothyroidism. I sent her to the lab for a repeat panel and her Free T3 was normal at 330, indicating that she had normal thyroid function and did not need Synthroid. I explained to the patient that the elevated TSH was an "artifact" of the iodine supplementation, and that nothing else needed to be done other than stop the iodine and retest the TSH every 6 weeks or so. Six weeks later her TSH had come down to 10 and six weeks after that it was down to 4. The lady said thank you and I never saw her again.
>
> All of this information is David Brownstein's Iodine book, which I credit for helping me immensely to figure out the day-to-day practicalities of how to actually do iodine supplementation in our practice. In order to safely use iodine, the biochemistry and physiology of the thyroid gland must be reviewed and understood by the practitioner. Much of this information is newly uncovered, and is beautifully presented in Dr Brownstein's Iodine book.
> [http://jeffreydach.com/2011/04/06/iodine-is-safe-and-effective-by-jeffrey-dach-md.aspx](http://jeffreydach.com/2011/04/06/iodine-is-safe-and-effective-by-jeffrey-dach-md.aspx)

Please see more on Hashi here:
[https://docs.google.com/document/d/...5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.8eltjvzb6ets](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_q9ixGwZFon9yidvmZ0IFbp3Vj5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.8eltjvzb6ets)

Also avoid drinking water with fluoride. The fluoride totally distorts your thyroid testing numbers.
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/fluoride-and-your-thyroid/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/fluoride-and-your-thyroid/)

Grizz

Between pregnenolone, progesterone and DHEAs (at good levels) you can expect good average cortisol.
Following dr Kruses protocols is Circadian Rhythm friendly.

### Grizz — 2013-10-02T14:11:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hshis-and-high-rt3-best-way-forward.8245/post-96692>

Flora,

Why is it that more women have serious thyroid problems than men? Recent studies indicate that 7 times more women suffer thyroid problems than men. Interesting? There is a reason.

This is because women require a LOT more iodine than men. The female organs are in competition for the meager supplies of iodine.
\* Breasts need iodine or they develop cysts - Fibrocystic Breast Disease
\* Ovaries need iodine or they develop cysts - PCOS
\* Uterus need iodine or it develops fibroids, endo

With the thyroid & female organs in competition for scarce supplies of iodine, one or more suffer disease. Usually more. Thyroid problems are usually followed by cysts and monthly cycle problems.

The solution is not drugs or surgery, the solution is iodine with the required co-supplements.
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)

To make matters even worse, we live in a sea of toxins: Fluoride, bromide & chlorine which are known as Goitrogens, or Halide Toxins:
[http://www.freegrab.net/Iodine update.htm#section18](http://www.freegrab.net/Iodine%20update.htm#section18)
Contained in food & water these nasty toxins displace what little iodine we have making the affected organs even sicker. The Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol detoxes goitrogens and supplies healing iodine:

**ALL WOMEN** should be taking a minimum of 12mg of iodine daily (Lugols or Iodoral) with selenium to prevent the above diseases ( or more \[50mg\] if you are eating bread & drinking fluoridated water)
Also read the book, "The Iodine Crises."
[http://lynnefarrow.net/reviews.html](http://lynnefarrow.net/reviews.html)

### Flora — 2013-10-02T15:34:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hshis-and-high-rt3-best-way-forward.8245/post-96695>

Many thanks for your input Grizz and JanSz.
I thought about taking Lugol's and have bought some 2% - but have been reluctant to start taking it because of some of the things I' read relating to Hashi's - but I now think I've read enough to feel ok about that.
However, I have been eating fish virtually every day (sometimes twice) for the last 12 months (and pretty often before that) including fresh oysters and mussels, prawns and scallops plus all different kinds of fresh fish from the Atlantic or Mediterranean and occasionally some tinned mackerel.
I also eat at least 2 Brazils most days for the selenium (but the fish is also full of it). There is no fluoride in my tap water (comes from an underground river in the limestone hills about 200 metres from my house and is minimally treated if necessary) and I only drink that or San Pellegrino.
Surely I wouldn't still be iodine defficient? JK says in the supplements blog that if you eat fish more than 2 x a week you likely don't need supplementation.
I am willing to try the Lugols but my main concern is not to damage the thyroid further.
I'm doing CT every day with Leptin rx and I think things are beginning to improve slowly, fingers crossed!
I am seeing a Naturopathic doctor later today so will see how I go there - maybe I can get iodine and selenium levels tested as well as Pregnelonone (as per JanSz suggestion). Think I will also ask for some oral Progesterone to take alongside the cream.

I really should start a journal but trying to limit my laptop time and there's so much to read (and re-read!) here...

Thanks

# Thread: The 10th.... YOUR STATE OF MIND (Part I)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-your-state-of-mind-part-i.8248/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barry — 2013-09-24T22:58:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-your-state-of-mind-part-i.8248/post-95970>

**The 10th.... YOUR STATE OF MIND.. Part I**

This is my attempt at connecting my dots. My dots are the previous 9 posts Iâ€™ve written about the most important things for your health and performance. I cover many of them again along with a few new ones, but the real message is in between the linesâ€¦.

â€œBefore I learned the artâ€¦..â€

Life is art. It consists of various different colours, consists of many different textures and many different forms. Arguably, and most importantly, its beauty and its worth are governed by your own individual perception.

We are all artists and we can choose whatever colours, textures and forms we like. More importantly, we can choose how our piece of art is perceived. However, there is a big problem in modern society and those that are trying to master their art. The problem is that people are not really choosing their own colours, they are not choosing their own textures and they are not choosing their own forms. These choices in todayâ€™s world are, for the majority, being made for you by others. These â€œothersâ€ are born out of research, literature, studies and reviewsâ€¦ interpretations of these, and the beliefs and dogmas that ensue. So with all of this freely available information, advice and recommendations, people have forgotten how to think and decide for themselves. They donâ€™t know how to decide or how to choose, the choices are many, which leads to a paradox of choice.

Furthermore, and this goes a bit deeper; but people seem to have lost the ability of self-perception. Living, health, performance and success are now all things that are dictated to you by society and not necessarily oneself. People are trying to succeed and achieve things that are determined by others and not themselves. It makes completely no sense and is probably one of the major causes of ill-health and unhappiness. Do not feel like you have to do what others or â€œdogmaâ€™sâ€ tell you to do. Do what makes you feel happy regardless. People want you to live the way they live and to stay that way. They donâ€™t like it when you do things differently and it works out. So create your own fishbowl and synthesize your own happiness. Perceive your own happiness, go on feel and do what you believe is right.

**Missing the Point**

With this optimized life that everybody is chasing, the bottom line is that you must get the point. The point is that health/fitness/performance is not a 1-dimensional being. Itâ€™s not just about nutrition, itâ€™s not just about exercise and itâ€™s not just about EMFs. The whole is greater than the sum of its parts. The physiological, physical, biological and even spiritual mechanisms that make us tick depend on an array of variables both controllable and for the large part, uncontrollable. This is all related to the dots and how they are connected. So a study on a food, medicine or type of exercise cannot be applicable to us all. It is simply impossible to accept the outcome of any study on a group of subjects, if the very dots that make them tick are not in place in the first instance.

We now have paleo this and paleo that. Grass-fed pasture raised meats, omega 3 eggs and kale smoothes. We have eccentric loading, HIIT and movnat. We have IF, Ketosis, PPP, Metcons and bulletproof x, y and z. But you know what, and this hurts me to say, but those things really mean jack shit (excuse the pun). Iâ€™ll try and put this into a bit more context. I have been a die hard â€œyou are what you eatâ€ for a long time. I make my living now working in the world of sport and performance and Iâ€™ve also been a competitive athlete and have lived a healthy lifestyle for most of my life. But the more and more I look at the woods, and focus on the big picture, and look beyond conventional wisdom, the more I realise that there are far superior things that govern our health and physical existence. These are the things which I have learnt here through Jacks blogs and others. These are the things which are the very essence of what makes us tick. The simple laws of nature, the process by which our bodies make energy, and the ways in which we process our thoughts. I am now so convinced that our health and fitness is governed by these factors that given the ultimatum of â€œPaleo foods and Runningâ€ or â€œNature and the Outdoorsâ€â€¦ I would choose the latter. Itâ€™s the reason Iâ€™ve stopped answering peoples questions about how many carbs I eat and how many miles I run. Not only are these things completely N=1 based, they are simply the bricks and mortar, not the foundations and framework.

What Iâ€™m trying to say here is that we have ironically covered ourselves in a cloud of magic bullets which are blinding our view of what really matters. This is why you have hard core low carb zealots that are still overweight. Itâ€™s the reason you still have marathon runners and triathletes that are still not fit. And itâ€™s the reason you still have â€œKrusersâ€ that still canâ€™t sleep, still have auto-immune diseases and still canâ€™t reach ketosis.

We need to take our heads out of the magic bullet clouds. We need to stop counting our carbs and stop timing our work-outs and stop looking at our keto strips. These are the very things that actually have the least influence on your health and fitness and are more likely to be causing you stress. The real bullets, that actually can work, are sitting right under our noses. Jack is explaining these things in detail. Your health and optimized life is governed by how your genes express themselves, and this is controlled by your natural environment and your state of mind.

**It ainâ€™t Rocket Science**

Everything is simple when you know how. The knowing part though is the challenge. Do you need a Masters or PhD to understand Jacks blogs and his concepts? If you want to understand the biochemistry and quantum mechanics and how these are tied together, then you probably do or at least be someone with an extremely high intellect, its not music for the masses. I have a degree and masters in this area, work the field of health and performance and spend a lot of my spare time learning more. However, I have a hard time trying to understand it all and I even read the â€œFor non-Geeksâ€ part of the blogs. However, and this is one of the most important points I want to get across, the application is simple. Cytokines and hydrogen bonding might confuse people but walking on the grass barefoot is simple. Semi-conductors and Schumann resonance are complex topics but reading a book instead of staring at the computer screen is easy. Cytochromes and transcription factors are for geeks but eating real food in season can be done by anyone.

I could go on. The point is that the science of rocket technology is complex but a monkey can press the button. Iâ€™m not quite sure why people just donâ€™t get this. I think itâ€™s a major hindrance and it could be related to the Bruce Lee quote which started this post with â€œOnce I understood the art, a punch was just a punch, a kick just a kickâ€. As intelligent beings, we want to understand things first before we do them. In order to really feel something, to truly live and breathe it, it does help to have a deep understanding of whatever that something is. I might be answering my own question here. It may be the stumbling block for people that is preventing them from doing the simple things (which have complex science behind them). Not having a deep understanding of ketosis, photoelectric currents, inflammation, methlyation and so on might mean that they canâ€™t truly practice or embrace the simplicity of the application.

**Move slowly and you see more**

What ties in with this simplicity is the practice of moving slow both physically and mentally. Nothing happens overnight and there are no golden tickets to health. Yet people appear to expect superhuman transformations after a few ice baths, a few grams of D-Ribose and a few swings of the kettlebell in the fasted state. However, the adaptations necessary to change the pathways and mechanisms that make us tick require a huge amount of molecular and cellular changes. The machinery such as enzymes, receptors, carrier molecules, hormones and signalling molecules take time to build. Once theyâ€™re built, they need time to work and they need to learn how to work efficiently and effectively. Finally, they need infinite time so that they can all work together in harmony.

The essence is time. Take your time marching up that mountain. Iâ€™ve mentioned this before in a previous post; pave your own path to the top. The growth occurs on the side of the mountain, not at the top. So spend your time and effort on the climb. This gives time for your machinery to be built, it allows them time to clock up some mileage and it means that they will have more time to get to know each other. This is all about the dots and how they connect. Itâ€™s as cut and dry as I can put it. So donâ€™t rush about trying to go ketogenic or intermittent fast or do metcons and WODâ€™s. More importantly, donâ€™t even think too quickly about any of this. If you move more slowly up the mountain, you will observe more. The more you observe the more you absorb. And this leads to the irony of the whole story, by moving more slowly and thinking less, you start to understand more.

Beautiful Barry.

you say this in every post - your path is different from mine, from Jack's, but the destination is the same. How we get there is our own journey, no one else's.

### cinnamon — 2013-09-25T17:02:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-your-state-of-mind-part-i.8248/post-96073>

Bravo, Barry!

It is so very unfortunate that a glitch in the forum programming is making it impossible for many to read this. I sure hope it is fixed quickly.

I loved this. I struggle a lot with understanding the science. It does not stop me from applying the protocols; but I want to understand why it works and I want to be able to explain it to others. I strongly suspect that my brain is still quite a ways from optimal and I am holding out hope that if I just keep going with the protocols, my ability to see and understand will improve over time. I guess that is part of the climb you are talking about.

People around me are noticing that I am different (in a really good way!) and they are curious as to why. When I explain what I'm doing, they have a hard time believing it as it is so contrary to all that ingrained conventional wisdom (I say ingrained, but much of the so-called conventional wisdom is pretty self-contradictory and we've all laughed at studies which tell us one week that we shouldn't eat chocolate and then next that it's the magic thing that solves all ills). They seem to focus on "having to give up so much", and how hard it is to swim against the tide of what everybody else is doing. They don't want to believe that the disconnect of all our tech gadgets, heated/cooled/lighted homes and offices is actually causing us to evolve in a negative way.

The way I see it is that each of us has to take charge of our own journey, but I feel that part of mine is going to include helping others with theirs. I feel I have been helped on mine by others on this forum. I do see us as being "quantum entangled". I felt proof of that when the forum was off-line.

I suppose I will take my time and be the pioneer, focussing on me for now. When those around me see that it hasn't killed me (despite their concerns that I am surely destroying my health) perhaps they will feel that it is safe to try their own version of the path to optimal.

### fitness@home — 2013-09-25T17:08:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-10th-your-state-of-mind-part-i.8248/post-96075>

Great post from you Barry (now that I've also figured out how to bypass the error and can read it). You've nailed it from your perspective.

# Thread: Anyone else watch the show "Curiosity"? Specifically, Season 2 Episode 4?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-watch-the-show-curiosity-specifically-season-2-episode-4.8250/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2013-09-25T01:36:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-watch-the-show-curiosity-specifically-season-2-episode-4.8250/post-96003>

The show itself is pretty cool, see more background here if you're unfamiliar: [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curiosity\_(TV\_series)](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curiosity_\(TV_series\))

But the episode I am referring to is called "Brainwashed". The goal of the episode was to see if it would be possible to "program" someone to kill "Manchurian Candidate" style (i.e. so they completed the killing as if it were a program in their brain that they could not control nor remember afterwards). They had a hypnotist evaluate a whole bunch of people to narrow it down to those most susceptible to hyponosis.

The part I found extremely interesting was the final test they used on the last 4 subjects: using an ice bath. At this point, they had already weeded out all of the actors, and those with weaker susceptibility, and they were down to the folks who could easily be hyponotized. In order to determine that they were deeply compatible, they hyponotized them and told them they'd be getting into a warm jacuzzi.

The first three were unsuccessful, and weren't hyponotized deep enough for this to work... but the 4th guy sat in the ice water for 2 minutes with almost no change in vitals!!

Think about the potential there... if ice baths could be framed like this for everyone, then they could be used as regular therapy with little to no impact on the individual's conscious mind.

Or, this could just be something to keep in mind in your personal endeavors. Mind over matter my friends, our brains and bodies are capable of far more than we give them credit for.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-25T01:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-watch-the-show-curiosity-specifically-season-2-episode-4.8250/post-96005>

or maybe the the cold allows you to control the current generated in layer one of your cortex?

### nonchalant — 2013-09-25T05:17:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-watch-the-show-curiosity-specifically-season-2-episode-4.8250/post-96036>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=96005)
>
> or maybe the the cold allows you to control the current generated in layer one of your cortex?

Hmmm... maybe I should continue to reread Jack's blogs while sitting in ice water.

### Marie — 2013-09-25T09:09:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-watch-the-show-curiosity-specifically-season-2-episode-4.8250/post-96051>

> [sjoshua said:](/goto/post?id=96003)
>
> Mind over matter my friends, our brains and bodies are capable of far more than we give them credit for.

YES!

Just an FYI about hypnosis...

ALL hypnosis is SELF hypnosis which allows you to access the 90% of your brain that has no "rational" filter. However, it does have a moral filter, so you can never be hypnotized to commit crimes.

Marie
(Who went to hypnotherapy school to learn to deal with some serious physical pain issues. My docs can't believe how high my paint tolerance is...as I've learned how to selectively turn off specific nerves.)

### boyshanks — 2013-09-25T20:40:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/importance-of-not-exercising-during-leptin-reset.8260/post-96093>

Good Afternoon this is my first post and I am excited to find this forum.

I did search for an answer but did not find one.

I'm looking for someone to help me understand why it is preferable NOT to exercise during the Leptin Reset program. I've seen where it says if you must exercise, do so after 5. That statement confirms that NOT exercising is the best.

Thanks in advance for your insight!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-25T21:00:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/importance-of-not-exercising-during-leptin-reset.8260/post-96096>

If you have inflammation present......you want to avoid exercise until you mitigate that risk using CT and the Epi-paleo Rx eating by Leptin Rx rules.......once your HS cRP, DHEA and D levels stop falling and stabilize then exercise can begin. If you read EE 5 closely the answer is buried between the words.......any amount of inflammation decrease electron flow to the neocortex and therefore less O2 is delivered to neurons. The less O2 delivered cause free radical formation and makes you more susceptible to electro magnetic radiation above the Schumann resonance.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-25T21:01:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/importance-of-not-exercising-during-leptin-reset.8260/post-96097>

Exercise works best when inflammtion is not present. It has to do with how the blood vessels on the surface cortex is aligned with the current of flow.......

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-25T22:25:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/importance-of-not-exercising-during-leptin-reset.8260/post-96109>

I've seen more success w/o exercising than I did when I was exercising. And Now I understand WHY. EE5 is connecting things together... the most I do is walk with my kids, enjoy nature and play with them or do yard work. that's it.

### caroline — 2013-09-26T02:59:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/importance-of-not-exercising-during-leptin-reset.8260/post-96144>

Welcome to the forum ....I totally agree .... the only things I do are walking and a bit of swimming and some kettle bell swings now and I have never looked healthier or been in better shape.

# Thread: Forum Update - And ... I'd change your password
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forum-update-and-id-change-your-password.8267/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tellmisty — 2013-09-27T04:00:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forum-update-and-id-change-your-password.8267/post-96257>

Hi y'all.

So, forums are back up (I'm slow to the party) and I think we've had a pretty uneventful week. Looks like the team did a good job scrubbing down the database to close up any bad scripts or holes meant to exploit vBulletin, the forum software.

We do not know how deeply the hackers accessed the site, but we have reason to believe they were able to get \*some\* access to the site's database. The database is what holds all the text that appears on the site: posts, comments, forum threads, users ... and passwords.

I personally freaked a little when I started thinking about the implications of a bad guy getting my password--anywhere on the web. S/he could take that info along with my email address and attempt to access my accounts anywhere I've opened them online. I immediately installed 1Password on my computer and devices and started changing passwords everywhere. I'm eliminating any two or more of my accounts having the same password for my own protection.

At the very least, I'd recommend making sure your password on forums like this is different from any account that gives someone access to your credit card info (that's saved in your profile) or banking info.

Anyhow. I have no evidence that there is reason for alarm other than ... logically thinking about it, I did not want to leave myself exposed and I don't want any of you guys to be exposed either!

Misty

### caroline — 2013-09-27T04:06:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forum-update-and-id-change-your-password.8267/post-96260>

Thanks Misty .... I was really worried about that. So different passwords for everything makes a lot of sense....just in case

Hi guys,

Just wondering if anyone has had any experience with using a bed canopy to shield RFs/dirty electricity?

Because it is not a full Faraday cage I would imagine that the EMFs would not get trapped inside.
Ideally one would have to measure first, but that is hard to do without purchasing one (and they are not cheap)

Here is a video that demonstrates: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9dluKqQ-MeE](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9dluKqQ-MeE)

Although I turn off the fuses in all the bedrooms at night I'm still concerned. Unfortunately, I still live in a condo about 300m from cell phone tower.

Hi guys,

I have been making a website to try and help people learn information presented on this blog but in a different way.

The content I have is very bare bones at the moment but I figure quite a few of you have rewritten the information presented on this blog in you're own way. If so could you send my what you have written?

Also the if you want to add anything to the glossary send me that too.

Either PM me or post it in this thread (copy and paste).

This is the website currently: [http://seanbingley.comyr.com/HealthAssistant/](http://seanbingley.comyr.com/HealthAssistant/)

Thanks, Sean.

### caroline — 2013-09-27T23:33:49+0300

just my two cents......

### seanb4 — 2013-09-27T23:55:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/launched-my-website-to-help-understand-kruses-blogs-need-youre-help.8270/post-96314>

Yeah, you're right. Maybe I should take dr kruse's name off the page... you got any articles/stuff written by you that I could upload?

### caroline — 2013-09-28T00:51:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/launched-my-website-to-help-understand-kruses-blogs-need-youre-help.8270/post-96320>

I would be really worried to do that Sean.......I definitely don't want to put words in Dr. K's mouth ....and you have to remember one very important thing......CONTEXT IS EVERYTHING

I have seen it happen on the forum so many times ......someone will co opt advice meant for someone else and think it is the gold standard for them ....you know that as well as anyone

This is why Dr Kruse is very careful about answering questions.....plus - he can't practise medicine on the interwebs. As it is - he takes a lot of heat for shining his flashlight .......we certainly don't want him losing his license.

I got into an argument about a year ago with a woman who was furious with Dr. K that he wouldn't tell her exactly how to
use a drug. He did tell her lots actually - but she was so mad at all of us. Dr. K told her to download the drug info and take it to her Doc and then they should decide together if it was right for her ....she was so furious and could not understand why he wouldn't tell her how much to take and for how long.

But - I do think your idea has some merit ....I can see where you are coming from. I worry about new people coming here and they are so confused and they have already been to so many paleo sites .......
I don't understand tho - we have the easy start guides- those are great. We didn't have any of that when we first started.
People seem to be landing here and not reading anything on the home page first ....they just dive into the forum and they struggle.

### seanb4 — 2013-09-28T11:13:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/launched-my-website-to-help-understand-kruses-blogs-need-youre-help.8270/post-96365>

Thanks for the input guys, lots of good idea's and good points.

I have updated the site to not include any mention of Dr K's name and a small disclaimer.

It should be noted that this website is just really intended for use by people who are already on this forum and this, I think, assumes a sort of intelligent audience. It will hopefully come at the content from various users perspectives and this may help the information "click" for some people. The site will hopefully have stuff on it that Jack hasn't really covered as much, for example I'm interested in learning more on functional dyspepsia and other stomach related problems. These are the main reasons I made it along with thinking it would be cool to have a large glossary of terms that can be viewed simultaneously to articles to help understanding.

@Patty Cakes
While this would be a good idea for some people who are say just discovering the content, or are slowly making progress, or are just interesting in general, I am non of these, I would have nothing of interest to write. Just check out my journal if you want to be bored to death.

So does anyone have any articles they can give me to upload? Or even just glossary terms. Would be appreciated but it's cool if not.

Reverse water micele (time 50:00)
and
Hydroxyl free radicals

\--------

Looking at my notes I have found
Hydroxyl free radicals correlates heavily with melatonin. Use melatonin to reduce them.

The Dangerous Hydroxyl Radical
[http://www.lef.org/magazine/mag95/95dec2b.htm](http://www.lef.org/magazine/mag95/95dec2b.htm)

\========================================================
[http://www.jackkruse.com/the-epcotx-rx/](http://www.jackkruse.com/the-epcotx-rx/)

> 4\. O = Onions/Garlic/Oxytocin The onions and garlic have huge local effects on endogenous gut health that we cover in detail in the webinar. **Oxytocin is another brain hormone that is a major sump for excess electrons that come from epigenetic damage. It is the third most important intracranial antioxidant. Melatonin and DHEA are the others.** Oxytocin has been epigenetically changed more than any other hormone in the mammalian clade but it can be used to help heal a bad gut if one understand how it directly impacts the cortisol/DHEA/melatonin cycles. Oxytocin is released during many different behaviors and activities but it plays a massive role in organizing the brains response in control of the chemical clocks of all the different organs in the gut. DHEA is another major protector of gut health and we have done a separate blog on its gut effects in Hormone #1: DHEA. Consider re-reading that one at some point.

\=========================================================

reverse micelles

[http://www.jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-4-light-water-magnetism/](http://www.jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-4-light-water-magnetism/)

> Water has amazing affects within cells. It is here where water is split by the photons and electrons from the sun to make more energy for life to use. How does this happen? When proteins are synthesized they unfold and take on their tertiary and quarternary structure because of ATP to expose water binding spots on their surface. This acts to make super conductive water cables surrounding proteins exposed to the photons and electrons liberated from this water shell around proteins called reverse micelles.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Protein\_tertiary\_structure](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Protein_tertiary_structure)
Protein tertiary structure

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quaternary\_structure#Quaternary\_structure](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quaternary_structure#Quaternary_structure)
Quaternary structure

[http://www.stanford.edu/group/fayer/articles/384-392/386.pdf](http://www.stanford.edu/group/fayer/articles/384-392/386.pdf)

Water dynamics in large and small reverse micelles:
From two ensembles to collective behavior.

> In large reverse micelles, the dynamics of water are separable
> into two ensembles: slow interfacial water and bulklike core water. As the reverse micelle size
> decreases, the slowing effect of the interface and the collective nature of water reorientation begin
> to slow the dynamics of the core water molecules. In the smallest reverse micelles, these effects
> dominate and all water molecules have the same long time reorientational dynamics. To understand
> and characterize the transition in the water dynamics from two ensembles to collective reorientation,
> polarization and frequency selective infrared pump-probe experiments are conducted on the
> complete range of reverse micelle sizes from a diameter of 1.6–20 nm. The crossover between two
> ensemble and collective reorientation occurs near a reverse micelle diameter of 4 nm. Below this
> size, the small number of confined water molecules and structural changes in the reverse micelle
> interface leads to homogeneous long time reorientation.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-09-28T19:14:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apple-and-jobs-fate-the-same.8276/post-96403>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=96401)
>
> [http://cellphones.procon.org/view.resource.php?resourceID=003054](http://cellphones.procon.org/view.resource.php?resourceID=003054)

Great resource .... thanks.

What is really interesting is that the SAR ratings of the FCC include this paragraph:

Power output listed is EIRP for Part 24. SAR compliance for body-worn operation is based on a separation distance of 2.0 cm between the unit and the body of the user. End-users must be informed of the body-worn operating requirements for satisfying RF exposure compliance. Belt clips or holsters not listed in this filing may not contain metallic components. The highest reported SAR values are: Head: 1.21 W/kg; Body-worn: 0.43 W/kg;

2.0cm is about 3/4 of an inch. No telling how much more radiation the user's body receives when pressing a cell phone directly against the ear.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
On a related note, I am reading the Robert C Kane book, Telephone Russian Roulette. Below is an interesting quote from the book:

"The researchers estimate that 72 percent of the radiated energy (from the cell phone) was absorbed by and "burnt off in the brain." Their choice of the phrase "burnt off in the brain" is very appropriate, as that is exactly what occurs. The radiofrequency energy is converted to heat, and the resulting heat, when sufficient, cooks the brain cells."

>
> ~ thanks for that quote ATL paleo. I wonder how much damage this does to all the men (I seem to be seeing all the men do it, at the store, in doctor's offices, at the movies, in airplanes, etc)...who are carrying their cell phones right on their hips or in their pant pockets. Though the other day I saw a young girl having an iPhone in her seat pocket on her butt. I'm like - whoa! Do all these people know they are FRYINg their testicles and other reproductive organs???? Ugh. It is killing me to see this......ugh.

This is why I stopped carrying my phone in my pocket a couple years ago. I also try to leave it in airplane mode more often than not.

I've also seen a lot of female friends trend towards carrying their phones in their bra... Another reason why breast cancer is on the rise, as if we needed more.

>
> I am not totally new to the forum, as this is my second post. I need to look around and find a place to post. Anyway, I thought my bones were strengthening but, all the time the cheats and sneaks and, face it, meals of rice, crackers and vegan treats, were weakening my bones. I knew better! With what I thought was my second lumbar spinal compression fracture- incredibly painful- two more, albeit older, fractured vertebrae were found. The extra two were painless and so I was unaware. I did not realize the fractures could be pain free! This is waking me up enough to seek help. I am beginning to follow Jack's Leptin Reset Easy Start Guide with one hitch. Every time I eat over four ounces of protein in a day, my face turns bright red. Problem. Sometimes even eating a dressingless salad in the afternoon will start the red face, even with only the four ounces of chicken, earlier. I will stay at four ounces a day for a week and begin to increase slowly, an ounce a day for a week and go from there. As osteoporosis is an autoimmune disease I will be strict. The red I think comes from Rosea, another autoimmune disease, so maybe meat is a trigger? Seems like a tough problem but I am hoping to work through it okay. Any thoughts or suggestions? Thanks.

Hi Kate ...and welcome! Have you tried adding more fat to your BAB?

Please start a journal in the journal section ...click on the forum and then scroll down until you see Journals.

Keeping a journal ups the commitment level and we can all lend a hand and you can help others too.

Have you watched Dr. K's podcasts? you get loads of info easy and fast. Do you have Jack's book - The epi paleo RX? - it is a great overview of all of this and a great section on labs.

If you can manage it - become a gold or silver member......There is a mind blowing new webinar now and there will be a live Q&A Sunday nite - where you can ask questions.

All of this is an investment in your health and future .......this is an awesome journey to optimal ...glad you are coming along for the ride

### QuantumRadical — 2013-09-30T18:16:38+0300

I do this regularly. At first it was a shocker, but now I can hold my breath with my head completely submerged for a while--at least a minute. I plan on moving up to using my snorkel this winter to extend the time. The experience is strikingly refreshing. I usually do it at the end of a good CT session in the tub. All in all, worth a try but it does take some adaptation.

Q'Rad Randy

### caroline — 2013-10-01T23:05:32+0300

I was thinking that too ......altho my water isn't as cold as yours .......but it sure hurts my head.......
How long do you spend in a cold shower Cinnamon?

### cinnamon — 2013-10-01T23:18:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tried-this-for-the-first-time.8290/post-96639>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=96636)
>
> How long do you spend in a cold shower Cinnamon?

I always start with warm water and clean my body and hair first. I like to drink a 500 ml bottle of icy cold spring water while I do the final rinse. It feels great to drink the icy water while warm water is cascading down around me. Then I start turning the temperature down. It takes me 3 or 4 minutes to get the temperature down so it is just the cold water. I find this is not very difficult (though it was much harder when I first started) provided I do the top of my head and my face first, each time I lower the temperature. Once it's really cold, I usually stay in for another 10 to 20 minutes. I like to stay in until my skin starts to turn pink - if there is no one else waiting to get into the bathroom.

# Thread: Could you spend an entire year disconnected on an Alaskan island?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-you-spend-an-entire-year-disconnected-on-an-alaskan-island.8291/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2013-09-28T19:58:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-you-spend-an-entire-year-disconnected-on-an-alaskan-island.8291/post-96406>

One of my classmates did

and here's his fb page: [https://www.facebook.com/AlaskanPioneer](https://www.facebook.com/AlaskanPioneer)
here's his last video blog [https://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player\_embedded&v=NJQwqoUPiVk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player_embedded&v=NJQwqoUPiVk)

I'll try to scan in the article from my alumni newsletter... and post her in a pdf

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-28T20:17:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-you-spend-an-entire-year-disconnected-on-an-alaskan-island.8291/post-96407>

a passage from the article in my alumni newsletter

" I keep running into the passage of time," Baird says. "On the island I would sometimes get word of certain weekly events taking place in the real world and marvel how quickly a full week had passed. It seemed more like a day and a half. The reason is that in my life I had very little going on. One day was like the last so my mind blurred them and compressed them cognitively, much like zipping a data file. After returning to society, I have busied myself with getting tags on my truck, buying clothes and food, doing laundry (with soap!), seeing people... I remember one Monday where at the end of the day I was thinking the next day was Thursday because clearly I'd done three days' work. But obviously that wasn't the case."

### Lava — 2013-09-28T22:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-you-spend-an-entire-year-disconnected-on-an-alaskan-island.8291/post-96408>

Awesome.....I could totally do it...

Thanks for sharing....

### fitness@home — 2013-09-28T22:15:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-you-spend-an-entire-year-disconnected-on-an-alaskan-island.8291/post-96409>

Sounds like heaven to someone who is in the rat race, daily grind, hamster cage, or whatever other metaphor you can come up with. Would love to have time slow down for a while

### nicld — 2013-09-29T01:55:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-you-spend-an-entire-year-disconnected-on-an-alaskan-island.8291/post-96421>

A month would be great especially in the spring or early summer.

### caroline — 2013-09-29T01:57:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-you-spend-an-entire-year-disconnected-on-an-alaskan-island.8291/post-96422>

Is that why I often don't seem to know what day it is? ahhhhh...the life of a beach bum....when your days don't have too much structure ......time slows down......

# Thread: Cpc #3
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cpc-3.8295/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-09-29T07:29:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cpc-3.8295/post-96433>

I just ran across this blog when I was lookin for something else. The first couple of paragraphs are so important ....at least they really hit home for me.....

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-29T16:56:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cpc-3.8295/post-96441>

You must become aware of the high cost of low living by cheap thinking. In biology, we often get what we deserve when we forget this. Today’s CPC will illustrate that point for you. Wasting your time doing things that do not get you to Optimal is losing opportunity to improve yourself. Today, and every day from here, become very aware of time. Be wise in the use of time. The question for living an Optimal life is never how much time do I really have, it is in how you use the time you have. And when you realize you can begin to reverse the errors you made in that time.

There is a great lesson here for those willing to think. I hope the sharpness of this message strikes you in the correct fashion. When you think average, you get average. Reject mediocre… demand optimal from me, your loved ones, and most importantly yourself. When you get this lesson, you will understand why there is much harm in wasting your most valuable asset: Time. The more sand that has escaped from the hourglass of your life, the clearer we should see through it. If you continue to think the way you do your life’s hour glass will remain opaque. Think, adapt, learn, become what you can only envision now through the actions those thoughts bring to you.

### caroline — 2013-09-29T23:32:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cpc-3.8295/post-96455>

This gives me goosebumps every time I read it.........

### jmayer54 — 2013-09-30T14:44:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tub-too-small.8303/post-96494>

Okay took my first cold bath yesterday just cold tap water too start. My question I cannot submerge my while body so what is most important to soak legs, chest back stomach or should zi lay on my side please let me know, and how often should I be soaking keep in mind I only have about 20-30 minutes to soak is this long enough?

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-30T15:05:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tub-too-small.8303/post-96495>

any coverage is good, for any temperature.

I had a similar problem so I went out Tractor Supply Co and bought a 100gal stock tank and use that. hopped in this am for about 20mins - I would have loved to go longer - but work, and school got in the way....

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-30T16:01:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tub-too-small.8303/post-96498>

Gretchen Tractor supply tub is a great option. I have a buddy who lives in NYC and put one of these on his roof and he uses it everyday because he does not have a tub in his 825 sq ft apt in the sky

### nonchalant — 2013-09-30T16:43:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tub-too-small.8303/post-96503>

Jmayer, you might alternate positions. 10 minutes legs, 10 minutes back, 10 minutes front. I believe that it is more important to do the back and front, if you don't have any major problems with your legs. For me personally, if I only had 20 minutes, I'd spend them all on my back.

### MeghanK — 2013-10-01T04:56:09+0300

# Thread: Sudden dry scalp
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sudden-dry-scalp.8306/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### K123 — 2013-09-30T18:13:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sudden-dry-scalp.8306/post-96511>

Hi! I have been trying to do the 90 day reset a couple of times but always drop off the wagon when friends are visiting from abroad.
I have now been sticking to it for the last three weeks now and I see some positive changes...less fatigue in the morning, plantar fasciitis has improved a lot, I don't feel so cold or hungry like I used to on carb fuel. I was also doing the iodine therapy (iodoral+ selenium+ vit c, ATP co-factors, pink salt etc) for a while there but then I have run out of iodoral for a while and I am still waiting for a misplaced package.
I usually used to have a greasy scalp with dandruff that wasn't so visible if I washed my hair a couple of times a week. But all of a sudden in the last month or so my scalp has become really flaky and dry. It is really embarasing to see these huge dandruff flakes. The little bumpy pains I used to have while combing my hair seems to have almost disappeared but I am not able to figure out what is causing this sudden dry flaky scalp when I am eating epi-paleo - fish, prawns, mussels, beef, chicken (have to for hubby eats only beef n chicken), duck and some veggies.
I have to accept I have gone away from being 100% complaint because I do eat cheese in UK because it is easy food for when I am not too hungry. Also ate some fruits every now and then. But none of it explains the dandruff yet!
Any insights would be highly appreciated. Pllleeeeeeeeeeeease help!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-30T19:11:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sudden-dry-scalp.8306/post-96519>

Dandruff is a sign of a bad vitamin D and A cycle.......So it is likely a circadian sign. The vitamin D blog and the pseudotumor blog may help you.

### cantweight — 2013-09-30T19:43:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sudden-dry-scalp.8306/post-96526>

> [K123 said:](/goto/post?id=96511)
>
> Hi! I have been trying to do the 90 day reset a couple of times but always drop off the wagon when friends are visiting from abroad.
> I have now been sticking to it for the last three weeks now and I see some positive changes...less fatigue in the morning, plantar fasciitis has improved a lot, I don't feel so cold or hungry like I used to on carb fuel. I was also doing the iodine therapy (iodoral+ selenium+ vit c, ATP co-factors, pink salt etc) for a while there but then I have run out of iodoral for a while and I am still waiting for a misplaced package.
> I usually used to have a greasy scalp with dandruff that wasn't so visible if I washed my hair a couple of times a week. But all of a sudden in the last month or so my scalp has become really flaky and dry. It is really embarasing to see these huge dandruff flakes. The little bumpy pains I used to have while combing my hair seems to have almost disappeared but I am not able to figure out what is causing this sudden dry flaky scalp when I am eating epi-paleo - fish, prawns, mussels, beef, chicken (have to for hubby eats only beef n chicken), duck and some veggies.
> I have to accept I have gone away from being 100% complaint because I do eat cheese in UK because it is easy food for when I am not too hungry. Also ate some fruits every now and then. But none of it explains the dandruff yet!
> Any insights would be highly appreciated. Pllleeeeeeeeeeeease help!

I have the same issue at almost every season change. Summer to fall being the worst. In a short period of time I go from hydrated dewy skin and locks to some of the driest most uncomfortable skin with hair like straw. It is so bad, cracked heels, dry scalp, even my face, lips and eyelids become ridiculously dry. No amount of coconut oil or upping my water intake helps.

I'll be re-reading the blogs Jack mentioned. Makes sense because I always experience sleep disruption at these times as well.

### Arborescence — 2015-07-17T19:28:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sudden-dry-scalp.8306/post-172189>

I get a lot of dandruff when I eat carbs, I wonder if this is an ubiquination issue?
I also get diarhea with fruits while on ketogenic I am at 3 on the Bristol stool chart.

This is hard because Jack says that Ketogenic diet is not good during summer but seems that I don't tolerate fruits.

Maybe it can be a sign of candida dysbiosis or gut hyperpermeability?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-18T02:11:41+0300

Hi everyone! I just wanted to introduce myself and say hello. I started today with my 50gm protein breakfast and I am patiently (not really) waiting on my labwork that was drawn on Friday. My goal is restoration of health and ultimately that being expressed as an improved body weight. I've been stopping and starting Paleo over the last few months because I would not see any results. I started reading JK's work and it really hit home with me. I'm excited to get started and I'm "all in"!

# Thread: Veelansu's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 77
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### veelansu — 2013-09-30T19:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-96522>

Hello everyone. Although I've started this journey in August, I want to start tracking my progress, get good comments to anything I may be doing wrong and keep myself honest. The impetus to begin is due to the fact that I am away from home for the next two weeks, I have found that when I'm not near my own kitchen, it's really difficult to stay on track. Sometimes because of all the temptations, or limited items to eat. The only good thing is that my travels have brought me to the gulf coast. I lived in Mobile, Al from 2006-2010 and fell in love with this area. I was forced to move to San Diego ( which some people think is an upgrade, but I personally do not feel at home there) Mobile is home for me. I made great friends here. I would give anything to come back. The consolation is that I make a decent living and can travel here when I get time off. This time around, I'm traveling to a conference in New Orleans so I drove the hour or so to Mobile to spend a few days with my friends. I've commandeered my friends kitchen and have been really good at staying on track. I filled her fridge with grass fed beef, wild salmon, pastured eggs and butter, lots of green veggies, and r/o bottled water. I believe the tap water here does not have fluoride, but better safe than sorry. I'm afraid that the 5 days at the conference will be a lot harder to work with. Living in a hotel usually stinks. I have to figure out how to eat within 30 min of waking. Not sure yet how to do that. I don't even know if they will have a mini fridge or a microwave. Need to research that. If they do, it will be easier, but if they don't, it may take longer for me to eat. Also, all the meals are catered. I tried to get them to accommodate my dietary requirements but I doubt that I can get wild seafood nor grass fed animal meat. Restaurants in NOLA are notorious for being non-paleo to be sure. And the French quarter at night, where I am sure to end as its hard to pass up, may throw me off course. Sigh. We shall see. I know my health is more important but I cannot stop living life and become a hermit either. Any suggestions for surviving this would be very appreciated if anyone has had similar experiences.

I have not started CT as I cannot handle the cold. I hate being cold. I feel miserable. This comes from living in NYC for the first 30 years of my life. I remember walking to school in the snow. Practically having frost bitten feet, hands and nose. My family was humble and my shoes were never waterproof. So I'm really resisting dunking myself in freezing water. I may one day start as it makes a lot of sense to do it, but again, I live my life in ways that make me happy. Being healthy and looking good make me very happy. But being cold reduces my happiness so I have to find that tipping point where being unhappy for a few hours a week in cold water, will outweigh not doing it. I'm not there yet.

Well enough for this first post.

Breakfast:
3 pastured eggs
1 oz mozzarella cheese
1/4 sweet potato
1/2 zucchini
1 uncured grass fed hot dog (no nitrates)
12 oz coffee with 2 tbs pastured butter and 2 tbs coconut oil

Plan for dinner
Salad with lettuce, fennel, avocado olive oil and apple cider vinegar
Grass fed hamburger patty

Code:

```
[B]Supplements[/B]
                                [U]Morning[/U]                                    [U]Evening[/U]
Omega 3 fish oil                 1400mg.                                1400 mg
Niacin                              500mg                                   500mg
Vitamin D3                      10000 ui
Ubiquinol+pqq.                 100+10 mg.                            100+10 mg
Schizandra                        580 mg.                              580 mg
Milk thistle                       350 mg.                              350 mg
7-Keto DHEA.                      25 mg.                                25 mg
Evening primrose oil.              1300 mg.                            1300mg
Chlorella.                      1000 mg.                            1000mg
Biotin.                        10000 mcg
K1.                            500 mcg
K4.                            500 mcg
K7.                             50 mcg

[B]Meds[/B]
Armour thyroid                    2grs                                  4grs
Progesterone.                                                              300 mg
Biest. 9mg/g                     3/4 g.                                    3/4 g
Testosterone 3.2mg/g.       3/4 g.                                    3/4 g
```

Happy Monday everyone.

### nicld — 2013-10-01T04:10:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-96571>

Welcome Vee,

I am with you, traveling can be such a pain but it sounds you are doing the best you can.

I too hated being cold and seemed to be cold all the time. Start off CT slowly, it does work. What I did was sit in my tub and let the cooler water slowly fill the tub. In the beginning it just worked better. Slowly the water got colder and colder and the time in it got longer and longer. Now I add frozen milk jugs to get the water as cold as possible but that has taken me almost 4 months and as weird as it sounds, after the initial shock I actually feel warm sitting in 50 degree water. Just take it slow and as warm as you need it and I agree, being cold is not good mentally. That's why I do my really long cold ones on the weekends since rewarming is easier then.

Good luck

### caroline — 2013-10-01T05:37:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-96580>

We used to call ....filling the tub slowly....the croak soak - after one of the forum members

The other possibility is to fill the tub right up ....jump in and utter a lot of profanity......

### veelansu — 2013-10-01T05:44:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-96581>

JanSz, I've tried taking less Armour but when I do, my hair starts to fall out and I get all sorts of hypo symptoms. I'm hoping that as I start to reset my body I won't need as much. I don't know what else to do as it works for me. And I know that I need to get my omega 3 from eating seafood, which I do, I love seafood, but it is one of Dr. Kruse's top ten supplements. I'm trying to follow the reset as best as I understand it. Should I really stop taking it?

Nicld, caroline, one day I am sure I will try it.

Dr. Kruse, thanks for the welcome.

\--------------------------

Journal continues

I'm from argentine descent. As some of you may know, this culture eats very late at night. Since I can remember. I never ate dinner before 8 or 9 pm. When I visit my family down south, we never eat till 11 pm most of the time. And so, I think that habits are hard to shake. I have been very strict about eating by 6 or 7 the latest. I always look out of the window to see that there is still some light when I sit down to eat. The issue is that I am still hungry late at night. I'm not starving, but I feel the need to feel the fullness in my belly that I always have when going to bed. It's very distracting. I've been drinking warm herbal tea to feel something warm. I've been skipping lunch because the BAB really does keep me full for most of the day. I'm thinking I may need to eat more at dinner.

### JanSz — 2013-10-01T06:03:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-96582>

> [veelansu said:](/goto/post?id=96581)
>
> JanSz, I've tried taking less Armour but when I do, my hair starts to fall out and I get all sorts of hypo symptoms. I'm hoping that as I start to reset my body I won't need as much. I don't know what else to do as it works for me. And I know that I need to get my omega 3 from eating seafood, which I do, I love seafood, but it is one of Dr. Kruse's top ten supplements. I'm trying to follow the reset as best as I understand it. Should I really stop taking it?

I am not saying that you should change you thyroid hormone dose, now.
I am saying that you likely may have low cortisol.
See where you are on (cortisol-7:30AM,serum) with good levels you may get satisfaction with less Armour.

Also good idea to do 4x saliva cortisol & DHEA.

\===========================================================

When you eat food, it comes balanced. It is not the case with supplements.
To figure them out you need good testing.
In this case good (detailed) fatty acid analysis.

At Mayo Clinic or LabCorp you can get:

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

Number of labs are selling tests that are evaluating O6/O3 ratios, those tests are misleading.
When you are full of EPA (and missing all DHA) you are still passing those test with flying colors.
And this is what you will get by eating fish or krill oils.

I have asked dr Kruse about this topic and he send me Patricia Kane PHD.

Read whole article or around the sentence I highlighted and make up your own mind.
[http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-4to1Oil.pdf](http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-4to1Oil.pdf)

> The elevated levels of EPA and DHA predomi-
> nantly come from suggestions by their regular physicians
> to try fish oil.

..

My RT3 levels were not right. Not sure of you have had a full thryoid panel done but mine was not noticed until I did. Just had blood drawn to see where they are at now that I have been on the new med and lower dose of Armour for a full month.
>
> Caroline - love the phrase croak soak, that's what I did in the beginning. Now it is fill it up, let the ice jugs cool the water a bit then get in, hold the breath and have the hubby laugh at me. He just will not take me up on my offer to try and make room for him. I have even offered to let him get in after me so the water is warmed up a bit.....hehehe.
>
> Vee, you'll get there and when you do, you will wonder why it took you so long. Also, this is not a quick process but you will get there, trust me

### veelansu — 2013-10-02T01:20:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-96658>

Okay, I will get the additional labs to see where I am at with the fatty acids and stop taking the fish oil. Thanks for that information. I am hoping I can lower my thyroid meds. I tried going T3 only, nicld, and it did not work for me. My RT3 did go down to nearly nothing but my body was still having hypo symptoms, even worse than before. I did it for 6 months and almost went bald. My apartment was full of hair, handfuls in the shower. It was a horrible experience. That's when I started taking the biotin and the evening primrose oil. I have a nice head of hair now since going back to Armour and adding those supplements. I still have tons of baby hairs but that's a good thing. .

I made it safe and sound to New Orleans. I stopped by Whole Foods in Metairie and picked up pastured butter for my morning coffee, some cooked shrimp for tonight. I will pick up a salad in this hotel and that should be good for today. My big concern is breakfast tomorrow. I will walk around tonight to see if I can find a place nearby I can eat at early tomorrow.

Then the real challenge starts with all the catered meals they have planned at this conference. Ugh! I'm hoping it won't be too hard to stay focused.

Wish me luck.

### caroline — 2013-10-02T01:54:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-96663>

Have you thought of getting some coconut manna and snacking on it while everyone else is gorging on their catered meals.

If you were Gluten intolerant they would need to provide you with an appropriate meal wouldn't they?

I always ask for gluten free on airplanes - it generally works out really well. Last time I flew to China I sat beside a lovely young man that couldn't believe the wonderful meals I was getting. He was very indignant with the flight staff that he couldn't get similar meats .....I wound up sharing mine with him

### veelansu — 2013-10-02T02:25:10+0300

The real issue is temptation!! I'm a sucker for desserts and my mom always says that I ate with my eyes not my stomach and she is right. If there is a dessert in front of me, it's so hard to walk away. I'm going to have to summon all the will power I can for the rest of this week.

### veelansu — 2013-10-03T02:03:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-96747>

Didn't sleep very well last night at all. I think it was just being in a strange environment or the excitement of the conference. Not sure. I didn't get to have the BAB today which sucked. I did drink coffee with butter and coconut oil, so that helped. I had catfish and salad for lunch and walked by the dessert table. Nothing looked good enough to cheat and I did keep telling myself sugar is poison. . That really helped. I am on my way to the next reception. I feel strong enough to be good. So after that event, day one of this trip will be done with and I will feel that much stronger for being able to stay away from all the bad food. It's not as hard as I thought it would be, I do miss my BAB though. I'm going to try harder tomorrow to get some food in me within 30 min of waking. We shall see.

### veelansu — 2013-10-03T04:26:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-96758>

### caroline — 2013-10-03T05:03:00+0300

### veelansu — 2013-10-06T06:16:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-96988>

Conference over. I got through it with flying colors.  today was kind of bad because I was checking out of the hotel so I didn't get to eat breakfast. I just has coffee with butter and coconut oil. Then I had to drive from New Orleans to Mobile, Al so I didn't get to eat till about 3. No food until 3, I can't believe that's a good thing. Thing is I wasn't really hungry until about 2. I ate shrimp and salad. Then at 7, I had red snapper with two mini sweet potatoes and about 1/2 oz of 70% organic dark chocolate. Lots and lots of water. I'm staying with a friend and she got upset at me for sautÃ©ing the fish, smelled up her house. Oh well, I have to eat healthy.  I'm here for another week, and I need the rest, being away from work, etc. but I miss my apartment and my own kitchen, and my own bed.

### caroline — 2013-10-06T06:49:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-96992>

Next time make seafood ceviche.....won't smell up her house and it is so delicious and way better for you.

I am so proud of you......you did great.....

After having a couple of BPC - I am not hungry for hours .....but I do eat a ton of fat!

### veelansu — 2013-10-07T07:32:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-97145>

That was such a great idea that I made ceviche with the rest of the snapper and I will eat it tomorrow for breakfast. Today was a very relaxing day. The rain from what was left of Karen came by. Nothing major. I was looking forward to sheets of rain, I love a good thunderstorm. Living in San Diego, the weather is very monotonous. Gorgeous, but it is monotonous. Coming to the south, I always hope for a good storm. It wasn't what I wanted, but it was still rain. I didn't get to walk barefoot outside because with all the lightning, I was afraid to become a human lightning rod. I stayed inside, and just listened to music and talked for hours with my friend who I am visiting. Breakfast was good. Buffalo sirloin steak and some zucchini, dinner was oysters and crab meat. I'm not drinking as much water as I should be. I realize that. I need to figure out a way to drink more. I never really get thirsty, this has always been my problem. Today, if I drank 3 -8 oz glasses I would be surprised. This is the one thing that I struggle with. I am looking forward to my week of rest. I have not done anything with EMF yet. I will when I get home, I ordered the grounding wire for my laptop at work, although with all the other ungrounded equipment around, it may not help much. At home, I am going to get a meter to measure hotspots (any suggestions? Anything I can pick up at lowes?) I will try to eliminate as much as I can, but living in an apartment building 3rd floor, there may not be too much I can do. Still not brave enough to CT. I have been lowering the temp in my shower, but it's not at all cold yet. I live right next to the Pacific Ocean so I may try to brave that at some point since it does get very cold, not sure how I'm going to do it yet. Baby steps.

### caroline — 2013-10-07T08:02:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-97146>

yep ...baby steps. I struggle with the water too - I don't seem to be thirsty and just forget about it.

There are threads talking about meters. It is quite complex.....

Have you disconnected your microwave?

I love ceviche and sushi. Did you know you can make sushi and instead of rice - make cauliflower rice?

### veelansu — 2013-10-10T17:44:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-97430>

The past few days have been interesting. I've been eating out more and drinking wine more. This is because I am around old friends and on vacation. but i still feel like i should be able to have more wil power to not do these thing i know are not good for me. I can't wait to get home. I still eat my BAB, and have gluten free offerings from the menu, mostly fish, but not all are wild caught. Last night I was tempted to eat half a pumpkin creme brÃ»lÃ©e. I kept saying "sugar is poison" with every mouthful, but that did not stop me from eating it. It made me sick. I guess I cannot tolerate that much sugar in one sitting. Up until yesterday I was still in light ketosis, definitely not today after that. I went to the gym a couple of days ago but just did weights. My arms are in pain. I haven't exercised in a while and I may have overdone it.

I am going to make ceviche again tonight and share it with my friend. I'm trying to convince her to follow this protocol. She is overweight and eating low cal. she's sees my physical transformation since she last saw me in July. We shall see. I'm going to keep trying till I leave. I've been food shopping for both of us, but she stills is horrified when she sees me drinking the BPC in the morning.  The nutritionist have brainwashed us too well. Glad we're getting our de-training here.

### veelansu — 2013-10-10T18:00:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-97433>

### caroline — 2013-10-10T23:49:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-97461>

Forgot to say .. It is hard on vacation and with friends - but you have to be strong and set an example ...easier said than done when you are just starting out yourself. About six months ago I spent a week with a friend and got mixed up with some port and gummie bears ....don't even ask!

I really can't tolerate any of that stuff anymore - makes me sick.....and now I enjoy being an outlier. ""

At my birthday party last month - we went to a restaurant. Everyone was eating fish and chips and pasta etc etc. I chose a salad with roast sweet potato and goat cheese and filberts. One friend said that is a shame that I am eating a salad on my birthday ....but I loved it and totally enjoyed it. Great birthday meal I reckon!

Now I truly hate kicking myself out of ketosis .... I hate how I feel .....I just love feeling healthy and lean and I think I would live on sashimi the rest of my life ...can't wipe the smile off my dial...
