### veelansu — 2013-10-16T06:47:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-97963>

Okay, it's been a few days since my last post. I kicked myself out of ketosis the last few days. I went to an old mentor's house for dinner. His wife cooked and I could not be rude and tell them I could not eat. She made a seafood casserole but it has breadcrumbs all in it. And then I went out with my friends and had a ridiculous amount of alcohol. I hardly ever drink, but when I do, I overdo it. I didn't get throw up drunk but I was can't drive drunk. Me t day, I started back on my path to optimal. It took me until today (3 days) to get into ketosis. The good thing was that I did nt gain any weight. I also did not lose any, but that's okay. I enjoyed my time in the south. Now I'm back in San Diego, looking out of my balcony at the bay. I will be down there tomorrow walking on the sand and seeing how cold the bay water is. I may decide to force myself into the water. It may not be that cold, the bay is never as cold as the pacific.

I'm still not eating lunch but today I was started to get hungry earlier so tomorrow I am going to take some food for lunch so I am not so ravenous when I get home. I had 3 eggs as some home made pork patties and some Brussels sprouts leftover from last night. Dinner was halibut with a small beet salad. I've been drinking more water.

I found out what the rash around my ankles is. While at my friends house. I decided to walk around her yard barefoot. I think I may have brushed against some poison ivy or something like that. She said there was some ion her yard. It's almost gone.

I'm going to try to inmerse myself in some sort of cold water. I'm starting to find some bravery.

### caroline — 2013-10-16T09:24:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-97976>

Have fun getting cold.........

### veelansu — 2013-10-17T07:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-98098>

After 2 months on the leptin reset I've decided to start working out again. I went inline skating with my daughter. I'm going to try running tomorrow. I haven't done that in at least 3 months. I know there's supposed to be some sort of change in sweating patterns but I'm not sure what I am supposed to see. More sweating? Less sweating? I was never much of a sweater, but when last time I skated I did start to profusely after I finished working out. It would have a delayed reaction. Today, I didn't sweat at all. I also did not exert myself as much as when last I worked out and it's cooler outside so I'm not sure if that had something to do with it. It was dark when I came in to eat dinner, about 7 pm, so no cold water for me. I ate and am now in bed about to sleep within the next 45 min hopefully. I did eat some lunch today since I'm still hungry during the day. I may need to add more fat to my BAB as I keep reading others are trying. My sweet tooth is still active. I think it's more a mental thing than an actual craving. I'm working on trying to eliminate it but I had to have some chocolate tonight after dinner. Dark 70%, less than an oz. I'm also trying to reduce my thyroid meds. Started today. Usually when I do, I stop losing weight but I think that the amount I am taking is elevating my SHBG and not leaving me enough usable free testosterone. It's all about finding the right balance. Now that I'm fixing my adrenals and leptin, I think I can start working on optimizing my thyroid which should help me overall. I'll let you all know how well this works out.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-98387>

I don't believe I am leptin e sensitive yet. I'm back on the straight and narrow, the 2 week vacation didn't help either. I'm not exercising hard. I. Just trying to do things I enjoy. I love inline skating and running. I get to be outside and spend time with my daughter who comes with me.

The extra fat in the BAB did wonders. It helped me stay satiated until dinner time. I've not lost any more weight, but at least I did not gain while on vacation. That's always a plus. .

I walked around the bay yesterday, barefoot, to ground some. I put my toes in the water to see how cold it was but the bay is still really warm so no natural CT. Although I am still chicken, I'm going to start like the process says, dunking my face. I may start tomorrow. Will let you all know how it goes.

### veelansu — 2013-10-26T07:58:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-99069>

I'm back in ketosis full force. I've started seeing some minor weight loss again. I started lowering my thyroid medication. Reduced it by one grain. I wanted to see if it would make me gain weight as it has done in the past. But given that I'm still losing, slower but still in the right direction, I will continue to lower it until I see my temps go down or my weight loss stopping.

I've been dipping in the bay. Not every day and not for very long, but it's feeling good. I've also been running with my daughter. We only do a couple of miles but I love the runner's high.

### caroline — 2013-10-26T09:02:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-99071>

You are such an athlete.....I am jealous....

### veelansu — 2013-10-27T02:40:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-99127>

Aww thanks. I have never been athletic. Always hated gym. I was always a bookworm. That helped me get far at work, but not with my health. I'm more committed to my health now, so I started doing things I find fun. I still cannot go to the gym or do HIIT. Ugh! Burpees! I would rather play football or kickball or run by the bay.

I rendered lard today since it's starting to get chilly and I'm supposed to eat seasonally, so I'm only using coconut oil for my coffee. I'm going to start cooking with lard. I cannot find duck nor duck fat here. San Diego has practically everything I need, except duck. Weird. I may have to find a local farm or something.

### veelansu — 2013-10-28T05:39:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-99212>

I missed the webinar today because I was cleaning out my apartment of all the clutter that I still have and by the time I remembered it was too late. I am organizing as much as I can and minimize what I own. I'm keeping only things that have meaning for me. Pictures of family and friends. Books I love, clothes I actually wear, etc.

I am also making bone broth, it's in my crock pot, I can't wait to taste it. I found a great butcher shop that sells all things grass fed. I bought enough meat for the month. Also went to the fish market, I had wahoo today for breakfast. I love trying new types of fish. It was delish.

I was thinking today about how I got to where I am today with my health, I was always so healthy, thin and vibrant. Had so much energy. And something clicked. Up until I was about 23 years old, I practically lived barefoot. I hated shoes and would only wear them if I absolutely had to. Even to his day, when I get home the first thing I do it take off my shoes. I lived in NY until I was 16 and every night I would have to wash my feet because they were black from walking barefoot in grass, on asphalt, wherever. I used to pull glass shards from my soles regularly (NY sidewalks always had broken bottles). Then I moved to Argentina and I would do he same thing. It was when I moves back to NY at the age of 23 that for whatever reason, maybe because I was officially an "adult" I just stopped. This is when I starts seeing my weight gain start. Not significantly, but slowly as the years past. I had stopped grounding! And EMF was a bigger and bigger issue. Cell phones became more prevalent, as well as cordless phones, etc. Such an epiphany. I need to start walking barefoot again and act like I did when I was 16. I used to walk under the rain. I still love to do that, but it never rains in San Diego. I miss thunderstorms,

We will have to rent that house and invite 20 George Clooney look a likes.....oh wait that isn't NOLA is it..... So - 1 week on the gulf eating Seafood 24/7and one week drinking Cajun martinis in NOLA.....not optimal I know - but if we drank martinis in an ice tub ...it would be fine.....

Will you eventually move back to the Gulf?

### veelansu — 2013-10-30T19:11:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-99435>

Maybe if I hit the lottery, since then money would not matter.  I will invite you all if that happens.

I have been really strict in my eating habits. I am in full ketosis, purple on my sticks. This is good, but for some reason, my weight loss has stopped. I also have had two really bad nights where I cannot sleep, I wake up regularly. I have been using the blue blocker glasses more, starting when the sun goes down, not sure if that is causing my body to react. It was horrible. I have not had that happen to me since I started this program. I stopped talking the melatonin, because I thought I was falling asleep at a decent hour and only walking up about 3 or 4 but then right back to sleep. These past few nights it my sleep was so light, I could hear my inner voice all night. I kept tossing and turning. Ugh! Last night was not that bad, I slept deep at least 6 hours, and stayed in be a couple hours more. I did not use the blue blockers except for two hours before sleeping vs. sundown. Could this have something to do with it? I am at a loss. I am going to use the glasses again tonight to test out the theory. Weird.

The good news is that I think my sweet cravings are going away. I no longer have a need to eat something sweet after dinner, and this morning, my coffee tasted TOO sweet. Progress! Now I have to figure out the sleep issue and then the stalled weight loss. I see a couple of pounds go down, then they come back up, then down, then back up. I think my body is fighting itself and I am not sure what to do to help it break that barrier.

Since my labs showed my SHBG number through the roof, I have added Stinging Nettle Root to my daily routine. I take 1gm a day, I am hoping this will increase my free T and estrogen as those number are low, allowing me to reduce my thyroid medication. I already eliminated one grain of Armour from my daily dose. I bought an RO filter for my countertop and plan on starting the Iodine heavy metal detox protocol. I am eating seafood at least 3 to 4 days a week, but I am going to try it anyway for a few weeks and see how it makes me feel. I am hoping it also helps me reduce my thyroid medication as well and help get my SHBG down.

The weird thing in my labs was that my Eos went from 1 to 11 in one month. I have no idea what this means. When I google it tells me that it's either an allergy or a parasitic infection. I have not done anything different than what I have been doing since August 15th when I started this journey, so not sure what happened between September and October. I am going to retest in November to see what it says. Weird yet again.

This morning I had some yummy Salmon with some grilled onions. I made lard this weekend so I cooked breakfast with it. Barely cooked as I love raw salmon, but I need some warm food for breakfast so I sauteed it. I didn't think it was going to be enough to keep me satisfied until dinner, but I feel pretty full 2 hours after eating. I am thinking of making some curry lamb for dinner. Not sure yet. My biggest hurdle is still drinking enough water. I just get so focused at work that I forget and my body just does not trigger the thirst sensation. I have added reminders on my calendar to help, but sometimes I ignore those too.

I still have not gotten into CT very much. It feels like torture. I need to figure out a way to make it part of my routine. I know it has great benefits, but I am still too chicken to really dive into it, no pun intended.

### sooperb — 2013-10-31T12:40:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-99509>

Why not have a bottle of water on your desk or close by your work station? That way you see it a lot and it's easy to pick up and take a mouthful, you don't have to drawn yourself in industrial quantities ☺️

### veelansu — 2013-11-01T05:22:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-99607>

Sooperb. I wish I could explain the laziness that's is me. . I actually have 2 bottles at work, I just have to walk almost a whole block to get to the RO water machine to fill them up. The close water is just tap. I have been making myself walk to the machine as soon as I walk into my office. It' was better today, I drank about 24 oz at work and about 16 oz at home so far. But it is something that I have to be conscious about. And believe it or not, if I don't do this, I could go all 9 hours at work without drinking a thing. That's how bad it is. If I am really focused, really in the zone! I forget about it and like I mentioned! I don't get the thirst signals.

### veelansu — 2013-11-04T22:49:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-99837>

I started the iodine protocol that Grizz outlines in the Iodine thread. I'm trying to cut down on my thyroid meds so I'm doing all the thing I think will help so I can get down to one or two grains of Armour a day. Or maybe even eliminate it al together. I have my thyroid, it just started getting sluggish when I lost my ovaries and my sex hormones went all out of whack. It was my lack of knowledge, and the medical communities lack of interest, that got me into this mess. Fighting it for the last 13 years has taught me a lot and I believe I am finally getting a handle on my own health, thanks to everything I have learned here. I have now cut down my meds from 6 grains to 4, 360 mg down to 240 mg. I have not experienced the usual weight gain that I have seen in the past when trying to reduce my dosage so that is a good thing. The only issue is that I have plateaued at 165 lbs. I was 182 when I started this journey on August 15th so I can't really complain. I am in ketosis so not sure what's happening in my body that won't let the fat go away. But I'm willing to wait it out and keep this protocol up. I have been better with my water, so that's a plus. I am going to the south for thanksgiving and hope to be out near a hunting camp. No cell towers, no internet. I can't wait.

### caroline — 2013-11-04T22:59:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-99840>

It sounds like you are doing well Vee. That weight loss is great....... how is your body comp? being really strict helps in my opinion. So many go off the rails every few weeks and then they have to start all over again ... not ideal!

### veelansu — 2013-11-04T23:51:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-99851>

I've lost a lot of fat, not sure what else you mean by body composition. I have gone down two pant sizes. I have before and after pictures in my underwear so I cannot share.  but the difference is pretty noticeable. I have really good will power. I have been strict since coming back from my conference where I tried to stay on track but did not succeed 100% of the time, I went to a birthday party on Saturday and I was able to say no to cake! A first for me. I did have a glass of wine though, so that's where I tend to do what I'm not supposed to. I do drink a glass of wine on rare occasion, maybe twice a month.

### caroline — 2013-11-05T00:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-99852>

Body comp .... I went down 2 sizes and my weight had actually gone up 2K.

When you knock out the cravings - everything is very doable! I think a glass of red wine every once in awhile is fine...

I think as long as we are hyper aware of what we put in our mouth .... it is our road to travel - and I, for one, want as much mileage as possible.....

I don't even think it is about will power anymore really ......I can now see the prize - and I want it! I can watch friends eat cake and I think really???? you are 50 lb. overweight and extremely unhealthy and on all kinds of drugs ....who are you kidding .... surely a piece of cake isn't worth that......

### veelansu — 2013-11-11T19:33:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-100435>

It's been a while since I wrote. A lot has been going on in my life. My mom fell and broke her hip last Monday. I had to takek a same day flight to Florida and spent the week with her at the hospital. She's still there. I had to fly back last night. She's supposed to transition to a rehabilitation facility as soon as they discharge her, but she's anemic so they are completing a transfusion to see if they can get her labs to look better. I am very distraught that I had to come back, but thankfully, I will be back there on the 20th, and I am planning to move her in with me the weekend after thanksgiving. I can't have her so far away. Even though my two sisters are very close to her, they live in the same city, they have not been the best support. Without me there she spends most of the time alone in the hospital. I just don't understand them. It was not that easy to stay on track in the hospital, but I think I did pretty good. I lost another pound but I think that was the stress more than the protocol. I also was not in any mood for sweets either. At one point I was trying to convince myself that it was okay to eat something bad, but I really didn't want it. I had no cravings at all. I am almost on the verge of being sensitive I think. I didn't sleep very well, as hospital recliners suck. But I passed out last night after getting home. It's not been a fun week. I cannot wait till I can get her here with me. It will be a lot less stressful, and I will be more in control of her health and her well being. I need to see if I can get her to eat like me, but she is 77 and so she may not want to as she is set in her ways. I just want to make the rest of her life happy and fulfilling. Can't wait for Thanksgiving to be here so I can be done there and pack all her stuff to bring with me.

I have been on the Stinging Nettle Root to deal with my extremely high SHBG, and I think I am starting to see results. I can tell that the Testosterone and Estrogen levels may be going up. I am going to get my labs for hormones done in about 2 more weeks to see if it's true or placebo. I also have started taking iodine, but very slowly, no news there. I should see more in a few weeks too. Labs should be able to tell me if I need to reduce my thyroid meds even more. Right now I am still at 4 grains. I want to go down to just 2 or maybe even none at all. Wouldn't that be great! to have a fully functional thyroid and endocrine system again. . The one pound loss was the high of my week. haha.

### nicld — 2013-11-11T20:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-100445>

Oh Vee, what a blow but sounds like you did the best you could.

Hopefully your mom starts to feel better but I cannot image the stress that you are going to be in when she moves in. I love my mother dearly but we could never live together (been there, done that, did not speak to each other for 9 months).

As for changing your mothers eating habits, if you are going to be the one shopping and preparing the foods then just say she has to eat what you prepare. You know, the way she did when you were growing up. My SIL needs to take better care of herself and when she comes to stay with us, she eats whatever I make. If she wants junk the DH and her have to go out and get it.

Hang in there.

### caroline — 2013-11-12T04:04:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-100475>

Vee - I am so sorry to hear about your Mom...... Thankfully she has you. I think, like Nicole said, you will be doing the shopping and preparing .....Maybe don't say much about it. - just do. There is a recipe for banana bread in the recipe section....would that keep her happy? And lots of coconut flour recipes for flat breads. Would that make it harder for you tho?

I am sending you a big hug .....xo

### sooperb — 2013-11-12T13:06:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-100494>

Aw Vee, how upsetting for you and mum. It's so difficult to care from a distance isn't it. My mum lived much nearer to my sister and although she kept an eye on her it was largely a mutually beneficial arrangement as my mum did a lot of babysitting for her. When she had her stroke it was my sister who took her to hospital, unfortunately she has little medical knowledge and whilst I doubt my mum could have been saved, there were things they should and could have done that weren't and my sister didn't ask because she has no interest in such things. Then when my daughter and I turned up and started asking, she got really shirty and thought we were interfering! Families eh!

I'm sure you'll cope but even so I hope it all goes well for you both  {{{{Vee + Mum}}}}

### veelansu — 2013-11-13T04:06:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-100592>

Thanks for all the kind words, you all.

Nicole, I've always thought that if my mom and I lived together we would kill each other but I've matured since then and she's gotten more dependent so I'm hoping it's not a bad idea. I'm actually looking forward to it.

caroline, I will look at the recipes. I think as long as she has some item that is breadlike she should be okay. Although she has some retirement money so I probably won't be able to keep her from buying and preparing her own food if she wants to. My mom is feisty. I take after her so it will be an interesting time during meals.

sooperb, I had some mega "conversations" with both my sisters and they've been a bit better. I'm sorry about your mom. It IS hard to manage something like that from a distance.

Mom was moved today to a rehabilitation facility so I feel a bit better since that means that her health issues have been managed and now they are going to work on her ability to walk and make her stronger.

The good news today. I was asked to go to Hawaii on a business trip. I'll be there just two days but still. I can use a little bit of paradise. It's Honolulu so I'm sure the emf will be huge but I'll still enjoy it.

I ate beef heart yesterday for the first time ever. I loved it. It was so good I'm going to buy some more. The meat after cleaning it, looked a lot like ahi tuna or very lean steak. Loved it!

Today I had fish and veggies. Nothing special but yummy.

### caroline — 2013-11-13T06:31:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-100601>

Vee - did you eat the heart raw? I love Hawaii - have a wonderful couple of days soaking up the rays!

So glad your Mom is doing better.... xo

### veelansu — 2013-11-15T05:15:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-100749>

No, I cooked it but very rare. I'm not brave enough to eat it raw yet.

Mom is doing great. I'm already making arrangements to bring her here.

For some reason the past two day, I've had crazy cravings for chocolate. Today I ate about 2/3s of a bar. 60% dark organic. But still the sugar in the bar had kicked me out of ketosis for sure. I haven't checked yet. I was doing so good not craving sweets and then bam! Not sure what happened. I'm not sure if I'm leptin sensitive or leptin resistant. I just cannot tell. I'm wondering because I'm not sure if I have to start the post leptin reset way of eating. Will it hurt if I keep eating as the leptin reset states if I've fixed my leptin pathways? I'm hoping not, since that's what I'm going to keep doing until early next year. The holidays will more than likely make it hard to stay on track. I won't be home for thanksgiving as I'm going to get mom. Xmas and New Years I will be, so it will make it easier.

Didn't eat dinner because that huge bar of chocolate at 2 pm took away my hunger completely. Weird.

My daughter is staying with me for a few weeks. I'm forcing her to eat my way. I'm hoping she can get used to it and keep it up on her own. She seems to be fine with it since she doesn't have to do anything but eat.  I cook, I clean.

Other than that, things are hunky dory.

### caroline — 2013-11-15T07:22:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-100758>

Hi Vee! I think women crave chocolate on a deep primal level sometimes ..... that is my story and I am sticking to it...LOL

Have you tried the Lindt 85% .... and others on here talk about 95% - I cant find that one...

so glad that your Mom is doing great.....

I will have to have a look at - after the leptin reset......can't remember what it says. I do know you can stop stuffing yourself with so much protein. Have a look at C/T #7 ....It is about why we do all of that kinda!.....basic stuff ......

I really had forgotten some of the why.....or maybe I just care more about the why now!

JK mentioned that this blog is a precursor for transition metals etc.

### veelansu — 2013-11-16T19:18:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-100835>

Okay, so I did the chocolate thing again yesterday at dinner. Was so sick after, it gave me the runs. I think it's just because it's there, that's why I eat it. And once I start, I can't put it down. I'm not buying chocolate anymore. I'm done with it for a while. Getting ready for my 3 weeks away from home. I'm looking forward to being away from work but I hate that I won't be anywhere I can eat right. I will do my best as I have been doing, I just feel like it makes me stall and not progress. And I'm either living in a hotel with no kitchen or in someone else's home with no control. Ugh!

I started losing weight again after my 2 month stall. It's a lot slower than when I first started but I guess that's normal for any weight loss.

Caroline, I've read what to do next, I'm just not sure if I should. I'll probably wait until next year. I want to get my thyroid meds down to 2 grain and still losing before I switch to post leptin rx. I'm afraid that if I start adding fruits and other non compliant items I'm just going to either stall or gain weight which would be depressing.

### caroline — 2013-11-16T23:43:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-100862>

I really would re consider fruits - at any time. Maybe a little in long light cycles and grown in your area. I now kind of think of fruit as mainlining sugar! I have always craved apples - and everyone knows ...an apple a day yada, yada, yada....

Now I realise, at least for me, I was just craving sugar.

I can't keep chocolate around either .... and cheese. If I buy cheese I am cooked .... not happy until it is all gone! I just can't stop .... and I hate that. I always promise myself to just have a little ........ what a joke!

### veelansu — 2013-11-26T05:59:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-101659>

I have been traveling these past few days. I had to go to Hawaii for work (what torture  ). I was walking on the beach away from the crowds of Waikiki. I found a little area that was isolated on the east coast of Oahu. I had my feet in the water, just staring into the horizon, not thinking, not moving, just living in the moment. It was so great. Then I look down at my feet, and take a step and I realize, a sea turtle is at my feet swimming in the shallow water, not 6 inches away. I was so amazed! It was such a beautiful experience. I almost cried. I watched it swim away and keep bobbing it's head as it swam back and forth. I took my phone out of my pocket and waited. Soon, it came right back to me! I took a picture of it, so close to me. Beautiful.

Now I am on the gulf coast for Thanksgiving. I will be in Alabama for the next few days. I have been staying as close to the diet as I can. I've been eating mostly seafood, oysters, shrimp, etc. I am not sure that will be the case for thanksgiving dinner. I will be eating at a friend's family's and I doubt I will have anything I can really eat so I may have to do the best I can. Then I go to Florida to pick up my mom from the rehab facility and bring her to live with me in San Diego. I'm so happy that I can do this for her. It will be life changing but I know rewarding as well.

Work on the other hand is not as rewarding. I need to try to find joy in what I do again. I used to love it, not so much anymore. Not sure why.

I'm still losing weight, not so much this week. It's slowed down but still headed in the right direction.

I will be getting new labs when I get back home in December. I am hoping my testosterone and SBHG have stabilized as well as trying to see where my thyroid numbers are. Can't wait to see if I'm making a difference in my labs.

I hope everyone has a great Thanksgiving. I don't think I will be back on the forum until early December.

### caroline — 2013-11-26T23:41:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-101691>

Have a wonderful thanksgiving Vee......

### veelansu — 2013-12-22T02:16:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-104014>

Okay, I finally feel like I'm settled after the whirlwind of a life I have been living. My mom is comfortably settled in my apartment. Most of her boxes we brought are empty and organized. I don't remember if I mentioned this in another post, but the other shoe dropped right about the same time. My daughter decided to leave her girlfriend of two years, so with no where to go, she came to live with me too. She's sharing my extra bedroom with my mom. It's been a busy busy few months. But life for me is starting to stabilize to a new norm. I've not been able to be strict in my eating habits but have not been very bad. I have lost only 2 lbs since thanksgiving but that's a good thing as I'm going in the right direction. I wanted to start eating fruit so I have added an orange about once a week to get my cravings down. I also started eating tomatoes every now and again. I have not done labs since late October so I will get them done in January. I want to see how my hormones are behaving. I feel fine so I don't expect significant changes from last time. I do hope my SHBG is down and my free T is up some. If not I'm going to have to figure out what else I may have to do to fix those mismatched numbers. I'm very positive about 2014. I'm going to continue to fix my health. I am looking to be more involved in my community. I have been so career focused that I never had time to volunteer or do anything other than work. This year I'm hoping to change all that, especially with my mom here, she needs to get out so volunteering together will help us both. I'm off work for the next two weeks so I'll be on the forum more often. Can't wait to catch up on all that I have missed.

### caroline — 2013-12-22T05:47:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-104028>

Hi Vee .....I can't wait to hear more of what you are up to! I am so glad that everything is working out. You must have a big apartment. .... Three women .....yikes!

Merry Christmas ....xo

### nicld — 2013-12-22T19:09:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-104049>

Wow, grandmother and granddaughter sharing the same room, how is that going? You are really a sandwich generation care giver. Wish you lots of peace this Christmas season and beyond.

### veelansu — 2014-01-18T06:50:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-106100>

Hahaha! I just read my last post and I just have to laugh. I thought things would slow down but not even close. Three women in one apartment is truly an experience. That, plus lots of doctor's appointments for my mom, dealing with my daughter's social life spilling in, and work has been a real challenge for me. There is just not enough hours in the day for all that I now have to do. It's funny how life just happens. One day, I'm sitting in my apartment, meditating, wearing my blue blockers, reducing EMFs, eating healthy, and then.... BAM! My life is tipsy turvy. Nothing I can't handle thank goodness. The holidays were rough from a food perspective, but I found it was easy to go back to eating healthy. I gained 4 lbs between Xmas and first week of January. But I buckled down after that and I was able to lose them again. I'm now at the same weight I was before the holidays. It's funny how fast I can put on weight, and that it takes twice as long to lose it.

I'm getting back into doing all the things I can with the time that I have. I have to figure out a way to get some me time. I was thinking of going to hotel once a month to just spend the day alone. I miss being alone, completely alone. I have not gotten my labs yet. I should have them done this month or see how things are going. Well, that's all for today. I'm hoping I don't disappear again but I cannot plan anything anymore. Life just takes over.

### caroline — 2014-01-18T07:30:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-106103>

Hi, Hi, Hi,!!! glad you popped in - I have missed you!

Have you registered for the free 90 day optimal reset?

A hotel once a month sounds like such a great idea - one with a spa and hot tub and a nice restaurant...... and a massage....

How is your Mom doing?

### veelansu — 2015-06-06T21:13:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/veelansus-journal.8308/post-166855>

Well, it's been over a year since my last post. I have to say my life has changed some. My mom moved back to Florida. My daughter found a nice boy, they decided to move in together and have had a baby since then. A little girl. I moved to Baltimore, MD from San Diego. The job has not stopped being stressful. The good thing is that I found a great doctor that has been helping me get my health in order, no more doing it by myself, blind with just a flashlight the is the internet. She has ordered all the blood, urine, stool and saliva tests necessary to see what is going on in this body of mine. I am gluten intolerant so that has been validated, my thyroid is acting wacky and so are my adrenals. My sex hormones were a mess, she's gotten them to a better level, so my SHBG is at normal levels now. She's helped me with getting at least 8 hours of good sleep a night. I have an app that I track my sleep with and it shows that I am doing a whole lot better than I did before. I used to always wake up a few times during the night, awake as in aware that I was awake. Not anymore. Now it happens rarely and maybe once around the 4 am time frame. Stress from work tends to be a trigger, but not much I can do about that. Melatonin helps a lot and along with the blue blockers are magic. My cholesterol is still not as good as it should be, my gut bacteria needs help, so I am megadosing some probiotics. I am still overweight even though I don't eat junk. I have been eating kind of paleo (no grains, no legumes, etc) I love fruit so I eat that even though I think the sugar in the fruit may be an issue. I avoid white sugar in anything I eat. I eat a mix of proteins beef, pork, fish, lamb. I don't think I am ketogenic due to the fruit and some other starchy vegetables. She's starting to zero in on my adrenals. I have few results to go over with her sometime next week and hopefully we can get my weight under control. If I concentrate really hard I can lose the weight, it's the keeping it off that is almost impossible. Around November of last year she was able to get me down to 163lbs using HCG. But even with the maintenance program, slowly the weigh came back and by January, I was back at 193lbs. I don't think HCG is the answer if something else is wrong. I have been yo-yoing in weight a lot more in the last 2 years and that, to me, is an indication that something else is up. Just hard to find. I know that this website and all it's advice is helpful and I will continue to read and use it. I just need to get all my body chemistry in shape for it to actually see any progress. I just wanted to provide an update in case anyone was interested . I think that the best advice I have ever heard on this site is about getting all the right lab tests regularly to see what is actually happening inside. That is the key and having found a doctor that is willing to do that has been a godsend. I wish everyone else luck in their journey to optimal. (I still can't get myself to dunk my head in ice).

I have been eating kind of paleo (no grains, no legumes, etc) I love fruit so I eat that even though I think the sugar in the fruit may be an issue. I avoid white sugar in anything I eat. I eat a mix of proteins beef, pork, fish, lamb. I don't think I am ketogenic due to the fruit and some other starchy vegetables. She's starting to zero in on my adrenals. I have few results to go over with her sometime next week and hopefully we can get my weight under control. If I concentrate really hard I can lose the weight, it's the keeping it off that is almost impossible. Around November of last year she was able to get me down to 163lbs using HCG. But even with the maintenance program, slowly the weigh came back and by January, I was back at 193lbs. I don't think HCG is the answer if something else is wrong. I have been yo-yoing in weight a lot more in the last 2 years and that, to me, is an indication that something else is up. Just hard to find. I know that this website and all it's advice is helpful and I will continue to read and use it. I just need to get all my body chemistry in shape for it to actually see any progress. I just wanted to provide an update in case anyone was interested . I think that the best advice I have ever heard on this site is about getting all the right lab tests regularly to see what is actually happening inside. That is the key and having found a doctor that is willing to do that has been a godsend. I wish everyone else luck in their journey to optimal. (I still can't get myself to dunk my head in ice).

How have made some great gains. Sleeping though the night is the BEST isn't it.

I agree that HCG does not work the best when there are underlying issues. The weight loss with the first set of rounds did not stay off due to underlying hormonal and circadian issues. Hoping the weight that has come off during these past three rounds will stay off.

I get light first thing when I get up in a morning (have done this for a long time), eat breakfast within 1/2hr, eat again after 4hrs, and have tea at 6pm, which is earlier than I used to, as since learnt to have 3/4hrs before bed.
have cut down on the carbs but still need to cut down more, although cutting the carbs has made my sleep worse unfortunately, getting even less sleep. All I know that when it gets to 6/7pm I start to get anxious and more alert,
I'm dead to the world in the day.

I have just spent all last night awake, cannot relax or feel remotely sleepy. and on the nights I have slept it ranges from 1 1/2 hrs to 3 hrs, so it is SEVERE, once I'm awake that's it I cannot fall asleep again, although sometimes I may, just may be able t dream a bit, but find they are disturbing, so not sure if that is down to the medication or Serine? ,it is 6am in the uk, and feeling very emotional with not sleeping, ( a complete wreck in fact) it is badly affecting my mental and physical state. All my hormones are not working properly like they should. I just cannot function. It upsets me what it has done to my body.

I will reread CT7 thanks, is there a particular time of day thats best to do this?

i have been doing cold showers (no facility to do ice), for the past 5 weeks.

# Thread: Want to be optimal but....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-be-optimal-but.8314/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sweetie86 — 2013-10-01T09:49:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-be-optimal-but.8314/post-96588>

Hello. I want to start the Leptin Rx, as eat small meals often and I don't think its optimal. I am tired all the time, my diet is clean, chicken, eggs, fruit (maybe too much fruit) and veg and 2-3 litres of water a day. I don't have anything seriously wrong with me that I need to take regular medication for except I have recently had CMV and EBV within two months of each other. However I was always tired before that. I do have sinus and allergies to dust mites (from a recent RAST test), and sleep issues, I am a poor sleeper, with bruxism so I wear a mouth guard at night. My eye is severely swollen right now from an allergic reaction to dust. I used to have chronic urticaria as a child but it has cleared up since age 14. Also extremely painful menstruation with causes, vomiting, migraines, IBS C and D reactions too. And I always have constant headache but it changes places, sometimes in my C-spine, sometimes at the front of my head, sometimes at the back of my head.

I also have IBS-C which I treat with magnesium citrate so as long as I take that then I'm fine but I do get scared one day I will run out of it or companies will stop making it. My digestive system gets worse (i.e. clogged) with things like prunes or dates or fiber supplements which is also strange as these help most people.
So my issues are just little annoying health issues but they add up. My new Dr says he might be saying its chronic fatigue. As I have to ration my activities for the day. Often as I am studying full time, this means studying for 7 hours then resting in an uncomfortable half awake stupor for a few hours til bed time which then takes me 4 hours to settle to sleep. I do get things done and get good grades I just have to plan for my energy levels on what I can do every day. I try and walk 45mins each day and get sunlight in the mornings too. I sometimes do more intense workouts but on more intense exercise programs I need afternoon naps and take a long time to recover once I've two weeks into the programs.

I have noticed this epi paleo requires fish and good fat. When I ate fish and prawns the other day I broke out in a big rash on my stomach and thighs and had an itchy throat. I never ate seafood as a child or until recently I've tried prawns only a handful of time. And if I eat high fat, i.e. olive oil, red meat (grass fed t-bones etc) with fat on, I break out in terrible acne. Deep stuff that hurts and just looks ugly. I stopped eating the fats and the acne goes away. Nuts generally hurt my stomach too but I stay away from them usually and I keep away from dairy because of acne issues. I did have a coffee addiction starting but Im done to one or zero cups a day, a few black teas.
I'm not overweight but would like to change body fat composition and drop 6lbs. And mainly get optimal of course! I think I'm having too much omega 6. Frustrating as I eat better than most people my age (27 years old), well I should say I eat 'cleaner' than most people my age but have way less energy. Obviously something is preventing me from being optimal, but each time I try and eat seafoods or good fat, I have these physical presentations of rashes or acne. Anyone with a similar experiences with fat and acne? Or seafood allergy? Or low energy levels that require small frequent meals? What did you do to overcome this? And going from a frequent small meals to 3 large ones how did your digestive system cope? I think mine may shut down from the BAB due to the IBS-C? I cant stand feeling clogged and will do almost anything to avoid it.

Sorry this post is so long thank you for reading. I wouldn't really dare talk to people in my life about all this, as all these little niggling illness aren't actually that serious but I am miserable (I know there are people that are worse off than me and I am grateful for what I do have. I'm generally pretty positive though it sounds like Im a whinger sorry!). I see Dr Kruse and the Leptin Rx as the way out but I feel like I can't tolerate increased acne or feeling clogged up. Thank you for reading my post. Hoping to hear any suggestions or similar experiences?

### Dali Dula — 2013-10-01T15:19:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-be-optimal-but.8314/post-96598>

Try this sweetie: do the leptin rx as directed. Incorporate bone broths from grassfed animals, fish bone broth, duck broth. Drop the chicken and don't eat shellfish right now you will tolerate it better after some detox. The broths are the miracle food try to include them in every meal. they are also inexpensive and delicious. Drop all sugars starches, and dairy. this includes fruit. Don,t worry about exercising right now. Walk and swim in cool water if it available. A few weeks of following these simple guidelines should make you feel better. If you smoke or drink alcohol that should stop or you are wasting your time. Then you can keep digging. It is a deep journey. Don't fear the fat, run from the low-fat.

### nicld — 2013-10-01T16:36:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-be-optimal-but.8314/post-96600>

Look into Ludol Iodine (check out the iodine thread). I had IBS-C and since taking that, it has gone. I was on Mirilax sometimes 2X a day because, like you, I feared getting blocked up. Once the system stopped, the pain started. Also started the disabled DD on it and we no longer have to give her Mirilax. Due to some of the meds she is on she had terrible constipation. I am only doing the 3 meals a day and I think it has helped the GI system. Just my N=1.

You should find alot of help and suggestions here. Good luck.

Nicole

Seanb4....you could be getting a lot of dirty electricity which is basically what travels on your grounding wire if the house electrical wires aren't done well, or are low grade wire and cable. There was a lot of activity around this topic during the EMF series. Grounding into the ground is great (as long as you aren't in a high voltage area) grounding through your building wiring is very iffy. AND it isn't a matter of just checking if you are plugged into the right hole in the outlet.

There was a huge difference in having a grounding sheet go out to the earth versus plugging into an outlet for me. I'm relying on the magnetico and a schumann resonator to provide a good field while I sleep now, but I used to use the grounding sheet with the wire out the window down to the ground. I do have my computer grounded through a wire out my window attached to a ground rod.
Good luck with all this!

I'm not currently doing that, it's on my todo list.

The orange juice is for potassium and carbs. I've found I experience less stomach symptoms with the least volume of food I have. Some months ago I found I could drink a cup of this "soylent" drink twice a day, with water in-between, and it would produce less bloating overall.
I stopped doing this as I assume I was missing quite a bit of good stuff found in real food. But yea so far that's produced the best results dietary wise than anything else I have tried and I am sure it's volume related.

### oliveview — 2013-10-01T14:30:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-96597>

Hi everyone! Here's my recap from Day 1

Got up and had my BAB with steak and eggs. I had a horrible day at work but stayed full until lunch. Normally I would have done some stress eating but I didn't! YAY! I ran home around lunch because the kids chameleon died (it was a bad day all around) and had ground beef for lunch. Supper was pork tenderloin and a bit of broccoli with butter. I had a nice icy cold shower in the morning and got the house dark by, well...dark.

I am still waiting on my labs to be posted. I had to pick and chose and not get all of my labs at once. I will be going for a physical next week and get some more of them done. I went ahead and got the ones I knew I would have to pay for. I won't get my female hormones checked until early December. I know it's not perfect but it's where I am.

My optimal goals
1\. Feel better
2\. Live longer

Those really are my ultimate goals. I am 46, almost 47, female and morbidly obese. In the last year, for the first time ever, I can't keep up with my family. We just got back from a trip to Disney and I was miserable. I have tried everything to lose weight and nothing works! I lose the first week or two and then that is it. I've even had lapband surgery a number of years ago and it's been a disaster. I am coming to Epi-Paleo with the mindset that it may take me months to start to lose weight, that this is more reset than it is a traditional "diet". Diets don't work and unfortunately I've been shamed and put down by the medical profession saying that I just need to eat less. I know that I have leptin and metabolism issues, maybe hormones as well. I've been talked into antidepressants as well, anything to deal with the pain and the shame of obesity. But I have know from the beginning that there was something wrong with me. It's just nice to find somewhere where people seem to understand!

### oliveview — 2013-10-02T00:39:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-96654>

Day 2 is going well so far but more on that later.

I live in the US, actually on the coast of Georgia.

Lab tests that I am waiting on are Leptin, HS CRP, Hgb A1C, rT3, T4, TSH, TPO, Thyroid Antithyroglobulin, Vit D. I am planning to get more labs next week when I go in for my physical: CMP, CBC, Ferratin, and others. I have not ordered the saliva test yet. Is there somewhere better to order it from?

I was always a active kid and enjoyed sports. When I was a teenager I got injured playing soccer and ended up having 14 knee surgeries in 2.5 years. At that point I gained 100# and my life has never been the same. I always struggled to get the weight off, but in my late 20's I went on phen-fen. I lost 100# in 3 months without even trying. That was the point that I knew there was something very wrong with me. When they pulled it from the market, I gained the weight back...plus some.

I had my 3 children over the course of 3.5 years in my mid to late 30's. I was very obese but did well with my first pregnancy. My second I was sicker and had lots of nausea and vomiting. I actually had a mild case of pneumonia and was on antibiotics when I delivered him. By the time we were pregnant with our third, I think my body was just depleted. I was hospitalized numerous times for dehydration, pre term labor, and I was profoundly anemic. Fortunately, I had great labors and delivered 3 healthy children.

The weight loss only got more and more difficult. We tried every diet there was to try and I just continued to gain weight. I gave in to frustration and had a LapBand done about 5 years ago...that's a whole other post but to sum it up...disaster! I am now up about 35# from the time that I and the LapBand. sigh.

Anyway, I have never been a sleeper. Even as a child I never slept well. I still don't sleep well.

We have very few computers at work and no wifi. We have both at home. I haven't read too much about the EMF exposure but I'm getting there. There is just lots of info to absorb.

If you're still reading, thank you! Comments and suggestions are welcome.

### oliveview — 2013-10-03T17:00:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-96795>

We drove last night to our place here in the mountains. We got to bed very late and I didn't sleep we'll being in a new place. We were able to open the windows and sleep in the cool air. Also, we could hear the trickling of a little creek by our window. Very nice. I've had to eat out dinner last night and breakfast in this morning but I was able to keep on track. It was more like an hour before I had my BAB this morning but that was the best I could do. I had eggs, steak and avocado. Vacation is nice but I look forward to being home and having control over my food.

We (my very supportive husband and I) have been reading about supplements. As I get a handle on the eating, they will be next. Well, that and CT. Lots to do but I'm still feeling motivated! I don't have lots of energy but I certainly don't have carb flu, thankfully. I was lc paleo for a month and then went off for two weeks before starting the leptin rx/epi-paleo. Maybe that's why I'm not feeling too bad.

So today starts Day 4...

### caroline — 2013-10-16T02:54:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-97937>

Isn't that amazing when the hunger and cravings go away??? Being craving free is practically worth the price of admission!

the changes will happen slowly and sometimes you don't even notice .... and then, one day, you go WOW ...when did that happen......

### nicld — 2013-10-16T17:09:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-98005>

> [oliveview said:](/goto/post?id=97930)
>
> Day 16 and something has changed. I no longer want food and my hunger is totally gone. I ate most of my BAB this am, a handful of macadamia nuts at lunch and then had a nice piece of grouper for dinner. No real hunger.
>
> I think I'm doing well with the CT. I get 60 degrees or a tad lower for an hour. My only issue is that I have to do this in the evening and I get so very cold as I am warming up while sleeping. The shivers wake me up numerous times at night until I get totally rewarmed. Not really sure how to handle this.
>
> Anyway, all is going well otherwise.

Same thing happens to me when I do night time CT. I have had to switch to only doing it 2X at night during the week since it seems to take me so long to rewarm that it stops sleep and right now, sleep in more important. I have been taking a warmer shower afterwards for a couple of minutes and that helps with the rewarming and probably is the wrong thing to do but I need SLEEP. I do take really long ones on the weekend when I have all day to move around and rewarm. It was really nice in the summer when I could go outside and let the sun rewarm but when the temp is only 55, it will not really be warming. Oh and it is really nice when the hubby lets be snuggle up next to him to rewarm and get some oxytocin release too

Good luck and we can do this.

### freesia — 2013-10-16T23:33:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-98045>

Just a suggestion for managing the later baths. Maybe experiment with a shorter time in the bath since you can't do it earlier, then as rewarming gets faster (it does happen!) gradually increase the time in the water again...to tolerance. Tolerance = to where it doesn't impact sleep negatively.
I had to use a hot water bottle on my feet a few times last winter so I could get to sleep. That was a transitional tool and it didn't hurt!

### oliveview — 2013-10-24T16:09:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-98842>

Thank you everyone for the advice on the CT. I will try and see if it helps. I've been a bit discouraged as my weight was down and now its back up. BUT--I'm still here and still following the program!

### caroline — 2013-10-25T01:30:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-98938>

please hang in there! keep a diary of what you eating .... account for everything.......Dr. K. calls it the "Hawthorne affect"

How could he get so fat on that?
IDK how to protect the other sheep now. But only he got sick. Maybe he was just more sensible to EMF. He was such a sweet guy. Very sad.

### nicld — 2013-10-02T20:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ultimate-emf-book-that-is-a-must-read.8321/post-96716>

### Justfourme — 2013-10-03T04:44:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ultimate-emf-book-that-is-a-must-read.8321/post-96759>

The livestock started getting sick, had miscarriages, and their milk supply decreased to almost nothing. The cows went from being healthy looking to gaunt looking. Which when most of your income is from the milk cows, basically shuts down your business. I don't recall exactly how my brother tied the problems to electricity, but they were basically the last house at the end of that electric line. Experts from the college came out to run tests which showed higher than normal levels of electricity in the milking barn. The electric utility company came out and said according to their tests, the levels were within the "acceptable range". At that time I asked if this electricity problem is causing all of these health issues with the livestock, what is it doing to you? They moved within a couple years.

### Gracelind — 2013-10-11T23:41:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ultimate-emf-book-that-is-a-must-read.8321/post-97561>

Neighbor boy across the street from us in the early 1970s had a brain tumor in his teens. Probably was benign though I don't know for sure. There was a transmission tower on the hill just a few hundred yards above their house. On damp foggy nights we could hear a "sizzle" and humming sounds from the wires. Even back then we wondered whether living directly under those wires contributed to this tumor. His surgery was successful and as far as I know he's still fine.

The book is an eye-opener and it calls into question all the research that tells us chemicals are safe that have been banned in Europe and Russia. Makes you wonder about climate change research and the overwhelming campaign to belittle those who call for a healthy skepticism.

It's clearly an economic decision that chemicals need to be proven conclusively that they are not safe to be removed from the market. Shouldn't a society concerned about health first require _proof of safety_ before a chemical could be introduced? And then there's the much more complicated issue of interaction among multiple chemicals . . .

We are at the mercy of certain manufacturers and bureaucrats--a sometimes deadly combination.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-11T23:42:52+0300

The only difference I've noticed is that all our evergreens (cypress, juniper, holly, magnolia, fire thorn, and pine trees have an abundance of berries and cones on them this fall. More so than ever before.

We know Jack has mentioned conifers as essential to our future survival.. initially I thought that it was b/c they are green year round and produce a good quantity of O2. Now I'm wondering about their ability to potentially mitigate EMF. off to ask jack about that...

> Why is it exactly that Europe and Russia are much more tuned in? and seem to put people first?

Audrey

my thoughts on this at least from an American POV - trace back to Manifest Destiny. This has a HUGE impact in how we do things as Americans, as does our dysfunctional gov't. The premise of who we are as a society - the good and the bad, and how we confront the world at large today - still resides in this principle [http://www.ushistory.org/us/29.asp](http://www.ushistory.org/us/29.asp)

now apply that to the concept in the movie Field of Dreams - when James Earl Jones says "People will come" baseball is a allegory of American History.

### JoeBranca — 2013-10-12T00:42:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ultimate-emf-book-that-is-a-must-read.8321/post-97569>

"my thoughts on this at least from an American POV - trace back to Manifest Destiny. This has a HUGE impact in how we do things as Americans, as does our dysfunctional gov't. "

which ties into Marino's account of his conversations at the international conference with all the power company bigwigs he was invited to speak at (about halfway through the book). It's actually difficult for me to accept the things were said by a couple of those folks as he reports in the book... they sound like the scripted lines of cheesy robber baron villains in old movies. But I suppose with enough drink in someone, they're liable to reveal anything...

Marino's reflections on what he heard and experienced are more interesting... that the push for above ground powerlines can't be fully explained by the pursuit of profit in context of the power companies. Rather they would want their visible monuments, to remind everyone how they maintain and make possible modern society. In other societies one instead might have numerous inescapable statues and images of their "benevolent" dictators for a similar reminder.

### Hope — 2013-10-12T00:59:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ultimate-emf-book-that-is-a-must-read.8321/post-97570>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=97566)
>
> Why is it exactly that Europe and Russia are much more tuned in? and seem to put people first?

Audrey..just my humble $0.02 as someone who spent 21 years growing up over in Europe (not Russia).....and the #1 clear reason to me is that they are NOT quite AS crazy greedy as they are in the US. Hands down. It's so sad but so so true, in my book. I see it so much in so many examples.

Just one teeny tiny example....over there, many employees by law get 4 weeks vacation a year. Paid. Have for decades. Employees and stuff is more expensive there, but they are saving money on healthcare expenses for sure. Plus people have about HALF the chronic disease rate they do here. Maybe that will change with the whole EMF thing etc. but it's all.about.money. and government not by the 'government' but a few select large corporations in the US. And it stinks to high heaven. It's also why lots of Americans call lots of Europe 'socialist', which I hate...I call it SMART. And not bad for the size....one of the countries is no bigger than the size of Wisconsin, but it manages to have the 3rd or 4th largest economy. In the world. Not bad for a place with 'socialist' healthcare, retirement etc. It's far from perfect too of course.....but to me this is simple and clear as mud.

I got the first part of my labs back and I was wondering if anyone had any thoughts or suggestions.

Test Name Flag Result Ref Range Units Graph Info
Hemoglobin A1c 5.6 4.8-5.6

T4,Free(Direct) 1.25 0.82-1.77 ng/dL

TSH 1.740 0.450-4.500 uIU/mL

Thyroglobulin, Antibody <1.0 0.0-0.9 IU/mL

Reverse T3 19.3 9.2-24.1 ng/dL

Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxy LOW 25.9 30.0-100.0 ng/mL

C-Reactive Protein, Cardiac HIGH 10.79 0.00-3.00 mg/L

Leptin 32.0 ng/mL

Thyroid Peroxidase (TPO) Ab <6 0-34 IU/mL

Triiodothyronine,Free 3.0 2.0-4.4 pg/mL

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-10-02T23:10:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-are-back.8323/post-96741>

> [oliveview said:](/goto/post?id=96737)

> C-Reactive Protein, Cardiac HIGH 10.79 0.00-3.00 mg/L

Your HS-CRP is extremely high, and indicates a very unhealthy condition. What can cause that?

\* Diet high in omega-6s (and probably very low in seafood)
\* Bad field (likely you spend a large part of your life in EMF hell)
\* Over exercising / over training

Review those items for starters, and start CTing like a mad person .... you need it.

# Thread: doing leptin reset and c.t for chronic pain, need help with labs please
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-leptin-reset-and-c-t-for-chronic-pain-need-help-with-labs-please.8325/>

### ash — 2013-10-03T00:01:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-leptin-reset-and-c-t-for-chronic-pain-need-help-with-labs-please.8325/post-96743>

hello everyone, I am 53yr old male and have suffered chronic pain in my side since having total hip replacement in 2000. also caught hep c at this time. was told the hep c had cleared up by hospital in 2002. pain is constant in my side, like someone sticking a red hot poker in my body. also have had unexplained aches and pains around chest area, neck, shoulders for a few years. these are easing off now since starting leptin reset and ice baths alternate days. the pain in my side is still bad and have been placing ice on the area in the bath. am reading jacks book and realise I need some labs but would like to know which ones to prioritise. I am 5' 6 and weigh between 125lbs-130lbs now. I have dropped over 10lbs in the 3 weeks I have changed to epi paleo and leptin reset. also I have horrible sleep, barely managing more than 3 hours at a time. I have been on 80mg-100mg oxycontin daily for approx 2years. the 6 years previously it was tramadol x 8 daily. at the moment im only taking 12mls methadone daily to help me come off oxycontin as the withdrawals are horrible. i cant get under 10mls daily as the pain in my side kicks in then and i cant go without any pain relief. i am finding all this stuff im learning really amazing and am praying this is going to be my saviour as have felt suicidal in the past with this pain that will never leave me. i hope that someone can give me some info as to where i can get labs done and how do i go about having a consult with dr kruse. thanks, ash. i don't know if this matters but i live in Southwales UK ( does the uk do these labs anywhere etc). am looking forward to meeting others on this forum. thanks ,ash. p.s there is other medical history before my hip replacement as i had a massive motorbike accident in 1983 and smashed pelvis, femur, radius. also have had crazy chaotic life taking many many class a drugs and totally screwing my circadian rhythms and sending my cortisol, dopamine etc all over the place. xxx

### caroline — 2013-10-03T00:52:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-leptin-reset-and-c-t-for-chronic-pain-need-help-with-labs-please.8325/post-96744>

Welcome Ash to the forum. It must just be so awful to be in that much pain on a daily basis. Jack's book has a great chapter on labs. I am in Oz and get saliva/ASI testing done thru ZRT labs in the U.S. Some one else will come along with more suggestions on labs for you.

Please start a journal in the journal section - maybe transfer the above to it. Journals are great for commitment and everyone here can follow your progress and offer help and support and Dr. Kruse likes to know more about you to help if he can.

Please have a look at Dr. K's podcasts - they are a quick and easy way to get information. Also, if you can manage it.....become a gold member and then you have access to all the webinars and live Q&A. There is a webinar every month and then a live Q&A a few days later where you can ask questions. Jack is starting a massive series now that is mind bending.....the first one was a week ago and he promises the next one \[October\] will be off the charts! He is very easy to listen to and very direct as well and he is leaving no stone unturned. Think of this as an investment in your health and your journey to optimal. That all being said ..... of course you don't have to ....we will support you any way we can.

That is great that your are eating epi-paleo and started the reset and doing C/T. C/T will be a game changer for you - can you manage it every day?

You need to be very, very aware of your EMF exposure - how is your home and work place for wifi etc.? You need to limit these as much as possible - as soon as possible.
There was an EMF bootcamp that I think you can still purchase if you are interested.

Observing light cycles is critical for you ....Go outside and look at the sun upon arising ...and when the sun sets - no fake light. Do you have blue blocker glasses? extremely important for you too. Especially limit wifi in your bedroom - no clock radio/TV etc. and cut the power if you can.

Get outside as much as possible - no shoes and as little clothes as possible. Do you have a river, lake, ocean near by?

Only drink spring water - you need to eliminate fluorides etc. pronto

Please read about the magnetico sleep system - I would think that would be critical for you as well.

Keep reading Dr.K.'s blogs and make sure you read the comments after each blog .....there is gold in the comments.

You often have to read the blogs many times to start connecting all the dots - please keep asking questions.

We are all on this Journey together and Dr. K. is shining his flashlght for us.

There are lots of great stories in the journals of folks working on there road to optimal - maybe you can offer some help to them too.......

### caroline — 2013-10-03T00:57:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-leptin-reset-and-c-t-for-chronic-pain-need-help-with-labs-please.8325/post-96745>

sorry that was so long ....

If you can believe it .....I forgot something!

Add in more good fat ...ie. GF butter, coconut oil etc.

Try to do ....high fat, medium protein and very low carb

### Linz — 2013-10-03T11:25:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-leptin-reset-and-c-t-for-chronic-pain-need-help-with-labs-please.8325/post-96772>

Welcome Ash,

There is lots of reallly good information for you here. Sounds as though you have already made a good start.

You want to look up Dr Myhill's website. Her practice is in Wales but she will order labs for you and your own doctor and give you a written explanation of the results if you need it.

### ash — 2013-10-03T12:32:14+0300

The Hep C has major effects at cytochrome 1 and on superoxide dismutase in the mitochondria. Hep C will destroy sleep and this destroys the DC current below the myelin level that resides outside the axons.......when you can not sleep well you can not regenerate proteins well and the result is increased pain as time elapses. The Epi paleo RX acts directly as a resetter of epigenetic signaling by directly affecting DNA methylation and histone de acetylation. Anything that acts an HDAC inhibitor in our cells, blocks mTOR signaling by increasing the process of autophagy. Autophagy happens in sleep normally. Sleep has the exact same effect as the Epi-paleo Rx. Autophagy occurs maximally in humans during sleep when we are regenerating. October 2013 webinar is about this very issue.

Chicken pox is a herpes virus and the latent infection is shingles. So the answer is yes these virus's incorporate and invade our genome. You really need to read Brain gut 2 to see why. HERV is why. Hep C single-stranded, positive-sense RNA virus. Positive-strand RNA virus replication complexes are universally associated with intracellular membranes like that on the inner mitochondrial membrane. This is why fat is best for Hep C. beta oxidation limits mTOr activation. HCV only infects the liver because all the receptors and proteins it acts upon are there. Once in it ruins hepatocyte mitochondria. HCV virion enters uninfected liver cells via LDL-c receptor. LDL-c receptor remains the same even after fats have been delivered to the liver's mitochondria. The more triglycerides are delivered the more HCV virus gets up taken to the liver mitochondrial to trash it. This is why high carb diets and HCV are toxic. Same thing with booze. Booze increases TG like mad.
This is why bad diets and Hep C show other disease caused by ROS. If you have hep C you need to eat a boat load of saturated fats that enter the ETC at cytochrome 2. Hepatitis C is also associated with SjÃ¶gren's syndrome (an autoimmune disorder); thrombocytopenia; lichen planus; porphyria cutanea tarda; necrolytic acral erythema; insulin resistance; diabetes mellitus; diabetic nephropathy; autoimmune thyroiditis and B-cell lymphoproliferative disorders.

# Thread: Creating Clarity ~ Clarissa's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creating-clarity-clarissas-journal.8327/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ClarissaK — 2013-10-03T03:59:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creating-clarity-clarissas-journal.8327/post-96754>

Hello everyone,

Just found this site and this group recently and have been reading and listening to Jack's advice and knowledge voraciously!

I have been on/off "Paleo" for a few years, though it never helped me for long and now I am understanding why it didn't answer my health issues in itself. The info I've been reading here is like an awakening! I feel that Jack has tapped into the very things that make us Human and I for one am ready to finally start feeling like one.

I wanted to start this journal today because I believe that MY NEW LIFE is beginning for me on this date. I met my husband 14 years ago today and instead of celebrating it we are in the midst of a divorce. I have been struggling with that, not wanting to do it, but realized I simply must live for myself now, LET GO of the things that are "no longer serving me." So today is my day to start falling in love with ME, caring for myself as if my life depends on it (which it does).

I was "lucky" enough to read this journal entry by Inger last night and it was so life-affirming to me, I plan to thank her sometime. I do believe that things can and will align to assist us in our journey if we allow them to. [http://www.jackkruse.com/winter-solstice-special-blog-the-inger-effect/](http://www.jackkruse.com/winter-solstice-special-blog-the-inger-effect/)

I also listened to one of Jack's blogs today, the one from March 2013. In it he says "Sometimes a Goodbye is a second chance for Life." ITA, and am saying goodbye to my old way of living, being co-dependent and lost, hungry yet overweight, tired and bored with life, ready to find some MAGIC.

A little bit about my road to ill-health:

I was raised on a pseudo-healthy SAD diet in the 70's/80's. I had chaotic, divorced parents (one of which was very controlling, one was a drug addict). As a little girl I was very thin, always had colds and painful legs, had energy but craved weird things like rock salt and dry dog food.

As a teen I was thin enough, though felt fat and started the diet-roller-coaster that got me where I am now, age 44. My 20's were simply not healthy, didn't exercise much (didn't think I needed to as I was still thin enough), ate out a lot, went from being a junk-food-junkie to a Vegetarian and back again. In my mid-20's I was addicted to cola, too, and the low-fat nonsense kept me from ever feeling very good. By my late 20's I had become hyper-vigilant and everything woke me up in the night. By the time I had my son, age 33, my sleep failed to restore me at all and I sank into anxiety/depression/insomnia followed Chronic Fatigue, IBS, and Fibromyalgia. And it hasn't ever gotten better.

The past 10 years have been a near-nightmare. I couldn't work. Lost friends. My marriage suffered. I crawled into a shell. My body felt like it was 80, not near 40. My moods were all over the place, and I tried EVERYTHING I could do "fix" my diet (fasting, raw veganism, the GAPS diet, juicing, supplementation, cleanses, low-carb, etc.). I found the only thing that helped was doing Paleo, but after a couple of months that would fail me too. However I kept going, kept trying, hoping something would click. Doctors were no help. I became despondent at times, what with my aching body and my foggy head not allowing me to have a life... BUT I am pretty tenacious and stubborn, wouldn't give up. I come from Pioneer stock and we keep on keeping on...

So... after digging down VERY deep into the depths during my divorce and rediscovering my strength I have decided that I am NOT going to stand for living in the "shell" of my life anymore. I want to be a Starfish! I want to live again!!

I live in Portland, Oregon, not too far from the ocean and my dream is to move nearer to it. I have always loved the ocean, won't ever live far from it (I lived in England for 7 years after getting married, just near the Kent coast). I feel that my urge to be near the coast is instinctual, and Jack is just adding to my conviction about that!

So, what am I doing now? Making my diet as Epi-Paleo as possible. Fish daily (ate little before), grass-fed meats (though less beef now and more pork and organ meats), good butter/ghee, eggs, seasonal veg/fruits, and I'm weaning off the sugar/starches. I like sweet potatoes and also allow myself grapefruit and apples. Sugar in my tea is very hard to give up though, ugh! I tried cold-turkey on the sugars/starches, but my son has been sick and I've been up for 2 nights and I have been a zombie (fell back on comfort foods, though much less than before)...

I am turning off the TV/wi-fi early in the evening, have low lights on from dusk, and am going to bed at 9:30 (I had been staying awake watching crime dramas and/or reading until I about fainted from tiredness). I am taking walks most days, and doing yoga before bed. And...so far there were 2 days when it all came together (sleep, food, lower stress, lower EMF's) and I FINALLY woke up with some energy and some hope. I plan to keep doing my best, be on this website every day, keep a journal, get off the sugars, learn the protocols, and love myself again.

Any and all advice is appreciated--I look forward to reading more of your journals, too (when I am not so tired).

I'm so excited about this journey, feel so blessed to be finding the CLARITY I have been seeking. XOXOXOXO

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-03T04:07:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creating-clarity-clarissas-journal.8327/post-96755>

welcome........you have joined at a time where the blogs will be foundational and key for wellness
