# Thread: Want to see how current my info is?  10/2/2013 WSJ article
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-see-how-current-my-info-is-10-2-2013-wsj-article.8333/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-03T17:24:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-see-how-current-my-info-is-10-2-2013-wsj-article.8333/post-96798>

By LAURA LANDRO CONNECT
Critically ill patients who survive a stay in an intensive-care unit, where they are often heavily sedated and ventilated, can find themselves mentally impaired long after release. A new study says the problem is far more common and lasting than previously believed.

Nearly 80% of patients with prolonged ICU stays showed cognitive problems a year or more later, and more than half exhibited effects similar to Alzheimer's disease and traumatic brain injury, according to a report Wednesday in the New England Journal of Medicine.

The researchers, at Vanderbilt University in Nashville, Tenn., and the Tennessee Valley Veterans Affairs Geriatric Research Education and Clinical Center, said the findings highlight the need to modify ICU practices that put patients at risk of cognitive problems, and to offer rehabilitation programs to restore concentration, thinking and memory function after patients leave the hospital.

Enlarge Image

Jason Andrew for The Wall Street Journal
Joan Healy said that after a stay in an ICU, she suffered 'brownouts' during conversations, when she couldn't fully grasp what was being said.

Intensive-care units traditionally keep patients heavily sedated, immobilized and on mechanical ventilation to keep them free of pain, anxiety and agitation as they heal or undergo invasive procedures. But prolonged sedation can trigger or exacerbate delirium, a temporary state of acute brain injury that can also be caused by conditions such as septic shock, an infection that spreads through the body and is a common reason for being admitted to an ICU.

Delirium, long linked to higher rates of death, is also associated with long-term mental impairment in ICU survivors, the study found. E. Wesley Ely, senior author of the study and founder of Vanderbilt's ICU Delirium and Cognitive Impairment Study Group, said some ICU-related brain injury could be prevented if the duration of delirium in the ICU is shortened. A new protocol Vanderbilt and others are following includes weaning patients from sedatives carefully, waking them regularly to see if they can breathe on their own sooner, and getting them out of bed and moving as soon as possible.

The study included 821 patients with respiratory failure or septic shock who had a median ICU stay of 5 days. Their ages ranged from 18 to 99. Only 6% had pre-existing cognitive impairment, and 75% developed delirium during their hospital stay. Assessed for cognitive function at one year after discharge, 34% still had scores similar to moderate traumatic brain injury, and 24% had scores similar to patients with mild Alzheimer's disease. In total, nearly 80% scored lower than predicted by age and education.

"Whether you are young or old and even if you are previously healthy, if you go into an ICU with significant breathing problems, are on a breathing machine or in shock, you may not be coming out with an intact brain," said Dr. Ely. And after discharge, he adds, "so many people are living in their own private hell of mental fog and no one around them knows about it, tells them it is going to happen, or tries to prevent or treat it."

Joan Healy, a 48-year-old who lives in Huntington, N.Y., spent a week in an ICU three years ago suffering from septic shock due to an infection. For two months after discharge, she said all she could do was sleep, and for the first year after recovery she would experience what she described as "brownouts," where she could do little more than nod and smile during conversations without grasping what was being said.

Her family was just happy she was alive, making every effort to help, but "they can't fathom that I can't follow directions, that a simple recipe to cook for dinner is torture," Ms. Healy said. "It's like being in a dim room at sunset, you can navigate but you don't feel you have a grasp on everything going on around you."

Ms. Healy, who does administrative work for her husband's orthopedics practice, reached out to experts at Vanderbilt, who referred her to a computer-based program for attention and memory problems, which she said has been helpful. "I am not the person I was, but from last year to now, I am doing better, and I am still working hard at it," she said.

Malaz Boustani, chief operating officer of the Indiana University Center for Innovation and Implementation Science, who wasn't involved in the study, said its findings add to the evidence about brain-health concerns for ICU survivors and highlight the need for recovery programs that "coordinate the complex cognitive, functional and psychological recovery needs of Americans who survived a critical illness."

Write to Laura Landro at [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])
[http://online.wsj.com/article/SB10001424052702303492504579111361055134336.html](http://online.wsj.com/article/SB10001424052702303492504579111361055134336.html)

Story starts like this.
I February this year I woke with severe swallowing difficulties and what felt like breathing problems, doctors have done various tests including ct scan. I have had tonsillitis all my life so earlier this year had my tonsils removed. I have still been having swallowing problems but the doctors cannot find what's going on. Some tests so far have come back abnormal, including raised platelets, very low testosterone levels and I am scheduled to see a endo but this is not for several months. I'm not sure if this is relevant but I also have a number of lipomas (fatty lumps). I started the paleo/bulletproof diet back when I first became ill but have gained weight, currently 16 stone, then whilst researching found jacks epi paleo diet and started this several weeks ago all was going well until this week when every time if have gone to eat dinner which is usually liver, heart etc I have had to leave it as I have been feeling really sick (I usually have seafood for lunch for past few weeks).
Yesterday I woke during the night with severe acid in my throat and the top of my chest and was violently sick 7 times with stomach cramps and pain in my liver and kidneys (I also for several moths have had a sick feeling in my throat constantly), I have also had no food today and today cannot stand the thought of eating seafood or sat fats or anything for that matter. I have been having cold baths for the past few weeks and did so for 20 minutes last night. Today I feel quite literally like I'm dying and have not been able to eat a single thing, I also started doing the iodine protocol 2 weeks ago aswell. I'm a 35 year old male and been feeling awful since February this year but this past week has got way worse

Any help advice would really be appreciated.
Apologies if this is the wrong category.

yep.......From a recent blog:

WHAT DOES THIS RESEARCH IMPLY?

When you lose your DC regenerative current in Schwann cells and the brain’s microglia and oligodendroglia for any reason at all, you can not regenerate the normal arms of neuro immunity or of cellular immunity from your thymus gland where the T Helper cells go to mature; This means you can’t activate T-Helper-3 cells so T-Helper-17 cells begin to dominate. When it TH3 dominates long enough what happens? Calcium leaves the pineal gland and circadian biology is altered, thus, we lose the yoking of the Vitamin A and D cycle in the brain, and the result is a spike in NF kappa beta and IL- 6 in the brain and peripheral tissues. These are the main inflammatory cytokines in the brain. These are the same effects seen experimentally in Blackman’s work mentioned earlier in the blog. The result is that antibodies are made from constantly activated B cells because the T cells can not turn them off and the subsequently ”tag” the different tissues in which this altered field occurs in.

When this happens in the thymus at a certain time of embryologic development, the result can Hashimoto’s disease or celiac disease. If it occurs at after the 8 embroylogic week of human development it will affect the developing hind brain and dentate nucleus in the cerebellum. This affects the posterior hind brain the result can be autism and its spectrum. Guess what the most common finding is in autistic brains on functional MRI? The answer is: Altered cerebellar development.

What happens to the neuro-immune system in the brain microglia and oligodendroglia in an altered field?

Once the calcium is lost from these cells it changes the resting membrane potential of cells. The resting membrane potentials of cell membranes of the microglia and supporting cells of neurons are altered immediately. It is in these cells where the brain’s hormone cascade is made from LDL cholesterol. This causes us not to make or secrete neuro-hormones on time or at optimal levels because of the calcium efflux. We get a chronic pregnenolone steal syndrome if we look at an ASI or salivary melatonin level. When calcium is released from cells we see a dramatic change in free T3 levels and vitamin A. Remember from the hormone 101 and 102 blog posts that pregnenolone steal syndrome that occurs when free T3 and vitamin A cycles are disrupted in the brain. When any stress response occurs in the brain the response it to raise LDL cholesterol, but we need to convert it to pregnenolone to make all hormones. We need T3 and vitamin A to do it. When these physiologic alterations occur the result is a cytokine storm from an the massive release of NFkappa beta and IL-6 from our cells. When these chemicals are present you can not make this conversion, nor can you activate T-helper-17 cells (supressor cells to stop the process) or T-helper- 3 cells because they are in arrested development in the thymus. Why?………because your electromagnetic field has been altered across your brainstem! The field is what causes these cells to mature and become expressed after they visit the thymus.
CIRCADIAN GEEKS: The entire outflow of the cerebral cortex must pass through the brain stem as it heads toward the spinal cord. At the bottom of the brainstem is the ponto-medullary reticular formation (PMRF) where the parasympathetic outflow of the brain is located. This allows is to calm down and digest our food naturally. Neither one works well in autoimmunity. This area is directly connected to the hindbrain in humans. The vagus nerve that connects this part of the brainstem to the gut as the major player to signal our body to rest and our gut to digest properly. This is why the SCN is entrained to light cycles alone but the gut organs which are entrained to feeding behaviors only. They communicate to the hypothalamus via the incretin system and bring their information to a different part of the hypothalamus. This may begin to explain why the Leptin Rx is built the way it is. When you eat is more important than what you eat in autoimmunity. In the upper brainstem is where the sympathetic outflow is located in a place called the midbrain reticular formation (MRF).

The MRF connects to the PVN to give us the sympathetic outflow to the adrenal glands you learned about here. This is the site of where adrenal fatigue really happens. When the PMRF is activated it automatically down regulates the PVN. THE MRF and PVN are where the stress response of cortisol, Nor Epinephrine, and epinephrine are felt body wide via the IMLCC of the thoracic spinal cord which connects to the peripheral nerves to access the the rest of the body. **Alterations of the IMLCC is why GERD occurs as the neurons in the esophageal plexus have their voltage gaited channels altered. This plexus controls the GE sphincter to acid backwash in the lower esophagus.**

### balor123 — 2013-10-05T01:09:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reversing-non-traditional-nerve-damage.8342/post-96897>

I had severe obstructive sleep apnea (AHI 50) that went undisagnosed most of my 20s. I've been on a CPAP for a few years now and I'm somewhat better but a lot of the damage is done. To name a few: groin pain (come and go), allodynia in one spot (come and go), varicocele recurrence (come and go, after surgery), eyelids that won't fully close (seems fixed), parathesia (seems fixed), gastroparesis (seems fixed), BFS (come and go), etc. Despite treatment, nocturnal clenching remains a big problem and I have a messed up bite (anterior open bite). Anyway, I seem to have hit a plateau in my recovery. I eat a solid Palo diet but that's it. I don't take any supplements. What dietary modifications should I be looking at that I might be missing? Should I be reintroducing supplements and if so which ones? I see Dr Kruse has some nice recommendations for diabetic neuropathy but in my case I have oxidative stress neuropathy from simple lack of oxygen - that is, years of fluctuating oxygen levels while I slept. At least on my sleep studies the nadir seemed to only be about 92%.

I will also teach you the key to re establishing the DC current during sleep in the October 2013 webinar.

A simple wrist sprain to a major crushing trauma can cause CRPS...were the ones who reacted to a minor trauma already in a more deficient state? I now think it is. The excruciating pain which seems to be related to inflammatory products locally generated. This too makes sense now (I hope I'm not lost here), less DC current=inflammation=pain. What about the other manifestations? Vascular (skin colour) changes, trophic changes, loss of sensibility (this one really puzzle people) feeling that the affected part of the body don't belong..which resolve as the CRPS gets better but not respecting classic innervation (funny), a lot of trouble with the sleep... all can be explained with what you exposed, no?
I need to look for epidemiological data on CRPS, I have the feeling that the trend will follow some other data.
The treatment then have to follow the same rules that you talked about in EE6. Last note. One of my patient has a 12 y.o boy who got a CRPS after a fall on his arm...he is treated at a kid's hospital with the traditional algorithm ( and not responding....) and she said they told her that CRPS was more frequent in ex-premature kids (she was pretrem also...) ### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-05T17:12:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reversing-non-traditional-nerve-damage.8342/post-96945>

It is very simple.........first issue.......if your DC current is low it is because you have poor energy balance in your body and your myelin begins to fall apart (EE1). Then after that, you get, say any injury, like a fall to your arm. An injury requires the DC current to get better and remember the DC current is below the myelin layer but outside the axon. The two things that increase the DC current? Epi-paleo Rx ketosis and quantum sleep. You know about the first one, and the October webinar will tell you about the next one. If you have or do neither, you begin to steal energy from other lower vegetative systems.........but you never get enough energy to make the ultimate difference and the energy hog system is decent but the other systems are running on fumes. This is why other diseases are always linked to these neolithic diseases.......WHY?

What is in EE 6 is why. It is all about energy. We partitioning energy on a fractal design. The most expensive systems pull energy from the basic systems......and cause general havoc over time if the energy situation is not fixed. Their signaling is changed and as energy is lost the structure of their matter (proteins) is changed. This leads to altered physiology because the biochemistry adjusts to the energy level. This is precisely how it is done in an atom......lowest energy levels in electron shells are filled first and the outer most last. This is a simple thumbnail of EE5 and EE 6 in a nutshell. How do you increase the DC current? You heard that in EE 1. Fat, water, and low EMF. The more energy you lose from myelin the lower your DC current will be........and you can never fix the system. This is why ketosis and low EMF are primordial situations for illness situations. Ketosis give 147 ATP and carbs give you 36 ATP. Simple third grade energy math.

M1 are inflammatory phenotypes and they stay around the sick CRPS nerves. With more Dietary DHA you are adding more energy to the nervous system and M2 macrophages are transformed. This is how energy transforms matter. Same way the sun turns hydrogen into helium. No different. The nerves get more DHA in their cell membranes and this allows them to transfer solitons, photons, and electrons better. Pain goes away. it also allows for more myelin to be made.......and the DC current comes back. The more fat you eat the more energy becomes available to help refill the energy losses due to the original injury. What solidifies it all? Quantum sleep makes sure the zero entropy system is favoring the anti inflammatory platform of quantum cell design and not the inflammatory way. When inflammation is present transition metals begin to collect in nervous tissue. They adsorb and absorb all kinds of EMF. This dehydrates you and destroys myelin..........and your DC current goes bye bye........if it lasts long enough your sleep goes too...........and you first get sick and then you die from some neolithic disease..........this is why all diseases are tied together.........EE5 and EE 6. **Endogenous retinoic acid mediates the early events in salamander limb regeneration**

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reversing-non-traditional-nerve-damage.8342/post-98835>

Can't believe I never saw this before...

**Participation of leptin in the determination of the macrophage phenotype: an additional role in adipocyte and macrophage crosstalk**

Macrophages exposed to leptin in culture expressed surface markers that were more similar to the M2 phenotype, but they were able to produce TNF-α, IL-6, IL-1β, IL-1ra, IL-10, MCP-1, and MIP-1α, as observed for M1 cells. Results suggest that leptin strongly contributes to the phenotype observed in macrophages found in adipose tissue.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23708919](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23708919)

# Thread: Autum - Am I the only one?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autum-am-i-the-only-one.8343/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2013-10-05T04:39:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autum-am-i-the-only-one.8343/post-96914>

As the days get shorter I am noticing a distinct change in my sleep. These days my blue-blockers get put on around 7:30 pm. By 8 I am yawning up a storm. My sleep has been very deep.

It isn't unusual for me to normally turn in for the night by 8:30-9:00 pm. Earlier this week I was in bed by 8! If this trend continues I'll be in hibernation stage in a coupke of weeks...

Anyone else noticing changes in the Northern hemisphere?

### caroline — 2013-10-05T04:59:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autum-am-i-the-only-one.8343/post-96915>

It sounds like you are doing exactly what you should be doing.......doesn't do a lot for your social life tho

### freesia — 2013-10-05T05:28:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autum-am-i-the-only-one.8343/post-96920>

Another Sthn Hemisphere person here answering your Q for Nthn peeps (we're cheeky like that!) but yep....six months ago I noticed the same thing.
I'd been using the blue blockers about 18 months by the start of autumn and when the lights went on inside at 6.30 pm my eyes and brain felt like they were burning. So the only thing to do was to put on the glasses.
I took up audiobook/podcast listening as I went into hibernation mode and never once got through a podcast or even a chapter each night before falling asleep.
Never mind a social life, I couldn't even get through a book!

### caroline — 2013-10-05T07:53:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autum-am-i-the-only-one.8343/post-96926>

So - Freesia......I was thinking about this today .....lately I want to go to bed at 7:30 and wake up really early. When the time changes tonite - it will be perfect.

### Inger — 2013-10-05T09:02:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autum-am-i-the-only-one.8343/post-96930>

Me too.. when it gets darker I want to go to bed earlier. It is not unusual I get tired at 8 PM. Actually I start to feel a little tired when it begins to get darker at 7 PM. I stayed up until 9:45 PM yesterday because I wanted to finish the new blog, but I really struggled! I had to get me a cup of fresh made elk bone broth to manage it. Yeah.. maybe not optimal because one should not get any calories before bed (I usually am very strict about this) but it does helped me to stay up a little longer! I slept until 7:30 AM today, lol. Nice

### caroline — 2013-10-05T09:16:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autum-am-i-the-only-one.8343/post-96933>

This is a supplementary sleep question.......There is a news show that I like to watch between 6:30 and 7:30. I invariably fall asleep about 7:15 and wake up around 30 minutes later. Why don't I just stay asleep? By the time I turn off the TV and make sure I have put things away in the kitchen and put my pyjamas on and brush my teeth - I am pretty awake. Now I put PJ's on and brush teeth etc. first - but the very act of moving from the sofa to bed is enough to disturb that drowsy feeling. There is a question in there somewhere ....LOL

When you start to get drowsy - should you just go to bed?

### Inger — 2013-10-05T09:47:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/autum-am-i-the-only-one.8343/post-96934>

EMF... (I always read on my darkened computer with f.lux and sunglasses on before going to sleep... makes me so tired) Maybe try to read something instead? That way you uses your brain in a different way... and maybe you get tired in a different way, being able to stay up a little later but then when you get tired you really need to sleep, so much that walking into your bedroom does not disturb the drowsiness. ### yewwei.tan — 2013-10-08T05:00:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-that-have-been-bugging-me.8345/post-97246>

Cold basically increases the DC current within cells (and hopefully we see increased current in our Schwann Cells). ie: Cold creates more usable energy for your body.

Cold adaptation is part of the process of becoming more energetically metastable (the mechanisms of which are described thoroughly in the CT series). If rT3 remains high during this period, it is due to latent leptin resistance and inflammation, which has nothing to do with cold.

Still, some people subjectively "react badly" to Cold Thermogenesis. Some reasons may be:

(a) a higher perceived level of stress due to the discomfort of being cold. When cold, you are in a more reduced state, your body has more energy, but that doesn't mean that it necessary "feels good", especially when you're just starting out

(b) toxin dump. Which is generally a good thing, though in some circumstances it may be undesirable (eg: when nursing a newborn)

(c) genetic. eg: you have a SNP in the [COMT gene](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catechol-O-methyl_transferase), and cold adaptation will be drastically slowed

(d) medications. eg: you are using beta blockers (beta 1,2,3 receptors are activated for the conversion of white fat to brown fat), and your adaptation is drastically slower.

(e) pre-existing illness. eg: you have hashimoto's (which also increases the likelihood that you're on offending medications), and your body doesn't have adequate levels of the right hormones to cold adapt

(f) other hormonal imbalances. eg: menopause, heavy antibiotic use, birth control pills, etc ... all of which can cause issues

All of these make withstanding the cold subjectively harder, but that doesn't mean that cold isn't already doing its thing and increasing your Schwann cell DC current.

Hope I got most of that right ....

### yewwei.tan — 2013-10-08T05:11:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-that-have-been-bugging-me.8345/post-97248>

Regarding calories, it's probably a complex topic.

On one hand, I'm pretty sure Jack has mentioned that when you are cold-adapted and leptin sensitive, your mitochondria have the ability to tap into uncoupling proteins to liberate calories as free heat. In theory, this means that the body can defend a specific level of fatness very well, since excess calories can be gotten rid off.

My personal experience has been that cold-adapting has allowed me to eat more (now in the 3000-3500kcal/day range, as a 6'2'' 187lbs male at 12-13% body fat. Fully ketogenic epi-paleo diet I should add).

I'm assuming that I'm cold adapted now, since body temperature rises a little after CT in sub 10C/50F water.

I should also note that I started my cold-adaptation only this June, and through the winter here in Australia. These are great conditions for cold-adaptation to take place. I'd be curious to see the effects as I approach summer time.

Maybe I started in an energy-deficient state, and after cold-adapting my body is now able to use more of the food that I consume to restore the optimal state of energy? I'm not sure, and will definitely continue to monitor my response.

Anyone have experience using a tanning bed for red light therapy? I came across this Mercola bed ([http://tanningbeds.mercola.com/](http://tanningbeds.mercola.com/)) - the Vitality D-Lite bed is the one I'm considering.

Just curious if anyone has any experience/opinions on this. I emailed Mercola and they said all of their beds have electronic ballasts so the EMF levels are much lower than those found in beds with magnetic ballasts, but how do we determine if the harm from laying in this thing is worth getting the benefit from the light?! I've been reading the blogs and trying to understand as much as I can. I get the basics, but not all the underlying science . . . YET - I will get it eventually though. I'm stubborn like that.

I do want to be sure this is the way to go though before I flop down $3k!

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-10-05T21:47:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-therapy-tanning-bed.8346/post-96960>

Regarding artificial photo-electric effect, Dr. K has stated a preference for red light over tanning beds. And regarding red light, his preference is red light up the nose over red light in a tanning bed.

About one month ago, I ordered this intranasal red light device ... [http://www.vielight.com/product/633-red/](http://www.vielight.com/product/633-red/)

Each session lasts 25 minutes. Recently I moved the timing of my sessions to the during last hour or two before bed time. My sleep has improved over the last month. Definitely can't give all the credit to the red light device ... it could be the change of seasons other people have mentioned. Either way, better sleep is definitely a win.

In summary, for red light therapy, I would recommend this device. I previously used red light in a salon ... after some time, I got busy and my visits became less frequent. The convenience of having a battery powered device at home is great. And I suspect, one AA battery emits way less EMF than red light in a tanning bed.

### Hope — 2013-11-02T00:44:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-therapy-tanning-bed.8346/post-99654>

> [RickD said:](/goto/post?id=99593)
>
> Hope,
>
> We feed them a raw food diet called Legacy made in Alberta. It comes as a meat patty that is 80-90% human grade with some veggies mixed in it. After losing our last two Vizslas to the same form of a supposed rare cancer I decided Kibble was part of the problem so we put the new pups on it immediately with great results. We also have two older dogs that I dragged back from the Middle East years ago when I was working there and with the diet shift they both started acting years younger. Needless to say I'm a total convert. And while I'm talking about the dogs on this forum I should also mention that one of the older mutts loves the Magnetico pad, I guess she can tell it's good for her.
>
> Our cat though is a entirely different story. 15 yrs old and down to three teeth refuses to eat anything but Fancy Feast.

That is so cool, RickD. Thanks for sharing. I watch a friend's doggie sometimes and trying to look for treats...and food I have started to. And whoa, I cannot believe the crap in most cans. Thankfully, he LOVES the sardines, cod and salmon leftovers I give him. He inhales it. So I will give him his own can of sardines sometimes

Oh, and at her house, in the mountains, the neighbor's dog (and hers too when I go out there), she is a border collie. She LOVES lying outside on the ground, loves the cold/cool ground, weather, will go into a nearby pool and creek in any weather. Whenever I see that, I think, what a smart puppy. Though the other one does too. I think when you let animals - like people - out in the wild, or give them free access to nature, they know what to do. Duh, of course, but it still amazes me. I have to see, I never thought I could learn SO much from a dear dog.

Anyway - thanks for this post on red light too...been thinking about it. I'm tempted with the brainy one, bc my brain..still pretty messed up especially now that I'm convinced my aMSH is low. We'll see.

### Inger — 2013-11-02T09:20:06+0200

My cat is just as old as your Rick and also has only 3 teeth..lol Years ago when I put her on a carnivore diet it was not easy! But I did not gave up. But she has an amazing, thick black and shining fur.. and is so happy and healthy, plays like a kid... lol (she just has a bit abdominal fat but I think it is because she is sterilized before we got her)

### Jude — 2013-11-05T01:52:43+0200

> [ATL\_Paleo said:](/goto/post?id=96960)
>
> Regarding artificial photo-electric effect, Dr. K has stated a preference for red light over tanning beds. And regarding red light, his preference is red light up the nose over red light in a tanning bed.
>
> About one month ago, I ordered this intranasal red light device ... [http://www.vielight.com/product/633-red/](http://www.vielight.com/product/633-red/)
>
> Each session lasts 25 minutes. Recently I moved the timing of my sessions to the during last hour or two before bed time. My sleep has improved over the last month. Definitely can't give all the credit to the red light device ... it could be the change of seasons other people have mentioned. Either way, better sleep is definitely a win.
>
> In summary, for red light therapy, I would recommend this device. I previously used red light in a salon ... after some time, I got busy and my visits became less frequent. The convenience of having a battery powered device at home is great. And I suspect, one AA battery emits way less EMF than red light in a tanning bed.

Did you start with short sessions and gradually increase the time?
Have you noticed any other benefits other than better sleep?
Thought this just might be the way to improve/lessen long standing tinnitus.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-11-05T02:09:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-therapy-tanning-bed.8346/post-99864>

I started w/ 25 minute sessions. The device has an auto shutoff at 25 minutes.

I have noticed other benefits, but since my n=1 has other variables, I can't attribute the benefits entirely to red light. Overall, my attitude has improved, somewhat better focus, and more mental energy. Life keeps getting better, and this device will continue to be one of the tools in my tool box.

I do have mild tinniitus, but so far no apparent improvement.

>
> I used my little device for the first time last evening and got a headache within about 2 minutes . . . anyone else experience this at first? Is it something you need to acclimate to?

BJ a few months ago on a ME/CFS forum people (most people who tried it) were running into problems using the vielight.
I settled for using it 3 mins every 3 days and had no problems but I recently got out of the habit.
I should start again and increase slowly.

I'm not sure how, but something was making people with ME worse...possibly before they might have turned a corner and made some improvement (??) it's hard to know. Most of the people trying it had moderate to severe ME/CFS and many like me had it for decades.

My advice would be to take it very slow and work up gradually. x

### bionaut — 2013-12-29T07:29:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-therapy-tanning-bed.8346/post-104386>

I should get some labwork done and see how these lights affect me. I own an e health nose light. I wanted a vielight for a while but the cost was prohibitive. This is surprising since it seems to simply be either a small laser or LED attached to a circuit board. The E health light shines at 650 NM and clears my nose when it is stuffy and I think very clearly after I use it. For less than a 50$ you can get a product like this that I would expect to be similar. However, the manual is translated from chinese and makes no sense... it is pretty funny actually. I don't know how they stack up against each other but I will say that this little device has a positive effect. BTW if you buy from China, expect to wait a while, shipping took about a month. I got mine off of ebay, not manafront but below is a link so you can see what I am talking about.

[http://www.manafont.com/product\_inf...eatment-for-nose-unconfort-relief-2aaa-p-7081](http://www.manafont.com/product_info.php/ehealth-superlight-therapeutic-apparatus-treatment-for-nose-unconfort-relief-2aaa-p-7081)

You can find an American marketed rhinitis stim called Bionase on amazon or ebay that seems to be the same thing. I haven't tried this one but it seems to be in the same vein.
[http://www.ebay.com/itm/BioNase-All...312?pt=LH\_DefaultDomain\_0&hash=item2585591ef8](http://www.ebay.com/itm/BioNase-Allergic-Rhinitis-Hay-Fever-Nasal-Light-Treatment-Device-Therapy-NEW-/161151000312?pt=LH_DefaultDomain_0&hash=item2585591ef8)

Also I own a red 660/880 LED matrix (I don't use those 2 wavelengths at the same time used at the same time) which I apply to my spine or stomach after icing to for an extra boost. I work a night shift for half the year, so I resort to some biohacks to help out. Gotta get mah photons! It has been a lifesaver.

[http://www.elixa.com/light/index.htm](http://www.elixa.com/light/index.htm)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-06T04:09:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-webinar.8347/post-96982>

it will........it is a biggie in importance.

Here is a clue..........PER 2 directly effects the cell cycle in mitosis in the cell cycle. Mitosis is the phase in the cell that occurs just before cell division to generate an offspring. The mammalian period 2 gene plays a key role in tumor growth in mice; mice with a mPER2 knockout show a significant increase in tumor development because of significant decrease in apoptosis. Here we can see the altered circadian signaling is tied directly to oncogenesis and epigenetic modifications made in response to environmental triggers. We also now know that expression of DNA is directly tied to methylation patterns and histone acetylation patterns in DNA/RNA also directly tied to abnormal circadian signaling in both systems in mammals. **This implies the analogue and digital systems you just learned about in circadian control are massively important in brain function**. This is why most of my protocols make a big deal about timing in many fashions.

This is all caused by mPER2 circadian de-regulation of common tumor suppression and cell cycle regulation genes, such as Cyclin D1, Cyclin A, Mdm-2, and Gadd45, as well as the transcription factor c-myc, which is directly controlled by circadian cycle regulators through E box-mediated reactions.

In cases like this, **we see large changes in the cortisol, melatonin, and Vitamin D cycles** in humans epithelial cancers. This complex quantum dance implies that sleep is tied directly into to cell cycle functioning, neurologic function and directly into cell mediated immunity at a very **fundamental level in neurogenesis** at a very early stage of development for all life forms. You learned about how the **neuro-immune system is linked to brain myelination** in Energy and Epigenetics 3. There is another deeper physiologic and anatomic tie of neurogenesis and the development of immunity in humans you will learn about as the series goes on. It appears from recent research that **sleep directly effects the chronic diseases of aging, and likely plays a role in cancer, autoimmune, and neuro-degeneration development at a very fundamental level.**

### caroline — 2013-10-07T03:34:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-webinar.8347/post-97117>

I was thinking more about sleep last nite - mostly because I couldn't - when will I learn to stay off my ipad......

When I get drowsy - I think my heart rate slows down and my body cools down and signifies that I am ready for sleep...so then I go to bed - but then I start getting hot.\[not for the right reasons btw\] and my heart rate is speeding up \[again - not for the right reasons\] It is like I am feeling an electrical charge thru my body. I have been feeling it in my head - but last nite I realised that I feel it slightly everywhere.

I just don't understand why I would become drowsy - and then loose it.

I changed things up yesterday - I stayed at the ocean until 7:30. When I came home I poured a small glass of wine and sat outside on my balcony for about an hour. I started to yawn - so I thought great - time for bed.
The same thing happened - within 10 minutes I was very hot and there was no hope of falling asleep. I finally got up and drank a cold glass of water and took a cold shower ....my body temp came down and seemed to stay down - but still, not a chance of sleep. I then got up and had some brandy - back to bed - still wide awake... bad night.

This worries me alot now - obviously bad things are happening - losing energy......

I need to work out a new game plan for today.....

The answer to this is easy yet complex but buried in the science of EE6. When you eat ketogenic you make more ATP.......36 vs 147 (glucose to fat) ATP function in a zero entropy quantum cell theory opens proteins structure up to expose more water binding sites in proteins.......the amount of ATP is stochastically linked to potassium concentration inside the cell. This is why potassium is inside the cell and sodium is not. It is a function of protein and ATP levels. So if you all of sudden eat ketotic, but you are dehydrated (high BUN/creat ratio) you can not fully take advantage of the energy surge.......see water is designed to bind to very specific parts of proteins (coo- groups)........potassium to other sites (beta and gamma carbonyl and imino groups). This make an "intracellular gelatin" that limits the rotational freedom of K and water. This is how a cell structures its water from bulk water to structured water to make a semiconductor. Structure water means to Ling that it lines up in an antiparallel fashion end to but and end to put. This puts the more electronegative side containing oxygen to the next two hydrogen atoms. Ling erred a bit because structured water can't be a dipole because dipole have neutral changes and we now know that the EZ has a net negative charge from Pollack's experiments. This allows water to become a superconductive cable in a cell to transfer energy and it allows water to gain a three dimensional form of a liquid crystalline structure of matter to perform quantum magic in tight spaces like microtubules or around mitochondria in the MINOS........so if you are dehydrated and then switch fuels you have only done step one correctly. Go back and look at the CT protocol closely........what does it say to due before CT? It says drink cold 32 oz of water before you begin!!! Why? what good is using cold to increase your current of flow if you dont have the water to make the superconducting cables to begin with? Got it?

ATP function is not its use of a high energy substrate as modern cell theory teaches.......ATP is designed to unfold proteins fully to open their carbonyl and imino side chain groups to intracellular water. to make them more hydrophilic. This allows water at the interface or surface to form an EZ that acts like a batter for light. ATP has zero to do with energy to live. (not totally accurate but the point is being made) ATP's main job in a cell is to opens proteins to bind water which transmits the electrons and photons and solitons you learned about in EE6. This is where the energy in a cell comes from. This is why people who go ketotic lose sleep efficiency because they are all dehydrated. If your proteins are missing electrons they are hydrophobic and they can't form an EZ. This is why your hormone labs are often useless. You need to know if the hormone is filled with electrons or lacking them to know what energy balance really is. And your observation of poor sleep with ketosis proves my idea that ATP is not a high energy anything.......if it was, ketosis should make you a zombie and in fact, it does not. Water is the key ingredient for sleep......and the Ocotober webinar is going to simply stun you to that reality.........in 3 D........your jaw will be left open.

Once the first water layer is laid down at a surface in this fashion other water layers layer on top more EZ thickness can be made. When water is not in EZ form the hydrogen bonding network effects its structure because water is a natural dipole, and dipole's within the Earth's magnetic field act differently than they do in a cell. Tap water and cell water are not equal players. Non native EMF affects this step in 2 ways, it dehydrates your cells, and it alters the hydrogen bonding arrangement due to the fact that water is a small magnet. When water layers around a protein it can becomes a reverse micelle and self organize or be fortified by a large EZ to exclude protons. these things alternate between day and night. This is why DNA and RNA only code for protein in all life forms and why it is highly conserved from simple life to complex life....... the key nucleation point (origin point) for this water crystal is built into the carbonyl and imino side chains of the proteins coded for. Water is a primordial thing found on Earth therefore it did not need to be coded for on DNA. It was used by DNA because it is the perfect magnetic dipole that is capable of allowing from proton tunneling that enzymes need to work. The reason potassium is critical and not Magnesium or sodium inside a cell ties to beta and gamma carboxyl groups also in proteins where potassium binds too. This bond allows K+ to ADSORB to this site and donate electrons to the water gel. Each molecule of ATP in a quantum cell controls 8800 water molecules and 20 potassium ions to make this liquid crystalline semiconductor inside every cell of your body. Potassium acts like the glue to keep your back bone intracellular semiconducting plates together. This is why when any life form dies.......potassium and water are released from a cell and salt linkage form between muscles because no more energy is present within the water supercables. Notice this is where Magnesium is bound up in salt linkages and shows you why it is not a major cation in energy transfers......it must have water to act. Mg2+ most important role is to make ATP in the mitochondria as a co factor........but as I said earlier.......without water........ATP or Magnesium are worthless and this is why one can still sleep like shit when you are in deep ketosis. This is also why people who are energy deficient, obese or diabetics have low potassium, high BUN/creat ratios and urinate a lot of water.......with their magnesium in it for the ride..............When you understand QED all the diseases make sense. When you eat ketogenic you make more ATP.......36 vs 147 (glucose to fat) ATP function in a zero entropy quantum cell theory opens proteins structure up to expose more water binding sites in proteins.......the amount of ATP is stochastically linked to potassium concentration inside the cell. This is why potassium is inside the cell and sodium is not. It is a function of protein and ATP levels. So if you all of sudden eat ketotic, but you are dehydrated (high BUN/creat ratio) you can not fully take advantage of the energy surge.......see water is designed to bind to very specific parts of proteins (coo- groups)........potassium to other sites (beta and gamma carbonyl and imino groups). This make an "intracellular gelatin" that limits the rotational freedom of K and water. This is how a cell structures its water from bulk water to structured water to make a semiconductor. Structure water means it lines up in an antiparallel fashion end to but and end to put. This puts the more electronegative side containing oxygen to the next two hydrogen atoms. In this fashion it creates the reverse water micelles I spoke about in EE4, 5, and 6. This allow water to become a superconductive cable in a cell to transfer energy and it allows water to gain a three dimensional for of a liquid crystalline structure to perform quantum magic in tight spaces........so if you are dehydrated and then switch fuels you have only done step one correctly. Go back and look at the CT protocol closely........what does it say to due before CT? drink cold 32 oz of water before you begin. Why? what good is using cold to increase your current of flow if you dont have the water to make the superconducting cables to begin with? Got it?
>
> ATP function is not its use of a high energy substrate as modern cell theory teaches.......ATP is designed to unfold proteins fully to open their carbonyl and imino side chain groups to intracellular water. This gives you alternating positive and negative poles. These poles ADSORB water and polarize and orient the water correctly for super conduction to take place. ATP has zero to do with energy to live. It opens proteins to bind water which transmits the electrons and photons and solitons you learned about in EE6. This is where the energy in a cell comes from. This is why people who go ketotic lose sleep efficiency because they are all dehydrated. And your observation of poor sleep with ketosis proves my idea that ATP is not a high energy anything.......if it was, ketosis should make you a zombie and in fact, it does not. Water is the key ingredient for sleep......and the Ocotober webianr is going to simply stun you to that reality.........in 3 D........your jaw will be left open.
>
> Once the first water layer is laid down in this fashion other water layers layer on top of the first due to hydrogen bonding network effect of it being a water dipole in the Earth's magnetic field. non native EMF affects this step in 2 ways, it dehydrates you from water and it alters the hydrogen bonding arrangement due to the fact that water is a small magnet. When water layers around a protein it become a reverse micelle and self assembles. This is why DNA and RNA only code for protein in all life forms and why it is highly conserved from simple life to complex life....... the key nucleation point (origin point) for this water crystal is built into the carbonyl and imino side chains of the proteins coded for. Water is a primordial thing found on Earth therefore it did not need to be coded for on DNA. It was used by DNA because it is the perfect magnetic dipole. The reason potassium is critical and not Magnesium or sodium inside a cell ties to beta and gamma carboxyl groups also in proteins where potassium binds too. This bond allows K+ to ADSORB to this site and donate electrons to the water gel. Each molecule of ATP in a quantum cell controls 8800 water molecules and 20 potassium ions to make this liquid crystalline semiconductor inside every cell of your body. Potassium acts like the glue to keep your back bone intracellular semiconducting plates together. This is why when any life form dies.......potassium and water are released from a cell and salt linkage form between muscles because no more energy is present within the water supercables. Notice this is where Magnesium is bound up in salt linkages and shows you why it is not a major cation in energy transfers......it must have water to act. Mg most important role is to make ATP in the mitochondria as a co factor........but as I said earlier.......without water........ATP or Magnesium are worthless and this is why one can still sleep like shit when you are in deep ketosis. This is also why people who are energy deficient, obese or diabetic have low potassium, high BUN/creat ratios and urinate a lot of water.......with their magnesium in it for the ride..............When you understand QED all the diseases make sense. DETAILS MATTER IN SEMICONDUCTION.

### freesia — 2013-10-09T01:29:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-webinar.8347/post-97316>

Funny you should ask about sleep because I think I'm adusting to the season shift and last week I woke in the night a couple of times! But this is unusual. It used to be a regular thing.

The best ways I can describe my sleep improvements in the past two years is:
\*I have no trouble _ever_ with falling asleep.
\*If I do wake in the night I'm not crushed the next day. By crushed I mean feeling like I'm physically and mentally pushing through concrete, with carbs suddenly and seductively appearing on the radar as a quick-fix "solution".
\*Sleep does actually feel restorative. _Antioxidative_ and _cooling_ is how best to describe how night time feels. I wake at sunrise feeling good. Magic.

Iced water and CT - more like 16 oz works for me, or 2 big glasses. I sip a glass while I'm in the bath...I clink the ice cubes and imagine I'm at a resort. ### Inger — 2013-10-09T07:18:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-webinar.8347/post-97322>

Wow, I slept so well tonight. I really have learned paleo treats are not good for me!

Yesterday I had zero nuts or carbs out of season, just cucumber and dirty carrots from own garden, wild lingonberries, stinging nettles and mint from the garden as a smoothie with water, seaweed smoothie, raw reindeer, raw lambs liver and meat from own lambs, organic Norwegian salmon, raw. For added fat 1 raw eggyolk, EVOO and coconut oil. Finished dinner by 5 PM. Took care to drink lots of own well water that had been out in the rain in glass jugs for hours. Had celtic sea salt in quite many glasses of it too.... because I craved it.
Huge difference in sleep to the night before, just amazing! I grounded as much as the day before, and did as much CT too (2 short swims in the river) so it was only food, the timing of my last meal and water intake that was different.

Now I really get it clearly. Inflammation -> dehydration-> bad sleep.......

Maybe to get healthy really is that easy

DH always had amazing sleep abilities... and he is very healthy too. He is an animal..lol I now understand why!

# Thread: Anyone familiar with DISH or osteoarthritis with calcification/bone spurs?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-familiar-with-dish-or-osteoarthritis-with-calcification-bone-spurs.8349/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-10-06T05:10:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-familiar-with-dish-or-osteoarthritis-with-calcification-bone-spurs.8349/post-96985>

I feel like anything I know about osteoarthritis is preventative....I am out of my league here.

My mother in law is the throes of this stuff diagnosed with DISH Diffuse idiopathic skeletal hyperostosis (DISH) , also known as Forestier-Rotes-Querol disease, and I want to help her but don't even know where to start and/or if it is too late.

I would really appreciate any knowledge y'all could share on this subject as there is very little on the internet aside from pain killers and surgery.

I just can't tell a woman in pain to go to the lengths and expense we all go to if it is too late to benefit her.

Thanks so much.

### nonchalant — 2013-10-06T20:09:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-familiar-with-dish-or-osteoarthritis-with-calcification-bone-spurs.8349/post-97064>

Perhaps you can do something small to get your foot in the door?
The first thing I think of when calcium is not where it belongs is Vitamin K2. The good stuff, MK-4 menatetrenone is expensive, though.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-06T20:21:56+0300

# Thread: I have been sick for over 11 years.  Need help.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-been-sick-for-over-11-years-need-help.8350/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 41
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Red20 — 2013-10-06T06:39:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-been-sick-for-over-11-years-need-help.8350/post-96990>

I have been chronically sick for over 11 years.

I used to be an elite athlete until I came down with a flu-like illness and have never been the same. I have been diagnosed with Osteoporosis, Hypercoagulation, Chronic Fatigue, and Fibromyalgia. I am now a 41 year old man who spends the majority of my day in bed, because I am tired and in pain.

I can't walk more than 2 blocks before my legs feel like they have filled with lactic acid and become stiff and sore. Same thing happens to my arms if I try to exert with them. My sacram is killing me. I am at the end of my rope.

Earlier this week I found out that I am low in Progesterone, free Testosterone, DHEA, and IGF-1. And my Sex Hormone Binding Globulin is abnormally HIGH.

2 years before I got sick I became addicted to ephedrine and took 6 Xenadrine every morning before working out. I also took 6 more Xenadrine in the afternoon before I worked out again. I loved the energy boost it gave me.

I have terrible brain fog and have problems with absorbing information. I have been dunking my face in ice cold water for about a minute a day for the past 2 weeks, but I really don't know what I am doing.

Can someone please give me a game plan to regain my health?

### Red20 — 2013-10-06T06:40:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-been-sick-for-over-11-years-need-help.8350/post-96991>

Here are some of my blood work and saliva results.

DHEA: 130.1pg/dL \*\*LOW\*\* Range: 139.7-484.4

T4 Free: .97 ng/dL \*\*LOW Normal\*\* Range: .82-1.77 ng/dL

TSH: 1.660 uIU/mL Range: .450-4.500 uIU/mL

Thyroxine(T4) 7.9 ug/dL Range 4.5-12.0 ug/dL

Free Thyroxine Index 2.1 Range 1.2-4.9

Thyroid Peroxidase (TPO): <6IU/mL Range 0-34 IU/mL

Triiodothyronine, Free Serum 2.9 pg/mL \*\*LOW Normal\*\* Range: 2.0-4.4 pg/mL

T3 Uptake: 26% \*\*LOW Normal\*\* Range: 24-39%

Total Testosterone: 599 ng/dL Range: 250-1,100 ng/dL

Free Testosterone: 40.5 pg/mL \*\*LOW Normal\*\* Range: 35-155 pg/mL

Homocysteine: 13.6 pmol/L Range: 5.5-16.2<(Dr said goal should be less than 7.)

IGF-1: 103 ng/mL \*\*LOW\*\* Range: 106-277 ng/mL

Progesterone: <0.5 ng/mL \*\*LOW\*\* Range: Follicular: <1.4, Luteal: 3.3-26.0, Mid Luteal: 4.4-28.0.

Cortisol
6:00-8:00 am: 8nM \*\*LOW\*\* Range: 13-24 nM
11:00-1:00 pm: 6nM Range: 5-10 nM
4:00-5:00 pm: 4nM Range: 3-8 nM
10:00-12:00 am: 4nM Range: 1-4 nM

Cortisol Load: 22 nM \*\*LOW\*\* Range: 23-42 nM

Estradiol: 20 pg/mL Range: 11-44 pg/mL

Sex Hormone Binding Globulin: 83 \*\*HIGH\*\* Range: 10-57

HDL Cholesterol: 68 mg/dL\*\*HIGH\*\* Range: 40-60mg/dL

BUN: 3 mg/dL \*\*LOW\*\* Range: 7-18 mg/dL

### caroline — 2013-10-06T07:24:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-been-sick-for-over-11-years-need-help.8350/post-96994>

Hi Red ....and welcome! Have you gone to the home page and read the easy start guides? Please do this if you haven't already.

There are plenty of people here turning their life around ......it certainly can be done if you are 100% committed and all in.

Please start a journal so we can follow along and help out if you have questions etc. Dr. Kruse likes journals so he can get to know you better and help out when he can.
Go to the top of the page and on the left hand side click on forum and then scroll down to the journal section.

Tell us about yourself....where you live etc. That is important to figure out your exposure to power lines etc

C/T will be critically important for you

Follow an Epi paleo diet as outlined

What is your EMF exposure at work and at home. - super critical for you - you need to reduce it as much as possible as soon as possible. Get rid of your clock radio and tv etc where you sleep and cut the power to that room if you can.

Spend as much time outside grounding with bare feet and as little clothes as possible - without scaring the neighbours!
Do you live near a river, lake, ocean ....if you do get in it as often as possible

Drink only R/O water

Follow circadian light cycles.....no fake light and wear blue blockers when using tech stuff

You need to eat lots and lots

of good fat.....GF beef, GF butter, coconut oil, Ghee......

I know this sounds daunting ....but it really is pretty easy once you get going!

Do you have Dr. kruse's book ....it will give you a great and easy overview of all of this and there is a great section on labs.

Listen to Dr. K's podcasts - you get a ton of information quickly and he is easy to listen too.

Consider becoming a gold member ....you will then have access to the whole library of webinars and the live Q&A that comes a few days later. You certainly don't have to - we will support you what ever way we can.

Please re post your labs in the lab section ....there are people here that are great at reading labs - but they might not see them here.

Please keep reading the blogs and the comments ....there is gold in the comments . Dr. K. Answers all the questions there.

Keep reading and asking questions..... We have all been there.....

You need to ask questions or Dr K. Doesn't know what we aren't understanding.

Make no mistake ....there is a lot to get thru .....but this is your amazing journey to optimal.....we aren't going to get a do over.

Follow other's journals too. ..and maybe you will have advice for them from your life experiences....we are helping each other here......

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-06T16:22:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-been-sick-for-over-11-years-need-help.8350/post-97030>

Laptops do give off EMF's but you can ground your laptop. There is a thread on here showing you how to do it.

1\. Start with the Easy Start guide on the site.
2\. You must begin to the Leptin RX and in your case with your labs you must begin the process of cold Thermogenesis protocol.
3\. Third thing is to limit all blue light in your life after the sun sets
4\. For fuel you must increase the amount of seafood you eat to increase the amount of DHA in your cell membranes. Read Brain Gut 6 blog post on the site.
5\. Drink a lot of reverse osmosis water or spring water.
6\. Avoid a lot or exercise until your labs improve.
7\. You must begin to walk outside or sit outside without your shoes firmly planted in the ground.
8\. When the sun is out your skin and your eyes need to be out in it as well.
9\. With your labs you will need a lot of patience.
10\. Finding an anti aging doc to help augment your labs would be helpful for your reversal if you can find it or afford it.
11\. I think a magnetico at some point would help you especially if you spend a ton of time in a bed.
12\. Ask lots of questions and the forum and read lots of blogs. When we direct you to the blogs make sure your read the hyperlinks.
13\. Start by reading the easy start guide, Hormone 101 blog, how the Leptin Rx works blog. Brain gut 11, CT 4, CT 6. Energy and epigenetics 4.
14\. Never give up or settle for illness.
15\. Embrace the panic and chaos of illness, because of its mere presence, you inspire your own creativity to find your pathway back to health and subsequently soothe your mind. People tend to buy only the beliefs they have had seared in their head over life, not the benefits of seeing life adapt before their eyes. Change is a hard barrier for humans to break. It require fluidity of thought and that requires both a physical and mental motion to be used to get on in the environment. The new relationship helps you learn new ways of thinking about your situation and your life. Ultimately, you look at the world in a way that would have been so foreign to you that it wouldn’t have made any sense before you changed. I practice thinking this way and it opens doors to my brain that were previously chained shut.

### Martin — 2013-10-06T18:01:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-been-sick-for-over-11-years-need-help.8350/post-97047>

Red20, dude, are we related?

Your labs and most of your story are very close to mine (except for the elite athlete part!). I started out in life with CF and Fibro. I've made some progress from using the data here, not to mention the support from some damn good people.

Your IGF-1 is hanging one foot in the crapper. DHEA, too. I know 'cuz mine are down there, too. Do you have a BUN/Creatine ratio yet? That's a biggie - hydration. Post it if you can.

Lots to assimilate here, but the big thing is to start living/thinking like a paleolithic polar bear who thinks he's a Great White shark.

The Big Headed Guy

### freesia — 2013-10-06T22:58:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-been-sick-for-over-11-years-need-help.8350/post-97078>

Stellar advice nicely laid out from Dr K back there Red.
I have ME/CFS and I have benefited greatly from the CT protocol (adopted slowly, and applied consistently), cutting blue light exposure after sunset and waking at sunrise, getting early sunlight in the eyes and eating a big breakfast.
I started the Leptin reset protocol and CT coming into Autumn last year (April in my part of the world) and haven't looked back.

I hear you on the brain fog and the overwhelm. Be kind to yourself, don't be afraid to ask for lotsa help. And take CT slow.
I'm glad Martin found you, he's the poster boy here for making solid headway  with complex chronic illness....thats why he gets to have his pic so big.

### sooperb — 2013-11-03T14:09:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-been-sick-for-over-11-years-need-help.8350/post-99712>

I guess. It would be a radical change for most people and like extreme or foolish diets, they enthuse, make a start, realise that the "ask" is a big one and can't meet it. This is a very different approach and the ask is not just to change yourself but to understand that if you do then you won't "fit" the world like you used to. Peer pressure is an enormous hurdle, especially when you are up against those who aren't on the same road and not only that but because of their lack of understanding or agreement that it's a good route to follow, actively or passively oppose you.

### caroline — 2013-11-04T00:12:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-been-sick-for-over-11-years-need-help.8350/post-99747>

I guess that is why we all enjoy "flying our freak flag" here ....LOL

Oddly tho ......I never found it hard at all .... it just made so much sense - after years and years of looking for answers. I was always buying diet and health books and listening to anything and everything!

This is just lots of seafood and some ice and rise and set with the sun .....what our forefathers did naturally. And of course, kick the non native EMF to the curb!

### sooperb — 2013-11-11T13:49:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-been-sick-for-over-11-years-need-help.8350/post-100413>

I think there are many reasons that people don't change themselves for the better, background, intellect, lifestyle and lack of interest, peer groups - if you're all fat or overweight you feel reassured that you aren't different, if your peer group are skinnies then you're more likely to diet to fit in, if that's your over-riding desire. Sometimes you have the information but don't use it, procrastination for example and making excuses that you haven't the time, the money perhaps because you don't think you can stick at it or there's always the danger that you may isolate yourself. It's way more complex than just eating the wrong foods in my opinion.

### Red20 — 2014-08-09T16:34:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-been-sick-for-over-11-years-need-help.8350/post-137380>

I did get overwhelmed and didn't continue last year. I worked with a Naturopathic Doctor for awhile. He had me go on a raw vegan diet, take iodine and take glandulars & herbs for my adrenals, hypothalamus, pituitary, thyroid, and pineal gland. He also said I needed to detox so I did a 40 day water fast. The water fast almost killed me.

I am still overwhelmed with Dr Jack's information, but I am going to give it my best effort with the Epi-Paleo RX. I am on day #2.

Yesterday I did 30 minutes in a cold bath tub with no ice. For breakfast I ate oysters, 3 eggs, coconut oil, fish oil, digestive enzymes, probiotics, Super Food Powder and Kimchi. I wasn't hungry for lunch. For supper I had 6 oz of grass fed beef, 3 eggs, 3 oz of oysters, digestive enzymes, fish oil, probiotics, wheatgrass powder, romaine lettuce and Kimchi.

For this morning's breakfast I had 2 eggs, 6 oz of red salmon with the bones, digestive enzymes, coconut oil, fish oil, probiotics, Super Food Powder and Kimchi.

The Super Food Powder is made by Dragon Herbs and it's called "Tonic Alchemy." It contains high amounts of Vitamin K, Iodine, Vitamin C, and B12.
[http://www.amazon.com/Tonic-Alchemy...&qid=1407590413&sr=8-1&keywords=tonic+alchemy](http://www.amazon.com/Tonic-Alchemy-Dragon-Herbs-Powder/dp/B003B6UL5Y/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1407590413&sr=8-1&keywords=tonic+alchemy)

Even though I didn't continue with Dr Jack's protocol last year I never gave up fighting for my health. The 40 day water fast was one of the toughest things I have ever gone through. I would rather go through Army Basic Training again than do another water fast. lol

Thanks for wondering about me Caroline and sooperb.

Hey Hope,

I served in the Army National Guard and an M.D. believed the vaccinations/inoculations I received are contributing to my health problems. I have been diagnosed with EBV, CPN and HHV-6. In 2010 I had a D.O. put me on 3 different antibiotics and an anti-viral for 14 months. My Hypercoagulation levels were so high she also put me on Heparin. She said it was a miracle I hadn't died from a vein thrombosis. All the drugs made me worse.

Thanks for the link. I will read it.

I have not kept on Dr Jack's blogs. Even though I bought the book last year I got overwhelmed and didn't touch it again until this week. I signed up to become a paying member so I can access the archives.

I drink R/O water and then restructure it and store it in a glass jar with crystals on the bottom.

I am trying to follow the leptin reset. I started doing CT and am eating high fat/protein with low carb meals. I eat within 30 minutes on rising. I am not eating after 6:00 pm and I do not snack.

I purchased a whole house EMF shield to protect from EMF. I also go outside to get sun and put my feet in the grass to ground to the earth.

I don't know anything about blue light or the blue blockers yet. This is really daunting information to try to absorb. With brain fog it makes it that much harder. I easily forget things and have to continually re-read information I have learned in the past. My back pain also makes it hard to focus for very long. Very frustrating but I won't give up fighting.

I think it's easy to feel overwhelmed when you don't feel well, have brain fog etc. I want to encourage you to focus on doing what you can each day..not at the mountain of work and changes ahead. As they say, the journey of a thousand miles begins with the 1st step. That's better than getting overwhelmed and quitting, IMHO  It would be great if you had a partner who could do some of the work for you, like getting the tools. Though getting blue blockers is super easy....

A super short and easy website is [https://www.lowbluelights.com/index.asp?](https://www.lowbluelights.com/index.asp?) though of course you can get what you need on amazon etc. cheaper.....all the best!

### caroline — 2014-08-10T02:06:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-been-sick-for-over-11-years-need-help.8350/post-137424>

Just take it slow Red ....baby steps. We are all here to help.

I would suggest that you read Dr. K's book first ......it is a fast read and really is a great overview.

You have made a great start!

Blue blockers are huge ...go to amazon .... they are likely around $10.00

You need to reset the rolex in your brain.

that is what the leptin reset is all about too. this is what your day should look like....

upon rising ...go outside and look at the sun ...barefoot and little clothing

Have a BAB within 30 minutes of rising - leave at least 4 hours between meals and before bed

drink R/O water

get lots of sunshine and grounding ..hug a tree, walk barefoot, laugh for no reason, hug, love, sing, dance!

get all electronics out of your bedroom.... so no clock radio,TV, iphone, ipad etc. Can you turn of the breaker to your bedroom?

please ask questions if you are unsure of something ...we have all been there!

Are you a "gold" member? the monthly live Q&A with Dr. Kruse are gold - you can ask him anything.

Good to see you here, Red. I did wonder what happened to ya. You should strive for a B/Cr ratio of 7-1, below 10 and you're getting there, and above 15 you really have non-native EMF (magnetic and electrical fields from devices, artificial light) issues big time. Excessive blue light in the form of ANY artificial light is knocking down your DHA and raising cellular inflammation. It can come from computers, TV, interior lighting, even during the day. Get some yellow light bulbs and use them after dark if you have to see. It's revealing to measure your EMF fields with good meters to really see where you can improve your "conditions of existence". Got a computer? Get f.lux, an ap that can turn down the blue part of your monitor. Wear the aforementioned glasses, but you know that.

The information here is complex, but many of the take-aways are much easier to grasp and implement. Live like your great great grandfather in every way you can. Don't stress if you can't always, just endeavor to get in as much as you can.

Eleven years. Ugh. But this is the place. It's based on observations of Nature, and a few modern tweaks we humans have learned along the evolutionary path.

NSH

# Thread: Eternally tired newbie
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eternally-tired-newbie.8351/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SleepyK — 2013-10-06T09:56:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eternally-tired-newbie.8351/post-97004>

Hi there, I was diagnosed with a severe case of Narcolepsy in 1992 having had symptoms for 5 years. I am almost 33 now and have been on bin loads of meds for 20 years.
I found this site whilst researching about narcolepsy and metabolism. Finding some of the stuff on here very interesting and am definitely going to look in to adjusting my diet.

Since the discovery of Orexin, or lack of it in the Narcoleptic brain, I have followed it's research and longed for the day when Orexin is available as a treatment for Narcolepsy, however
I could be 40 by the time the FDA approves it, the pharmaceutical companies just don't think it is worth investing in, despite the potential for it also being a treatment for weight loss.
There is a hokey looking company selling an 'Orexin dietary supplement' but despite their awesome claims, there is no evidence that it is the real deal and I can only find negative feedback
from a few Narcoleptics that have tried and reviewed it. I am interested in the links between Gluten and Narcolepsy and Leptin resistance.

I will try to keep you posted about my findings and if I make any progress, however, the eternal tiredness means I am not always the best at keeping up with posting things.
My aim is not only to help my tiredness but also to reduce my BMI. For many years I have been convinced that there is a link between narcolepsy and metabolism, long before
I began to find articles on the subject, having read a great deal on the subject now it seems I was not at all wrong.
Anyway ... nice to meet you

Please keep reading and asking questions.....

# Thread: Sick for over 11 years.  Told to post my labs here.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-for-over-11-years-told-to-post-my-labs-here.8355/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Red20 — 2013-10-06T16:12:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-for-over-11-years-told-to-post-my-labs-here.8355/post-97028>

I have been chronically sick for over 11 years. I used to be an elite athlete until I came down with a flu-like illness and have never been the same.

I used to be dependent on Ephedrine. I took Ephedrine every morning and afternoon for about 14 years, which I'm sure caused adrenal exhaustion.

I have been taking an adrenal glandular for 8 months and that has brought my blood pressure up from 95/60 to 117/70. However, I still don't have any energy and wake up tired. I also have a lot of muscle pain and spend the majority of the day in bed. I am a 41 year old male and have been diagnosed with Hypercoagulation, Osteoporosis, Chronic Fatigue, and Fibromyalgia.

I can't walk more than 2 blocks before my legs become stiff and sore like they have filled up with lactic acid. Same thing happens to my arms when I try to do any exertion with them.

Below are some of my blood work and saliva results. If you need more information please ask. Thanks!

DHEA: 130.1pg/dL \*\*LOW\*\* Range: 139.7-484.4

T4 Free: .97 ng/dL \*\*LOW Normal\*\* Range: .82-1.77 ng/dL

TSH: 1.660 uIU/mL Range: .450-4.500 uIU/mL

Thyroxine(T4) 7.9 ug/dL Range 4.5-12.0 ug/dL

Free Thyroxine Index 2.1 Range 1.2-4.9

Thyroid Peroxidase (TPO): <6IU/mL Range 0-34 IU/mL

Triiodothyronine, Free Serum 2.9 pg/mL \*\*LOW Normal\*\* Range: 2.0-4.4 pg/mL

T3 Uptake: 26% \*\*LOW Normal\*\* Range: 24-39%

Total Testosterone: 599 ng/dL Range: 250-1,100 ng/dL

Free Testosterone: 40.5 pg/mL \*\*LOW Normal\*\* Range: 35-155 pg/mL

Homocysteine: 13.6 pmol/L Range: 5.5-16.2<(Dr said goal should be less than 7.)

IGF-1: 103 ng/mL \*\*LOW\*\* Range: 106-277 ng/mL

Progesterone: <0.5 ng/mL \*\*LOW\*\* Range: Follicular: <1.4, Luteal: 3.3-26.0, Mid Luteal: 4.4-28.0.

Cortisol
6:00-8:00 am: 8nM \*\*LOW\*\* Range: 13-24 nM
11:00-1:00 pm: 6nM Range: 5-10 nM
4:00-5:00 pm: 4nM Range: 3-8 nM
10:00-12:00 am: 4nM Range: 1-4 nM

Cortisol Load: 22 nM \*\*LOW\*\* Range: 23-42 nM

Estradiol: 20 pg/mL Range: 11-44 pg/mL

Sex Hormone Binding Globulin: 83 \*\*HIGH\*\* Range: 10-57

HDL Cholesterol: 68 mg/dL\*\*HIGH\*\* Range: 40-60mg/dL

BUN: 3 mg/dL \*\*LOW\*\* Range: 7-18 mg/dL

### Red20 — 2013-10-06T19:02:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-for-over-11-years-told-to-post-my-labs-here.8355/post-97058>

Creatinine: 0.8 mg/dL Range: 0.6-1.2

Bun/Creatinine Ratio: 5.3

### ThatRonSwanson — 2013-10-16T07:26:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-for-over-11-years-told-to-post-my-labs-here.8355/post-97969>

I know for the cortisol level you might want to look into supporting your adrenals in the morning and lowering cortisol at night. I would suggest in the morning taking iodine, Ubiquinol with Pqq, Vitamin B5, and Vitamin C to support your adrenals. With dinner I would take Fish Oil (Nordic Naturals Cod Liver Oil at least 4,000 MG) and Seriphos 1-2 Capsules. You also need to eat within an hour of waking up. Also drink 1/4 to 1/2 tsp. of salt in the morning mixed with water upon waking. Finally, wear your blue light blocking glasses starting at 8 p.m. and limit RF pollution at night if all possible. Get as much sleep as you possibly can and go to bed before 10 p.m.

Your Sex Hormone Binding Globulin is high because your thyroid is having issues this is also binding up your free testosterone.
