# Thread: James' Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/james-journal.8356/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JamesC — 2013-10-06T17:17:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/james-journal.8356/post-97039>

Hi everyone! I have been reading Jack Kruse's work for a long time now and have not been following his recommendations as much as I would have liked too. Part of it was the fact that I was at school, but really I don't have any legitimate excuses. Im starting this journal in hopes that I can use it as a way to reflect on my day and keep on track, and hopefully get some support along the way. I am really just interested in the extent of human potential and ready to take the deep dive!

I little about myself before I get start. I am 6 foot 3 and about 180 lbs, with a good amount of muscle and very low body fat. Weight loss is not the reason I am doing this, and I would rather pack on the muscle with minimal time commitment. I just graduated from university and will finally be starting a career in my field on October 14th! . I have been stressing almost the entire 6 months looking for work and it now feels like I am starting to get my life together and organized. This is the other reason I would like to follow the principals laid out on this site, I would like to be successful in my career and not have any brain fog, which has been an issue of mine for a long time. I also used to be an elite cyclist, giving it up this year because a big hip misalignment issue that is still a problem. I hope to maybe try racing again next year but don't want to train for it the way I used to. Training took up way to much of my life and made me miss out on too many life experiences. I did try deep keto and CT a while back because of Jack and Ben Greenfield but didn't take the time to adapt or wouldn't reduce my training and cause my self some really bad adrenal fatigue for the second time in my life.

The things I am going to focus on for now are light, CT, and food. I know EMF is a huge problem and I will work that into my life gradually, especially because I will be working near the airport and there are about 9 cell towers within a 500m radius and air plains flying over my building all day... I also still live at home and have a hard time convincing my parents to hardwire the house and get rid of wifi. I currently have a wifi antenna outside my room but turn it off when ever I can and at night when I sleep.

Light exposure in minimal during the summer but harder during the winter because my parents tend to keep the lights on longer than I would like and the upstairs where my room is is open to downstairs. I also don't use my computer much at night But I will be getting blue blocker glasses soon to help when I do.

I am going to exercise based on jack Kruse's prescription. I have tested my VO2 max multiple times for cycling and the highest Ive recorded is 76.4 ml/kg/min so I am excited to see if I can improve this with minimal training and lots of CT. I started my exercise protocol yesterday which is will HIIT 3 times per week. Initially I am starting with 6 intervals up a steep narrow trail in a provincial park near my house, with 30 seconds of rest in between. I will increase the duration when I feel I can handle it. The other part of my training will be weights 2-3 times per week. It will be a mix of power lifting and some general weight lifting but focused on explosive movements.

For CT I am going to be rather aggressive. I have been taking cold showers for over a year now and can handle them no problem, even now that its getting colder. I was working landscaping this summer and recently on some colder days (5 degrees) I wore nothing but a t-shirt all day. I will try to do CT 3-5 times a week. Every time I do the sprints in the park I will also go the the river and swim around a bit. I did this yesterday and lasted 5 minutes before my skin started to burn, I never went white though. On top of using the river I will sit in ice in my tub 1-2 times per week for as long as I can. Today will be the first time and Im exited to see how long I can last!

As for my diet, I am getting closer to Keto every week. The days keep getting shorter and shorter and ive been adding in a lot more fish in the way of Sardines. I will share my favorite sardine recipe a bit later. I still eat a few apples and some potatoes every once and a while because they are in season. Almost all my produce comes from a local farmer right beside my house or has a small organic operation. Im very lucky to have worked with him and created a good relationship. He is even going to teach me how to slaughter chickens some time in the upcoming weeks to prepare me for when I start bow hunting this winter.

But enough background, here is my first official post and hopefully the start of something great.

So yesterday was the start of my adventure. I decided to start with an HIIT workout and CT session in the river. I choose to do this all in the park near my house because one section is in a valley with lots of trees and a river which 50% of gets no cell phone reception, while the rest gets 1 bar and no data. It's really my escape everything going on and I find it easy to reset my mind and focus. I chose a narrow trail going strait up one side of the valley that is extremely steep and narrow to do my sprints. I run in Vibrams and have worked hard on a proper bare foot strike, all though I think I am going to switch to complete barefoot a few weeks for more grounding. I was able to do 5 out of the 6 intervals today before I started to taste a little blood in my throat which is when I decided to stop. Over all it felt great and I'm looking forward to the next session. I then walked 20 minutes down the hill and got some sardines, butter and coconut oil in me before jumping in the river. I forgot my thermometer but the water must have been 55 deg f or lower but I had no problem jumping right in. It did sting and I decided to cut it at 5 minutes. I got changed and walked the 25 minutes back to my car in shorts and t-shirt and it felt great. It was about 15 deg c out and a lot of people I passed were in full rain gear because it just started to rain when I got in the river . Below are some pictures I took while walking around the park, I love this time of the year .

PS. Ill keep the posts shorter form now on!

[https://www.dropbox.com/sh/e1tvzjsti63q1aa/ouiYo5U1Ze](https://www.dropbox.com/sh/e1tvzjsti63q1aa/ouiYo5U1Ze)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-06T17:30:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/james-journal.8356/post-97043>

welcome.......do not forget water! the key for your hip is adding energy back to your leg. CT grounding water and a magnetico. cycling disconnects your legs and hips from earth pulses........this causes a slow energy leak of black box radiation and the structure of you hip alters..........when energy changes in the biologic system the structure of matter changes. When it changes it change the ligaments, bones, tendons, and the tensgrity of the system and your gait changes back to your primordial genome. The memory is in the water around your DNA even if the tissues have forgotten because the environment you crafted changed the your body.

### JamesC — 2013-10-06T17:49:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/james-journal.8356/post-97045>

Thank you Jack, I did forget water. I drink filtered tap water atm. I know our water comes from the ground locally (The station is about 1km from my house) and it does not have fluoride. Thankfully. I will have to save up for a RO machine or convince my parents. They would not like the idea of getting rid of the minerals though so that may take a while. I know a lot of my hip issue has come from extremely tight muscles causing forward rotation and a mis alignment at the symphasis pubis. I have Kelley Starett's book and am doing yoga and mobility work to help. I also sleep with a grounding sheet placed around my lower back. It's really aggravated when I sit for long periods of time, especially when I was at school, so now I try and stand when working or at my computer. I am going to push for a standing desk at work because my primary position will be at a computer.

But I will catch up on your last blog posts and really try and focus in on the water issue and let you know how it goes. Its such an important part of life, I really need to optimize it.

### Martin — 2013-10-06T18:06:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/james-journal.8356/post-97049>

When it changes it change the ligaments, bones, tendons, and the tensgrity of the system and your gait changes back to your primordial genome. Whoa. Heavy fecal matter.

This applies to all that we do to "elevate" ourselves from the earth on our machines living places. Great summation, Doc!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-06T20:04:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/james-journal.8356/post-97063>

Martin........this is the info on EE 5 and 6.......how you apply the 3 basic laws in EE 4...........EE 6 tells you this answer.

### caroline — 2013-10-07T02:28:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/james-journal.8356/post-97108>

It sounds to me like your summer job was perfect .............

### JamesC — 2013-10-07T03:44:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/james-journal.8356/post-97120>

Today I managed to make it to the gym. I did a max strength workout focusing on the squat. I also did Romanian dead lift, press, Weighted dip, and barbells rows. I ended up setting records for all exercises which could be due to my 2 week break from lifting anything. Afterwards I had a little bit of the shakes which is a sign of very fatigued muscles, I definitely pushed my limits on some of the reps.

I also did my first Tub CT session in a long time. My tub is supper small so I could only get half of me submerged but got my upper shoulders, back hips and feet which are important parts. I will have to bag some crushed ice for my chest next time. The water started at 53 and ended at 49 degrees. I lasted 21 minutes total before I started to feel my core temperature getting cold and I was becoming uncomfortable. The shivering started 5 minutes after but was more manageable then it had been in the past.

I probably only ate 50g of carbs the whole day and tried to eat a ton, although I was always kind of full and found it hard to eat more at some points. My problem is that I am all ready very low body fat and I do not want to loose any more weight so I'm trying to eat a ton of fat. I did make sure I got my Cod Liver oil and Sardines in though!

Tomorrow I am going to attempt the sprints again in the park after work. It will be interesting to see how my legs hold up. I'm going to follow it up with a dip in the river and try to break 5 minutes!

@Caroline it was nice to be outside but I was also under the airport traffic and downtown most of the time, and walking upwards of 15 km in steel toed boots takes it tole. It also doesn't pay the bills.

### Red20 — 2013-10-06T22:39:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red20s-journal.8363/post-97075>

I am a newbie, and just ordered Dr Jack's book, so I have a lot to learn. A friend of mine told me about Dr Jack and CT. It helped him and it resonate with me, so I'm going to give it a try. I have tried everything else and have nothing to lose.

I am a 41 year old male. 11 years ago I used to be an elite athlete until I came down with a flu-like illness and have never been the same since.

I have been diagnosed with Osteoporosis, Hypercoagulation, Chronic Fatigue, and Fibromyalgia. My morning and daytime cortisol levels are low and my nighttime cortisol is high. My DHEA, IFG-1, Free Testosterone, and Progesterone are low and my Sex Binding Hormone Globlulin is high.

I haven't had energy for 11 years and I wake up tired. I have severe muscle pain and can't walk for more than 2 blocks before my legs become stiff and sore. It is like they are filling up with lactic acid and it becomes unbearably painful. Same thing happens in my arms when I try to exert them also.

For the past year I have been a raw vegan eating a lot of fruit. I also liked to juice and sometimes will become comatose tired after drinking juice.

Even though I ate a ton of fruit all day long I was still hungry now I know why.

I still need to educate myself, so I won't be posting again until after I read Dr Jack's book.

### Red20 — 2014-08-10T19:33:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red20s-journal.8363/post-137489>

I got overwhelmed and quit last year. I am now back and going to give the Epi Paleo RX my best shot.

Day #1:
30 minutes in a cold bath tub with no ice.

Breakfast: 3 oz oysters, 3 eggs, coconut oil, fish oil, digestive enzymes, probiotics, 20,000 IU of D3, Super Food Powder and Kimchi.

Lunch: I wasn't hungry for lunch.

Supper: 6 oz of grass fed beef, 3 eggs, 3 oz of oysters, digestive enzymes, fish oil(Omega 3: 2 g, EPA: 600mg, DHA: 400mg), Udo's Oil(Omega 3: 6 g, Omeha 6: 3 g, Omega 9: 2.5 g), probiotics, wheatgrass powder, romaine lettuce and Kimchi.

Day #2:

30 minutes in cold bath tub with no ice.

Breakfast: 2 eggs, 6 oz of red salmon with the bones, digestive enzymes, 20,000 IU D3, coconut oil, fish oil, probiotics, Super Food Powder and Kimchi.

Lunch: Not hungry.

Supper: 6 oz grass fed beef, 3 oz salmon, 2 eggs, coconut oil, Super Food Powder, broccoli, cauliflower, and Kimchi. I ate supper outside with my bare feet in the grass.

The Super Food Powder is made by Dragon Herbs and it's called "Tonic Alchemy." It contains high amounts of Vitamin K, Iodine, Vitamin C, and B12.
[http://www.amazon.com/Tonic-Alchemy...&qid=1407590413&sr=8-1&keywords=tonic alchemy](http://www.amazon.com/Tonic-Alchemy-Dragon-Herbs-Powder/dp/B003B6UL5Y/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1407590413&sr=8-1&keywords=tonic+alchemy)

### Red20 — 2014-08-22T20:02:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red20s-journal.8363/post-138591>

Feeling down today. After 2 weeks of doing the Leptin Reset I have gained 5 pounds. I checked my blood ketones yesterday and they were only at 0.8. My meals are consisting mostly of a cup of broccoli/cauliflower and a cup of green leafy veggies with 10-12 oz of fish, oysters, crab, or clams with 2 tbsp of Ghee, and 1 tbsp of Coconut Oil, 2 tbsp of Kimchi, and 2 tbsp of salsa. Sometimes I do half beef and half seafood, but I am having seafood at every meal.

Every day the sun has been out I have sun gazed with my feet in the grass. I am doing CT 1 to 2 times a day. I have been able to sun bath naked 10 times also.

I have been getting to bed before 9:00 pm every night and waking between 6:30 am and 7:00 am.

My pain and brain fog are worse, but I'm not giving up.

### kovita — 2014-12-03T17:22:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red20s-journal.8363/post-149115>

Red, you are certainly a case of very complicated recovery. You have for sure internal highly likely intracellular stressor(s) driving your problems daysa and nights. Remember it takes a lot of time to come out of this, because your internal stressor never sleeps and is always a step before you. All chronic infections are really complex and difficult. I have myslef lyme and maybe a lot more. I have been fully epipaleo, CT etc. for more than a year and I started to see progress somewhere Round 9 months hallmark. Very slow, very subtle but on the good direction. I have th1 shift with Nitrosostress, luckily not dierected against my own body as per the immunology. The body fights as it can, I would think it may not be completeliy wrong to have high oxidative stress, after all, you are trying to support and strenghten your body to effectively fight the underlying chronic infection. The fight itself may not by nice and hood looking sometimes. I am not smart as JK or other people on this forum, I can just say be patient, do not loose faith, build the faith in yourself every day, give yourself time. JK said many times your kind of health issues are very difficult to resolve and it usually takes 2-3 years...I think it may take even more case depending, but the estimation seems right.

### louise — 2014-12-04T15:15:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red20s-journal.8363/post-149212>

hi red, sometimes its difficult to recognise if you are feeling any better at all......this is not some magic bullet that is going to instantly transform you from sick one day to full of health and vitality the next. we are so used to instant gratification in the form of a magic pill or potion from our doctors and this makes us believe that other ways are rubbish, don't work, waste of time etc etc. I know for me and my husband it has been a long journey and we are just at the bottom of the mountain with a long way to go....he often says that he has only just noticed that some pain or ache or whatever has disappeared or doesn't affect him as much anymore.
its difficult to explain but having tried different things in the past then this seems like the whole package .......do you keep a written journal where perhaps you write how your day has been or how your moods are etc....it suits me more to use a written diary than my online one which gets neglected because I can read back over how my moods change according to how I have been acting .
its hard to change our lives.....you are doing amazing stuff for yourself and you will reap the rewards red, I know you will

It's very ugly - I'm working with a doc who has worked directly with Dr Shoemaker for over 11 years. I"ve been fighting this crap for over 2 years and still no progress. It's a nasty disease.

MSH also controls Cortisol and Leptin - docs will say your Cortisol panel shows adrenal fatigue. Nope - it's thanks to Mold. Wonder why you can't lose weight no matter what you do? Yep - MSH is screwed up so is your leptin.

MSH is an anti-inflammatory, regulatory hormone made in the hypothalamus. It controls production of hormones, modulates the immune system and controls nerve function, too. It is made when leptin is able to activate its receptor in the proopio-melanocortin (POMC) pathway. If the receptor is damaged by peripheral immune effects, such as the release of too many pro- inflammatory cytokines, then the receptor doesn't work right and MSH isn't made. Leptin controls storage of fatty acids as fat, so MSH and leptin are a major source of interest

MSH controls hypothalamic production of melatonin and endorphins. Without MSH, deficiency creates chronic non-restful sleep and chronic increased perception of pain, respectively. MSH deficiency causes chronic fatigue and chronic pain. MSH also controls many protective effects in the skin, gut and mucus membranes of the nose and lung. It also controls the peripheral release of cytokines; when there isn't enough MSH, the peripheral inflammatory effects are multiplied. MSH also controls pituitary function, with 60% of MSH deficient patients not having enough antidiuretic hormone. These patients will be thirsty all the time, urinate frequently and often will have unusual sensitivity to static electrical shocks. 40% of MSH deficient patients won't regulate male hormone production and another 40% won't regulate pro- per control of ACTH and cortisol.

[http://forums.prohealth.com/forums/...e-hypothalmus-and-pituitary-shut-down.180976/](http://forums.prohealth.com/forums/index.php?threads/msh-deficiency-why-the-hypothalmus-and-pituitary-shut-down.180976/)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-09-29T03:40:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red20s-journal.8363/post-178092>

Ocular manifestations of Lyme disease have long been noted by me but not by Lyme docs. Ocular Lyme = low ocular melatonin = sleep disturbance = altered pineal melatonin = addiction tendency = alteration in VLPO neurons in hypothalamus that come from the central retinal pathways. This is why Lyme disease is often associated with sleep and addiction issues. The spirochete invades the eye early and remains dormant, accounting for both early and late ocular manifestations. It dramatically effects the RPE of the retina. The RPE of the retina needs UV light to work optimally. Lyme disease is destroyed by UV light. Do the mental gymnastics. Still think Lyme is the problem or a lack of UV light is the issue? A nonspecific follicular conjunctivitis occurs in approximately 10% of patients with early Lyme disease. Keratitis occurs often within a few months of onset of disease and is characterized by nummular nonstaining opacities. Inflammatory syndromes, such as vitritis and uveitis, have been reported; in some cases, a vitreous tap is required for diagnosis. Neuro-ophthalmic manifestations include neuroretinitis, involvement of multiple cranial nerves, optic atrophy, and disc edema. Seventh nerve paresis can lead to neurotrophic keratitis. In endemic areas where the quantum yield is low, Lyme disease may be responsible for approximately 25-50%% of new-onset Bell's palsy. I think that number is growing year by year. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9176782](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9176782)

Less UV exposure is required to kill bacteria in the human body than is necessary in the laboratory. When a small part of the infected bloodstream is exposed to UV light for less time than is required to kill bacteria in the laboratory, the pathogenic bacteria in the body are usually completely destroyed. In fact, many organisms are destroyed by an amount of irradiation that merely stimulates normal body cells.

Blood cells are much larger compared to bacteria and they are loaded with two UV absorbing proteins, namely hemoglobin and porphyrins. UV light stimulates RBC's to seek and destroy Lyme in the blood stream and in tissues with a large blood supply like the retina. They UV light stimulates the immune system and RBC's to destroy the spirochete while improving retinal function.

Full spectrum UV light exposure has the remarkable ability to allow the ill body to make rapid readjustments back to normal biologic and circadian functioning.

Also as mentioned in the Ubi 23-25 blogs, certain significant amounts of two photo-sensitive amino acids -- phenylalanine and tyrosine -- that are absent in eukaryotic cells are loaded in Lyme's spirochete. These amino acids absorb additional UV energy that kills the bacteria photonically.

There is also biophysical reasons to believe that by destroying the bacteria in the irradiated blood in the eye and skin is analogous to an "autogenous vaccine". It is produced on our surfaces where UV light is found and assimilated and can aid in rapid destruction of bloodstream bacteria in a way that is quantized by frequencies.

The first article that I know of on the benefit of artificial phototherapy was published in June 1934 by Hancock and Knott. By June 1942, 6,520 patients had been treated with UV therapy. The treatment was successful nearly every time. There was complete absence of any harmful effect according to the article.

Epigenetics is where modern systems biology intersects with quantum mechanics. It provides a new framework for the rules of heredity, who we are, our relationship with disease and our personal and societal responsibility for wellness. Epigenetics research recognizes that our DNA is not our destiny, but that DNA simply establishes the initial conditions for how the cells that comprise our body communicate and respond internally and externally with our environment. Epigenetics research and practice takes place within the various realms of advanced biology, physics, chemistry, conventional and alternative medicine, advanced physics, engineering, materials science, psychology, genetics and other fields. It is a meta-discipline.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thread-of-massive-power.8365/post-97721>

Ref: the blog post with Art Devaney and Jack's reply to same. Is this how it works? The protein (or enzyme) opens up by using the charge from the ATP. The protein exposes its hydrophilic moieties. The water becomes structured around the hydrophilic moieties (using energy derived from the sun, particularly infrared energy). The structured water gets a negative charge (because it is structured) and the bulk water on the outside of the structured water gets a positive charge. Chaotropic and Kosmotropic substances (potassium, sodium, Chloride, etc) are attracted or repelled from the structured layer enabling the chemical reaction to take place. The energy for the enzymatic reaction is derived from the negatively charged structured water. Would it not make sense that the ATP is not expended but rather just lets go of the protein and moves onto the next reaction. Wouldn't this be the most energy efficient way for biology to work? Am i understanding the process correctly?

# Thread: G'Day from WA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-wa.8368/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### yarralea — 2013-10-07T08:18:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-wa.8368/post-97147>

Hiya,

I have just discovered this forum today, and rather than poke around, stalk the threads, I figured I would come and just show myself.
I'm 39, female, from Perth Western Australia. Which has a great climate- if you like the beach. Cold nights, as the capital is surrounded by water or desert, but generally the weather is lovely. Being an Australian, I think of temperature in terms of celsius- I'm sure I'll need to get a grip on the temperature conversion eventually.

In terms of weight and health- I'm 39 and not suffering in any way, very lucky. My parents are both sick however, and as long as I can remember my mother has been heavy- she's probably about 120 kgs ( mmm pounds.... er, about 270) and my father is diabetic, with alzheimer's- probably from statins as he had a heart attack 40 years ago and has been on a host of meds. My older sister 44, is currently on hormone inhibitors post chemo and BC- she's arthritic and unhappy because of her treatment and current hormone state. So I see my genetic future, and want to change it. I had been doing a low carb diet since 2002 to control my weight- but it morphed into a low fat, super low calorie, high diet drink disaster. I was able to pull myself back, but would swing from out of control eating to ultra controlled olive counting. I seemed to have found some solace in paleo, being able to loosen up and have the odd bit of non ideal food and not spiral into a month long binge fest. I have been paleo for the good part of 8 months, dropping 20 kilos. I have a lot of butter in my diet, and for a period I was having some raw goats milk and raw cows milk. My weight is stable at 70 kilos now, but I would like to be a few kilos leaner. I don't exercise much, a few hours of roller derby each week and a few hours of softball 3 times a week.

I have been looking into CT, albeit a bit slowly. Here's how I've been going so far. I started by having some cold shower blasts and going for 20 minute dips in the ocean, current water temperature is 18 C. That has been easy. I have also started having cold baths. Cold water at home, in a standard tub with just a couple of kilos of ice added. I'm not wearing any compression garments, just au naturale. I'm not sure of the water temperature as I've not measured it. I have frozen 3 bags of water in ziplock bags as I refuse to buy ice for this. When I get more freezer space I will freeze more. I can stay in for about 30 minutes- depending upon what else is going on in the household. I usually do this at early night. My sleep has been the best thing since becoming more committed to turning off the iphone and teev. I know for sure that this stimulates me and keeps me from sleep. The part that I do find difficult is on roller derby / softball training nights when I finish sometimes at 9 pm, I have a hard time winding down and sleeping. I have started just leaving training earlier, choosing rest over exercise.

Eating..... er, I usually eat 5-6 egg yolks only in the morning, with some kimchi and at times an avocado. It's not high protein, its high fat, and I usually eat within half an hour of rising, and that usually keeps me going for most of the day, at work I'll have a can of sardines in oil and a few days a week I'll just snack on butter until dinner. Dinner is usually grassfed beef with vegetables- too often sweet potato roasted in beef tallow. We were having rice once a week, boiled in bone broth to save a bit of cash from the household budget, and the other people in the house are not as strict as I would like myself to be.

My daughter is where my story gets interesting. Diagnosed with global developmental delay at aged 3 we went from doctor to doctor looking for causes and solutions. Not one said try a gluten free, casein free diet. I was part of a low carber forum and someone suggested I take her off gluten. I knew instantly I was doing the right thing, because her withdrawal was horrendous. I joke now that I would have given her away to a bad home; she was about 4 at the time. I saw improvements, but they were not long lasting. As I type this she has just had a meltdown- she is now 7. She has been paleo since march, and we are seeing some improvements, eg the bed wetting instantly ceased, but still the odd epic meltdown when she does not get her own way. Its always at home- we have large power lines 500m from our house, I wonder if they are affecting her. She sleeps on a bunk bed, as her room is small to create space, I wonder if that affects her. We have wifi, I wonder if that affects her, and if she ever uses an iPhone/ iPad and at some level teev and computer, she is horrible. We are with a nutritional review service- treating her with amino acids, nutritional supplements and aloe vera juice to restore her gut, she has high levels of mercury and low levels of zinc. I am looking into doing some neuro feedback with her. I will struggle to convince the DH that the wifi needs to go and the other changes to improve our water and EMF cannot be afforded at this time due DH being out of work.

I have been trying to read as much of JKs articles, but I have garnered most of it from podcasts. I love podcasts, I can listen to them for hours and its my preferred way of getting information. I found links to JK on locarber.org and when I started podcasting I spotted him with Sean Croxton, Ben Greenfield and others- I certainly prefer listening over the blogs which exhaust me. I will continue to read them, I'm sure I will eventually cease to be overwhelmed by them. I do like the geek alert / non scientist sections and the way that is set out.

So I am hoping to get connected with people who understand where I am coming from- cartainly any other aussies, or hopefully another Perth person with whom to talk supplies with.

See you in the soup (primordial soup lol)

### caroline — 2013-10-07T10:16:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-wa.8368/post-97154>

Hi yarralea....and welcome! I am on the other side of the country - just south of Sydney.
It sounds like you are doing great alrady. The podcast are so easy to listen to aren't they - love them.

Your daughter's EMF exposure will be critical. You have said when she uses an iphone or computer she is horrible - that tells you everything that you need to know right there. ipads are terrible btw - they emit EMFs front and back.
I used to tether my iphone to my laptop and my feet would go numb! and I couldn't sleep at night!
Does she go to school? Do they have wifi?

Can you get your daughter in the ocean? outside as much as possible - grounding with bare feet? hugging a tree or two?
That would be huge if you can spend as much time as possible outdoors with her.

The blogs..... we all have to keep reading and then re reading them again and again ....it takes awhile to start connecting the dots - no question about that.

Dr. Kruse is teaching us how to be responsible for our own health and maybe help our Doc's along the way too. Do you have a good partner Doc?

Please start a journal in the journal section and then we can follow along and help where we can if you have questions.
Dr. Kruse likes journals so he can get to know you a bit and offer help when and if he can.

Just keep reading and asking questions. Have a look at other's journals especially Mr PInkies and Cantweight's journals.
Maybe you will have something to offer them as well. We all need to support each other to get where we need to go

Cheers Caroline for the welcome.
Yes, she's at school. It would have wifi, and not much I can do about that, sadly. I'll work on the home. As for a doc, there's a functional med practioner here in perth, but I really can't afford him. The Nutritional review service will probably be able to support me in many tests, if I know what to ask for.

In other news, I just had a 50 minute ice bath, getting out when my ice bags were no longer ice. One thing, regarding recovery, I played 5 games of softball on saturday- catching, so squatting for hours, and on sunday I was really sore as this was the opening tournament of the season after 6 months off. It has probably been 15 years since I played 7 games in a day, never having caught 5 games, which any softball enthusiast would agree is kind of epic. I had an ice bath last night, as after sunday of almost hobbling about I felt that would be a good idea. I had roller derby training tonight and did a new personal best for the number of laps in 5 minutes, adding a lap (28) woo hoo.

I'll look for the journal section..... thanks loves!

### nonchalant — 2013-10-07T17:44:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gday-from-wa.8368/post-97178>

Hi Yarralea, welcome!
Caroline is completely right about the EMF. Grounding barefoot after school for an hour or so would help. You might try turning off the breaker to your daughter's bedroom at night. And even if you have to string computer cable around the house, it would be such a relief for her. She must feel awful, on a deep level that she can't explain.

Sounds like the CT helped you a lot! It is awesome for soreness. After you get cold adapted, you will find that you recover well after exercise even if you don't get a chance to get into an ice tub for a few days.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-08T05:15:22+0300

So when a star looses energy......it forms an Fe core. This adsorbs and absorbs energy.......simultaneously......other metals are altered.......once again showing you how energy transforms matter....... interactions occur at every step of Fe and Zn metabolism; Fe is more efficient in altering Zn storage than the reverse. Why is this important? Schizophrenic have lower brain zinc and higher brain iron........the higher your brain iron the less you sleep.......lower zinc more depression and mental illness. In people with inflammation, vaccines with other transition metals, zinc tends to fall and kids excrete zinc to a great degree........zinc has been shown to be more effective than drugs in treating mental illness in many studies..........but what is the KEY with zinc? Iron is...............and that is why the Sept webinar is a big deal........the more energy inefficient a brain is the more iron is collects........and the more iron it collects the more EMF it absorbs...............now think back to what happens to a star...........There is a ton of implications in this dance I just gave you all and your sick kids..................

### Inger — 2013-10-08T10:41:12+0300

I wonder if people with low iron can have their iron collected in the brain instead of other places of their body where needed? And one could say with some mood issues too.... and their doc says they need to take iron supps... but what if it goes into the brain again and makes everything worse.... and that is why many cannot get their iron up with supps either... maybe that is why seafood is better for those people and not so much red meat with much iron? # Thread: Copper and electrons?

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HoneyChild — 2013-10-07T13:50:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-and-electrons.8369/post-97166>

I saw this and wondered about the implications of using copper in general such as copper bracelets or implants?

Copper in hospital rooms may stop infections

"How does it work? Copper is used to transmit electrons in walls for electricity. Similarly, bacteria will donate electrons to the copper metal, which places the organism in an electrical deficit. As a consequence, free radicals are generated inside the cell. The cell's proteins essentially get bleached, and its DNA get fractured. The electrical potential of the cell also gets collapsed."

[http://thechart.blogs.cnn.com/2013/05/14/copper-in-hospital-rooms-may-stop-infections/](http://thechart.blogs.cnn.com/2013/05/14/copper-in-hospital-rooms-may-stop-infections/)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-07T18:16:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-and-electrons.8369/post-97185>

here's the labs I run regularly

Lipid Panel - VAP, NMR or Berkley/
Fasting blood glucose Test # 001818, CPT Code 82947
CBC Test # 005009, CPT Code 85025
Comp Metabolic Panel Test # 322000, CPT code 80053
Thyroid:
TSH, Test # 004259, CPT Code 84443
Free T4, Test # 001974, CPT code 84439
Free T3, Test # 010389, CTP Code 84481
RT3, Test # 070104, CPT 84482
Total T4 (Dzugan requested) Test # 001149 CPT Code 84436
Total T3 (Dzugan requested) Test # 002188, CPT code 84480
Progestrone, Test # 004312, CPT Code 84144
Estradiol, Sensitive, Test # 140244, CPT Code 82670
Total Estrogens Test# 004549, CPT Code 82672
SHGB Test #082016 CPT Code: 84270
Testosterone Free Direct, & serum with Total Testosterone: Test # 140103, CPT Codes 84402, 84403
Prolactin Test # 004465, CPT code 84146
cortisol Test # 004051, CPT Code 82533
DHEA-S Test # 004020, CPT Code 82627
IGF-1 Test #010636, CPT Code 84305
RBC magnesium Test # 080283, CPT Code 83735
Vitamin D25 Hyrdoxy Test # 081950, CPT code 82306
Serotonin Test# 120204, CPT Code 84260 (requires frozen specimens)
Pregnenolone Test# 140707, CPT Code 84140 (requires Frozen Specimens)

# Thread: Is Resistance Futile?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-resistance-futile.8371/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-10-07T22:09:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-resistance-futile.8371/post-97203>

Where's a borg when you need one?

In the stock market, analysts are always talking about resistance. Stocks hit resistance that keeps them from going any lower and they also hit resistance that keeps them from going any higher. So when you look at a stock chart, you will tend to see a stock hit the same high a few times before it finally breaks through. Usually something has to change before a stock breaks through resistance.

So I was thinking that weight loss (or gain) is somewhat similar. I started at 320 and had a hard time getting through 270. The next big resistance level was 235. My current is 210 which I will likely hit for the 3rd time in about a week. I can go to bed feeling great and wake up the next day with cravings that won't go away until I feed them - again and again. Usually 5-10 pounds of weight gain is enough to stop them, but then it is a struggle to lose it again knowing the same thing is likely to happen.

So what causes this resistance and how do we get through it the 1st time? Evidently Jack, his son, and nephew all did or they couldn't have lost that much weight in such a short time. So there must be a way.

I'm sure water and grounding are a big part. I'm wondering how much toxins stored in fat effect it. As we lose fat, the toxins that had been stored there get dumped into the blood system. I'm dumping 30 year old toxins! Could this resistance be our body telling us it can't get rid of the toxins fast enough and we need to put a few pounds back on and reabsorb some of the toxins? If so, what supplements will help flush them out a little faster?

So what do you believe causes these resistance levels and how do you get through them?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-07T22:36:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-resistance-futile.8371/post-97204>

losing weight in the face of cancer is a huge task.......Re read EMF 2 why.......cancer is an energy losing disease too......your body is fighting for its life so your cytokines are constantly elevated and this hurts your ability to lose weight. you situation or "field" does not equal mine, my son, or nephew so the results wont be congruent.

### Destiny — 2013-10-08T02:00:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol.8375/post-97231>

I got back my salivary cortisol. Not really sure what to do with that. Since last year I was taking daily PS 200 mg to decrease my night spike. Maybe I should stop taking it?

**Sept 2013**
Cortisol (saliva) **3.8 ng/ml** 3.7-9.5 (morning)
Cortisol (saliva) **2.6 ng/ml** 1.2-3.0 (noon)
Cortisol (saliva) **0.5 L** ng/ml 0.6-1.9 (evening)
Cortisol (saliva) **0.6 ng/ml** 0.4-1.0 (night)

**Jan 2012**

Cortisol (saliva) **1.2 ng/ml** 0.6-1.9 (evening)

### ThatRonSwanson — 2013-10-16T07:18:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol.8375/post-97967>

Cortisol in the morning and evening needs to be better supported, where cortisol in the afternoon needs to be lowered. I would suggest in the morning taking iodine, Ubiquinol with Pqq, Vitamin B5, and Vitamin C to support your adrenals. I would also have you take Vitamin B5 and Vitamin C in the early evening as well. Take your PS after lunch with your fish oil since it lowers cortisol. You also need to eat within an hour of waking up. Also drink 1/4 to 1/2 tsp. of salt in the morning mixed with water upon waking. Finally, wear your blue light blocking glasses starting at 8 p.m. and limit RF pollution at night if all possible.

### Destiny — 2013-10-16T13:54:16+0300

>

I would suggest in the morning taking iodine, Ubiquinol with Pqq, Vitamin B5, and Vitamin C to support your adrenals. I would also have you take Vitamin B5 and Vitamin C in the early evening as well. Take your PS after lunch with your fish oil since it lowers cortisol. You also need to eat within an hour of waking up. Also drink 1/4 to 1/2 tsp. of salt in the morning mixed with water upon waking. Finally, wear your blue light blocking glasses starting at 8 p.m. and limit RF pollution at night if all possible.

Thanks, TRS. I have been taking and doing everything you suggested except taking the Vitamin B5. I will add it to my daily vitamins. Also why do you think it is better to take PS after lunch instead in the evening?

### ThatRonSwanson — 2013-10-16T17:04:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol.8375/post-98002>

>
> Thanks, TRS. I have been taking and doing everything you suggested except taking the Vitamin B5. I will add it to my daily vitamins. Also why do you think it is better to take PS after lunch instead in the evening?

Because of your cortisol curve being higher in the early afternoon. You would need to take the PS during lunch to lower it.

# Thread: Beware the Potential Hazards of Robot Surgery
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beware-the-potential-hazards-of-robot-surgery.8378/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-10-08T15:58:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beware-the-potential-hazards-of-robot-surgery.8378/post-97279>

[http://www.bloomberg.com/news/2013-10-08/robot-surgery-damaging-patients-rises-with-marketing.html](http://www.bloomberg.com/news/2013-10-08/robot-surgery-damaging-patients-rises-with-marketing.html)

Below are selected passages from this article.

Robotic surgeries are on the rise, fueled by aggressive marketing by doctors, hospitals and Intuitive Surgical Inc. (ISRG), which manufactures the $1.5 million robot. Advertising on hospital and doctor websites, YouTube videos, billboards, and on radio and television has hyped the advantages of robotic surgeries, often claimed fewer complications without proof, and ignored contradictory studies finding no advantage in some cases.

U.S. hospitals used robot-assisted surgery in more than 350,000 operations last year, a 60 percent jump since 2010. Robotic surgery is used to perform hysterectomies, gall bladder removals, prostate cancer treatment, heart valve operations, and many other soft tissue operations. And half of general surgeons plan to add robotic systems within two years in response to general demand, according to a JPMorgan Chase & Co. survey reported Oct. 3.

â€œIf there was a Nobel Prize for marketing, it would go to Intuitive Surgical,â€￾ said John Mulhall, an urologist at Memorial Sloan-Kettering Cancer Center in New York, which performs prostate cancer surgery with and without the robot.

When a hospital got a robot, Intuitive Surgical provided an extensive marketing kit with everything from material for radio spots to television clips to Web pages the hospital can use in its marketing, said Peter Carnegie, a former Intuitive Surgical cardiothoracic program development manager, who left in 2010 and now provides consulting services for hospitals on complex robotic surgery.

For hospitals, the â€œprimary investment valueâ€￾ of the robot â€œis growing the hospitalâ€™s market share with new surgical referrals,â€￾ wrote Ryan Rhodes, an Intuitive executive, and a colleague in a chapter on the economics of robotic surgery in a 2008 book.

The robot â€œcan attract new patients, new referrals, and new hospital revenue,â€￾ they wrote. â€œWe can provide marketing support, including source files for a variety of promotional materials, and we work with hospital and practice marketing staff to coordinate promotional events.â€￾

### nonchalant — 2013-10-08T18:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beware-the-potential-hazards-of-robot-surgery.8378/post-97287>

When I buy real food, I'm amazed at the lack of any marketing/advertising involved. All the fake food has plenty of radio/television ads, coupons, promotions, pretty packaging, sales, etc.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-08T18:50:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beware-the-potential-hazards-of-robot-surgery.8378/post-97290>

# Thread: Starting with Neck Deep Immersion?  Achieve the benefits without any ice?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-with-neck-deep-immersion-achieve-the-benefits-without-any-ice.8380/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tripaleo — 2013-10-08T22:46:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-with-neck-deep-immersion-achieve-the-benefits-without-any-ice.8380/post-97305>

I'm brand new to this fascinating world and have immediately jumped into self-experimentation with diet and CT. So quick background-have been eating intelligently Paleo for about 7 weeks and officially started CT training last week. There is a "Cold Plunge" at my gym with temps in the low 50s that I have used for the past year inconsistently on my own as a form of meditation but slowly built up to 5 minutes neck deep. In the past week, I have build up to 15-20 minutes and I've started drinking about 24 oz of ice water as soon as I get in.

Based on my personal situation, is there any risk of going straight for immersion?
Am I on the right track? I am planning on slowly building up to 45 minutes to do 2-3 times per week. This is for optimal health and performance, not weight loss.

Any suggestions or other forums/blogs you can link here?
What are people doing to warm up afterward?
Can you achieve the benefits with just the 50-55 degree water submerged like this without any ice?

### freesia — 2013-10-08T23:42:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-with-neck-deep-immersion-achieve-the-benefits-without-any-ice.8380/post-97307>

Hi, yes you're getting benefits!
Sounds like you're adapting really nicely at your own pace. 5 mins in low 50's at the start is plenty. Lots of people need to start closer to 70 F.
In my experience so long as you're not getting white or numb extremities, or suffering hypothermia it's safe to proceed. Proceed slowly with increasing the time like you planned to and I don't think you can go far wrong.

How are you doing at 15 - 20 mins? Any big muscle shivering afterwards? If and when this happens it's sending a message that Winter is here  and your body needs to activate more brown fat. It's after that the non shivering thermogenesis really starts to kick in and the whole re-warming process becomes very pleasant.
Some people exercise after. For a faster adaptation you probably want to keep your skin as cool as you can for as long as you can.
....In the beginning though if it's too uncomfortable or you've pushed it too far and your body needs help to warm then I think you can use some heat judiciously. This may slow adaptation slightly i.e. it signals your body there's not such an urgent need to get it's internal warming mechanism up to speed, but in the scheme of things it won't hurt it may just take a little longer. You'll keep falling forward because you're designed to.
Good luck.

### caroline — 2013-10-09T00:11:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-with-neck-deep-immersion-achieve-the-benefits-without-any-ice.8380/post-97312>

Welcome Tripaleo .....it sounds like you are doing great. Freesia definitely knows what she is doing - she has made huge progress.

# Thread: Tommie Copper Swoon?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tommie-copper-swoon.8381/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gracelind — 2013-10-09T03:20:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tommie-copper-swoon.8381/post-97317>

I have an autoimmune neuromuscular condition that has targeted my thighs causing severe atrophy of quadriceps. It's not true polymyositis--working Dx is inflammatory myopathy. With significant muscle wasting my balance is now terrible, so I rely for support on a cane, furniture, walls, whatever is close to hang on to. Two weeks ago I fell, badly twisting my knee and wrenching lower back muscles, right side.

In a what could it hurt?--my knee needs support--moment I ordered a Tommie Copper knee sleeve and T-shirt. When they arrived today I put the knee sleeve on first. After about half an hour I put on the T-shirt under a long sleeve cotton T and got ready to go shopping.

Within 10 minutes I suddenly had a flush that rose from my chest into my head, making me very lightheaded. Had to hold on to the kitchen counter to keep from falling. It was intense but only lasted a few seconds. Took the T-shirt off and it hasn't recurred.

The sensation is hard to describe--like a rush of energy but not in a good way. The closest would be that feeling right before fainting with a bit of warmth.

I'm in the mountains at 6400 ft in a rural community where I live part time, so it's not needing acclimate to altitude. Most neighbors do have wifi, as do I. Usually, two or three show up when I log on to my computer. After a major remodel most of our electrical is new. Power lines aare across the street, above ground due to winter snow (regulations against digging in the winter time even for repairs). Time to check out our field here.

Slight headache now. Not an experience I want to repeat. Luckily it happened at home and not in the car while driving.

Any thoughts appreciated.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Yarralea's journal with DD7 in mind
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 220
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### yarralea — 2013-10-09T09:31:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-97326>

Okay, journal started. Cheers Caroline for the pointers in helping me to locate this. I know from my past that a journal works well with me.

I might copy my first entry in welcome area, so if anyone cares to peep they can get the lowdown on me and my issssues.

EE4- cheers JK, I'll start to get onto reading that several times. Thanks also to others who have responded to my first entry, I love community support- I know that community is really important in journey and transformation.

### yarralea — 2013-10-09T09:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-97327>

Hiya,

I have just discovered this forum today, and rather than poke around, stalk the threads, I figured I would come and just show myself.
I'm 39, female, from Perth Western Australia. Which has a great climate- if you like the beach. Cold nights, as the capital is surrounded by water or desert, but generally the weather is lovely. Being an Australian, I think of temperature in terms of celsius- I'm sure I'll need to get a grip on the temperature conversion eventually.

In terms of weight and health- I'm 39 and not suffering in any way, very lucky. My parents are both sick however, and as long as I can remember my mother has been heavy- she's probably about 120 kgs ( mmm pounds.... er, about 270) and my father is diabetic, with alzheimer's- probably from statins as he had a heart attack 40 years ago and has been on a host of meds. My older sister 44, is currently on hormone inhibitors post chemo and BC- she's arthritic and unhappy because of her treatment and current hormone state. So I see my genetic future, and want to change it. I had been doing a low carb diet since 2002 to control my weight- but it morphed into a low fat, super low calorie, high diet drink disaster. I was able to pull myself back, but would swing from out of control eating to ultra controlled olive counting. I seemed to have found some solace in paleo, being able to loosen up and have the odd bit of non ideal food and not spiral into a month long binge fest. I have been paleo for the good part of 8 months, dropping 20 kilos. I have a lot of butter in my diet, and for a period I was having some raw goats milk and raw cows milk. My weight is stable at 70 kilos now, but I would like to be a few kilos leaner. I don't exercise much, a few hours of roller derby each week and a few hours of softball 3 times a week.

I have been looking into CT, albeit a bit slowly. Here's how I've been going so far. I started by having some cold shower blasts and going for 20 minute dips in the ocean, current water temperature is 18 C. That has been easy. I have also started having cold baths. Cold water at home, in a standard tub with just a couple of kilos of ice added. I'm not wearing any compression garments, just au naturale. I'm not sure of the water temperature as I've not measured it. I have frozen 3 bags of water in ziplock bags as I refuse to buy ice for this. When I get more freezer space I will freeze more. I can stay in for about 30 minutes- depending upon what else is going on in the household. I usually do this at early night. My sleep has been the best thing since becoming more committed to turning off the iphone and teev. I know for sure that this stimulates me and keeps me from sleep. The part that I do find difficult is on roller derby / softball training nights when I finish sometimes at 9 pm, I have a hard time winding down and sleeping. I have started just leaving training earlier, choosing rest over exercise.

Eating..... er, I usually eat 5-6 egg yolks only in the morning, with some kimchi and at times an avocado. It's not high protein, its high fat, and I usually eat within half an hour of rising, and that usually keeps me going for most of the day, at work I'll have a can of sardines in oil and a few days a week I'll just snack on butter until dinner. Dinner is usually grassfed beef with vegetables- too often sweet potato roasted in beef tallow. We were having rice once a week, boiled in bone broth to save a bit of cash from the household budget, and the other people in the house are not as strict as I would like myself to be.

My daughter is where my story gets interesting. Diagnosed with global developmental delay at aged 3 we went from doctor to doctor looking for causes and solutions. Not one said try a gluten free, casein free diet. I was part of a low carber forum and someone suggested I take her off gluten. I knew instantly I was doing the right thing, because her withdrawal was horrendous. I joke now that I would have given her away to a bad home; she was about 4 at the time. I saw improvements, but they were not long lasting. As I type this she has just had a meltdown- she is now 7. She has been paleo since march, and we are seeing some improvements, eg the bed wetting instantly ceased, but still the odd epic meltdown when she does not get her own way. Its always at home- we have large power lines 500m from our house, I wonder if they are affecting her. She sleeps on a bunk bed, as her room is small to create space, I wonder if that affects her. We have wifi, I wonder if that affects her, and if she ever uses an iPhone/ iPad and at some level teev and computer, she is horrible. We are with a nutritional review service- treating her with amino acids, nutritional supplements and aloe vera juice to restore her gut, she has high levels of mercury and low levels of zinc. I am looking into doing some neuro feedback with her. I will struggle to convince the DH that the wifi needs to go and the other changes to improve our water and EMF cannot be afforded at this time due DH being out of work.

I have been trying to read as much of JKs articles, but I have garnered most of it from podcasts. I love podcasts, I can listen to them for hours and its my preferred way of getting information. I found links to JK on locarber.org and when I started podcasting I spotted him with Sean Croxton, Ben Greenfield and others- I certainly prefer listening over the blogs which exhaust me. I will continue to read them, I'm sure I will eventually cease to be overwhelmed by them. I do like the geek alert / non scientist sections and the way that is set out.

So I am hoping to get connected with people who understand where I am coming from- cartainly any other aussies, or hopefully another Perth person with whom to talk supplies with.

See you in the soup (primordial soup lol)

Hiya- the wifi- which is the highest priority, is on the other side of the wall, in the hallway. EEK I know. We have just moved here and are still customising. And its really now starting to occur to me that this is a huge factor in her slowed progress- I can only really afford to do one thing. Switching the wifi to cabled is first and foremost.
As for the circuit breakers, I'm really not technically minded, so I guess that means we work out which ones can be turned off. I've turned a heap of switches (microwave- which I'm working on the husband to be rid of it- thinking of making it blow up and then be not replaced.) This will be easier when my husband is back at work, to lose the Mwave. It is currently used to heat up my daughters lunches. As a gluten free, grain free child who attends school, so many options are hot lunches- I'm hoping she will warm to the tuna in oil idea soon. My perfect world would include her devouring sardines lol.

Me- my FBB (fat based breakfast) is usually 6-8 egg yolks cooked in grass fed beef tallow, served with a big spoon of my homemade kimchi and a whole avocado chopped, maybe a slice of organic butter after, kind of as I am walking out the door. sometimes theres some organic bacon too. My nitrite free organic bacon is so good, I love my guy who makes it. He and his wife are the grandparent types that my daughtyer never has access to, as my parents live in Melbourne. Lunch- sometimes not- sometimes a small amount of macadamias, and I know this sounds bad, and it freaks out my co workers, but I'll have a slice of butter on a square of lindt 85%. I love my king oscar sardines in olive oil, and occasionally I will make an ommelette at work, as I have a full kitchen at my disposal. Dinner is always grass fed beef with vegies- sometimes steak, sometimes slow cooker- as we have just purchase half a cow (Annabelle we have named her- because we all know that animals with names that have lived a good life are better for you) Usully I am into the sweet potato roasted and sprinkled with screaming seeds seasoning. If your an aussie- look up screaming seeds- so delish! But the penchant for sweet potatoe has to stop. Broccoli, cauli, asparagus are usually there and my husband and daughter eat potato- roasted in beef tallow. love my beef tallow! I also have some kimchi as well, I love it. I make about 20 jars a fortnight, giving at least half away or swapping them. Dinner is usally about 6, breakfast about 6am.

I'm big on brewing water kefir and kombucha. I brew about 12 litres a week- I do give some away, and I'm going to stop drinking as much as it does use suagr in the fermenting process and I'm concerned I'm getting some of those sweet carbs. Its a toss up- cos as a probiotic I'm a fan, and really I have 5 kilos to lose and no health issues. I eat kimchi everyday and now we are on lovely water that is not fluorodated I may need less. I should just sell it, heaps of my friends want it, because I do enjoy making it. If anyone drinks this on the program, or has an opinion, I would like to hear from them.

Grounding, I need to know more of this- if I'm walking around barefoot, and my house is ceramic tiles on a concrete slab- is that grounded?
I've been walking more barefoot of late. Husband has a pet hate of barefoot children, so I need to work on him. Where we live- Fremantle is very hippy / barefoot / organic/ lets be naturale/ commune baby! so he is getting more exposed to barefoot children and starting to warm to it. Slowly he likes his change. He is down to one slice of bread a day, and is given up the cheap crap food that is microwaveable- but that has been a slow progress. When he goes back to work no doubt his lunches will be pretty gross. I would love him to live longer and healthier with me, drink his coffee black with CO but he is 16 years older than me and set in his ways. That I have inspired him to change in any way at all is a miracle. He hasnt time to do all the research that I have, he has been looking for work and has ben committed to that for most of his time. ### yarralea — 2013-10-17T17:56:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-98160>

Kimchi- I just made 10 jars. I usually make it every four weeks. 2 purple cabbages, chopped medium small, soaked in salted water for 6+ hours. Then strain off the water. I then add 4-6 carrots, grated, a half cup to a cup of pureed ginger, the zest of 3 lemons, a few chopped chilies a whole bulb of garlic, crushed, a huge bunch of radishes, as much coriander as you can or like (I LOVE CORIANDER!) spring onions are also good. Then add another 1/4 cup salt- I just freepour so I guess its about this amount, and a dollop of honey to feed the ferment. Push hard into clean jars and tip out any rising liquid (salt draws it out) I leave on the sink with lids on, and each day I press down a bit more and tip out extra liquid. Then stick the jars in the cupboard for at least a week- although I have had jars for up to 8 weeks. Heaps of recipes online- you really dont need the starter yeasts etc IMO.

Kombucha- you need a scoby to start (Symbiotic Culture of Bacterial Yeast) which is this jelly gooplike disk that sits atop the kombucha brew. A two litre glass jar, make black tea (4 tea bags is what I use) and 1/2 cup of sugar. Make 1600ml, cool then add 200 mls of previous batch and the scoby. Cover with a cloth and secure with a band and keep in a place out of direct sunlight. 7-10 days later, strain and bottle. Keep the bottles in a cupboard for 3 days and it will carbonate. Then go again! Kombucha does not like metal objects and despises fluoridated water. I make at least 8 litres a week, but give a lot away. If you dont know anyone with a scoby you can grow your own my mixing the tea and sugar liquid with a commercial PLAIN kombucha and leaving in a dark place for a few weeks- it will form a scoby, the first batch will be a bit vinegary, but the results are good and I very much enjoy it.

Kimchi, every day, usually twice a day, kombucha or water kefir every day too. Now I'm not exposed to fluoridated water I would expect my gut bacteria to be ...world class! Lol.

Caroline, re my daughter food being microwaved, I know its not ideal, but my husband is doing it- hey tomorrows the last day! and the microwave is turned off when not in use. A few purchases on the weekend- a kettle and a cast iron pan. I've been avoiding buying one, but now DH has a job starting monday, I can relax the purse.

### yarralea — 2013-11-12T14:11:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-100497>

My daughter and I are in Melbourne for a few days. A massive 20 degrees c difference in the temperature. I'm staying near the beach so tomorrow I'm hoping for a swim. Everyone thinks I'm crazy. Lol.

Neuroptimal is the name of the system. My daughter myself and husband have all had sessions : 4,3 and 1 session respectively. Michii has recently started to tie her shoe laces and demonstrated some cognitive improvements and I can't believe the increased clarity of my vision and thinking. Paul who suffers from head aches had a two week long head ache cease. So it made fiscal sense to buy a machine rather than the treatments. I will let you know how it works. Many neuroptimal clients are seeking relief from PTSD, add OCD etc. I have seen early improvements so we have committed to it. How it works is electrodes are applied to the head, along with an earthing point, the patient listens to music and the device reads the brain waves and slightly interferes with the music signal which lends the brain to create more alpha waves to make the correction or adjustment in response to the slight interruption. There are different stages of the 32 minute sessions. There are other neurofeedback systems as well however many require a practitioner determining what the brain should look like and pushing and pulling the brain with more aggressive neurofeedback. I have spoken in person with a few people that had positive experiences with neurofeedback, and the neuroptimal system and we have decided to fully explore this. We will continue to do the nutritional review and of course the diet will remain paleo. That of course is a brief description that is very basic. I'm on my phone at the moment, I will add some links when I'm not on an iPhone.

### yarralea — 2013-11-17T08:10:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-100880>

had another 50 minute ice bath last night. I have a cleaned out wheelie bin with a little stool inside for getting out with some level of grace. I listen to podcasts in it- it helps pass the time. I'm sure my neighbours would think me crazy.

What is strange is that a few kilos have crept on. I was 69kg, and now I am 74. I wonder if its a summer thing. The only thing I think has changed in my diet is the fat content has dropped a bit, and so have the calories, as I am not getting into the fruit. Market day is usually an icypole (cucumber lime and chilli- these girls have an icypole standand I like to support them in their venture). I am also going to slow my keffir and kombucha konsumption (lol) because maybe theres a bit too much sweet getting in, even though they are very fermented. I wonder if the el cheapo magnetic underlay is having an ill effect, and the week that went on the bed was week the scales started going the other way. I have also just had a week away, out of my own control zone of eating exactly how I like and need, although mostly good choices were made. I might have a month where absolutely no lindt 85% is taken- even though it has never slowed my progress before. I will tweak a few things and see if the scales can go back to the under 70 mark. I know its just a number, but as I wear dresses all the time, clothes are not a good measure tool. I guess I haven't been to roller derby for a while- since my husband has gone back to work it has not been practicable with my daughter- it also finishes so late I tend to not b e able to sleep for a while.

Back to CT. Might be a bit TMI but I had to get out half way to go to the bathroom- I wonder if CT has a habit of ...er... getting things in motion? Also I do recall Jack saying on a podcast that one tends to sleep better after a CT session- this is not the case with me, maybe I am missing a detail or two. I also recall him saying the details matter, so maybe I'm missing a detail or two. Does it matter if my head doesnt submerge, (mammalian dive reflect / vagus nerve) when I am CTing? I do this when I swim in the morning, but its kind of hard to get the face in when using the wheelie bin (aka the bogan jaccuzzi lol) method. Also returning to a normal temperature- that needs to be done in a certain way- I just get out and dress lightly. I usually go to bed with the hour and its just a sheet, maybe with my feet with an additional layer. Also does it effect menstrual cycle, or should one not CT whilst menstruating? If anyone is reading this, I would love to know if the book The Leptin Reset has a lot of detail on CT.

Are there some cheap ways to make blocks of ice that people are doing? Because while 15 kgs of ice is $15 here, its not something I want to continue to do twice a week. I have some freezer space, but really dont have any containers as we dont buy anything with a container. I think one litre milk carton that are square might be a good idea. Tessalating, standing without leakage then laying down when fully frozen in various stackable ways.

Okay- weight has crept up to 75, with the summer I believe. I'm trying to work out what sort of changes I need to make to my diet in order to alter for the season.
My daily food is such as: 5-7 egg yolks cooked in beef fat, sometimes with 3 rashers of bacon, kimchi and a whole avocado. Coffee with CO every other day and sometimes a tin of sardines and some organic butter. Lunch has consistently been spinach, avocado (whole) some chili mustard and coriander and some tuna in EVOO. Dinner is asparagas, brocoli, cauliflower, kimchi and GF beef cooked in beef fat. There's been a bit of dark chocolate here and there. There has been no dairy, no wheat or grains getting in.

Any tips of how people transistion to summer- I'm in OZ, so the weather is warm already. I've been doing a weekly ice bath and a weekly dip in the 18 degree ocean. I have also started a bit of neuroptimal neuro feedback.

### yarralea — 2013-12-11T06:24:05+0200

>
> I read your posts about Neurooptimal with interest, I am always on the lookout for new experiences especially those that appear to have benefit. Well, I searched the net and I haven't actually found that much feedback from users but I read this and found it interesting, how does it compare with your experience so far? [http://totallyaddconnect.com/forums/topic/experience-with-neurofeedback-neuroptimal/](http://totallyaddconnect.com/forums/topic/experience-with-neurofeedback-neuroptimal/)

This is not a very scientific response, but I was asked whether I was taking medication before having some sessions- I think some meds that end in pam inhibit the brain's response to the program. I'm sure all users experience this treatment different. My experience with myself has been positive so far. My daughter initially showed a steep curve of improvement, followed by a decline in her compliance, which seems to have turned towards the positive- we will see. All I csan say, is I say enough promise in the technology after having two sessions, my daughter having three and my husband had a session and changed his mind from being very reluctant to drop $11k on a system, to saying yes. I speak to people in person from the NFB community in perth and feel positivity. I think its imperative for people to do their homework.

Having said that I think people who do not address other aspects of their lifestyle are not going to see dramatic improvements. The poster of the above blog did not give any info as to what else she was doing to alter her situation.

### yarralea — 2014-01-23T13:50:53+0200

### yarralea — 2014-03-18T14:56:34+0200

Okays.

Its been a while. Summer time here in Australia, but we are now on the down hill towards Autumn. Which I think is a good thing. A few (like 4) kgs crept on over summer- so "i'm 74 kgs, I think I was down to 69kgs at the end of last winter. I have probably had only 5 CTs since november. My friends think I'm crazy, but I don't mind. I like crazy. I have kept the carbs quite low- probably a few in the water keffir that I am making, and a mango here and there, but generally I have been eating well and feeling well.

Oh and I started two new facebook groups. Perth Wellness Community which I run with another health enthusiast, albeit a person with raw vegan tendencies. The other is my page: Naturally Optimized Wellness. So business is commencing. Pretty sure I have no plans to quit my job and be fulltime at this stage, but I leave myself open to the possibility if things go crazy. Okay. Its 9pm and I'm outta here. ### yarralea — 2014-03-31T15:59:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-119048>

I missed out on the ice machine. But the good news is, there might be another one available.
One thing that has come with the slightly cooler weather- as in below 30 degrees centigrade.... is that tap water doesn't melt the ice so rapidly.

This is my ice bath recipe. If I am doing it wrong, someone give me some tips.
I start with my wheelie bin. (aussies will know- a total bogan spa) Its a recycling wheelie bin, cleaned out. And since we don't eat anything with a recyclable package, it does not get utilised for anything other than ice baths.
I run the hose for a minute to get the warmest water out, then half fill the wheelie bin. I have about 15 kgs of ice in bigger containers like ice cream buckets etc, and they go in, and they chill the water so the ice lasts a bit longer. when the two smallest blocks that are 500g each are melted, I get in. I usually wear socks, and in bathers. Q: Is the compression shirt advantageous? Because I wore it the other day and never have before..... was I supposed to start with that...oops.

Then I get 4 5kg bags of tube ice, so 20 kg and in that goes. Then the wheelie bin is almost at the brim and I'm in with my hands and fingers out. The water gets down to about 6 degrees. I'm in a comfortable squatting position, and I move my legs about to distribute water temperature- i notice behind my knees feels warm, so I move evry minute or so. The ice melts after about 30 minutes and I can do 60 minutes. I think I might do longer but my phone always goes flat. I listen to podcasts whilst CTing. It stops the boredom and is a great distraction. If there is anyone in perth, western australia who wants to talk crap with me in a wheelie bin, bring your vessel and we will CT (near fremantle)
The difficult part of CT for me is scheduling it. I don't like to do it before bed as I tend not to sleep well. Being a full time teacher and mother means days are out, and I might start to do mornings. I was also having sessions before softball games- about an hour before scheduled warm up. My team mates thought I would get injured, pull drivers etc, but my only injury was following a dodgy, ill-timed slide into third. I did notice that running felt faster and automatic- you know, when something happens in sport and there is no memory of it, not consciousness to it. Just a "wow, I got to first so fast" sort of thing....

Would adding epsom salts have any advantageous effect I wonder? Like boosting magnesium or keeping the water colder? I'm trying to think where I heard / read / found that idea.

Okay 9pm here. Lights out, screen off, have some sex.....

I'm hoping that I can work on that, not sure if any one has any comment about anything helpful. I was thinking of getting hypnotised to sleep on my back- I think I snore and am subconsciously self conscious about it. Its odd, I am quite comfortable on my back, but the instant I drop into sleep, I roll into UGLY.
Earthing mat for the computer, and a Vielight 330. I have tried the Vie light, I will search the threads to see what others have stated about this. I had a 25 minute session in my car, roof off 20 degrees C, I'm sure other drivers thought me a little cray cray.
In other news my daughter is changing schools, she will be going to an alternative setting, where shoes are optional and glass bottles encouraged. Yay.
