# Thread: What gives???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-10-14T16:46:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97796>

I am still trying to figure out why the weight is not coming off yet, it is getting so frustrating. I am getting cold adapted, the thyroid tests shows that the thyroid is getting better, still eating my BAB with fats and protein, limiting the carbs, reduced exposure to EMFâ€™s (still have work to do on this though), wearing blue blocker glasses at night, getting better sleep, using more magnets, trying to eat more seafood along with taking a number of supplements to support the whole body.

What gives????? I am tired of the pants getting tighter and tighter

How long did it take after changing everything did people start seeing the weight come off, specifically women?

(More specific details are in my Optimal Blog).

Thanks

Nicole

### HoneyChild — 2013-10-14T16:50:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97797>

If you're leptin sensitive you might want to consider intermittent fasting where you eat 500 calories a day, a couple of times a week. That can kick-start weight loss in some people.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-14T17:38:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97802>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=97796)
>
> I am still trying to figure out why the weight is not coming off yet, it is getting so frustrating. I am getting cold adapted, the thyroid tests shows that the thyroid is getting better, still eating my BAB with fats and protein, limiting the carbs, reduced exposure to EMF’s (still have work to do on this though), wearing blue blocker glasses at night, getting better sleep, using more magnets, trying to eat more seafood along with taking a number of supplements to support the whole body.
>
> What gives????? I am tired of the pants getting tighter and tighter
>
> How long did it take after changing everything did people start seeing the weight come off, specifically women?
>
> (More specific details are in my Optimal Blog).
>
> Thanks
>
> Nicole

Nicole - my experience showed most of my weight loss occurs after winter solstice - I've gained weight recently - as we've moved from summer to fall, following a natural cycle.

If you go back to EE6 - its all about energy in the brain. when your body is lacking energy in the brain, its going to sacrifice things such as weight loss to conserver energy to support brain function. the more I understand - the challenge is bringing your energy quotient up so that your brain sees it has enough energy, and lets go of the weight w/out issue. This isn't easy. While I've lost weight, and gotten back into my pre-pregnancy clothes, I still have more to go. and since its not budging, I'm still deficient on the receiving energy from the triumvirate of Magnetism, Sunlight, and Water. I'm still making changes, and I'm beginning to see more improvements - obviously not at the speed I'd prefer....

hang in there... those who have access to the ocean on a daily basis, like Audrey, seem to have faster success compared to us city dwellers....

As for IFing - it will occur naturally as you get into the PPP. you WON'T WANT FOOD. and it won't drive your every waking thought.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-14T17:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97804>

Nicid read Quantum biology 10.......my bet is that your vitamin A photoperiod is off.......this is the most common reason ladies on thryroid replacement stall. After EE 7 drops it may really hammer the point home why.

### nicld — 2013-10-14T18:15:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97808>

Thank everyone for the inputs.

Gretchen – I will make sure to add EE6 to my reading list for the day. Never thought of weight gain as seasonal, monthly cycle yes, but not seasonal, interesting. I wish had access to an ocean but alas, just the fresh country air. There is no WAY I could ever live in a city so I do have that going for me. Just the thought of it sends shivers down my spine. My next place will be on 10 acres with no neighbors surrounded by pine trees with hopefully a creek running though it and no major power lines nearby. Not that I have put any thought into this, as my husband says….hehehe.

Dr. Jack – thanks for the input. I will also add Quantum Biology 10 to my list for today as well. I have read about the lack of Vitamin A and it’s effects. Now I just need to remember where I saw it and what foods to eat to get it up since if I recall right, supplements will not really cut it.

Honeychild – not sure if the intermitted fasting would work for me right now. I am in the process of recovering from an eating disorder and that much restriction would not be good for me mentally, especially if it did not work.

### nicld — 2013-10-14T18:25:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97810>

Just checked and I was starting to read EE6, will finish today and I did read Quantum Biology 10 so that is where I saw the vitamin A reference. I will reread that one too.

### cantweight — 2013-10-14T19:03:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97813>

Hey Nicole...I am a year and a half down this path and I won't even try to say I have done it to perfection because I have not but I have pushed and done a lot of good for myself it just hasnt shown up in my weight....at all. I hate to say this out loud but I am actually up 30 lbs from where I started. Obviously no poster child for optimal.

I am at the point now that I believe it has just taken me this long to undo what was done. Jacks story is so swift and successful...he was fat, injured, but not destroyed hormonally and metabolically. And he is male. I have had to remind myself of that so many times! I was very unwell, very very unwell. Practically every blog touches on some kind crap my body is trying to heal, my children have issues so we know just how deep it runs, lived in a horrendous field...etc. There was no quick diet fix ever going to occur for me.

I forced BABs forever. Reached a point where my body would no longer accept them, craved fat like mad, then suddenly stopped wanting much of anything....like some sort of energy has stabilized in my body. When this happened my thyroid meds needed to be lowered for the first time since 2006. From 150mg Armour to 120mg...this is huge to me. I have been losing about a lb a day for a few weeks now....I use to get excited when this happened but i have lost these same lbs so many freaking times now.

Most recently I have also felt this odd change in my body..I went through a stage of kind of intense hip and low back pain....now it is gone and I am standing taller and walking different and my pelvis feels looser or less under pressure than it has in a long time. I had a car accident in 1997 that changed my posture and the way I walked and carried myself, thoracic disc damage...cant say for sure but it feels as though it has healed. After my accident my height decreased by 1/2 inch. I feel taller than have ever been right now....it is so weird.

Despite the gain of weight I do not feel unsuccessful. I have thought 1,000 times to drop this and hcg again....which never ended well for me although I dropped weight like bombs....it came back faster than it was lost. I have read story after story of everyone's optimal journey and thought its just not going to work for me...like every freaking other thing i have tried, but I kept on because the science was undeniable.

I am trusting my brain that it knows what it is doing. That it is healing and providing energy to the parts of me that need it most. Someone wrote on here before and it stuck with me that the body/brain has its own hierarchy...just because weight loss is at the top of my list does not mean my body feels the same.

Sorry to have rambled, but wanted to share that I am not svelte and radiating, yet....but my body has experienced profound changes...hell at 39 yrs old I have gotten taller..lol. Maybe its not a weight problem i have but height problem...lol.

Hang in there

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-14T19:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97819>

cantweight the next blog is going to help you and nicid......it is a synthesis blog without a lot of science.

### nicld — 2013-10-14T20:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97825>

Thanks so much, glad to know that I am not alone and I have tried real hard NOT to compare my journey to that of a man (sorry, men just have different hormones). The urge to do another round of HCG is so strong right now and I even have some at home but I do know that may be just a temporary fix and cannot overcome the hormone imbalance that is NOT leptin related.

I like that phrase “the body/brain has its own hierarchy...just because weight loss is at the top of my list does not mean my body feels the same” but I wish it did. I am going to keep trusting in the science because everything else I have tried has failed me and left me sicker.

### Destiny — 2013-10-14T21:06:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97834>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=97796)
>
> I am still trying to figure out why the weight is not coming off yet, it is getting so frustrating. I am getting cold adapted, the thyroid tests shows that the thyroid is getting better, still eating my BAB with fats and protein, limiting the carbs, reduced exposure to EMF’s (still have work to do on this though), wearing blue blocker glasses at night, getting better sleep, using more magnets, trying to eat more seafood along with taking a number of supplements to support the whole body.
>
> What gives????? I am tired of the pants getting tighter and tighter
>
> How long did it take after changing everything did people start seeing the weight come off, specifically women?
>
> (More specific details are in my Optimal Blog).
>
> Thanks
>
> Nicole

Nicole,
My breakthrough came when I stopped eating any fruit in addition to eating LC. At the beginning of the whole process back in October 2011, I was 152 lbs and it took me 12 months to lose 10 lbs. I was very frustrated. Then last October, I stopped eating fruit (which I love) completely, and lost 25 lbs in a few months. The weight just went down and down. Now, I am steady at 117 lbs. since May. I am not saying that this will work for you but it sure worked for me.

### nicld — 2013-10-14T21:15:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97836>

Thanks Destiny, not sure if that is my issue right now. The only fruit I do eat are berries every couple of days. That is great that stopping fruit worked for you.

### Destiny — 2013-10-14T21:22:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97837>

Yeah, you have to figure out your issue. I used to eat only a few berries as well and apparently it was a big issue for me. Now, I only smell them when my hubby munchies on them !

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-14T21:32:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97838>

berries are not the top of the list to eat in this situation

### Hemming — 2013-10-14T22:12:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97846>

Nicole, FWIW (and I realise we have the opposite problem) I've been gaining weight this fall as we are now in the apple season here in Denmark (actually that was an advice from Jack). I've been out picking a lot of them at my uncle's farm and there seems to be a striking positive correlation between my weight gain and eating one or two apples each morning. To a lesser extent I've noticed it with the berries I've been picking.

### nicld — 2013-10-14T22:57:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97847>

It's apple season here too and we have such great apples but I have not had any

### caroline — 2013-10-14T23:35:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97848>

me too ....no fruit was a game changer........

### nicld — 2013-10-15T03:43:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-gives.8440/post-97870>

Wondering that too Meghan.

For me, I just started working out again and I have found that exercise just kills the appetite. I have been doing leptin reset since May though. This time around I am NOT doing it to lose weight or punish myself for eating and it feels pretty good.

# Thread: Required Labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/required-labs.8441/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JNV — 2013-10-14T17:00:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/required-labs.8441/post-97798>

Where do I get a list of required labs necessary to get an entire picture of my health?

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-14T17:30:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/required-labs.8441/post-97801>

The following are the labs I run quarterly - Iv'e also got the test # and CPT codes for labcorp listed.

Berkley or a VAP, NMR or other lipid panel
Fasting blood glucose Test # 001818, CPT Code 82947
CBC Test # 005009, CPT Code 85025
Comp Metabolic Panel Test # 322000, CPT code 80053
Thyroid:
TSH, Test # 004259, CPT Code 84443
Free T4, Test # 001974, CPT code 84439
Free T3, Test # 010389, CTP Code 84481
RT3, Test # 070104, CPT 84482
Total T4 (Dzugan requested) Test # 001149 CPT Code 84436
Total T3 (Dzugan requested) Test # 002188, CPT code 84480
Progestrone, Test # 004312, CPT Code 84144
Estradiol, Sensitive, Test # 140244, CPT Code 82670
Total Estrogens Test# 004549, CPT Code 82672
SHGB Test #082016 CPT Code: 84270
Testosterone Free Direct, & serum with Total Testosterone: Test # 140103, CPT Codes 84402, 84403
Prolactin Test # 004465, CPT code 84146
cortisol Test # 004051, CPT Code 82533
DHEA-S Test # 004020, CPT Code 82627
IGF-1 Test #010636, CPT Code 84305
RBC magnesium Test # 080283, CPT Code 83735
Vitamin D25 Hyrdoxy Test # 081950, CPT code 82306
Serotonin Test# 120204, CPT Code 84260 (requires frozen specimens)
Pregnenolone Test# 140707, CPT Code 84140 (requires Frozen Specimens)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-14T17:38:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/required-labs.8441/post-97803>

anylabstestnow.com and you can read my book which has a full chapter on the labs.....it is listed in the Epi paleo store on this site or on amazon

### JNV — 2013-10-14T19:49:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/required-labs.8441/post-97823>

Awesome. Thanks for such a quick reply from both of you. I'm going to do this 100%.

### JanSz — 2013-10-14T21:38:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/required-labs.8441/post-97840>

For menstruating ladies timing is important.

Day 2-4 is good place to start, it gives minimum progesterone and estradiol levels.
Progesterone should be at max on days 20-22

Good time to draw is 7:30AM after 9-12 hr fasting and 12-16oz water around 6AM.

Take a look at some of my posts on this thread:
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)

note there, meant for LabCorp:
17 ------- Estradiol, Sensitive Test Number: 140244 CPT Code: 82670
18 ------- Estradiol Free, Equilibrium Dialysis, With Total Estradiol, HPLC/MS-MS (Endocrine Sciences) Test Number: 500649 CPT Code: 82670; 84999

Often we worry more about Free and BioAvailable part of hormones, and less about Total Hormones.

Number of people have high SHBG that binds hormones.

\-----------------

Within last few days dr Kruse, on one of his posts was addressing SHBG.

With my luck, computer crashed and I lost that post, can't find it.

I would be grateful to someone who would find it for me.

/////////////////////////

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-15T06:15:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/required-labs.8441/post-97887>

it was in the top ten paleo supplement blog I believe tied to iodine and ketosis.

### JanSz — 2013-10-15T17:35:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/required-labs.8441/post-97903>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=97840)
>
> Within last few days dr Kruse, on one of his posts was addressing SHBG.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=97887)
>
> it was in the top ten paleo supplement blog **I believe tied to iodine and ketosis.**

It is not what's in top-ten-paleo-supplements.
It was more like you are describing now, (iodine, ketosis) but with longer description.
SHBG value is resultant of many forces. T, E2 & T3 are most commonly known. Iodine ties easily with T3, but that is small part of iodine functions.

**I would not mind good info on this topic. Perhaps separate blog?**
Another pressing issue. Reasons for and how to correct low cholesterol.

High SHBG is very common among both sexes.
On the other side, part of population suffers from SHBG in single digits.

> What are the Top 10 Paleo Supplements?
> [http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-top-ten-paleo-supplements/](http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-top-ten-paleo-supplements/)
>
> If gut dysbiosis is present (and it usually is to some degree) or there is a lot of fructose in the diet, Iron absorption could be a real problem. When one eats a lot of fructose in juices or in fruit (or HFCS products), one will increase absorption of Iron in the gut while also increasing serum Albumin. High Iron levels are not good. High Albumin levels can cause our free testosterone or estrogen levels to dramatically fall because the increased albumin binds them, **much like SHBG does in obesity or in hypothyroidism.** None of this is good for optimal.

Testosterone bound to albumin is considered easily BioAvailable by many doctors.
Should I question that?

..............

# Thread: Relocating . . . needs vs. wants
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-needs-vs-wants.8443/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-10-14T20:04:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-needs-vs-wants.8443/post-97827>

Itâ€™s beginning to look like we might have the opportunity to relocate to either the Carolinas or Florida in 12-18 months. I donâ€™t want to go into details about the job, but it looks like weâ€™ll be able to choose where we want to live. I know itâ€™s still early, but weâ€™ve been making a list of needs versus wants in our new home and location. I thought it would be great to get some input from all of you.
Hereâ€™s what weâ€™ve come up with so far:

NEEDS
\- Rural area with private well (We have this now and definitely think itâ€™s a MUST)
\- Low EMF area â€“ hoping a rural area will make this easy, but obviously we still need to look out for big power lines, cell phone towers, etc.
\- Lots of conifer trees to mitigate/block any unwanted EMF
\- Access to fresh seafood
\- Affordable land (Weâ€™re shooting for 5+ acres
\- Good soil so we have the ability to farm the land
\- Lots of sunlight . . . I NEED this

WANTS
\- Good homeschool opportunities
\- Within 1-1.5 hours from the ocean

What else can we add? Any suggestions on locations? At this point weâ€™ve been doing some looking into Northern Florida, but really haven't done much research yet.

### Lahelada — 2013-10-16T03:07:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-needs-vs-wants.8443/post-97942>

You say you need the sun . You could do a calculation of how far you can go and still make vit D in winter by calculating the height of the sun solar elevation in the places you are considering in winter months. If it is over 50 degrees you still get Vit D in winter months. Here for example I have a gap of 5 months

[http://www.esrl.noaa.gov/gmd/grad/solcalc/azel.html](http://www.esrl.noaa.gov/gmd/grad/solcalc/azel.html).

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-16T05:02:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-needs-vs-wants.8443/post-97955>

2\. Clean water
3\. Sea food
4\. Few people who use modern things.........with lots of conifers

That is the simple recipe.

### veelansu — 2013-10-16T07:09:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-needs-vs-wants.8443/post-97964>

Love is definitely #1. People tend to forget that sometimes.

Lahelada, what is someone supposed to look for in that solar calculator? I'm confused.

### Lahelada — 2013-10-16T13:40:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-needs-vs-wants.8443/post-97983>

It is a way of calculating how long your actual "sun winter " is or the period where you cannot make vitamin d from the sun Take Miami FL for example . If you page through the month option on the calender to dec 2013 to 12.30 pm letÂ´s say you will see the solar elevation as 43.44 degrees. It is below 50 so not enough to make vitamin D but your stores would probably eaily take you over that period . If you were to move to Minnesota you would have 8 months of sun winter as the sun is too low in the sky. I have not had the chance to look for the localities you mention but if you look up the latitude and longitude and then input them in the calculator you can personalise it
Reason I specified it is that you mentioned that you NEED sun .For me that was tantamount to saying that you need the feelgood effect of vit D. Hope I got that right.

# Thread: like a furnace!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Julie5 — 2013-10-14T21:55:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97844>

I have been on the leptin RX for 2 weeks now. Not perfectly, but I'm getting there. After about a week into it I noticed that I am hot all the time (except my toes, which are always cold). Will this calm down in time? will my toes warm up? It's making sleep more of an issue. I wake up too hot during the night, but uncovering makes my arms cold.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-15T00:18:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97853>

Great signs as you make forward progress....

### nicld — 2013-10-15T04:06:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97876>

> [Julie5 said:](/goto/post?id=97844)
>
> I have been on the leptin RX for 2 weeks now. Not perfectly, but I'm getting there. After about a week into it I noticed that I am hot all the time (except my toes, which are always cold). Will this calm down in time? will my toes warm up? It's making sleep more of an issue. I wake up too hot during the night, but uncovering makes my arms cold.

Good question. I too notice that I sometimes wake up in the middle of the night too hot and uncovering the arms gets me too cold. My feet are the first to get hot and I try to keep them uncovered too.

### cinnamon — 2013-10-15T04:23:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97878>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=97876)
>
> Good question. I too notice that I sometimes wake up in the middle of the night too hot and uncovering the arms gets me too cold. My feet are the first to get hot and I try to keep them uncovered too.

I seem to remember that Dr Kruse sleeps naked in the freezing cold. Some of us mere mortals are not fully cold adapted yet and still use sheets and blankets.

I find it helps to fold the bottom corner of the blanket up on an angle before I get in. If I get hot, I can get my feet to a cool spot easily by stretching down to that corner.

### Julie5 — 2013-10-15T18:58:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97910>

I"ve had to do that too: fold the blanket back a little. I have always had a problem with the backs of my arms being too cold, so years ago I took a pair of sock and cut off the tops, making arm warmers that I used just like leg warmers. I may have to do that again since even though the chest, back and shoulders are sweltering, the arms still get cold and prevent better sleep.

### Julie5 — 2013-10-15T18:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97911>

WEll, sleeping naked is out of the question for me if i want my hubby to leave me alone long enough to sleep!!!!

### cinnamon — 2013-10-15T19:28:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97912>

Julie5,

I was thinking about your question some more this morning. I remembered that I had a few weeks in December of last year where I was getting very hot an hour or two after getting into bed. I mean like sweating hot and very uncomfortable - very disturbing to sleep. At first, I thought maybe the effects of CT were turning on my "inner furnace" and that seemed like a good thing. I thought it would just be a transitional thing, like detox, and I figured I would push past it.

It was just before the Christmas holidays when I had been cleaning the house all day and was just exhausted from not having had a good night's sleep in three weeks. My DH ordered a pizza for dinner and, despite my misgivings, I gave in and ate that for dinner. Now, normally, in those days, I was having a vitamin K2 capsule at about 8 pm. I suppose because I was already eating bad food (and much too late) I skipped the K2 that night. When I went to bed, I slept much better - not waking in a sweat at all. The next day, I took the K2 as usual and the sweating happened right on schedule. I stopped taking the K2 at that point and over the next few days, my sleep got better and better and the overheating stopped.

Now, about the K2. I had been successfully using K2 for about 6 months already at that point. The difference was that I had been using the MK-4 formulation (menatrenone) and in early December, I hadn't been able to find MK-4 and had gotten a brand that used MK-7 (menaquinone) from natto beans. Shortly after I started using the MK-7, I noticed that I had a pain in one of my big toes when walking. I thought it was odd but figured it was temporary. The other thing I found was that after two or three weeks, I developed an odd mild pain in my mid-section. Difficult to describe - not strong, but definitely a feeling that something wasn't quite right. A couple of days before the pizza, I woke to find a small round bruise, about the diameter of a pencil, just to the left of my belly button. I was just about to give in and go see about this as it was disturbing. It was going to be right after the holidays, you know? And then the pizza thing happened. And I discovered the night sweats, the painful toe and the pain in the mid-section all completely disappeared when I stopped taking the MK-7. The symptoms were not linked in my mind until they disappeared together.

Now, this is all just my n=1. I know many people use MK-7 with no problems. For me, I only use MK-4 now.

I thought maybe my story might help you to consider if something else might be going on. Has anything changed for you before the night sweats started other than doing the leptin reset? Are you trying any new supplements?

cheers,
cinnamon

### BJK77 — 2013-10-15T22:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97918>

Hi Julie,

I had this happen to me too, but for me it was several months after starting the leptin rx. It would come and go for me and finally this Spring it seemed like it was gone for good . . . until recently when it's starting all over again

I have no idea why. I'm trying to look at things I'm doing differently, but I just can't pinpoint the cause. I lowered my thyroid meds (NT) based on labs and I did start eating a bit higher fat due to the changing season, but I've since backed down on the fat and no change in the overheating. I believe I do have a tooth infection now, so I don't know if that could be contributing . . . I imagine it could even be a combination of things going on.

In the past I used to think the overheating had to be hormonal, but part of me questions whether or not it isn't sometimes related to a detox effect.

### Julie5 — 2013-10-16T08:24:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97970>

Hmmm....interesting. I hadn't considered that it might be one of my supplements. When I went to see my doc after my labs, he prescribed a whole bunch of supps that I hadn't taken before. Maybe I will try eliminating one at a time for a few days each and see if the heat cools off. When my hubs says I'm hot, I don't want it to be because I'm sweating!

### caroline — 2013-10-14T23:44:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97849>

### caroline — 2013-10-14T23:46:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97850>

### JNV — 2013-10-15T00:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97852>

Would love to know why Dr. Kruse does not feel like Mercury is an issue by eating too much fish? Does anyone know? Also, how do I get RO water? He mentioned it is something I can buy, but I went to the store today and they didn't have it in the Boston area. Is it at a Whole Food perhaps?

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-15T00:27:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97854>

Smoked canned oysters - are my low budget oyster buy. Of course I grew up eating oysters for saturday and sunday lunch/dinner as we'd watch football. Even my kids love them... my family learned quite quickly if you want smoked oysters when Chaos and Havoc are in town - don't let them know you're eating them b/c they'll scarff them all up.

however, we try to hit up fresh oysters as often as possible. the local international market charges 5 for $2.00 which is reasonable. we shuck them ourselves, top w/horseraddish and lemon juice and down the hatch - just swallow them. no chewing. that's really the key.

### caroline — 2013-10-15T00:33:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97856>

It isn't the oysters I object to .... it is that artificial smoke thing going on ..... ugh...

### Julie5 — 2013-10-15T02:28:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97861>

**RO water**

" Also, how do I get RO water? He mentioned it is something I can buy, but I went to the store today and they didn't have it in the Boston area. Is it at a Whole Food perhaps?"

RO water was half our business for 35 years. RO stands for reverse osmosis. Basically, it's bottled water. If the bottle doesn't say reverse osmosis on it, then it isn't. You can pay for a bottled water service to deliver 5 gallon bottles to your home along with renting (or you can purchase) as water cooler. Most of the chubs of water you buy are either mineral water, meaning it hasn't been thru RO, but be sure to look b/c sometimes the minerals are added back in after going thru RO.

### nicld — 2013-10-15T04:10:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97877>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=97854)
>

Even my kids love them... my family learned quite quickly if you want smoked oysters when Chaos and Havoc are in town - don't let them know you're eating them b/c they'll scarff them all up.

Wow, that is a great price. I don't even know where I can go in my area to get oysters and I know that they would not be that cheap. When I was out in Boston this summer I did notice that fresh seafood was way cheaper than beef so seafood the whole time was enjoyed.

# Thread: Symptoms only the tip of the iceberg
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Julie5 — 2013-10-15T03:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/post-97863>

Hi everyone. I've been reading and asking for a few weeks now, and I'm in awe of Jack's willingness to answer so many questions!

I'm 58 and thought I was generally in good health, except I struggled with insomnia for the last 4 years (severe, can't sleep without drugs). Then I started noticing even the drugs didn't work. I developed a runny nose, a tingly toe, my hair started falling out like crazy (I have to wear a wig now). And no matter what I did, I couldn't get the extra 40 lbs off. In frustration I started searching online and found Jack Kruse. What a blessing that has proven to be! I asked him my first question and he put me on the road to recovery. He suggested i needed to have a serious talk with my doc. Since I knew a regular family doc couldn't do all the tests I knew I needed to do (per Jack's recommendations), I found an MD/naturopath. He had never heard of Jack, but his recommendations and the tests ordered, the ketogenic diet, the supplements suggested and the prescriptions given were right on target. To me, a second doc was verification that what Jack suggests is exactly what I need.

When my tests came back there was only one piece of good news: my HDL is 84. In January I had 5 stents put in my heart, but the cardiologist said there is no heart disease. The naturopath told me that without the high HDL I would have died of a massive heart attack like my brother did at the same age. All my other numbers were either way too low or too high. All my supps/meds now cost me about $250 a month, but I feel that's better than an early death. Everyone in my family died early. I'm the only one left.

I also found out I have 2 genetic mutations that prevent, if I understand correctly, enzymes from being utilized, and I learned I have celiac disease. That was a shocker since I have for many years made all our own homemade whole wheat bread. Now instead of feeling like I am generally healthy, I feel like I am a long way from there.

So for the last 2 weeks I have been eating the leptin RX (except for oysters). We started using oil lamps in our bedroom since the breaker is off in that room and I wear amber goggles in the evening/early morning. Compression shirt comes this week and I will have to wrap my head around being cold. Being cold has always been my least favorite thing, so maybe being cold adapted will help me tolerate our cold winters in eastern Washington.

Since learning all this, at least 2 of our 5 sons are exploring their own health. One, at 36, has osteopenia...way too young. The other, 30, is an elite runner with no health problems, but needs to learn about CT for training.

Hubby is 100% behind me, others are skeptical, some mocking. Tough bananas, I say. That iceberg of illness is gonna melt and I'm going to be OPTIMAL!

Here's to ya, Jack!

### fitness@home — 2013-10-15T03:17:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/post-97865>

Julie5, I love your attitude! Good luck in your journey...

### caroline — 2013-10-15T03:31:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/post-97868>

Julie - great post - thanks for sharing! Please consider starting a journal in the journal section. It is a great way to commit to this journey and we can follow along and follow your progress and you can help others too. Just copy this post to a journal. Let me know if you need help finding it or with anything else!

JK likes journals so he can get to know you a little. Please tell us about where you live and how your pregnancies went and your EMF exposure at home and at work etc. etc. It is all massively important .........

### nicld — 2013-10-15T03:52:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/post-97874>

Julie, great saying "That iceberg of illness is gonna melt and I'm going to be OPTIMAL!" Welcome

### Julie5 — 2013-10-15T04:39:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/post-97879>

THanks for the welcome! ANd I think I will start a journal. Great suggestion. I AM committed to this. My doc said, after I asked how long I should be on a ketgenic diet, that considering my makeup, the rest of my life. This is the perfect place for support!

### caroline — 2013-10-15T05:11:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-only-the-tip-of-the-iceberg.8447/post-97881>

This is an amazing journey of a lifetime..... welcome aboard!

When my tests came back there was only one piece of good news: my HDL is 84. In January I had 5 stents put in my heart, but the cardiologist said there is no heart disease. The naturopath told me that without the high HDL I would have died of a massive heart attack like my brother did at the same age. All my other numbers were either way too low or too high. All my supps/meds now cost me about $250 a month, but I feel that's better than an early death. Everyone in my family died early. I'm the only one left.

I also found out I have 2 genetic mutations that prevent, if I understand correctly, enzymes from being utilized, and I learned I have celiac disease. That was a shocker since I have for many years made all our own homemade whole wheat bread. Now instead of feeling like I am generally healthy, I feel like I am a long way from there.

So for the last 2 weeks I have been eating the leptin RX (except for oysters). We started using oil lamps in our bedroom since the breaker is off in that room and I wear amber goggles in the evening/early morning. Compression shirt comes this week and I will have to wrap my head around being cold. Being cold has always been my least favorite thing, so maybe being cold adapted will help me tolerate our cold winters in eastern Washington.

Since learning all this, at least 2 of our 5 sons are exploring their own health. One, at 36, has osteopenia...way too young. The other, 30, is an elite runner with no health problems, but needs to learn about CT for training.

Hubby is 100% behind me, others are skeptical, some mocking. Tough bananas, I say. That iceberg of illness is gonna melt and I'm going to be OPTIMAL!

Here's to ya, Jack!

### Julie5 — 2013-10-15T05:54:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-97884>

It was suggested that I give a bit more history of myself. I had a typical childhood, growing up in wet and dreary Western Washington. I didn't know summer was sunny in June and July in other parts of the country. Nevertheless, living on a lake and near a river, I was barefoot and carefree most of Spring into Fall. By the time I was in high school I began to have minor weight issues. that have lasted to one degree or another all my life.

When I got married at 21, I immediately got pregnant, and in spite of every method of birth control, I had 5 sons in 6 1/2 years. That was during the 70s when having children naturally was the rage. Only with the last one did i have a small shot of narcotic to take some of the pain away during transition. I nursed them all for as long as they would. We laughed that for nearly 10 years I was barefoot and pregnant! Every pregnancy was normal.

One of our sons ended up with ADD. He was medicated until 8th grade when he decided to go off it and control his behavioral responses himself. Another had dairy allergy.

We live in a small town in Eastern Washington. Very agricultural. As a result we have been exposed to lots of farm chemicals. On the bright side, there is no other pollution. We live in the middle of farmland now on the bank of the Yakima River. Daily we get fresh air and beautiful, restful views. Ours is section of river that is a refuge for birds of all kinds. Calms the nerves and reminds me that God created all this for our pleasure.

Mike and I love to ride recumbent trikes, but that is now put on hold until I am healed and the labs say so.

I have always been a stay at home mom and now grandma (of 20!). I enjoy baking bread, reading, crocheting, and playing Words With Friends. Now I do it with goggles.

Emf exposure: typical. 2 computers, iPad, iPhone, 2 TVs,but not in the bedroom.

THat's me! I love my life, my family and all the things that we are involved in. I want to be around awhile to enjoy it all.

### Julie5 — 2013-10-15T05:59:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-97885>

Caroline, we always had a girls name picked out. And at first I thought the 3 boys I had would drive me nuts. Then by the time I was pregnant with the 5th, I was AFRAID I'd have a girl. They were a handful when they were little, but by the time they were teens, we were having a great time with them. The only downside, and this is the only place I can express it, is that my daughters in law haven't embraced us as family. Therefore we are left out of a lot of family things that their parents are included in. It breaks my heart, but I will not interfere. Mom did that enough for several generations.

Hi to Julie5.

5 stents and no disease? Did your cardiologist say what the problem was then? I have read a fascinating book called Reverse Heart Disease now by Dr. Stephen Sinatra. He goes into a lot of detail about the heart and it's arteries, cholesterol and its influences. It's worth a read to see what, if anything, you could do for yourself that you aren't already doing. I wish we had more forward thinking doctors in the UK who were prepared to look in a little more depth at the health issues that our health care service is not addressing properly. They all seem afraid to stick their necks out and say "hey, this isn't working is it, let's try something else". Like Dr. Kruse, Dr. Sinatra believes that all illness stems from inflammation and that inflammation is caused by a lack of insight into how our bodies function and what they need so to do. I think they are almost singing from the same hymn sheet but maybe with a slightly different perspective, not surprising as one is a cardiologist, the other a neurosurgeon.

I hope you make progress in optimizing your physical self, having a supportive OH is a huge bonus, whilst mine has permitted me to tinker a fair bit with his diet, mention earthing, reduced evening lighting and horror of horrors removing the bedroom TV would be grounds for having me committed

### sooperb — 2013-10-17T23:03:25+0300

### PaulG — 2013-10-18T02:34:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-98197>

High homocystein levels can be caused by methylation defects, for example if you are homozygous on the C677 gene then your body has 70% reduction in methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase enzyme activity

this enzyme converts 5,10-methylenetetrahydrofolate to 5-methyltetrahydrofolate. This reaction is required for the multistep process that converts the amino acid homocysteine to another amino acid, methionine. The body uses methionine to make proteins and other important compounds

Suggest you get a 23andMe test, get the results analyzed at mthfr.net, look at the methylation thread here and the phoenix rising forum

Anyway, thats what I did - need to get my homocystein rechecked.

### Julie5 — 2013-10-25T05:10:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-98979>

I went to see my doc today. He verified that both my progesterone, testosterone and estrogen were all too low. He put me on prometrium and wants to put me on estrogen in pellet form that is embedded under the skin once every 4 months.. I'm already being treated for the low testosterone.

Another good thing that has come of all of this is that my eyesight has improved. My doc said that that's not unusual when the body is finally getting nourished.
He also gave me the test results of my saliva test. Yup, sure enough, bad results. I am a mess! And its costing about $500 a month in meds, supplements and doctor visits, which isn't covered under our major medical coverage. Better to spend the money and do Dr.K's protocols, though, than keel over young like my dad, brother and sister did.

But the real reason I went to see him today is because, for the last month, every time I exert myself the least little bit, my upper chest starts to burn, and if the exertion is a bit harder, it radiates down my arms. Doc verified that I don't have an infection but said I need an angiogram ASAP. Since I just had 5 stents put in my heart in Jan, I'm surprised that I'm having symptoms of angina again. As luck would have it, the cardiologist's office is closed until Monday. I am going to lay very low until then.

My appetite is under control. No cravings. I am sleeping some better but have chronic back pain. Doc wants me on hydrocodone, but I"m too sensitive to it. I start CT in earnest tonight, so hopefully in a few weeks the inflammation will be a thing of the past and my back will stay in alignment.

### Julie5 — 2013-10-29T15:47:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-99344>

Last night we had 2 of our sons and their families over (8 children between them) to carve pumpkins and roast marshmallows. It was cold enough that everyone had jackets on. But not me. I was warm enough that I had short sleeves on and went barefoot most of the time. I asked my doc about being warm all the time now. He felt it was because my metabolism is higher.

We have a son who is a chiropractor. He was trained in the typical way (and he is an excellent chiropractor), but that training included the standard heart health way of eating. When I first started the Leptin RX he was not onlly skeptical, but concerned for me. Last night though, I told him about my blood work. Since doing the reset my cholesterol has dropped from 224 to 180-ish. He was so amazed he immediately told his wife. I sent all my 5 sons an email this morning to get their testing done. They may be young still, but my dad had a heart attack at the age of 30. My brother had quad bypass at the age of 40. Both died in their 50s.

Good health and longevity, here I come!

Since I got kind of a late start on having a family I don't have any grandchildren yet.

How wonderful to live in the middle of farmland!

I like to play Words With Friends too, although now I'm trying to limit the time on my iPad

Wishing you all the best on your journey towards optimal health, Julie!

### Julie5 — 2013-10-31T06:17:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-99495>

My journey continues, but I keep running into obstacles: dinner invites later in the day than I should eat; meetings that take me into the night and too much exposure to EMF. Not finding time for CT.

On the plus side : I had to make an appt with my eye doc for a new prescription. My distance vision has improved so much that i have to look over the top of my glasses. They office secretary was both skeptical and interested and requested that I bring a list of all my meds and supplements.

Tonight we had dinner with friends. The hostess was kind enough to cook to my needs. We had beef and sauteed mushrooms with shrimp, broccoli with butter and salad. Since they all knew I had had a stay in the hospital, they asked about my health and what the doc says to do. What a natural way to begin telling them about JK, ketogenic diets, myths of heart disease, etc. One man especially is now interested and wants me to send the link to JK.com.

My doc is concerned about my sleep. because I have chronic low back pain, which I believe is simply from inflammations and will be taken care of when I get to that point with CT, he prescribed a low dose of hydrocodone. He said sleep is my number one need right now if I want healing to begin. So with the help of the hydrocodone, supplements and 2 other prescriptions for sleep,I am finally getting 7-8 hours of sleep. It feels marvelous!

THanks for all your good wishes for my health!

It's slow, but the changes ARE being made. I don't seem to be able to focus on more than a thing or 2 at a time.

Last night I started CT. I used a wide straw to breathe through and was able to keep my face in the water for several minutes until I began to feel claustrophobic. I made my ice packs day before yesterday using rubbing alcohol and water. I think I will try to do the face dunks twice a day for a week and then use the packs on my torso twice a day.

Then you do it bare skinned until you tolerate it for the same amount of time. You have to be careful to monitor skin temp. I got an infrared thermometer off Amazon for that purpose. Then you proceed to ice baths. I am anxious to get to this phase because of the inflammation in my arteries.

THanks for the chastising. Hubby gave me what for this morning too. We don't keep things in the house that tempt me. Never have. Halloween candy should have been handed over to someone else.

There are some things we keep in the house that I can't eat, but they don't tempt me. I make our own bread so hubs can have sandwiches. I'm celiac, and I wouldn't have thought that would be a blessing, but it is until I get past this rocky start. We have a storage room for food. Since we are isolated, we keep a year's supply of most things in there. We have for years and years. My brother in law went in there one day to get something. He came out and said, " You don't have a section for junk food?" NO WAY. Just canned goods, stuff I can myself, and basics like spices, wheat, Co oil, etc. So I "shop" from there and only go the grocery store when I run out of eggs and other fresh things. Hubby has supportively adapted to fending for himself for breakfast and lunch. I wish he would switch sides and eat like I do. But he's a big boy.

New morning, new determination.

Thanks to all of you!!!

### Julie5 — 2013-11-03T06:59:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-99698>

Ahhh....back on track and it feels good. I think one of the reasons I had a meltdown was not reading enough of the blogs. I went back and read the latest one and read JK's really good explanation of why a ketotic diet is important for post menopausal women. It also answered my question about why I shouldn't be supplemented with estrogen. My doc says my estrogen level is low. Well, I just learned that's a good thing, and that it may just help with iodine absorption which would help with my sleep. I liked what he said so much that I printed it out.

JK also answered my specific question about seafood and mercury. He said it's not an issue b/c seafood is also rich in selenium (and i am supplementing selenium as well.)

i had some of my grandchildren today while mommy was away. WE had so much fun, but boy, having kids is definitely for the young! I ran up and down the stairs several times. What a treat to not have my knee hurt! It hasn't hurt since I"ve been doing the Leptin RX.

Time to face dunk and then off to bed.

### Julie5 — 2013-11-04T07:33:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-99777>

Today was a pretty good day. I started the day with leftover fish heated up with a lot of butter. I had to have lunch, sort of. It was 2 pm and then I had dinner at 6. Although it's not ideal, I made a shake out of spinach, heavy cream, coconut oil, cocoa, stevia and a half scoop of protein powder. My doc wants me to drink 1/2 C of heavy cream at bedtime, but I'm compromising by having it at dinner. He says itwill help with my sleep.

My husband is a prayerful man. Today he came away from his devotions, took me in his arms, and said that this Leptin RX will work for me, sooner rather than later. I asked about that, and he said he felt it very strongly as he was praying for me. Bless his faithful heart! He has such love for me and for God. I couldn't be a more blessed woman.

### Julie5 — 2014-02-19T05:58:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-111124>

i've been absent from the forums and the blogs for awhile. My father in law has been dying of old age for the last few months, and we were his primary care takers. He died 2 weeks ago at 89, having been married to his sweetheart for almost 70 years. We had a joyous family reunion as we celebrated a life well lived.

As for my own optimal journey, I have been faithful to the principles I know to do for the last month. I haven't given in to temptation, and now temptations don't exsist: yoohoo! I find it very difficult, though, to eat well and right when we are traveling and staying with well-meaning relatives. I recently discovered (the questionably good taste of) sardines and how easy they are to tote around. If anyone has a brand they prefer, I'd like some suggestions. I can only get the King Oscar and Bumblebee locally,but I'm willing to order.

After the last webinar i got out my October lab results. What a disaster my body is. But even so, I know I can change it with persistence and study and application. Today was my first cold bath. The water was only 60 degrees, but it's a start and it's progress because I conquered my fear of cold. I found that if I distracted myself with music and reading that it was easier to tolerate. I made it 30 minutes.

My next hurdle is to figure out how to stay in bed until sunrise. My sleep is bad even with a hand full of supplements and prescriptions. I get about 6 hours. And with my chronic low back pain, staying in bed while in pain is...a pain.

Jack, if you read this, I have a question. Our bathroom faces east and we have 2 big windows in it. If I CT in the morning with the sun streaming in,does that count for sun exposure? It doen't actually shine in my face; it shines above my head.

I was talking about all this with my sis in law. She said "Some things become religions of their own and distract from the more important things in life." I shot back, "It's true that there is a learning curve for all new ventures. When these things become habitual and my health has improved, I will be better prepared to serve others." She had no answer to that.

Well, I'm off to bed.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-19T12:13:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-111161>

Julie.........glass blocks UV spectrum. Open the window and your issue is solved. You only need a a bit of sunlight on the retina to set the photoperiod. this is how the vitamin A cycle begins in the AM. Darkness is when it ends. UV light sets the vitamin D cycle in the brain.

### Julie5 — 2014-02-27T06:53:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-112413>

Well, progress is sometimes slow, but I'm trying to be steady. My CT today was 57 deg up to my neck, but arms out. I have discovered that if my arms are in the whole time, then the whole time is significantly less. So today it was 35 minutes total with 5 min including my arms. AS soon as my arms hit the water I started shaking all over. Surprisingly the 57 didn't make me overly cold until my arms went in.

On a lighter note, I had to pee afterward. The seat in our usually cold bathroom felt quite warm!

THe diet is going awesome. Occasionally I add just a tiny bit of something (like bell peppers) for taste. Or a little hard cheese. My life saver has been fat bombs after dinner since I have a hard time getting enough. Or hot chocolate with half coconut milk and half water.
The other night I made a cauliflower soup and added a ton of seafood. I rivaled any clam chowder I have ever had, and I didn't even miss the flour or corn starch. Tonight hubs came in and said that he was sorry, he must have tempted me beyond my ability to resist. He had put a bag of caramels on the counter. He thought I ate them. HA! I just put them in the cupboard without having eaten a single one.

In the last 6 weeks I have lost only 8 lbs, but hubs says he thinks my body composition is beginning to change. When I asked him why, he said, "Because you look so beautiful to me." Not very objective, but hey, I'll take it anyway.

### sooperb — 2014-02-27T10:36:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-112424>

Oh sniff, what a lovely thing to say. I'm glad you're shifting something even if slowly. Well done on the caramels, I find I think about maybe buying something sweet and mostly I then think about eating it and suddenly it just loses it's appeal, it's as if the taste just isn't attractive any more. Except for jelly snakes , just occasionally I find myself wanting some, buy them and then find I only need a few and that's it, the taste isn't so good and I stop. This is a big change for me!

### Julie5 — 2014-02-28T16:34:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-112646>

The last few days I have been waking up at 4 a.m. And that's with 4 sleep meds and a half dozen supplements which are supposed to help me sleep. It doesn't help that on Thursday mornings I HAVE to be up at 4:30. When I have one morning like that, the next couple of days are also like that. Then, that morphs into every day being an early rise day. And I can't just stay in bed until daylight because of chronic back pain. I am considering resigning from my Thursday morning commitment and being consistent with going to bed at 9:30.

The hardest leg of the 3 legged stool for me is light. Even though I am up plenty early, I haven't gone outside at sunrise enough to make it a habit. And it's too bad too because we live on the bank of river in the countryside and the view of the sunrise down stream is always lovely, clear or cloudy. I know that's an ideal time to ground as well. I simply have to find a way to make it a habit. The cold weather keeps me inside. But I will unearth my sheepskin rug and ground myself sitting or laying on it while the sun comes up. When it warms up I may even lay on it nude since we have no neighbors to peek in on me.

CT continues to challenge my resolve,but I am doing it anyway. I have so much inflammation, between heart disease, chronic back pain, constantly runny nose, carrying around too much weight. One day it doesn't seem so bad; the next I shake and shiver so badly. If I keep my upper arms out of the water I can tolerate it a lot more. So i only immerse my arms the last 5minutes. We are into our second week of CT. I hope I see some signs of improvement SOMEWHERE soon. But probably not until I get all three legs going.

### sooperb — 2014-02-28T16:59:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-112654>

Julie could you not buy a pair of sheepskin slippers or leather moccasins just for standing outside in the morning? As long as the underside is skin you can ground with them.

### nonchalant — 2014-02-28T17:03:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-112656>

Julie, I think your future self will thank you when you resign that commitment.

### Julie5 — 2014-02-28T18:15:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-112670>

As for sheepskin slippers or moccasins, that is great idea. And I do think a resignation is in order. Our 2 year commitment is up in 7 weeks, and the next 2 weeks the facility is closed.

### Julie5 — 2014-03-04T19:13:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-113386>

Bad night, last night. I ate my normal breakfast, but by 1 pm I felt surprisingly hungry. So I had shrimp with coconut oil. Then around 5:30 I felt hungry again. I wanted to focus more on fat, so I ate a tablespoon or so of coconut oil and made myself a smoothie with cream, coconut oil, raw eggs, cocoa powder and stevia. It was more than I really could get down, and I ended up drinking too much of it. Within a half hour I felt too full and rather nauseated. By bedtime at 9:30 I was feeling downright ill. I went to bed and surprisingly I went to sleep rather soon. But I woke up at 2:30 with diarrhea, and every hour I had to get up.I don't know if it was too much fat, generally too much food, or what. But I wont' be doing that combination again. Ugh.

### sooperb — 2014-03-04T19:40:13+0200

do you generally eat eggs?

### Julie5 — 2014-03-07T01:45:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-113864>

Caroline, it was the coconut oil. I am quite accustomed to eggs.

I am having a bit of success--I am down to 50 degree water for CT, and after the initial shock, it felt great! I still can't get my shoulders in, or I will last only a few minutes. And yesterday I was down a lb and today another 1/2 lb. My experience since starting this is that when the scale goes down, it stays down. I'm down 10 lb in 6 weeks. And that's with a less than perfect performance.

Another bit of success. I have been able to reduce my sleep meds. I had to. Staying on the same dose has been making me woozy during the day.

### caroline — 2014-03-07T01:47:46+0200

You are doing great Julie!

### sooperb — 2014-03-07T11:53:44+0200

I am so glad it's working for you. Maybe I should just spoon CO from the jar and have nothing else for a week to see if I can kick start any significant weight loss lol. I won't of course, I like CO but generally it has to be married with something more palatable even if it's only coffee.

### Calum — 2016-03-03T01:37:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iceberg-meltdown-begins.8448/post-190109>

> [Julie5 said:](/goto/post?id=97880)
>
> I'm 58 and thought I was generally in good health, except I struggled with insomnia for the last 4 years (severe, can't sleep without drugs). Then I started noticing even the drugs didn't work. I developed a runny nose, a tingly toe, my hair started falling out like crazy (I have to wear a wig now). And no matter what I did, I couldn't get the extra 40 lbs off. In frustration I started searching online and found Jack Kruse. What a blessing that has proven to be! I asked him my first question and he put me on the road to recovery. He suggested i needed to have a serious talk with my doc. Since I knew a regular family doc couldn't do all the tests I knew I needed to do (per Jack's recommendations), I found an MD/naturopath. He had never heard of Jack, but his recommendations and the tests ordered, the ketogenic diet, the supplements suggested and the prescriptions given were right on target. To me, a second doc was verification that what Jack suggests is exactly what I need.
>
> When my tests came back there was only one piece of good news: my HDL is 84. In January I had 5 stents put in my heart, but the cardiologist said there is no heart disease. The naturopath told me that without the high HDL I would have died of a massive heart attack like my brother did at the same age. All my other numbers were either way too low or too high. All my supps/meds now cost me about $250 a month, but I feel that's better than an early death. Everyone in my family died early. I'm the only one left.
>
> I also found out I have 2 genetic mutations that prevent, if I understand correctly, enzymes from being utilized, and I learned I have celiac disease. That was a shocker since I have for many years made all our own homemade whole wheat bread. Now instead of feeling like I am generally healthy, I feel like I am a long way from there.
>
> So for the last 2 weeks I have been eating the leptin RX (except for oysters). We started using oil lamps in our bedroom since the breaker is off in that room and I wear amber goggles in the evening/early morning. Compression shirt comes this week and I will have to wrap my head around being cold. Being cold has always been my least favorite thing, so maybe being cold adapted will help me tolerate our cold winters in eastern Washington.
>
> Since learning all this, at least 2 of our 5 sons are exploring their own health. One, at 36, has osteopenia...way too young. The other, 30, is an elite runner with no health problems, but needs to learn about CT for training.
>
> Hubby is 100% behind me, others are skeptical, some mocking. Tough bananas, I say. That iceberg of illness is gonna melt and I'm going to be OPTIMAL!
>
> Here's to ya, Jack!

Can I please ask what goggles you are using?

Cal

# Thread: Some beginning labwork

### Rick — 2013-10-15T16:19:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-beginning-labwork.8451/post-97901>

Hello all,

Today I see Dana Cohen in Manhattan from the PPN but I wanted to ask if anyone on JK forum might have some insights as well: thyroid panel shows T3 at 4.2 out of 4.4 and T3 is 34 times > rT3 after scaling the units. HS-CRP is .71 mg/l but Pg/E2 ratio is fine but progesterone is quite low and the ratio is only good because estradiol is extremely low. Cortisol diurnal profile is generally the right shape curve and even tho' midnight is relatively elevated, all four samples are on the edge or below the shaded areas for low med & high (noon is way low).

I was expecting pregnenolone steal because T levels are low with long term hypogonadism but the low cortisol would suggest adrenal fatigue?

Yes Dr. Cohen will order a boatload of more labs to pin this down, but any comments or suggestions from here would be much appreciated!

Thanks, Rick H.

### Lahelada — 2013-10-16T02:03:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-and-showering.8455/post-97924>

Just read that Vitamin D might be produced in the sebum of the skin and not immediately either. Furthermore that washing with soap and water "washes off" the vitamin D or rather the cholesterol derivative in our skin which is in the process of being converted to vitamin D in the course of 48 hours.
So prudent to leave non strategic bits unwashed or just a bit of hokum ? Supposedly it is new research but have not found the study.

### Lahelada — 2013-10-16T03:54:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-and-showering.8455/post-97948>

"a few more tidbits prephase 7-Dehydrocholesterol (7-DHC) is found at its highest level in the epidermis.7-DHC is also the preface to cholesterin necessary for the construction of cell walls. " So in other words the question is whether synthesis takes place in the epidermis or the sebum layer. Dry skin would therefore also be an influencing factor in the manufacture of D3 and there might be a co relation of moisturising creams,also that may be perceived to do the job but might effectively form a barrier.

_Professor Binkley and his colleagues found that surfers and skate-boarders in Hawaii had levels around 30 ng/ml with the highest level being 60 ng/ml. Why would light-skinned sun-drenched outdoor sport enthusiasts in Hawaii have an average level of 30 ng/ml, while very dark-skinned hunter-gatherers in Africa have levels of 50 ng/ml?_

### Lahelada — 2017-08-14T06:29:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-and-showering.8455/post-221628>

### Lahelada — 2017-08-14T15:49:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-and-showering.8455/post-221643>

Age
BMI play a role in skin vitamin D synthesis as well as
skin type,
the pre-tanning status,
the thickness of the callus
current air pollution
angle of radiation (direct radiation, indirect reflections, etc.)Think albedo effect especially.

I listed these as there is differences of opinion. Hollick says that skincare products do not play a role, while Dr Wunsch says they do. Ingredients in these products can alter the skin callus, act as solar filter ( paraben being a particularly ubiquitious offender) On one hand when you wash, the keratinous layer of the skin swells temporarily ( less penetrable to light)but on the other hand you slough off dead skin cells ( more penetrable to UV again). Before you start sloughing, this increases risk of erythema, which allows UV to penetrate to deeper skin layers which again you do not want.

Practicals.
So our skin is really not made for daily washing for the above mentioned reasons. I restrict use of products to strategic parts in summer. It works just fine. I use soap with animal tallow only. I also use an Epsom Salt (magnesium sulphate)/water mix on my skin. Preloading with IR light ( morning sun before UV shows up) helps too

Another thing I learned is that it is important to sun year round. At the beginning of the UVB season you have the least tan,so you have the potential to make more D. Some people have measured and find that at the beginning of the season they have the highest rapid climb and by end off summer it is declining. The early season climb seems to be more drastic in people who were previously low in Vit D. I hypothesize that this is a protective measure for a winter,not modern winter with central heating ,but winter of our ancestors. So if you test it might be advisable to bear that in mind. A lot about Vitamin D synthesis is not yet understood.

### JoeT — 2017-08-15T01:33:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-and-showering.8455/post-221666>

Lahelada, thanks so much for the answer!

Sorry I thought i sent this link about it looks like i did not.
[https://www.vitamindcouncil.org/washing-away-vitamin-d/](https://www.vitamindcouncil.org/washing-away-vitamin-d/)
It states that " There is definitive evidence that the secretions from the skin contains precursors of vitamin D, which after irradiation are to be reabsorbed by the body, and the removal of which tends to produce a dearth of the vitamin unless it be supplied in some other form.”

So in your excellent response, I see you reference the effects of skin care products in relation to their ability to alter the callus and/or to act as a filter. However, are you aware about the idea posed in the article about this re-absorption period that could be up to 24-48 hours?

Thanks! Joe

### JanSz — 2017-08-16T15:28:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-and-showering.8455/post-221766>

Whatever I do is a subject to variety of unknown and known variables.

So, I like to bounce all considerations against tests.
And now I wonder.
I test my vit D two ways.
One is the usual
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
the other is using
Spectracell Micronutrient Analysis.
Note that Spectracell test is functional and sort of similar to HgA1c as it test averages over many months.

I tested in 2016 and year latter, couple weeks ago, so in 2017.

in 2016 I had very high (over 100 on 25-hydroxy ) and satisfactory on Spectracell
that was mostly due to sunlight and supplementation (10000iu/day)

in 2017 tested only Spectracell, my satisfactory on Spectracell (minimally) went up
that was mostly due to sunlight plus
**16min/day Red Sperti from touching the lamp distance**
and 2000iu/day supplement

2016 ---(Spectracell) Vitamin D3 (Cholecalciferol)= 66 >50%
2017 ---(Spectracell) Vitamin D3 (Cholecalciferol)= 67 >50%

### Outlier — 2017-08-18T05:49:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-and-showering.8455/post-221859>

So did anyone find anything definitive about whether or not you can 'wash off' Vit D? I totally get it there are ar a lot of factors involved. However, when I was reading about this before joining the blog, I found the information confusing can contradictory. On person said there are D3 receptors were found on the skin surface (but did NOT) state conclusively that synthesis occurred there. However, someone else stated it in fact did and quoted that 24-48 hour interval. Then someone else said it was deep in the dermis and couldn't be washed off any more than a tan could be. It is not clear to me from general Internet reading exactly where that photosynthesis happens in/on/under the skin. Was it just me that missed it?

### greentrees — 2019-06-25T16:01:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-and-showering.8455/post-273251>

Is there any source on what Dr Wunsch says regarding skincare products or having wet skin? (I can't find)
Is the 'be careful before sloughing' (exfoliating) due to the top layer of dead skin cells protecting against UVC light? What do people think of Rubin and Jack using dry brushes in the webinars to absorb more light? Has that advice changed?

There's a lot of wishy-washy talk with vitamin D. Best advice I've heard is some of it is produced in the natural layer of oil on the surface of the skin and to allow that to reabsorb for at least a few hours, but its not a hard science case [https://vitamind3-cholecalciferol.com/vitamin-d-from-safe-sunlight-exposure/](https://vitamind3-cholecalciferol.com/vitamin-d-from-safe-sunlight-exposure/)

This raises interesting questions like what happens when we interfere with the top two layers of our skin with exfoliation, tanning oils like mineral oil and moisturisers that feature coconut oil. Could a coconut oil based moisturiser raise skin SPF versus a mineral oil based one? Moisturiser's can also double skin hydration after 2 weeks, is this kind of intervention helpful or harmful? Is it swelling for example. [(Click research tab: https://dermaltherapy.com.au/products/body-care/dry-skin-lotion)](https://dermaltherapy.com.au/products/body-care/dry-skin-lotion)

To the rabbit hole. My biggest question is what happens when our skin is devoid of cholesterol because of skin care products and if that occurs at all. There's an interesting one called Cerave which includes cholesterol, skin lipids (ceramides) and nicatonamide (niacin) with coconut oil fats. But it has parabens, if they act as a solar filter or reflector it might rule it out (need a source on this too, listening to Wunsch 2016 talk hoping to find)

# Thread: Magnets or metal while CTing?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Julie5 — 2013-10-16T08:35:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-97971>

I thought I saw a post that talked about using magnets or metal while CTing. What's that all about? Or did I misread? Countdown to CT: 3 days.

### nicld — 2013-10-16T16:57:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-97999>

I have seen that people bring a magnet with them and it might help with shivers. I bring my #250 pull one and set it beside my tub and I think it helps. I am pretty sure that it helps with my rewarming since it makes the legs tingle. That's just my N=1 though.

Good luck with the CT. Are you just going to get in a full tub or slowly fill? Now I just jump in and it still takes the breath away for about 2 minutes, then all good

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-16T16:58:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-98001>

You don't want to use metal when CTing - it can cause issues for your skin etc.

but magnets can be beneficial...

### Julie5 — 2013-10-16T20:25:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-98023>

Good luck with the CT. Are you just going to get in a full tub or slowly fill? Now I just jump in and it still takes the breath away for about 2 minutes, then all good \[/QUOTE\]

In a swimming situation I just jump in, but the first couple of times I think i need to go the slow route. Do you use a compression shirt?

### nicld — 2013-10-16T21:47:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-98033>

No, I don't use a compression shirt. I might think about that this winter though. Most of my fat is in my thighs and booty so as long as they are under I think I am doing good plus I have a deep tub.

Oooh swimming would be fun. I might suggest getting neoprene shoes and gloves to wear. I know when my hands and feet get too cold, i'm done but that is my n=1.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-16T21:53:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-98036>

FWIW I started w/a compression shirt and by mid winter i was getting in the tank au nautral...

### nicld — 2013-10-16T23:10:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-98038>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=98036)
>
> FWIW I started w/a compression shirt and by mid winter i was getting in the tank au nautral...

What is the benefit of a compression shirt? I have never used one since my water goes up to my tatas.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-16T23:16:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-98041>

IIRC it helps you tolerate the cold as you progress into the full body CT.

I started out CTing using compression gear and ice packs, then slowly transitioned to CT in ICE Water w/ compression gear, then to bikini, then au natural.
