Thanks, I think I will try getting more magnets and ditch the earthing pad idea. For the winter I must might have to spend some time in the basement.

I wonder if having outdoor carpeting on it reduces the effect?

### Cosmo — 2014-02-22T21:04:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-111720>

I personally sleep MUCH better now that I'm earthing - deeper and more complete it seems. With earthing, there may be a thing where it is more beneficial to put your own grounding rod in the ground outside your bedroom window rather than plug into house electrical grounding ports in the outlets in your home. I am still trying to uncover the full truth on that but for now, I have put my own stake in the ground out the back and sleep connected to it. It is awesome.

### scottyc — 2014-03-04T05:19:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113283>

I met with an EMF consultant (Steve Magee) and he advised against the grounding sheets due to stray voltage. He said that while plugging directly into the ground is better than the wall socket, even the rods plugged into the ground can have stray voltage 'contamination' due to underground power lines. He said, that he doesn't even walk barefoot in his own home due to this 'stray voltage' issue.
Like Cosmo, I slept grounded in my last house, and noticed deeper sleep. However I think the stray voltage issue is something to keep in mind, depending on where you live and your proximity to underground power lines.

### Cosmo — 2014-03-04T07:34:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113289>

> [scottyc said:](/goto/post?id=113283)
>
> I met with an EMF consultant (Steve Magee) and he advised against the grounding sheets due to stray voltage. He said that while plugging directly into the ground is better than the wall socket, even the rods plugged into the ground can have stray voltage 'contamination' due to underground power lines. He said, that he doesn't even walk barefoot in his own home due to this 'stray voltage' issue.
> Like Cosmo, I slept grounded in my last house, and noticed deeper sleep. However I think the stray voltage issue is something to keep in mind, depending on where you live and your proximity to underground power lines.

That's awesome that you met steven magee - I've been watching his youtube channel for a while now. Lots of fascinating content there! I want to get to the bottom of the stray voltage controversy because it seems like a lot of people are getting benefits from simply plugging into the house electrical ground and yet I had so many sleepless nights when i did as such.

I actually am part of a discussion about that where there is a very interesting theory about grounding and restless sleeping. It has to do with getting earth energy from the K1 accupoint. If you want to read the full details the thread is on the earthing forum here: [http://earthingforum.com/does-your-ground-rod-have-stray-voltage-t8.html](http://earthingforum.com/does-your-ground-rod-have-stray-voltage-t8.html)
In fact, that whole thread is a discussion on stray voltage and how it affects earthing.

### scottyc — 2014-03-04T20:25:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113404>

Cosmo - thanks for the info! Yes, Steve mentioned to me the same method for testing and it's on my to do list .

### Tanya — 2014-03-04T21:25:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113413>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=99133)
>
> Does anyone have this and only ground it outside? Did it help? I read that it is not advisable to use this if you need to plug it in to an outlet.
>
> I do not live in a high EMF house and just cannot swing a magnetico pad for our king size bed but would like to do something. With winter coming it is going to get really hard to get outside to ground when there is 2 feet of snow out.
>
> Also anyone use the auto set pad? I spend almost 10 hours a week in my car.
>
> Thanks
>
> Nicole

Nicole, I have and use just about everything that the Earthing website has to offer. When I read here about all that controversy, I immediately discontinued doing that. After awhile, I could really tell what a mistake it was on my part - I was in so much more pain again. So, the sheet, pillowcase, throw (I use it instead of the blanket) are back in my bed - connected to a rod in my backyard.

About the car seat - it was not doing anything for me while i kept it on the seat, but when I placed it on the car floor and kept one foot on it while driving I noticed a huge difference. I also tested myself with the body voltage meter and that was also helpful to see.

One more thing, I've had Magnetico for over 4 years now and I found it helpful, but it alone could not relief me of all that pain and inflammation I was dealing with until I started using the earthing products. Funny thing is, I don't have any choice but to use the outside grounding rod, when we tested our outlets, they were not grounded....

### sooperb — 2014-03-04T22:36:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113430>

I've had the earthing half sheet for around 2 years, 18 months of that using the house earth via the socket and then I changed to a grounding rod sunk into my front garden. I also have a mat which I use under my computer and put my bare feet on. My experience is a positive one, before earthing my sleep was awful, after using it for a few weeks, it improved a great deal. Most mornings I had ankle pain on rising, this has disappeared completely.

There is an earthing thread on this forum somewhere, it was the reason I signed up, I only found the other stuff after a few visits when I decided to look around.

### [email redacted] — 2014-03-05T06:48:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113514>

I use an earthing sheet actually a 'fitted' sheet on a queen bed (better than the half sheet). I think it has helped my sleep I notice also I 'like' my bed more kind of a subjective thing but it feels real. I stick the rod in the earth not the socket which I feel more comfortable doing. But one think which I thought was kind of 'funny' and maybe a bit deceptive on the part of the company................they have this 'tester' and supposedly when the sheet is grounded a green light come on on the tester. I used this and sure eneough on ANY other surface it does not light up but bingo on the sheet it does!! Well one day a few weeks ago the gardeners pulled the wire out of the rod by mistake it was completly disconnected from the rod BUT as a lark I thought let me test it now and would you believe it it lit up just as before. So that 'tester' is testing SOMETHING but NOT that it is grounded. Now I found that kind of deceptive as I say. I don't have an explanation of WHAT it is testing. Any ideas anyone?

### Cosmo — 2014-03-05T07:32:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113517>

That is an oddity to be sure.

### [email redacted] — 2014-03-05T18:39:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113614>

Well the thing is Cosmo it seems to test ONLY for the 'main wire' being attached (fastened) to the fastener on the sheet. If you unhook those things the light goes out. BUT what I find kind of deceptive is and maybe this was my mis-understanding is the implication is once that little green light goes on it is GROUNDED. I imagined it was some kind of measure of electrical exchange between the sheet and the ground and it is NOT! It is merely telling you it is connected (fastened) and then I wonder how does that work? Like what is the 'electrical exchange' that causes that little green light to go on. And then I'm thinking 'scam' like did they deliberatly do this just to create an 'impression' I don't really know what more testing I can do, if you have a grounding sheet and my impression is you do just unplug it either from the outlet or from the ground itself if that is what you have and then test it..............does the green light still go on? You're right I could contact the company and maybe I will I just don't 'enjoy' doing that kind of thing.

Hi Tanya, I had a similar experience. My back was much worse at the time, and I loved the way my ground sheet (attached to a rod in my garden) made me feel. I tried discontinuing it and discovered quickly that it was a mistake. Gradually my back got stronger and better, thanks to the EpiPaleo diet and water. Eventually I discovered that I could either take or leave the grounding sheet; I didn't need it so much. Later on, I think it was setting me back, at least while I was in the city, so I stopped using it here. I still use a ground sheet while in the country.

So perhaps a grounding sheet has both good and bad effects, and sometimes the good effects outweigh the bad. I know it was awesome for my back, at one point in my health journey.

### [email redacted] — 2014-03-06T18:09:18+0200

### Cosmo — 2014-03-06T20:42:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113813>

Pat, the tester might be faulty.

But what Tanya said about body voltage is the answer.

Get a multimeter that can test AC voltage. You can see how this works here:
[How To Use A Multimeter To Test Body Voltage](http://earthingforum.com/how-use-multimeter-test-body-voltage-t10.html)

This will let you know if the earthing product is working and then you will have something to compare against when testing your current earthing tester.

### nonchalant — 2014-04-29T17:29:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-123884>

@[Tanya](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6641/), a couple nights ago a storm came through out in the country, and I made the decision to leave my grounding sheet attached to the ground rod. (Usually I hastily disconnect!) I noticed quite a bit of tingling that night, even before it started to rain. It wasn't on the same level as my first night on the sheet, but it was quite pleasant. I kept thinking about my grandfather, who said lightning was fertilizer for crops. I was sure soaking it up! And no, I didn't die after all.

Oh, btw, we got the largest hail I have personally seen that night. The stone DH retrieved was over 2 inches in diameter, and it sure was not the largest one. Nature must realize we need more energy.

# Thread: Heart disease
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heart-disease.8573/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 14

### Julie5 — 2013-10-27T04:04:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heart-disease.8573/post-99136>

So I spent yesterday and today in the hospital. I had angina for several days, then went to my primary care doc. He told me to go to the hospital and get checked out. I had some illusion that it might have been some thing minor. As it turned out I had a small heart attack last night. When the doc did an angiogram and looked at my heart, he said the artery I just had stents put in in January is in bad shape. One stent was collapsing and there were 2 other blockages he stented. He told my hubby that eventually I will have to have a single bypass.
My question is, can I turn that artery around, at least the parts not already stented? Can I keep the stents I have in healthy? Can I prevent open heart surgery? With these protocols?

The meds I am on didn't do their job, so they are being switched. Also I am on a double handful of supplements, some of them for heart.

### caroline — 2013-10-27T05:15:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heart-disease.8573/post-99139>

Julie - am so sorry to hear all of the above....I hope you find answers......xo

you are in my thoughts and prayers..

### Inger — 2013-10-27T08:45:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heart-disease.8573/post-99140>

> [Julie5 said:](/goto/post?id=99136)
>
> So I spent yesterday and today in the hospital. I had angina for several days, then went to my primary care doc. He told me to go to the hospital and get checked out. I had some illusion that it might have been some thing minor. As it turned out I had a small heart attack last night. When the doc did an angiogram and looked at my heart, he said the artery I just had stents put in in January is in bad shape. One stent was collapsing and there were 2 other blockages he stented. He told my hubby that eventually I will have to have a single bypass.
> My question is, can I turn that artery around, at least the parts not already stented? Can I keep the stents I have in healthy? Can I prevent open heart surgery? With these protocols?
>
>
> The meds I am on didn't do their job, so they are being switched. Also I am on a double handful of supplements, some of them for heart.

Julie.... I personally is convinced our body is able to heal in amazing ways. It just depends on, how far are you willing to go, how much comfort and old way of living are you willing to leave behind.

Run away from EMFs. They are poison for your heart. I speak from own experience. I have not a strong heart at all, and I can feel it in my heart when I have been in to much of EMF. It is scary!
What material are the stents in your heart made of?

Nude sunbathing, or at least as much bare skin as you can (nude is magic and makes huge difference in gaining energy from the sun IMHO)

Earthing with your bare feet on the ground or swimming in a river etc. if you can manange, if the temperatures allow, try to get as much bare skin in touch with the grass/earth as possible.

Super strict diet. No dairy, nuts or eggs. A lot of raw seafood and seaweeds. Try raw heart (grassfed or wild), I think it helps strengthen a weak heart, at least it helps me. Sea-buckthorn [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hippophae\_rhamnoides](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hippophae_rhamnoides) is known to help healing your condition too, I think it is a great thing. Eat the whole raw berries if you can get any, or take some juice every day. Great for supporting heart. You might find other herbs too good for cardiovascular health.

I think go careful wit CT. Increase it slowly, not too much in one setting. Start with face dunks and go slow. Take cold showers but only on legs and arms at first. Increase the amount very slow. Keep it cool at home, try to be much outside with as little clothing as you are comfortable with = at the edge of freezing. Do long slow walks in the forest etc. Maybe to put an icepack on your heart is a good idea. I think it will help healing. But only when you are warm otherwise. Because if I get too much cooled down in my whole body I feel not too well in my heart after, like it has troubles working when trying to heat me up again.. so I think if you have a sick heart maybe be careful to get too frosty... like shivering-for-long-after ice cold!.

Sleep... you know how important it is for healing. Avoid stress at any costs.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-27T16:29:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heart-disease.8573/post-99148>

How do you scope your life? The size and outlook of any situation can be impacted by the lens you are looking through. When we examine tiny things we use a microscope, when we look for new things we look via a telescope. When we are buried in our own small world of one we can lose ourself. We can surface to change the setting we are on using a periscope. When you discover where you are, then you can focus on who you are. Who you are is tied to your connection to others as the energy that exists between folks. They feel and value your connection. When this bond is not present we use a stethoscope to check our wellness quotient. If you want to find the secrets of "your universe", think in terms of energy, frequency and vibration connecting you to others. The more connections you find, the more life support you'll have coursing through your veins. Today, kindle the flame you've tended your whole life and let the wind scatter it to those you care about. Share your warmth with others, because this is what determines your life course. Today is today, only today. Tomorrow...it will only be yesterday..............yes you can change the course of that artery but you must begin to get things right more than you get them wrong.........otherwise you'll run out of time.

Today, kindle the flame you've tended your whole life and let the wind scatter it to those you care about. Share your warmth with others, because this is what determines your life course. Today is today, only today. Tomorrow...it will only be yesterday..............yes you can change the course of that artery but you must begin to get things right more than you get them wrong.........otherwise you'll run out of time.

I love the philosophy you just shared. Life is indeed about relationships, and I am fortunate enough to have all my important relationships intact and healthy. I feel I have been spared so I can do more of that scattering you mentioned.

The doc told me that my smooth muscle cells proliferate too fast and closed off one of the stents. He put me on a $250/mo drug (effient) to counter that action. If I have another failure it will mean open heart surgery. He also told me to read Dr. Ornish and go vegetarian and not to do the high fat, high protein diet. But in the month I have been on the Leptin RX my cholesterol has dropped from 224 (ratio of 2.8) to 180.

So here is my question: doesn't it make sense that if it isn't cholesterol or life style causing heart disease, but heredity, and if my cholesterol is righting itself on Leptin RX, then why should I go vegetarian?

After using the magnetico he immediately broke out in a rash all over his thighs and developed an orange crust on his scalp primarily on the occipital lobe area. I called and spoke with Dr Bonlie and he was not surprised at all, I guess it is common, he suggested DMSA due to detox being from metals.

I have not given my little guy any and he is doing quite well. Energy has gone up, tics are rare, no crazy behavior or rage issues. Rash was clear within a week, scalp stuff continues

He also wakes most mornings and says that he didnt sleep at all, which is not true but maybe his sleep is not restful. His night terrors and bed wetting have been rare also.....a huge plus.

Granted we do a lot of stuff with him....he CTs on occasion, decent diet, RO water, grounding.....etc Hard to say what is the biggest help but a lot of the improvement has been post magnetico....cant wait to have one myself!

Hi Nicole,

We have been using the 5G Magnetico on our 3yo's bed for about 4 months now. He has/had bad allergies and eczema. Definitely noticed some interesting effects on his sleeping patterns. His sleeping was always pretty poor due top itching at night, sleeping, waking, itching, crying then sleeping, waking itching and so on. With the magnetico he slept restlessly, so much so that my wife slept with him for about a month but it was a different pattern altogether. No itching and crying, just tossing and turning waking and sleeping. She spoke to Magnetico, who are great to chat with, and the advice was to put the Magnetico on the floor under the bed which drops the field to 2G until he is used to it then bring it back up to 5G.

So not awesome nights sleeps but a big improvement over the blood and guts and crying show we used to get during the night. We were able to get some great advice from Jack which we follow as best as we can with a 3yo, epi-paleo, spring water, no lights on at night, EMF at night as low as we can make it and minimal during the day. We have seen some really interesting results, we have won back chicken, beef, oysters, lamb, pork as well as other things that he was allergic to in a fairly short amount of time, plus his skin is much much better than it was, still a long way from perfect. We haven't had to use antibiotics, since we changed things around and we are using heaps less creams, its been a pretty big shift thanks to Jack!

We gave our 6yo a burst of 5 days on it and just thrived, had heaps of energy, so much so that we will soon buy one for him as well.

Hope this helps,

# Thread: Food intolerances affect intestines and NOT stomach?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2013-10-27T20:38:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99170>

Hi guys,

Eating elimination diets and super clean hasn't helped my stomach problems at all and I think this is because my stomach has a problem mechanically. All my problems start as soon as I consume anything, if food intolerances were an issue then I assume problems would only start when the food reaches the intestines (leaky gut). I don't think they would start in the stomach. Also water shouldn't cause any problems whatsoever with intolerances however with me it gives the same symptoms. This makes me believe it is not anything to do with WHAT I am eating but just anything making my stomach throw a fit.

An example: Say I drink some water after a light meal I can feel the water go down my oesophagus and then push hard to get from the end of my oesophagus into my stomach. As soon as this happens I get an uncomfortable, pressure like, pain in my stomach, this uncomfortable pain maintains for hours after a meal, depending on size. It's as though my stomach is really full and is struggling to fit in anything more. Afterwards I will most likely experience a feeling of food coming back up but with no burning or vomiting (yet?).

Is this true that food intolerances should only affect the intestines?
Does this information give anyone any ideas what could be wrong?

### caroline — 2013-10-27T22:07:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99176>

Sean - you are really struggling ... but trying to think it thru. Sorry - I have no knowledge about this.

I hope someone else will have something to offer.

Are you going to listen to the webinar with Dr. Jackson today?

### nicld — 2013-10-27T23:57:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99187>

I would highly recommend that you find a good GI doctor. They can do a barium study to see of there is something mechanically going on.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-28T00:10:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99192>

Sean.. the more I read about your symptoms, the more I think there's a disconnect btwn your brain and gut via the vagus nerve. I'd first start w/CT6, then go back through the BG series... something in your environment is causing a disconnect btwn the two. initial gut reaction, wants me to look at light & non-native EMF - and your vitamin A cycles. But then there the bigger picture - other things could be causing this stress on your system thus resulting in the disconnect....

Do you have any recent labs? I know you're in the UK... and we need to help you figure out what's going on. I don't know if you can get your hands on one - but I'd see if you can get a metametrix/genova GI Effects stool test.

### Gracelind — 2013-10-28T04:47:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99211>

Hi Sean,

You sound like a good candidate for a barium swallow video exam. You drink a barium "shake" then the camera rolls while you swallow. You will also swallow a barium pill. This shows the passage of a bolus from your mouth down into your stomach. You may have a stricture or inflammation near the sphincter at the end of your esophagus, or possibly a hiatal hernia. It's important to check out the "mechanics" then you can work on cause for what is happening. I agree with the thought to go to a good GI doc.

Re: food intolerance I tested positive to sensitivity to foods such as avocado, cashews, celery (including celery salt), cranberries, spinach, parsley and others. Before the testing, with muscle weakness-caused swallowing problems I was often coughing and choking when eating to the point that 4 different docs were suggesting a feeding tube soon. Since avoiding those foods I rarely cough or choke, though due to muscle weakness have to eat slowly. So, at least in my case, food reactions begin from the moment it enters my mouth. It's not psychosomatic as I have reacted when the sensitive ingredient, especially celery, was hidden in the food and I had no way to know it was there.

Also, I have had reflux appear on the barium swallow video that did not burn or hurt. Actually, my last test had to be aborted due to aspiration of barium into lungs, but that's another issue.

Do think about the GI doc. Good luck!

### seanb4 — 2013-10-28T10:10:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99231>

@caroline I think webinars are for members only.

@nicld I have had a barium swallow test were I drank a small cup of fizzy drink with barium in it. The results came back normal. I did not expect this. I think maybe because it was only a small amount of liquid and it was all carbs that it wouldn't have upset my stomach as much and therefore the emptying times would have been only slightly longer than average and fell into the normal range. Maybe if I had the same test done but with an actual meal the results would be different?

@Shijin13 Well the thing that seemed to have started it all was when I took too many NSAIDs. Could this have caused a disconnect between the vagus nerve and the brain? Everything slowly went down hill from there. I am sure this is because the constant stresser in my life is my stomach. When I did the dry fast, my hair suddenly got 10x better, excema went away, skin felt good again, and I felt way more awake mentally. I am sure the stress caused by my stomach is why I feel so fatigued now but didn't feel fatigued after taking the NSAIDS.
I have been saving up for, and trying to get via the NHS the following tests: adrenal stress, T3 and rT3, comprehensive stool test, stomach ph test, estrogen and pregestorone. The waiting list is 4 months however and it is a real struggle to get the NHS to agree to doing tests. I'm slowly saving up money but it will be a while before I have enough.

@Gracelind I saw a gastroenteroligist before. First session the guy said he's going to try everything to figure out what's wrong with me and gave me some prokinetics and told me to see him again in 3 months. Prokinetics didn't work so I came back and this time it was a different doctor who just said sorry theres nothing we can do. Felt a bit short changed to be honest, what happened to the first doctor?
I find that interesting that the food intolerances don't only affect the intestines but like I said, I still don't think it's food intolerances in my case as water has the exact same affect as other foods.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-28T12:20:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99234>

I would consider two major things about how we consume energy today...

We talk alot about what and when we eat but not that much about how and where. If we still struggle with some things in life i think one should step it up one level. Consider how and where you consume your meals. Are you eating in a warm adapted place? Are you eating ungrounded? Do you eat in front of your computer, tv or anything else "modern"? If you have WiFi nearby, do you use a fork and spoon made of metals? Do you eat without stress and with this i mean stress from the fields you live in.

Many things can change but if we still do not try out to go back in our ways of doing things we cannot go forward also. The more we force feed us with energy killing elements the more we have to use ancient methods i think.

Just my take, do question everything you do with yourself every single moment.

Hey Sean so here is my N=1. I have had HORRIBLE GI symptoms for years. Like you, I felt as if the GI system would just stop and when it did, the pain would be horrible. @Gracelind Thanks, yeah mine was like sherbert (spelling???), not milkshake. Also had an endoscopy that came back normal. Maybe that was because my stomach lining isn't getting irritated, instead my stomach is having an automatic swelling reaction to any stretching of it's walls, this presumably would not leave any evidence for an endoscopy to find.
Yea it's awful, I have had stomach problems for 2+ years now and have spent most of that time on waiting lists.

@nicld Wow, glad you found out and healed your GI issues! But yeah I've tried horrendously high doses of betaine HCL, taken plenty of digestive enzymes, etc. Doesn't make a blind bit of difference. I'll check out the webinar as soon as I can.

### caroline — 2013-10-29T22:31:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99387>

### Gracelind — 2013-10-30T00:24:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99390>

Sean,

This article on Gastroparesis has some of the issues Gretchen mentioned, including involvement of the vagus nerve. It does indicate possible cause may be from meds. [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gastroparesis](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gastroparesis)

FWIW my first barium swallow and endoscopy in 1992 showed no cause for the tightness around throat I felt. Most literature at that time said such a sensation was psychosomatic ... in your head. 10 years later the next one showed esophageal dysmotility due to muscle weakness. Sometimes we sense problems long before exams can pick them up.

I hope you find a solution--sounds very uncomfortable.

### caroline — 2013-10-30T00:48:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99392>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99406>

# Thread: Cold adapting but still have a few issues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapting-but-still-have-a-few-issues.8578/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-10-27T23:44:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapting-but-still-have-a-few-issues.8578/post-99186>

Since my thyroid has really improved, I found myself looking forward to colder weather for the first time I can ever remember. I've been getting out and hiking with the temps in the 50's and I can't believe how great it feels. Just last Fall I was turning on the heat if it got colder than 72 in the car. Im loving being able to enjoy the cooler temps, but I'm still having a couple of issues that I can't figure out.

My ears start hurting after being out walking for about 20 minutes. They hurt down deep and not just on the outside. At first I thought it was when my heart rate went up, but that doesn't seem to be the case. It is really painful and I can't figure out why. Also, I can tolerate lower temps when I'm outside better than when I'm inside. Why is that?

Thanks for any thoughts.

### caroline — 2013-10-28T00:30:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapting-but-still-have-a-few-issues.8578/post-99196>

Do you wear a hat when outside? you are much more active outside? and getting grounded and sunlight........just being in nature is wonderful.

### [email redacted] — 2013-10-28T02:09:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapting-but-still-have-a-few-issues.8578/post-99201>

I don't wear a hat. I used to wear ear muffs and am thinking I need to wear them again, but I would really like to know why it bothers my ears so much.

I love hiking. I've been wearing my Juil sandals so that I get lots of grounding!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-28T03:21:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapting-but-still-have-a-few-issues.8578/post-99205>

ear muffs would be fine

# Thread: Apartment Living - Celiac Disease - Am I Screwed?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apartment-living-celiac-disease-am-i-screwed.8579/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### IoDiner — 2013-10-28T01:09:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apartment-living-celiac-disease-am-i-screwed.8579/post-99198>

Hey everyone,

I was dx'd with celiac disease at the end of 2012. I've been researching health and diet, got into paleo, then ketosis, and now here. I've been testing alot of Dr. Kruse's ideas and they have been working! I would not be here or be paying attention unless they did something for me.

So I'm wondering how badly am I screwed if I live on the second floor of an apartment in a big city surrounded by lots of EMF, assuming I follow the rest of the protocol? I do CT, very strict ketosis, eat seafood everyday, etc. How important is it for me to move?

Up until very recently I was still skeptical of the grounding, until I started practicing it. I was working on a 3rd story at my job, and I could feel how draining it was. My energy was being sapped like crazy. I couldn't wait to get back down. Somehow I was of aware the height the whole time. Btw I never would have noticed something like this if I was still burning sugar. I also can't help but realize that, although I've always had issues with digestion, I got the celiac after the first year I moved into the apartment.

I have been following Dr. Kruse's recommendations and they have really helped me along with ketosis. I feel better than I have in years. But if the EMF and not being grounded is that big of a problem for me then I would consider moving back to a house with family. How major is EMF and apartment living? I have just started to notice these little things but I realize that I am only about a year out from my gluten free diet so I can't really make any big decisions right now. I'd like to hear some thoughts from the forum.

### caroline — 2013-10-28T01:37:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apartment-living-celiac-disease-am-i-screwed.8579/post-99199>

EMF is huge .... more important than food. Have you read Dr. Kruse's book? have you listened to his podcasts?

There is a free community wide webinar today ...please listen.....

Do you live near an ocean? have you reduced fake light? You need to follow circadian cycles .......rise and set with the sun and get as much grounding as possible.

What about C/T ?......hugely important for you......

Please start a journal in the journal section ....go to top of this page and click on "forum" and then go down to the journal section and start a new thread.

Dr. Kruse likes journals so he can get some context and offer suggestions if he can......

Welcome!

### freesia — 2013-10-29T07:49:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apartment-living-celiac-disease-am-i-screwed.8579/post-99330>

The easiest way to figure the time (hour & minutes) conversion is to pick a city in the zone given in the email i.e. in this case Central - so I picked Houston. Then Google "time in Houston now" and a clock will come up telling you the current time there.
So, when I consulted Mr Google at 9.36am NZ time he told me it was 3.36pm Houston time. Which meant it was 2h 24 minutes time until the Webinar start time - of 6pm, or midday here.
Does that make sense?
FWIW the hosting site was one hour wrong. But Mr Google didn't let me down.

### IoDiner — 2013-11-03T22:30:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apartment-living-celiac-disease-am-i-screwed.8579/post-99732>

Thanks Dr. Kruse and everyone for responding. I've been so busy avoiding my computer lately lol. This blog has just started a new adventure in my life. Last night I got 3 whole sardines from Whole Foods, stewed them in water for a few hours and ate their heads and spines and everything, it was amazing!! It tasted like something very dark, like coffee or dark chocolate. But this was the best meal I had eaten so far in my gut healing journey and I've eaten alot of strange things and different organ meats.

The best thing was how, I've been slightly anemic for a long time, and having taken iron pills for 6+ months made it alot worse and destroyed my gut flora. Eating this fish seemed to be the cure for it. I looked at my face in the mirror and my gums were just bright red, instead of being pale like they usually are. I was so full of iron and life! I'm definitely doing it again. No other foods do this for me, not even liver!

Those blog posts were really fascinating. It makes sense on some nights when I was lying in my bed trying to sleep, it felt like my brain was going to explode. I don't really get migraines or headaches but I just noticed how I had this pressure in my head. After reading the blogs I have started to notice the effects of the EMFs. I'm alot more aware of them after practicing grounding and being in ketosis. I feel them in my head with the pressure, and in my small intestine sometimes, there is inflammation there. Ketosis really gave me a 6th sense for EMFs (and improved my other 5 too).

### IoDiner — 2013-11-04T10:07:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apartment-living-celiac-disease-am-i-screwed.8579/post-99788>

It was the first time I ate a fish head and I wanted to have it cooked first.

I would like to try it raw but is it safe for me to do that? I mean I have celiac and I seem to be okay with raw beef, but isn't one of the points in the Brain-Gut series that the hominids we evolved from got alot of viruses and things from eating raw seafood (which was good for our species not so great for me if I get one)? What is the philosophy here on getting a parasite from eating raw fish?

I haven't had a chance to listen to any of the podcasts yet. I'm so busy with my job and everything else.

### caroline — 2013-11-04T10:34:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apartment-living-celiac-disease-am-i-screwed.8579/post-99790>

No - probably don't have any raw yet. But what about salmon or tuna ceviche? the lemon or lime juice cooks the fish.
but - do whatever is comfortable and instinctive for you......

Are you making bone broths?

### IoDiner — 2013-11-05T00:50:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apartment-living-celiac-disease-am-i-screwed.8579/post-99860>

Oh yes I drink broth with every meal. I learned all about GAPS before I found this site, now I like to do GAPS with seafood broths and no carbs. I made a broth one so thick that it stuck in the pan when held upside down. The best one I ever made was with octopus, squid, mussels, and shrimp together. I cooked it for h ours and hours, and at the end it made this almost syrupy gel, it wasn't as thick as the bone broths but it was better.

I know I can get raw fish at Whole Foods like a salmon fillet or a sardine or w/e, and there's even an oyster bar near me that serves raw oysters, I'm just nervous about eating raw seafood. I would do it though, there's really nothing that is 'too gross' for me anymore.

### Gracelind — 2013-10-28T04:04:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/webinar-with-dr-tim-jackson.8581/post-99207>

A major message--as with most physiology/metabolic/biochem issues--gut health is key. How to accomplish that is still not clear to me, especially since we all have different issues and conditions. Time to do some GI testing for a base level and then some rereading of Brain Gut and other of Jack's blogs.

I have done the Genova hormone test that Misty mentioned and it's so informative. Results are returned in graph form with text explanations. My doc gave me the physician's version with lots of good info. One of my low results was noted that it could be a result of COMT and ... bingo! According to 23andMe I do have some mutations there. Lots of work to do to improve that profile with my results highly catabolic (makes sense since I have a degenerative autoimmune condition.)

Looking forward to the bootcamp.

### nonchalant — 2013-10-28T18:42:57+0200

> I track my micros and macros at Paleotrack.com - I know calories are not important, but to give you a range, they are usually between 1200-1700, mostly in the 1200-1400 range, protein is usually around 20-30%, fat 60-70%, carbs 3-9%. Each day I'm low on fiber, Folate, Iron, Phosphorus and Calcium and wondering whether I need to look at supplementing those.

I don't think anyone eating well should need fiber, and Jack discourages calcium supps. The others should be fine if you eat plenty of seafood.

If you can get your spring water intake up, and eat seafood daily, you might be astonished in the changes in your skin.

### caroline — 2013-10-28T23:04:08+0200

Hi Bonnie ..... the great thing about the webinars \[if you are a gold member\] is that you can listen to them multiple times and the followup Q&A are live so you can ask questions on anything that you need to.

### Bonniebelle — 2013-10-31T15:43:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonnies-journey.8584/post-99527>

Day 17 - Finally making some progress on the scale! After 4 days of weight at a plateau and after gaining a lb yesterday, today the scale showed a 3.4 lb. loss this morning! The weight is coming off slowly though... Total lbs lost so far since I started the Leptin Rx = 6.2 lbs. I'm trying not to be discouraged at the slow loss, I keep telling myself that the weight does not matter as much as my health does and each day I'm making choices to be a healthier me!

I know we aren't supposed to exercise but yesterday I had so much energy and it was such a beautiful day I just had to go for a walk. I saw this beautiful eagle soaring overhead...so majestic! I walked 45 minutes and did a few minutes of HIIT sprints at the end. Had a 45 minute CT session yesterday and an earthing session. Do you think the exercise is what caused the scale to finally start moving in the right direction? Do you think I can safely add in exercise now? I'm eating more seafood, wearing my blue light blocking glasses, getting to bed earlier and waking earlier. I do earthing and meditation every day and CT most days. I'm still not up to a gallon of water yet, I really need to keep trying to increase my water intake....the most I have been able to get down is 13 glasses (104 ounces)

So far I am noticing great improvements!

1\. Sleep is so much better and I feel rested when I wake up in the morning
2\. Energy has increased big time
3\. Mood has leveled out, I feel like I am finally at the acceptance stage of grief and I have hope in the future. Little things that set me off before don't seem to matter now.
4\. Skin tone looks better, complexion is starting to glow
5\. Carb cravings are gone
6, Hunger is gone....I really have to make myself eat sometimes

I have always been one to sweat a lot, so I can't use that as criteria that I am becoming Leptin sensitive, but all the other signs are there. Even though the weight loss is really slow at this point, I do feel like I'm making great progress! Still waiting for an appointment with the doctor to have updated labs done.

Last night I listened to a great class on intentions and affirmations that a friend of mine led! My new affirmation is " I radiate health and vitality. I naturally release excess weight."

### Bonniebelle — 2013-10-31T20:25:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonnies-journey.8584/post-99543>

Yay! I have an appointment to have lab work done next Tuesday. This appointment will be for the blood work and they will give me the saliva kit that day, so I can do the saliva testing the next day and mail it. They said it takes about 2 weeks for them to get all the results back, so will not be able to have an appointment with the doctor until all results are back. Will post the lab results once I get them.

### caroline — 2013-11-01T00:44:32+0200

You are doing so great... Has your body comp changed?

### Bonniebelle — 2013-11-01T01:07:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonnies-journey.8584/post-99581>

@Caroline - my BMI has dropped a little since my weight has dropped, and according to the body fat scale I use, the percentage of fat has dropped and percentage of water/hydration has gone up. I don't know how accurate these scales are though. Clothes are maybe fitting a slight bit different and my waist and hip measurements have decreased by 1.5 inches each...so yes, I guess that means body composition is changing for the better even though I am not seeing huge downward movement on the weight loss  I'm trying to be patient and view the journey as the process and not get so hung up on the destination! I am not the most patient person though, lol!

### caroline — 2013-11-01T02:12:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonnies-journey.8584/post-99586>

Bonnie.....my body is still changing - almost 2 years in.....it is quite remarkable actually. I would not even have hoped(at my age) for what I am winding up with.....I am loving every single second of this journey ....in so many ways.

You are seeing so many positive things happening....always remember - it took a long time to get to the broken state you are in - so it will take time - but it is so worth it ...I promise. You do have to stay committed and 100% all in.
If you slip up for whatever reason - put it behind you right away ....you don't want anything to derail you from this optimal journey of a lifetime. We only get one crack at this .... No do overs.... Why not be the best version of yourself?

@caroline, thanks for the encouragement! I'm in this for the long haul...every day is a step closer! That is great that you are seeing so many wonderful changes after doing this for almost 2 years! I think it helps when we actually learn to love the journey

### caroline — 2013-11-01T22:12:24+0200

my absolute pleasure Bonnie! Just so you know - the reading never stops...LOL. I never feel caught up ...not even close!

### Bonniebelle — 2013-11-04T17:35:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonnies-journey.8584/post-99805>

Day 21 - Weight is plateaued again, but I'm continuing to follow the Leptin Rx/Epi Paleo and adding in things whenever I can. I'm not discouraged about the slow weight loss because I realize that I did not get this way overnight and I won't fix it overnight. Each day is a new opportunity for learning and growth and I can honestly say that I am enjoying this process! I'm working on the EMF/artificial light situation currently. I asked hubby to take the T.V., DVD player, and cable box out of our bedroom and we did that yesterday. I have removed all electronics from the bedroom now. I'm still trying to get hubby to let me shut off the bedroom circuit breakers at night, but he is not open to that right now. He is more supportive of the changes I'm making than I thought he would be, but he still thinks I'm nuts about this EMF stuff and artificial light. I'm hoping that the further I get into this, he will understand how important this is once he sees improvement in my own health and life.

He (my husband) had a heart attack in 2009 and is on a boatload of cardiac meds, including a statin. I had been trying to get him to stop taking the statin and to see an anti-aging doctor about his Testosterone level and other lab markers even before finding this site and so far he hasn't, but at least he is open to having discussions about it. I asked him to view The Doctor's Hormone Replacement webinar and so far he hasn't done so, but he promised that he would. I have been urging him to take Co-Q-10 and Vit. D for quite a while now and he just ignores me usually, but yesterday he went out and bought both Co-Q-10 (ubiquinol) and Vit. D3 for himself and our son. That was a big improvement! I think it is definitely going to be baby steps with him for sure, but at least he is listening now.

Our 26 year old son lives in another town 2 hours away, but he is trying to adopt some of the things I'm doing with my diet. I gave him a crockpot and some cookware the last time he came home for a visit and he is trying to cook more meals at home instead of getting fast food and is trying to cut out grains and dairy and eat more seafood and drink more water. That is really a big step for him!

I passed on what I am learning to my younger sisters, both of whom are suffering from chronic health issues and they are trying to change their diet and adopting some of the circadian cycle interventions. Tomorrow I am driving to Montgomery for my lab work and that is where my dad lives. He is open to hearing about some of the changes I'm making and wants to know more about it when I am in town tomorrow! It is going to be hard to get my dad to give up starchy carbs and grains, so any advice on how to get him onboard would be great.

So far my 17 year old daughter is rebelling against any changes in her diet and artificial light. She laughs at me and thinks I am nuts and will not eat anything I make, except I did get her to eat some sauteed kale, some mashed cauliflower and cauli-rice and she did say those weren't bad. Still hoping that I can make an impact on her at some point before she leaves home. It looks like she will be my biggest challenge in getting my whole family onboard with these changes. Any advice on how to help get a teenager open to change in diet and lifestyle would also be greatly appreciated!

I continue to feel so much better and am optimistic that the further I get into this, the better I will feel!

### sooperb — 2013-11-04T17:59:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonnies-journey.8584/post-99807>

I sympathise with you about your daughter's reluctance to embrace the diet. I have two children, now in their 20s and 30s, both of whom have had weight issues, as we all have had really. My son took himself off to Slimming World and eventually lost his weight but he struggles with it going up and down. My daughter only once lost weight and although she doesn't know I know, she was taking a product called Alli in the UK, the one that whizzes fat through your system! Since I have moved forward in my dietary knowledge and learned to embrace fat etc. I have tried to pass this knowledge on to my children. My daughter just brushes it aside by saying she likes carbs. She uses butter thank goodness but still buys semi-skimmed milk and eats sweetened cereals. She has a sweet tooth as I do but indulges hers far more. It saddens me to see a pretty girl slowly expanding but she no longer lives at home and isn't into cooking so buys ready meals. She is aware and does look out for what's in them but she isn't to particular. I keep sending my son all the updates on eating butter etc. but he's been so indoctrinated by the Slimming World method that he can't bring himself to embrace full fat anything. He doesn't eat much bread but he loves pasta and as he lives alone, he finds those kind of quick meals useful. He does love cooking though and likes Asian food but always has rice or noodles. I can't imagine either of them would be turning off the lights or unplugging their TVs! Or my OH for that matter. At least he doesn't mind sharing my earthing sheet

I'm pleased your OH is making small changes to help you, I am working on the basis that when I make a definite real start with the leptin reset I'll end up looking so good that he'll want to follow lol.

### Bonniebelle — 2013-11-04T18:10:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonnies-journey.8584/post-99808>

@sooperb- Thanks for your feed back! It is challenging isn't it? I guess all we can really do when dealing with our grown children is to keep planting the seeds and hope that they will blossom at some point

My son could stand to lose some weight, so at least he is trying to change his diet and walk to class instead of driving now. He has gone back to college and I know it is a challenge to find healthy nutrition when off at college.

My daughter is thin, and right now doesn't have a weight problem at all. She is currently a "Cast Of, Model" for Hollister and wears a size 0-2...But I know how quickly things can go south, so I would really like to influence her before her lifestyle and environment impact her health.

Good luck with your family and with your own journey! Aren't we fortunate to have found this community and this knowledge? I am grateful to have found this site! Thanks again for your feedback!

### caroline — 2013-11-04T23:38:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonnies-journey.8584/post-99848>

You guys are great, caring Moms ..... hardest job in the world I think ..

Remember - Dr. K. always says - you can only fix yourself. All you can do is be there for your family and hope they eventually come around.

It is most important to fix yourself first - and then you can help others in your family. Dr. K. was telling us lately that he was five years into this before his wife jumped on board.

That is why he always says he is just the Dude with the flashlight ...... he can't fix anyone - not even his own family....

"When we get sick and tired of being sick and tired " .... that says it all really...

### Bonniebelle — 2013-11-08T20:17:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonnies-journey.8584/post-100201>

I had my labs drawn on Tuesday and will be doing my saliva test in the next few days. It will be a few weeks before I have the results and the earliest I could get in to have an appointment with the doctor is Dec. 4....until then I will keep plugging along. Some days I start to get discouraged that my weight keeps plateauing in spite of being very low carb and staying on the protocol. How can my body not be losing weight? Then I remember that I did not get here to this point overnight. It was years in the making and it is not going to turn around overnight. It is a process and no matter how much I would prefer that I lose the weight quickly, I have to trust the process and have faith that the weight will come off in it's own time and keep following the plan and doing what I can to support this process.

I had a good CT session this morning after my BAB and water...the water temp was a brisk 46 degrees....brrr. I stayed in for an hour which is the longest that I have been able to manage so far and this was the coldest water temp so far. I filled the tub last night and let it sit overnight and since the temperature has dropped I guess the water temperature in the tub is a little colder. I feel invigorated now but it took me a while to warm up. I can't say that I enjoy the CT sessions yet, I really really dread doing them. I have to really psyche myself up to get in. Once I'm in the cold water I tell myself that it is not so bad...I try to read, watch a webinar or do some meditation to keep my mind off the cold and how badly I want to jump out and put on some warm fuzzy pjs and dive into the covers or go sit in a sauna. Someday I hope that I will start to enjoy the CT sessions and look forward to them! Until then, I will keep at it

I'm off to do grocery shopping at Earth Fare, Whole Foods and Publix and may stop by Organic Harvest as well. Each store has a little something the other stores don't have, so I am stuck going to all of them to get all the things I need. I just found out the other day that both Whole Foods and Organic Harvest have RO water!

### caroline — 2013-11-08T23:12:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bonnies-journey.8584/post-100205>

Bonnie .... You are doing great with C/T! Reducing inflammation.

Hi everyone, I'm new to the site and just wanted to say hello. I'm a wife and mother, interested in getting optimally healthy and losing weight. I live in Birmingham, AL and look forward to getting to know the members and continuing to learn all that I can about how to live a healthier life.

I found Dr. Kruse's site a few weeks ago and have been trying to absorb as much as I can since I found the site. I believe that when the student is ready, the teacher will appear! I feel fortunate to have stumbled onto the site. I'm currently on Day 14 of the Leptin Reset, working on my circadian rhythm, doing CT and just trying to add in what I find is needed as soon as I can. There is just so much information and only so many hours in the day, and patience has never been one of my strong points, lol. I have had several AHA moments while reading Dr. Kruse's blogs and I plan on upgrading my membership shortly so that I can catch some of the webinars.

I started a journal in the Optimal section and you can read about my journey up to this point over there. The way I look at things now, I'm starting a new journey now and plan to transform this caterpillar into a beautiful butterfly one step at a time!

# Thread: Interesting Air Polltion article
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-air-polltion-article.8586/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HoneyChild — 2013-10-28T20:23:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-air-polltion-article.8586/post-99271>

Particulate Matter in the air (or PM) can be very bad for our health. Staying away from traffic as much as possible is one way to keep our exposure levels down, but what about in our actual houses? More often than not, houses are on roads â€“ and roads carry traffic. So is there a way that we can help reduce the concentration of PM that creeps into our houses?
The Big Air Pollution Experiment

Michael Mosley and surgeon Gabriel Weston help test a new pollution filter developed by Professor Barbara Maher at the University of Lancaster: silver birch trees.

We set up a row of 24 young silver birch trees in tubs along the pavement of the busy A9 in Lancaster. Behind the trees were 4 terraced houses, and next to those, 4 identical terraced houses that didnâ€™t have birch trees between them and the traffic on the road.

At the beginning of the fortnight experimental period, Barbara and Gabriel cleaned the TV screens and computer monitors inside each of the 8 houses. These were then left on standby for the next 2 weeks, and acted as very good dust-traps for the PM pollution.

Two weeks later, Gabriel and Barbara came to wipe down an identically sized area of the screens in the front room of each of the houses. Back in the lab, the magnetic properties of each of these wet-wipes was then measured in order to assess the quantity of (iron-bearing) particulate traffic pollution on each one, giving us a measure of the level of pollution in each of the 8 houses (4 protected by trees and 4 not protected by trees).

The results were astounding: the fortnightâ€™s pollution in the 4 houses with trees was 50-60% lower than in those without.

So how were the trees giving so much protection?

Electron microscope images of the leaves of silver birch trees show why they are so good â€“ they are covered in tiny hairs and ridges which help trap the pollution particles. Their sparse structure also helps keep the air circulating and flowing past the leaves to filter it effectively (rather than trapping pollution near the ground as bigger and denser trees do). Each time it rains, the PM pollution is washed off the leaves, allowing them to start trapping more. So whilst the birch trees are in leaf, at least, they are providing an excellent pollution filter. And given that heart disease (which is seriously affected by particulate air pollution) is Britainâ€™s biggest killer, a couple of small silver birch trees in your front garden â€“ or planted along the pavement by the local council â€“ could be a huge boost to your health.

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p01dgd9c/features/pollutionexperiment](http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p01dgd9c/features/pollutionexperiment)

### HoneyChild — 2013-10-28T20:26:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-air-polltion-article.8586/post-99272>

However, by fitting people with personal pollution monitors, Rossa’s team have managed to find simple ways in which we can all reduce our PM exposure when outside:

They found that children were at risk of exposure when being dropped at the school gates. They breathe fast and being shorter, are nearer the exhaust pipes of cars. At the school gates, cars are often left idling and particulate pollution can build up. So a quick drop-off, and fewer cars at the school gates is important.

People are exposed to a lot of pollution when driving. This could be because air is drawn into the car from directly near the exhaust fumes of the car in front. So leave as much room as you can behind the car in front, and keep windows and air vents closed when in heavy traffic.

When walking, keep on the side of the pavement furthest from the traffic, and choose quieter back streets when possible. And when cycling, also avoid the most traffic-heavy roads if you can.

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p01dgd9c/features/pollution](http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p01dgd9c/features/pollution)

Where I live now - there are lots of conifer trees etc. and shrubs and flowers and grass and the ocean is 10 minutes away.
There are a ton of birds ... and every morning I wake to the sound of birds singing and I can go out on my balcony and see the ocean. There is such a sense of peace and calmness..... now I know why...

there are lots of units around but everyone is so friendly here and there is a great mix of people of all ages. I have always said that this is the nicest place I have ever lived .... so everyone is getting a huge payoff.

I have always consistently and persistently said, since I came here, that there is so much positive energy here....

From the first seconds of looking at this apartment - I knew this is where I wanted to be and I was also positive that I would get it. I filled out the paper work and dropped it off at the office and knew I would hear from them before I got home..... and I did!

So - we really have to pay attention to our initial instincts and our feelings of energy and wellness.

I think vibrancy and energy are very tangible if we allow ourself the experience.

This is all in hindsight of course ..... but now I understand it ....That in itself is Quantum magic....

### freesia — 2013-10-29T02:28:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-air-polltion-article.8586/post-99311>

> [HoneyChild said:](/goto/post?id=99271)
>
> Particulate Matter in the air (or PM) can be very bad for our health. Staying away from traffic as much as possible is one way to keep our exposure levels down, but what about in our actual houses? More often than not, houses are on roads – and roads carry traffic. So is there a way that we can help reduce the concentration of PM that creeps into our houses?
> The Big Air Pollution Experiment
>
> Michael Mosley and surgeon Gabriel Weston help test a new pollution filter developed by Professor Barbara Maher at the University of Lancaster: silver birch trees.
>
> We set up a row of 24 young silver birch trees in tubs along the pavement of the busy A9 in Lancaster. Behind the trees were 4 terraced houses, and next to those, 4 identical terraced houses that didn’t have birch trees between them and the traffic on the road.
>
> At the beginning of the fortnight experimental period, Barbara and Gabriel cleaned the TV screens and computer monitors inside each of the 8 houses. These were then left on standby for the next 2 weeks, and acted as very good dust-traps for the PM pollution.
>
> Two weeks later, Gabriel and Barbara came to wipe down an identically sized area of the screens in the front room of each of the houses. Back in the lab, the magnetic properties of each of these wet-wipes was then measured in order to assess the quantity of (iron-bearing) particulate traffic pollution on each one, giving us a measure of the level of pollution in each of the 8 houses (4 protected by trees and 4 not protected by trees).
>
> The results were astounding: the fortnight’s pollution in the 4 houses with trees was 50-60% lower than in those without.
>
> So how were the trees giving so much protection?
>
> Electron microscope images of the leaves of silver birch trees show why they are so good – they are covered in tiny hairs and ridges which help trap the pollution particles. Their sparse structure also helps keep the air circulating and flowing past the leaves to filter it effectively (rather than trapping pollution near the ground as bigger and denser trees do). Each time it rains, the PM pollution is washed off the leaves, allowing them to start trapping more. So whilst the birch trees are in leaf, at least, they are providing an excellent pollution filter. And given that heart disease (which is seriously affected by particulate air pollution) is Britain’s biggest killer, a couple of small silver birch trees in your front garden – or planted along the pavement by the local council – could be a huge boost to your health.
>
> [http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p01dgd9c/features/pollutionexperiment](http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p01dgd9c/features/pollutionexperiment)

Fascinating! Thanks.

When we collect this information in the form of electromagnetic radiation from sunlight we are recollecting part of the beginning of the universe.......to recreate how the big bang happened. In the beginning.......all matter was life as a vase. At the big bang God dropped his vase. The parts of the vase scattered.........now we are the fundamental particles and single atoms of that dropped vase that are trying to put the vase back together........both cognitively and scientifically..........to do it........we need a lot of energy to overcome the entropy, the randomness, the chaos of the single event when God dropped his vase..........

This implies there is information to be gained in every part of the subatomic world. Life is designed around collecting that information. It means that molecules have information in them. It means when we collect enough particles of the vase........we acquire information to make us conscious. How is that for thought provoking...............This is why we need CERN. We need the Higgs boson to be further energized.........I think there is more to the Higgs field because we do not know a damn thing about gravity or dark energy............Higgs was discovered........but it has more questions for us than answers.......and y'all know I like questions. They keep me fresh and curious.

The homo sapien tissues is a system of excitable media we call semiconductors. They are excitable cells and tissues poised to respond specifically and disproportionately to weak low frequency electromagnetic signals, because large amounts of energy are stored everywhere in our tissues automatically and this acts to amplify and rectify the weak electromagnetic signals Becker found in the 1960's into the macroscopic actions we all see in life today. Sleep is the greatest example of a macroscopic quantum event. Mitochondrial respiration to make ATP and O2 is another.

Life is designed to catch the information and energy found in photons. These photons contain information and energy from the day God decided to drop that vase.............and I am trying to figure out how the standard model of physics and quantum mechanics dictate how our biology works. Pretty deep stuff.
